iso-27001.full_name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Information security management systems — Requirements

pb_application.why_chosen: Foundational standard. ISO 27701 (privacy) and ISO 42001 (AI management) extend it; SOC 2 overlaps roughly 70-80%; COBIT and King V map onto it from above. Lawyers, financial services and healthcare buyers ask for 27001 in vendor due diligence as a baseline.

on_prem.rationale: Customer hardware. PrivateBox scope: hardened OS image, supply chain integrity, update channel security, admin tooling, customer-managed risk treatment guidance. Customer is data controller and processor on their own hardware. PB retains a management plane for telemetry and support which remains in scope.

ai_factory.rationale: Partner DC hosting. PrivateBox operates ringfenced infrastructure. Partner DC is sub-processor — full chain governance applies. Cross-border transfer rules apply if the partner DC is offshore.

top_risks: Open-source model and weights supply chain (HuggingFace, model registries) → A.5.21, A.8.30
Partner DC sub-processor breach in AI Factory model → A.5.19-22
Multi-tenant isolation weakness in management plane → A.8.31, A.8.33
DGX Spark hardware lifecycle and secure disposal → A.7.10, A.7.14
Remote support access into customer on-prem environments → A.5.15, A.8.2, A.6.7

soa_guidance.principles:
The Statement of Applicability is the bridge between scope, risk
treatment and control implementation. Auditors read it first. PrivateBox should
treat the SoA as a traceable record of risk-based decisions, not a checklist.

Required SoA columns at minimum: control ID; title; status; applicability
decision; risk-based justification; owner; evidence reference; shared-
responsibility note. PrivateBox should add a deployment-model note explaining
whether the control is PB-owned, CL-owned or SH for each business model.
That makes the SoA defensible in enterprise procurement and easy to defend in
audit.
soa_guidance.na_justification_rules:
Justify non-applicability sparingly and specifically. "Not applicable
to our business" is not enough. Tie every exclusion to scope boundaries,
architecture facts, accepted residual risk, or alternative controls. Examples:

- A.5.23 may be excluded only if no in-scope cloud or SaaS is used at all.
- A.7.11 may be largely customer-owned for on-prem but still partly applicable
  to PrivateBox premises or labs.
- A.8.30 may be excluded only if no outsourced development exists in scope.
soa_guidance.honesty_rules:
The SoA must not imply controls are implemented when they are
planned, customer-owned, or out of scope. Show the implemented/planned state
honestly. Preserve a line back to the risk register. Point to evidence that
exists now, not evidence we hope to gather.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB offices, labs, storage areas
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer dataroom, site perimeter, local entry controls
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Contract should state site-security expectations and evidence needed.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB staff endpoints, support laptops, engineering devices
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer user/admin workstations unless managed by PB
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: For high-risk support, PB should define minimum endpoint requirements for any device used to access customer systems.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB corporate and support systems in scope
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer-hosted runtime if contract says customer operates it
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: If PB offers managed backup or recovery guidance, ownership becomes shared and must be explicit.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB internal admins and support engineers
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer internal admins
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Break-glass, approval and logging responsibilities should be split clearly.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB systems under its control
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer infrastructure logs if customer operates hosts
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: For shared systems, agree retention, access and evidence-preservation expectations.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB code, dependencies, images, build pipeline, support tooling
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer OS/network layers that PB does not manage
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Product patching and environment patching often split; do not blur them.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB incidents and PB-side response role
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer site incident commander for local infrastructure
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Escalation and evidence-handling should be jointly modelled in playbooks.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB software releases, configs, support tools
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer local infrastructure and operational changes
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Shared where PB changes affect customer runtime or recovery assumptions.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB removable media, staging assets, support exports
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer media and dataroom assets
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Shared when hardware is shipped, returned or serviced between parties.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB support systems and any managed central services
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer-hosted runtime if customer operates it
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Recovery objectives and who tests what must be explicit.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB-owned equipment and records
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer-owned hosting equipment
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Shared when PB services customer-hosted hardware or handles returned devices.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB support process, tools, credentials and logs
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer approval process and local-session oversight
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: One of the highest-value shared-control areas for PrivateBox to define well.

evidence_families.name: Governance documents
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Establishes the management system architecture.

evidence_families.name: Risk artefacts
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Shows risk-based decision-making.

evidence_families.name: SoA artefacts
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Proves Annex A decisions are deliberate.

evidence_families.name: Asset records
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Validates scope and protection coverage.

evidence_families.name: Access evidence
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Tests least privilege in practice.

evidence_families.name: Supplier files
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Tests third-party risk governance.

evidence_families.name: SDLC evidence
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Demonstrates secure development is real.

evidence_families.name: Change records
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Proves controlled change.

evidence_families.name: Monitoring and logging
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Shows detection and accountability.

evidence_families.name: Incident evidence
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Tests preparedness and response maturity.

evidence_families.name: Continuity evidence
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Shows resilience is operational, not promised.

evidence_families.name: Training evidence
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Supports clauses 7.2 and 7.3.

evidence_families.name: Review evidence
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Core to clauses 9.2 and 9.3.

evidence_families.name: Improvement evidence
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Tests clause 10 credibility.

evidence_families.name: Customer deployment evidence
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Critical for the on-prem model.

open_questions.question: Which DC partner(s) for AI Factory deployments?
open_questions.notes: Partner DC selection cascades into physical perimeter, entry, monitoring, environmental, equipment siting, utilities, cabling, and maintenance controls. Until selected, AI Factory ownership of A.7 controls is pending.

open_questions.question: Which legal jurisdiction(s) for AI Factory hosting?
open_questions.notes: Jurisdiction determines applicable laws (POPIA only vs POPIA+GDPR+others), cross-border transfer rules, and data residency commitments to customers.

open_questions.question: Tenancy isolation posture for AI Factory?
candidate_answers: Single-tenant ringfenced (1 customer per ringfenced infra)
Hybrid (single-tenant for high-sensitivity, multi-tenant for others)
open_questions.notes: Tenancy model determines multi-tenant isolation control posture, test data handling, and environment separation requirements.

open_questions.question: How is the partner DC governed contractually as a sub-processor?
open_questions.notes: Partner DC becomes a sub-processor under AI Factory model. Contractual posture determines supplier governance controls applicable to that relationship.

open_questions.question: Data residency commitments to AI Factory customers?
open_questions.notes: Residency commitments shape customer-facing claims, contract templates, and constrain DC partner selection. Cross-references oq-partner-dc-selection.

open_questions.question: Who handles which incidents in the AI Factory operational stack?
candidate_answers: PB owns all incidents, partner DC notifies PB
Layered: partner DC owns physical/network, PB owns app/AI
open_questions.notes: Incident response split affects shared-responsibility documentation, incident playbooks, and customer-facing incident notification commitments.

open_questions.question: What does the customer contract look like for AI Factory?
candidate_answers: Adapted on-prem template with hosting addendum
open_questions.notes: Contract template determines what PB commits to, what's customer-owned, what's the partner DC's obligation, and how regulatory/legal duties allocate.

open_questions.question: What exactly is the AI Factory product?
candidate_answers: Ringfenced seats on shared partner DC infra
Dedicated server(s) per customer at partner DC
Customer-owned hardware co-located at partner DC
open_questions.notes: The AI Factory product definition cascades into nearly every framework. Until defined, AI Factory scope decisions are placeholders.

open_questions.question: Customer exit / data portability mechanism for AI Factory?
open_questions.notes: Exit/portability affects continuity, deletion controls, customer trust, and contract termination clauses.

open_questions.question: Hardware lifecycle for AI Factory (procurement, sizing, disposal)?
candidate_answers: PB procures and owns; partner DC operates
Partner DC procures; PB pays operating fee
open_questions.notes: Hardware lifecycle determines secure disposal posture, equipment maintenance responsibilities, and storage media controls.

requirements.title: Understanding the organisation and its context
requirements.requirement: Determine external and internal issues that affect ISMS outcomes.
requirements.auditor_intent: Auditor checks the ISMS matches real business context.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Maintain a context register covering local-hosting model, optional remote support, AI misuse risks, regulated sectors, supplier dependencies and climate relevance.
requirements.operational_evidence: Leadership interviews; evidence that context feeds scope and risk criteria.
requirements.failure_modes: Generic text; no local-hosting detail; no climate consideration.
requirements.remediation: Formalise context review and change triggers.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's ISMS is aligned to ISO/IEC 27001 clause 4.1.
requirements.scope_note: Treat customer-site realities as context inputs.

requirements.title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
requirements.requirement: Identify relevant interested parties, their requirements, and which requirements are addressed by the ISMS.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies the ISMS reflects mapped interested-party requirements.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Map customer, regulator, insurer, staff, supplier and hosting-site requirements; for PrivateBox include procurement demands for on-prem deployment, support access, privacy and uptime.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence that requirements map into scope, objectives and controls.
requirements.failure_modes: No customer security obligations captured; no split between PB and client duties.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's ISMS is designed against clause 4.2.
requirements.scope_note: (SoA note) interested-party requirements help justify Annex A applicability.

requirements.title: Determining the scope of the ISMS
requirements.requirement: Define the boundaries and applicability of the ISMS.
requirements.auditor_intent: Auditors look for scope that is precise, coherent and defensible.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Scope should cover product development, build pipeline, admin/support tooling, deployment services, update mechanisms, documentation, corporate support functions and any optional remote access. Customer-hosted runtime must be explicitly described as client-owned or shared where appropriate.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence that assets, people, sites and suppliers referenced in the ISMS match the scope.
requirements.failure_modes: Vague scope; hidden exclusions; support plane omitted.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox has defined an ISO/IEC 27001-aligned ISMS scope for its locally hosted AI service model.

requirements.title: Information security management system
requirements.requirement: Establish, implement, maintain and continually improve the ISMS, including process interactions.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies processes exist, interact, and are used in practice — not just on paper.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Build a process map for governance, risk, HR, supplier management, development, release, support, incident response, audit and improvement.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence that processes exist, interact and are used in practice.
requirements.failure_modes: Documents exist but no process owners or no interaction map.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates an ISMS designed in line with clause 4.4.
requirements.scope_note: (SoA note) not used directly, but process interactions must support selected controls.

requirements.title: Leadership and commitment
requirements.requirement: Top management must lead and support the ISMS.
requirements.auditor_intent: Auditors look for real ownership, not delegation in name only.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Board/founders must approve scope, policy, objectives, resourcing and reviews; leadership must understand product-specific risks of support access, AI misuse, updates and customer trust.
requirements.operational_evidence: Leadership interviews, evidence of decisions and resourcing.
requirements.failure_modes: "Security belongs to IT"; no executive evidence.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox leadership governs the ISMS in line with clause 5.1.

requirements.title: Policy
requirements.requirement: Establish an information security policy appropriate to purpose, objectives, applicable requirements and continual improvement.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies policy is approved, communicated, and current.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Issue a top-level security policy plus topic-specific policies for access, SDLC, support, incident response, suppliers, privacy and acceptable AI use.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence of communication and availability to relevant parties.
requirements.failure_modes: Boilerplate; unsigned; not communicated.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox has implemented policies consistent with clause 5.2.
requirements.scope_note: (SoA note) selected controls should point to policy references.

requirements.title: Organisational roles, responsibilities and authorities
requirements.requirement: Assign and communicate roles relevant to information security, including ISMS conformance and reporting.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies role clarity through staff interviews and reporting evidence.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Define owner roles for ISMS lead, engineering, support, infrastructure, HR, legal, privacy, incident lead and customer-contact roles.
requirements.operational_evidence: Staff interviews confirm role clarity; evidence of reporting to leadership.
requirements.failure_modes: Shared inboxes; informal ownership; no escalation paths.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox has assigned security responsibilities in line with clause 5.3.
requirements.scope_note: Distinguish PB roles from customer administrator duties.

requirements.title: General
requirements.requirement: Plan actions to address risks and opportunities from context and interested-party requirements.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies planning items are integrated into ISMS processes, not parked in a register.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Maintain an ISMS planning register that captures both risks and improvement opportunities, including product hardening, evidence automation and support-model improvements.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence items are integrated into ISMS processes.
requirements.failure_modes: Risk captured but no action integration.
requirements.remediation: Link actions to owners and milestones.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses risk- and opportunity-based planning consistent with clause 6.1.1.

requirements.title: Information security risk assessment
requirements.requirement: Define and apply a repeatable risk-assessment process with criteria, risk owners, analysis and prioritisation.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies live risk register with owners, scoring and review history.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use a documented method that assesses confidentiality, integrity and availability impacts for product, support, data flows, build systems and deployment patterns; include AI-specific failure modes and customer-site dependencies.
requirements.operational_evidence: Live risk register with owners, scoring, review history.
requirements.failure_modes: Inconsistent scoring; no owners; no re-assessment on change.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox assesses information-security risk in line with clause 6.1.2.
requirements.scope_note: (SoA note) this row is the foundation for Annex A selection.

requirements.title: Information security risk treatment
requirements.requirement: Select treatment options, determine necessary controls, compare with Annex A, produce the SoA, create a treatment plan and obtain approvals.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit traces every selected control through risk → treatment → SoA → implementation evidence.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Build a traceable chain: risk → treatment decision → control selection → SoA entry → implementation evidence → residual-risk acceptance. Add extra AI and product-security controls where Annex A alone is insufficient.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence that selected controls are implemented or planned and exclusions are justified.
requirements.cadence: Review with every significant risk or scope change
requirements.failure_modes: Checklist SoA; exclusions without rationale; no residual-risk sign-off.
requirements.remediation: Link every SoA line to risk treatment.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains an ISO/IEC 27001-aligned SoA and treatment plan.
requirements.scope_note: Controls can be additional to Annex A where needed.

requirements.title: Information security objectives and planning to achieve them
requirements.requirement: Set measurable objectives, monitor them, communicate them and plan how to achieve them.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies objectives are measurable, tracked, and reviewed by leadership.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use objectives such as privileged-access review completion, patch SLA adherence, backup restore success, incident-response timing, training completion and control-testing coverage.
requirements.operational_evidence: Trend reports, dashboards, management review inputs.
requirements.failure_modes: Vague goals; no metrics; no evaluation method.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox manages information-security objectives in line with clause 6.2.

requirements.title: Planning of changes
requirements.requirement: Changes to the ISMS must be planned.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit traces changes through pre-change review, security impact, approvals and follow-up.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Apply formal change planning to scope changes, new support models, new integrations, customer telemetry options, new AI components, supplier changes and office/site changes.
requirements.operational_evidence: Change records show pre-change review, security impact, approvals and follow-up.
requirements.failure_modes: Informal changes; no security review; no unintended-consequence review.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox plans ISMS changes in line with clause 6.3.
requirements.scope_note: Especially important for new deployments and support options.

requirements.title: Resources
requirements.requirement: Determine and provide resources needed for the ISMS.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies capacity to deliver required controls.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Resource budget for security tooling, staff, training, audits, penetration testing, backup testing and evidence management.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence of capacity to deliver required controls.
requirements.failure_modes: Unfunded controls; single points of failure.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox provides ISMS resources consistent with clause 7.1.

requirements.title: Competence
requirements.requirement: Ensure people affecting security performance are competent and retain evidence.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit samples competence evidence by role.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Define competence needs for developers, support admins, incident responders, internal auditors and managers; include secure-development and on-prem deployment knowledge.
requirements.operational_evidence: Sampling of staff competence evidence.
requirements.failure_modes: No role-specific competence baseline.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains role-based information-security competence in line with clause 7.2.

requirements.title: Awareness
requirements.requirement: People must understand the policy, their contribution, and implications of nonconformity.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit checks campaign completion, testing results, and observed behaviour.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Run awareness tailored to engineering, support, sales and leadership; include phishing, local-hosting handling, remote-support boundaries and sensitive-customer scenarios.
requirements.operational_evidence: Campaign completion, testing results, observed behaviour.
requirements.failure_modes: One-size-fits-all slides; no completion evidence.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox runs security awareness consistent with clause 7.3.

requirements.title: Communication
requirements.requirement: Determine internal and external communications relevant to the ISMS.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies communications happen in events and routine reviews.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Set formal communications for incidents, customer advisories, vulnerability notifications, policy updates, supplier escalations and audit reporting.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence of communications in events and routine reviews.
requirements.failure_modes: No owner; no customer comms path.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox has defined ISMS communications in line with clause 7.4.

requirements.title: Documented information — general
requirements.requirement: The ISMS must include required documented information and other documentation necessary for effectiveness.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit checks required docs exist and are actually used.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Maintain a controlled documentation set sized to the actual business, not a template bundle alone; include supportable records for on-prem deployments and customer hand-offs.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence that required docs exist and are actually used.
requirements.failure_modes: Missing mandatory artefacts; obsolete duplicates.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains documented information consistent with clause 7.5.1.

requirements.title: Creating and updating
requirements.requirement: Documented information must be properly identified, formatted and approved.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies documents are approved before issue.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Apply version control, ownership, review and approval to all ISMS artefacts including customer-facing security docs and deployment runbooks.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence that documents are approved before issue.
requirements.failure_modes: Unapproved docs in active use.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox controls document creation and update in line with clause 7.5.2.

requirements.title: Control of documented information
requirements.requirement: Documents must be available when needed and protected against loss, misuse, integrity issues and uncontrolled changes.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit checks access logs, version history and restore tests where relevant.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use access-controlled repositories, retention rules and archival for policies, runbooks, audit records, customer security packs and incident records.
requirements.operational_evidence: Access logs, version history, restore tests where relevant.
requirements.failure_modes: Shared-drive chaos; broken retention; uncontrolled external docs.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox controls documented information in line with clause 7.5.3.

requirements.title: Operational planning and control
requirements.requirement: Plan, implement and control processes, criteria, change effects and external providers.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies controlled execution and change review.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Define operational criteria for deployment, release, remote support, backups, access review, incident handling and supplier management; include controls for externally provided services used by the ISMS.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence of controlled execution and change review.
requirements.failure_modes: Criteria undefined; supplier controls weak; support shortcuts.
requirements.remediation: Document control criteria and exception handling.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates controlled ISMS processes in line with clause 8.1.

requirements.title: Information security risk assessment
requirements.requirement: Perform risk assessments at planned intervals and on significant proposed or actual changes.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies recent reassessments following real changes.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Trigger reassessment for new integrations, new support channels, major releases, customer telemetry changes, new threat patterns and major supplier changes.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence of recent reassessments following real changes.
requirements.failure_modes: Annual-only review in a fast-changing product.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox re-performs risk assessment in line with clause 8.2.

requirements.title: Information security risk treatment
requirements.requirement: Implement and retain evidence of treatment results.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit traces tickets, config evidence, procedures, approvals, and implemented-control proofs.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Track treatment actions to completion and evidence the implemented state of controls, not just the plan.
requirements.operational_evidence: Tickets, config evidence, procedures, approvals, implemented-control proofs.
requirements.failure_modes: Action plans without closure evidence.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox implements risk treatment in line with clause 8.3.

requirements.title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
requirements.requirement: Decide what to measure, how, when, by whom, and evaluate security performance and ISMS effectiveness.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies analysis happens, not just data collection.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Define metrics for control operation and outcomes, not just activity volume; include patching, logging health, privileged reviews, training, restore tests and incident metrics.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence of analysis, not just data collection.
requirements.failure_modes: Vanity metrics; invalid methods; no reproducibility.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox monitors and evaluates ISMS effectiveness in line with clause 9.1.

requirements.title: Internal audit — general
requirements.requirement: Conduct internal audits to determine conformity to own requirements and the standard, and effectiveness.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies completed audits and findings exist.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Internal audit scope should cover governance plus sampled operational and Annex A controls across engineering, support and admin functions.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence of completed audits and findings.
requirements.failure_modes: Paper-only audits; no effectiveness testing.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox conducts internal audits consistent with clause 9.2.1.

requirements.title: Internal audit programme
requirements.requirement: Maintain the audit programme, considering process importance and prior results; define criteria, scope and objective auditors.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies schedule adherence, auditor competence and follow-through on findings.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Maintain a rolling audit plan that covers clauses 4-10 every cycle and Annex A controls over time, weighted by risk and past findings.
requirements.operational_evidence: Audit schedule adherence, auditor competence, follow-through on findings.
requirements.failure_modes: No risk-based scheduling; auditors auditing themselves.
requirements.remediation: Formal programme ownership and independence checks.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains an internal-audit programme in line with clause 9.2.2.

requirements.title: Management review — general
requirements.requirement: Top management must review the ISMS at planned intervals for continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies top management participated and decisions were taken.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Run a formal management review rather than an informal security chat; drive decisions, priorities and accountability.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence that top management participated and decisions were taken.
requirements.cadence: At least annual; quarterly is stronger for a growing vendor
requirements.failure_modes: No formal review; no outputs; attendance gaps.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox performs management review in line with clause 9.3.1.

requirements.title: Management review inputs
requirements.requirement: Review prior actions, internal/external changes, interested-party changes, performance trends, audit results, feedback, risk results and improvement opportunities.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies the input pack aligns with the clause's mandated inputs.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Include scope changes, customer demands, support incidents, metrics, audit findings, supplier changes and product-risk changes.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence pack aligns with the clause inputs.
requirements.failure_modes: Missing interested-party changes or risk-treatment status.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's management review covers clause 9.3.2 inputs.

requirements.title: Management review results
requirements.requirement: Record decisions on improvement opportunities and changes needed.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies decisions were implemented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Capture decisions, owners, due dates and follow-up linkage into corrective action or project planning.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence decisions were implemented.
requirements.failure_modes: Minutes without actions or closure.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox records and tracks management-review outputs in line with clause 9.3.3.

requirements.title: Continual improvement
requirements.requirement: Continually improve the ISMS's suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies improvements are selected, prioritised and completed.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Treat ISO as an operational management system, not a once-off certification project; use findings, metrics and customer feedback to improve.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence that improvements are selected, prioritised and completed.
requirements.failure_modes: No improvement pipeline beyond corrective actions.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox continually improves the ISMS in line with clause 10.1.

requirements.title: Nonconformity and corrective action
requirements.requirement: React to nonconformities, correct them, identify causes, address recurrence risk and retain evidence.
requirements.auditor_intent: Audit verifies closure, effectiveness review and systemic fixes.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use a CAPA process for audit findings, incidents, missed reviews, access failures, change failures and customer-reported control gaps.
requirements.operational_evidence: Evidence of closure, effectiveness review and systemic fixes.
requirements.failure_modes: Symptom fixes only; no root cause; no effectiveness check.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox manages corrective action in line with clause 10.2.

requirements.title: Policies for information security
requirements.objective: Set management direction; prevent ad hoc security decisions.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB policy suite must cover product, support, SDLC, suppliers, privacy and AI use; customer local policy complements at site.
requirements.implementation: Top-level policy plus topic policies; approval and annual review; controlled repository.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Aligned to Annex A 5.1 within PrivateBox scope.

requirements.title: Information security roles and responsibilities
requirements.objective: Clarify who owns what; prevent control gaps.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Assign owners for ISMS, engineering, support, privacy, incidents and audits; customer admins remain separate roles.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Roles are defined against Annex A 5.2.

requirements.title: Segregation of duties
requirements.objective: Reduce fraud, error and override risk.
requirements.applies_rationale: Especially relevant to access, releases and support.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only if scope is tiny and conflicts truly absent.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Separate code approval, release, access grant, customer support escalation and evidence approval where practicable.
requirements.implementation: SoD standard; role design; access workflow.
requirements.evidence: Access matrix, approval chains, exception register.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox applies segregation-of-duties controls aligned to 5.3.

requirements.title: Management responsibilities
requirements.objective: Ensure managers enforce policy; reduce "security by exception".
requirements.pb_interpretation: Managers must require secure behaviour in engineering, support and operations.
requirements.implementation: Manager responsibilities in policy and handbook.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Management responsibilities align to 5.4.

requirements.title: Contact with authorities
requirements.objective: Maintain regulatory and incident-reporting channels.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only if no relevant authorities exist, which is unlikely.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Keep current contacts for regulators, law enforcement and incident escalation.
requirements.evidence: Contact list, exercise records, incident playbooks.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains authority-contact arrangements aligned to 5.5.

requirements.title: Contact with special interest groups
requirements.objective: Improve awareness through peer groups and security communities.
requirements.applies_rationale: Strongly recommended for a security vendor.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only if deliberately not used and alternative threat intel exists.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Participate in relevant security, privacy and AI-security communities.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox uses external security communities consistent with 5.6.

requirements.title: Threat intelligence
requirements.objective: Understand threat landscape; reduce blind spots.
requirements.na_justification_template: N/A hard to defend for an AI/security product.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Track threats relevant to on-prem appliances, remote support abuse, supply chain, ransomware, credential theft and AI misuse.
requirements.implementation: Threat-intel process; intake, triage, response links.
requirements.evidence: Feeds, advisories, triage records, resulting actions.
requirements.pitfall: Collecting intel with no action path.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Threat-intelligence practices are aligned to 5.7.

requirements.title: Information security in project management
requirements.objective: Ensure projects include security from the outset.
requirements.na_justification_template: N/A only if no projects; not realistic.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Security gates for releases, integrations, deployments and major customer customisations.
requirements.evidence: Project artefacts, sign-offs, security review records.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Project security governance is aligned to 5.8.

requirements.title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
requirements.objective: Know what must be protected.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Maintain inventory of code repos, build systems, laptops, support tools, documentation, secrets stores, test data and customer-facing components.
requirements.implementation: Asset inventory standard and owner assignment.
requirements.remediation: Reconcile inventory to scope and risks.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Asset inventory controls align to 5.9.

requirements.title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
requirements.objective: Prevent misuse of assets and data.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Rules for laptops, repositories, customer data, removable media, shared admin accounts and support exports.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Acceptable-use controls align to 5.10.

requirements.title: Return of assets
requirements.objective: Prevent retained access or data after exit/change.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Covers employees, contractors and loaned deployment/support assets.
requirements.evidence: Offboarding tickets, return logs, access revocation.
requirements.remediation: Link HR exit to IT workflow.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Asset-return controls align to 5.11.

requirements.title: Classification of information
requirements.objective: Apply proportionate protection to information.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Classify product docs, source code, credentials, customer configs, support exports and legal records.
requirements.evidence: Labels in systems, staff awareness, sample artefacts.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Information-classification controls align to 5.12.

requirements.title: Labelling of information
requirements.objective: Make classification visible.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only where other handling mechanisms make labels unnecessary and justification is documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use labels in documents, ticketing, repositories and exported files where useful.
requirements.evidence: Label samples, system configs, user guidance.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Labelling practices are aligned to 5.13.

requirements.title: Information transfer
requirements.objective: Protect information in transit and exchange.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Covers customer file exchange, support logs, backup movement, email, ticket attachments and update packages.
requirements.implementation: Transfer policy, encryption rules, approved channels.
requirements.evidence: Transfer logs, encryption settings, procedure samples.
requirements.pitfall: Ad hoc transfers outside approved channels.
requirements.remediation: Formalise channels and disable risky ones.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Information-transfer controls align to 5.14.

requirements.title: Access control
requirements.objective: Restrict access by business need.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Access model must cover product, code, support tooling, evidence stores and customer environments.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Access-control governance aligns to 5.15.

requirements.title: Identity management
requirements.objective: Manage identities throughout lifecycle.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Unique IDs for staff, contractors, service accounts and optional customer admin integrations.
requirements.evidence: IdP settings, account records, disablement logs.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Identity-management practices align to 5.16.

requirements.title: Authentication information
requirements.objective: Protect secrets used for authentication.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Covers passwords, keys, tokens, API secrets, break-glass credentials.
requirements.evidence: Vault configs, MFA evidence, reset controls.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Authentication-information controls align to 5.17.

requirements.title: Access rights
requirements.objective: Ensure access is granted, reviewed and removed appropriately.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Formal approvals and recertification for admin, support and sensitive engineering access.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Access-right controls align to 5.18.

requirements.title: Information security in supplier relationships
requirements.objective: Control risks from suppliers.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Includes code hosting, ticketing, cloud SaaS, MSPs, device vendors, model/tool suppliers.
requirements.evidence: Supplier register, risk ratings, review records.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Supplier-security governance aligns to 5.19.

requirements.title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
requirements.objective: Put security expectations into contracts.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Contracts should cover confidentiality, access, incident notice, deletion, audit rights and subcontracting.
requirements.implementation: Contract clause library; legal review workflow.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Supplier-agreement controls align to 5.20.

requirements.title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
requirements.objective: Manage technology supply-chain risk.
requirements.na_justification_template: N/A hard to defend for software vendors.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Cover dependencies, libraries, build tooling, update signing, hardware components and third-party code.
requirements.evidence: Dependency inventories, review records, signing evidence.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: ICT supply-chain controls align to 5.21.

requirements.title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier service
requirements.objective: Ensure supplier risk is managed over time.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Review key suppliers periodically and on material change or incident.
requirements.implementation: Supplier review schedule and change trigger.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Supplier monitoring and review align to 5.22.

requirements.title: Information security for use of cloud services
requirements.objective: Govern cloud-service use.
requirements.applies_rationale: On-prem product may still rely on cloud/SaaS in corporate scope.
requirements.na_justification_template: Justify N/A only if no in-scope cloud or SaaS is used.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Even a locally hosted product often still uses SaaS for code, tickets, email or CI; if so, this stays in scope.
requirements.evidence: SaaS inventory, approved-usage rules, exit considerations.
requirements.pitfall: Claiming on-prem means no cloud anywhere.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Where cloud/SaaS is used within scope, controls align to 5.23.

requirements.title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
requirements.objective: Be prepared for incidents.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Build playbooks for product vulnerability, credential theft, ransomware, customer-support breach, supply-chain issue and customer-hosted incident escalation.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Incident-preparedness controls align to 5.24.

requirements.title: Assessment and decision on information security events
requirements.objective: Triage events consistently.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Define what is an event vs incident and who decides escalation.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Event-assessment practices align to 5.25.

requirements.title: Response to information security incidents
requirements.objective: Contain, eradicate and recover.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Cover internal incidents and customer-site incidents where PB has a support role.
requirements.implementation: Incident response procedure and role matrix.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Incident-response controls align to 5.26.

requirements.title: Learning from information security incidents
requirements.objective: Prevent recurrence; improve controls.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Feed lessons into SDLC, support restrictions, monitoring and training.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Incident-learning controls align to 5.27.

requirements.title: Collection of evidence
requirements.objective: Preserve forensic and legal value.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only if justified by narrow scope and external arrangements.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Define evidence preservation for logs, devices, tickets and customer-site escalation.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Evidence-collection practices align to 5.28.

requirements.title: Information security during disruption
requirements.objective: Maintain security when operations are disrupted.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Ensure degraded operations, failover and manual workarounds keep minimum security.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Disruption-security controls align to 5.29.

requirements.title: ICT readiness for business continuity
requirements.objective: Ensure ICT can support continuity needs.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only if no ICT dependency, unrealistic here.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Define RTO/RPO for support systems, licensing, repos, update channels and any managed service used in the ISMS.
requirements.implementation: BC/DR standard, recovery objectives, test plan.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: ICT continuity readiness aligns to 5.30.

requirements.title: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
requirements.objective: Know and meet obligations.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Maintain obligations register for POPIA, labour, contracts, IP, security commitments and sector-specific customer terms.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Compliance-obligation controls align to 5.31.

requirements.title: Intellectual property rights
requirements.objective: Protect and respect IP.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Covers own code, open-source licences, customer material and third-party tools.
requirements.evidence: Licence reviews, code notices, contract terms.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: IP-rights controls align to 5.32.

requirements.title: Protection of records
requirements.objective: Preserve records appropriately.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Protect audit, HR, incident, access-review, change and contractual records.
requirements.evidence: Retention settings, archive controls, retrieval tests.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Record-protection controls align to 5.33.

requirements.title: Privacy and protection of PII
requirements.objective: Protect personal data.
requirements.applies_rationale: Usually applicable if PB staff can access personal data or process employee/customer data.
requirements.na_justification_template: Justify N/A only if scope truly excludes any PII processing or access.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Even on-prem, PB usually still processes employee data and may access customer personal data via support, logs or diagnostics.
requirements.implementation: Privacy standard, DPA handling, minimisation rules.
requirements.evidence: Data-flow maps, DPA terms, support-access controls.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Privacy controls are aligned to 5.34 where PrivateBox processes or can access PII.

requirements.title: Independent review of information security
requirements.objective: Gain independent assurance.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use internal audit independence, external assessments, penetration tests and management challenge.
requirements.evidence: Audit reports, pen tests, advisory reviews.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Periodic independent review with action closure.

requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Independent-review controls align to 5.35.

requirements.title: Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security
requirements.objective: Verify internal rule compliance.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Run compliance checks across engineering, support and operations against internal standards.
requirements.pitfall: Policy exists but no compliance check.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Internal compliance review aligns to 5.36.

requirements.title: Documented operating procedures
requirements.objective: Standardise critical operations.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Runbooks for deployment, patching, support access, key rotation, restore, evidence collection and incident handling.
requirements.evidence: Procedure samples, staff use evidence, review history.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Operational procedures align to 5.37.

requirements.title: Screening
requirements.objective: Reduce hiring-related risk.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only where legally inappropriate and alternative checks are defined.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Apply proportionate screening to staff and contractors with code, admin or customer-data access.
requirements.pitfall: Same screening for low and high risk roles.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Screening controls align to 6.1.

requirements.title: Terms and conditions of employment
requirements.objective: Embed security obligations in employment terms.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Contracts should cover confidentiality, acceptable use and security duties.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Employment terms align to 6.2.

requirements.title: Information security awareness, education and training
requirements.objective: Build human capability.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Role-based training for developers, support, leaders and auditors.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Training controls align to 6.3.

requirements.title: Disciplinary process
requirements.objective: Deter and address policy violations.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Define fair, lawful escalation for breaches of security policy.
requirements.remediation: Map policy breaches to HR process.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Disciplinary controls align to 6.4.

requirements.title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
requirements.objective: Prevent lingering obligations and access gaps.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Remind leavers and movers of continuing confidentiality and return duties.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Post-employment responsibilities align to 6.5.

requirements.title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
requirements.objective: Protect confidential information legally.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use NDAs for staff, contractors and relevant partners; cover customer-confidential info and product IP.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Confidentiality controls align to 6.6.

requirements.title: Remote working
requirements.objective: Manage work-from-anywhere risk.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only if no remote work occurs.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Cover support staff, developers and executives using remote endpoints; define restrictions for customer data and admin access.
requirements.implementation: Remote-working standard; endpoint and VPN rules.
requirements.remediation: Define remote admin controls and exceptions.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Remote-working controls align to 6.7.

requirements.title: Information security event reporting
requirements.objective: Ensure users report suspicious events quickly.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Provide clear reporting routes for staff and contractors, with special emphasis on support and engineering teams.
requirements.evidence: Report channels, ticket samples, campaign evidence.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Event-reporting controls align to 6.8.

requirements.title: Physical security perimeters
requirements.objective: Prevent unauthorised physical access.
requirements.applies_rationale: Applies to PB premises and often customer hosting areas by contract.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only if scope excludes all physical facilities, which is unlikely.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB controls its own workplaces and labs; customer controls dataroom perimeter; contract should state local-hosting expectations.
requirements.evidence: Office controls, customer-site requirements, visitor logs.
requirements.pitfall: Assuming customer site is "someone else's problem".
requirements.remediation: Define facility requirements for PB and customers.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Physical-perimeter controls are addressed with shared-responsibility logic aligned to 7.1.

requirements.title: Physical entry
requirements.objective: Control and record entry.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB premises need controlled entry; customer-hosted environments need local controls or contractual assurance.
requirements.evidence: Badge logs, visitor records, customer evidence if relied upon.
requirements.remediation: Require evidence for hosted customer sites.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Physical-entry controls align to 7.2 within scoped sites.

requirements.title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
requirements.objective: Protect physical workspaces.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB offices, engineering areas and any storage or staging rooms must be secured; customer facilities addressed contractually where relevant.
requirements.implementation: Physical security standard and office rules.
requirements.evidence: Locks, room access rules, site walkthroughs.
requirements.pitfall: Sensitive work done in uncontrolled areas.
requirements.remediation: Harden sensitive areas and document rules.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Facility-security controls align to 7.3.

requirements.title: Physical security monitoring
requirements.objective: Detect physical intrusion or misuse.
requirements.applies_rationale: Depends on site risk and security model.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only where monitoring is unnecessary and justified by low risk/alternative controls.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB may rely on landlord/building monitoring or own CCTV; customer site monitoring is usually a customer control.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Physical monitoring is applied where risk justifies it, aligned to 7.4.

requirements.title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
requirements.objective: Reduce fire, flood, power and environmental risk.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB addresses own premises and requires appropriate conditions for customer-hosted hardware.
requirements.evidence: Risk assessments, landlord/customer evidence, inspection records.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Environmental threat protections align to 7.5.

requirements.title: Working in secure areas
requirements.objective: Manage behaviour in sensitive areas.
requirements.applies_rationale: Relevant if PB has secure areas or customer sites require them.
requirements.na_justification_template: If no secure areas exist and justified.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Applies to labs, storage or other restricted areas; customer-site secure-area rules must be followed where applicable.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Secure-area working aligns to 7.6 where such areas exist.

requirements.title: Clear desk and clear screen
requirements.objective: Reduce incidental exposure.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Applies to PB workplaces and temporary support scenarios.
requirements.remediation: Start with awareness and spot checks.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Clear-desk and clear-screen controls align to 7.7.

requirements.title: Equipment siting and protection
requirements.objective: Protect equipment from damage or compromise.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB devices and any staged/customer hardware must be placed securely and appropriately.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Equipment-protection controls align to 7.8.

requirements.title: Security of assets off-premises
requirements.objective: Protect assets outside controlled sites.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Covers laptops, removable media, loan hardware and transit to customer environments.
requirements.evidence: Asset logs, encryption evidence, courier controls.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Off-premises asset security aligns to 7.9.

requirements.title: Storage media
requirements.objective: Protect media through lifecycle.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Covers USB, backup media, shipped media and any customer-returned storage.
requirements.remediation: Restrict removable media and track exceptions.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Storage-media controls align to 7.10.

requirements.title: Supporting utilities
requirements.objective: Ensure power, cooling and utilities support security and continuity.
requirements.applies_rationale: Very relevant where PB controls facilities, otherwise customer/site assurance needed.
requirements.na_justification_template: If completely outside scope and justified in contracts and site model.
requirements.pb_interpretation: For customer-hosted appliances, this is usually CL-owned but should be specified in deployment requirements.
requirements.implementation: Site requirement checklist and continuity assumptions.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Supporting-utilities controls are handled through scoped shared responsibility aligned to 7.11.

requirements.title: Cabling security
requirements.objective: Protect cables carrying power/data.
requirements.na_justification_template: If no relevant in-scope cabling exposure exists.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Usually CL-owned in customer datarooms; PB should define required standard where product depends on it.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Cabling-security expectations align to 7.12.

requirements.title: Equipment maintenance
requirements.objective: Maintain equipment securely.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB-owned devices need secure maintenance; customer-hosted hardware maintenance responsibilities must be clear.
requirements.implementation: Maintenance standard and vendor handling rules.
requirements.evidence: Maintenance logs, vendor controls, sanitisation rules.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Equipment-maintenance controls align to 7.13.

requirements.title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
requirements.objective: Prevent data leakage from retired equipment.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Applies to PB devices and any customer-returned or staging hardware; chain must be explicit.
requirements.implementation: Disposal/destruction standard and re-use sanitisation rules.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Evidence complete and linked to asset record.

requirements.remediation: Require disposal proof and register updates.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Secure disposal and re-use controls align to 7.14.

requirements.title: User endpoint devices
requirements.objective: Secure endpoints used by staff.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB controls employee endpoints even if product is on-prem; customer endpoints are separate unless managed by PB.
requirements.evidence: Device inventory, config compliance, EDR coverage.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Endpoint controls align to 8.1.

requirements.title: Privileged access rights
requirements.objective: Limit and control elevated rights.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Covers servers, CI/CD, secrets vaults, support tools and customer environments where PB has admin access.
requirements.evidence: Admin list, approvals, session evidence, reviews.
requirements.remediation: Shrink admin set and add review cadence.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Privileged-access controls align to 8.2.

requirements.title: Information access restriction
requirements.objective: Restrict access to information by need.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Granular restrictions for code, configs, logs, customer artefacts and evidence stores.
requirements.implementation: Access matrices and system ACL rules.
requirements.remediation: Tighten groups and review high-risk stores.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Information-access restriction aligns to 8.3.

requirements.title: Access to source code
requirements.objective: Prevent unauthorised code access/change.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Protect repos, branches and CI credentials; restrict contractor access.
requirements.remediation: Implement branch protection and review rules.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Source-code access controls align to 8.4.

requirements.title: Secure authentication
requirements.objective: Strengthen authentication assurance.
requirements.pb_interpretation: MFA, strong auth and secure authenticator handling for admin and staff systems.
requirements.remediation: Enforce MFA on all admin paths.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Secure-authentication controls align to 8.5.

requirements.title: Capacity management
requirements.objective: Prevent performance-related security failure.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Capacity planning for PB-operated systems and clear sizing guidance for customer-hosted deployments.
requirements.pitfall: Customer deployments undersized with no guidance.
requirements.remediation: Add sizing guidance and trend review.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Capacity-management controls align to 8.6.

requirements.title: Protection against malware
requirements.objective: Reduce malware compromise.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Endpoint and server protection for PB systems; deployment guidance for customer-managed hosts.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Malware-control practices align to 8.7.

requirements.title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
requirements.objective: Identify and remediate vulnerabilities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Manage OS, libraries, containers, dependencies and appliance components; define customer responsibilities for local patching where applicable.
requirements.evidence: Scans, advisories, patch tickets, exception approvals.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Vulnerability-management controls align to 8.8.

requirements.title: Configuration management
requirements.objective: Keep secure, known-good configurations.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Baselines for servers, endpoints, CI, secrets, containers, appliances and remote-support tooling.
requirements.implementation: Configuration baseline standard and drift control.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Configuration-management controls align to 8.9.

requirements.title: Information deletion
requirements.objective: Remove information securely when no longer needed.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Covers logs, tickets, temp files, backups, test datasets and returned equipment.
requirements.evidence: Deletion jobs, purge logs, procedure records.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Information-deletion controls align to 8.10.

requirements.title: Data masking
requirements.objective: Limit exposure of sensitive data, especially in non-production and support contexts.
requirements.applies_rationale: Highly relevant if support or test uses sensitive data.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only if sensitive data never enters non-production/support contexts.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use masked or synthetic data for testing, demos, troubleshooting and analytics whenever possible.
requirements.evidence: Masking methods, dataset approvals, tool evidence.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Where sensitive data is handled, masking controls align to 8.11.

requirements.title: Data leakage prevention
requirements.objective: Prevent/detect unauthorised extraction or disclosure.
requirements.na_justification_template: N/A hard to defend for a security-sensitive vendor.
requirements.pb_interpretation: DLP or equivalent controls for email, endpoints, cloud storage, repo secrets and support exports.
requirements.remediation: Start with critical channels and secrets.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Data-leakage-prevention controls align to 8.12.

requirements.title: Information backup
requirements.objective: Preserve recoverable copies of information.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB-backed-up systems need tested backups; for customer-hosted runtime, backup responsibility must be contractually clear.
requirements.implementation: Backup standard, schedule and test plan.
requirements.evidence: Backup reports, restore tests, exception logs.
requirements.remediation: Set restore test cadence and ownership.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Backup controls align to 8.13.

requirements.title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
requirements.objective: Improve resilience of critical processing.
requirements.applies_rationale: Depends on scope and service commitments.
requirements.na_justification_template: If justified by scope, design and accepted risk.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB should define where redundancy is required in its own systems and what the customer must provide for local deployments.
requirements.implementation: Resilience design standard and architecture requirements.
requirements.evidence: Architecture docs, failover tests, customer requirements.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Tested failover or accepted risk documented.

requirements.remediation: Document resilience assumptions by deployment model.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Redundancy controls are applied where required, aligned to 8.14.

requirements.title: Logging
requirements.objective: Record relevant events.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Log authentication, admin actions, support access, key changes, release events and security-relevant system events.
requirements.implementation: Logging standard, retention and review rules.
requirements.evidence: SIEM/log configs, retained logs, review evidence.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Logging controls align to 8.15.

requirements.title: Monitoring activities
requirements.objective: Detect anomalies and misuse.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Monitor high-risk admin, support and security events; define response workflow.
requirements.evidence: Alert rules, SOC/ops reviews, incident linkage.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Monitoring-activity controls align to 8.16.

requirements.title: Clock synchronisation
requirements.objective: Preserve event chronology.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Ensure PB systems and supported deployments use consistent time sources.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Clock-synchronisation controls align to 8.17.

requirements.title: Use of privileged utility programs
requirements.objective: Restrict powerful admin tools.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Control shell tools, debugging utilities, remote admin tools and emergency access utilities.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Privileged-utility controls align to 8.18.

requirements.title: Installation of software on operational systems
requirements.objective: Prevent unauthorised software changes.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Only approved software on PB-managed operational systems and support devices; define customer guidance where relevant.
requirements.evidence: Allow lists, package controls, change tickets.
requirements.pitfall: Local admin installing tools ad hoc.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Operational software-installation controls align to 8.19.

requirements.title: Networks security
requirements.objective: Protect networked systems and traffic.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Covers PB corporate networks, build networks, admin access paths and any recommended customer network patterns for local hosting.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Network-security controls align to 8.20.

requirements.title: Security of network services
requirements.objective: Secure network services used by the organisation.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Validate VPN, DNS, remote access, proxies and provider-managed network services.
requirements.evidence: Service configs, supplier assurances, test results.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Network-service controls align to 8.21.

requirements.title: Segregation of networks
requirements.objective: Limit lateral movement and blast radius.
requirements.applies_rationale: Always needed for admin and build environments.
requirements.na_justification_template: Rarely defensible for admin and build environments.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Separate corporate, build, admin, management, test and customer-facing zones as appropriate.
requirements.pitfall: Management interfaces exposed in flat networks.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Network-segregation controls align to 8.22.

requirements.title: Web filtering
requirements.objective: Reduce web-borne threat and risky browsing.
requirements.applies_rationale: Useful for staff endpoints and support/admin systems.
requirements.na_justification_template: If justified by alternative egress controls and low exposure.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Apply safe-browsing controls especially on privileged endpoints.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Where deployed, web-filtering controls align to 8.23.

requirements.title: Use of cryptography
requirements.objective: Protect information with appropriate cryptography.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Define encryption in transit and at rest, key management, signing and certificate controls for product and corporate systems.
requirements.evidence: TLS settings, disk encryption, key rotation, signing evidence.
requirements.remediation: Document approved crypto and key ownership.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Cryptography controls align to 8.24.

requirements.title: Secure development life cycle
requirements.objective: Build security into development.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Security requirements, reviews, testing and release gates across product and support tooling.
requirements.evidence: SDLC artefacts, review gates, issue tracking.
requirements.pitfall: Policy exists but no gate evidence.
requirements.remediation: Require security gates in workflow tooling.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Secure-SDLC controls align to 8.25.

requirements.title: Application security requirements
requirements.objective: Define security requirements for applications.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Capture auth, logging, secrets, tenancy, admin control, data handling and supportability requirements before build/change.
requirements.remediation: Add security requirement sections to stories/PRDs.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Application-security requirements align to 8.26.

requirements.title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
requirements.objective: Embed secure design principles.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use principles for least privilege, compartmentalisation, secure defaults, tamper resistance and support-boundary minimisation.
requirements.evidence: Design docs, architecture reviews, exception records.
requirements.pitfall: Principles not applied to real designs.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Architecture decisions show them in use.

requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Architecture principles align to 8.27.

requirements.title: Secure coding
requirements.objective: Reduce coding-introduced vulnerabilities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Secure coding standards for relevant languages and frameworks used by PrivateBox.
requirements.implementation: Secure coding standard and code-review expectations.
requirements.pitfall: Standard exists but reviewers ignore it.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Secure-coding controls align to 8.28.

requirements.title: Security testing in development and acceptance
requirements.objective: Validate that security requirements and controls work.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, configuration review and targeted manual testing; add acceptance criteria for major changes.
requirements.remediation: Formalise test gates and severity thresholds.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Security-testing controls align to 8.29.

requirements.title: Outsourced development
requirements.objective: Control risks of external developers.
requirements.applies_rationale: Applies if any third-party development is used.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only if no outsourced development exists in scope.
requirements.pb_interpretation: If contractors or partners write code, govern access, review, delivery and IP/security obligations.
requirements.evidence: Supplier reviews, repo access controls, deliverable assurance.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Where used, outsourced-development controls align to 8.30.

requirements.title: Separation of development, test and production environments
requirements.objective: Prevent unintended impact and leakage between environments.
requirements.na_justification_template: Rarely defensible for a product vendor.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Separate envs, accounts, secrets and data; for customer-hosted systems clearly distinguish PB testing from customer production.
requirements.implementation: Environment-separation standard and release pipeline rules.
requirements.remediation: Segregate accounts, secrets and data paths.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Environment-separation controls align to 8.31.

requirements.title: Change management
requirements.objective: Control system changes to reduce risk.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Formal change control for code, infra, support tooling, customer-impacting advisories and configuration baselines.
requirements.evidence: Change tickets, approvals, testing and rollback evidence.
requirements.remediation: Standardise change records and CAB logic.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Change-management controls align to 8.32.

requirements.title: Test information
requirements.objective: Protect test data.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use synthetic or masked data; tightly control any real data used for testing/support reproduction.
requirements.evidence: Dataset approvals, masking evidence, access controls.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Test-information controls align to 8.33.

requirements.title: Protection of information systems during audit testing
requirements.objective: Ensure audit/assessment testing does not harm systems or data.
requirements.na_justification_template: Only if no such testing occurs in scope.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Applies to internal audits, pen tests, customer audits and security assessments on PB or customer-connected systems.
requirements.implementation: Safe-testing rules, approval and rollback plan.
requirements.evidence: Test plans, approvals, scope controls, post-test review.
requirements.pitfall: Test activity on production without controls.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Where audit testing occurs, controls align to 8.34.

iso-27001-cl-4.1.title: Understanding the organisation and its context
iso-27001-cl-4.1.requirement: Determine external and internal issues that affect ISMS outcomes.
iso-27001-cl-4.1.auditor_intent: Auditor checks the ISMS matches real business context.
iso-27001-cl-4.1.pb_interpretation: Maintain a context register covering local-hosting model, optional remote support, AI misuse risks, regulated sectors, supplier dependencies and climate relevance.
iso-27001-cl-4.1.operational_evidence: Leadership interviews; evidence that context feeds scope and risk criteria.
iso-27001-cl-4.1.failure_modes: Generic text; no local-hosting detail; no climate consideration.
iso-27001-cl-4.1.remediation: Formalise context review and change triggers.
iso-27001-cl-4.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's ISMS is aligned to ISO/IEC 27001 clause 4.1.
iso-27001-cl-4.1.scope_note: Treat customer-site realities as context inputs.

iso-27001-cl-4.2.title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
iso-27001-cl-4.2.requirement: Identify relevant interested parties, their requirements, and which requirements are addressed by the ISMS.
iso-27001-cl-4.2.auditor_intent: Audit verifies the ISMS reflects mapped interested-party requirements.
iso-27001-cl-4.2.pb_interpretation: Map customer, regulator, insurer, staff, supplier and hosting-site requirements; for PrivateBox include procurement demands for on-prem deployment, support access, privacy and uptime.
iso-27001-cl-4.2.operational_evidence: Evidence that requirements map into scope, objectives and controls.
iso-27001-cl-4.2.failure_modes: No customer security obligations captured; no split between PB and client duties.
iso-27001-cl-4.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's ISMS is designed against clause 4.2.
iso-27001-cl-4.2.scope_note: (SoA note) interested-party requirements help justify Annex A applicability.

iso-27001-cl-4.3.title: Determining the scope of the ISMS
iso-27001-cl-4.3.requirement: Define the boundaries and applicability of the ISMS.
iso-27001-cl-4.3.auditor_intent: Auditors look for scope that is precise, coherent and defensible.
iso-27001-cl-4.3.pb_interpretation: Scope should cover product development, build pipeline, admin/support tooling, deployment services, update mechanisms, documentation, corporate support functions and any optional remote access. Customer-hosted runtime must be explicitly described as client-owned or shared where appropriate.
iso-27001-cl-4.3.operational_evidence: Evidence that assets, people, sites and suppliers referenced in the ISMS match the scope.
iso-27001-cl-4.3.failure_modes: Vague scope; hidden exclusions; support plane omitted.
