iso-27001-cl-4.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox has defined an ISO/IEC 27001-aligned ISMS scope for its locally hosted AI service model.

iso-27001-cl-4.4.title: Information security management system
iso-27001-cl-4.4.requirement: Establish, implement, maintain and continually improve the ISMS, including process interactions.
iso-27001-cl-4.4.auditor_intent: Audit verifies processes exist, interact, and are used in practice — not just on paper.
iso-27001-cl-4.4.pb_interpretation: Build a process map for governance, risk, HR, supplier management, development, release, support, incident response, audit and improvement.
iso-27001-cl-4.4.operational_evidence: Evidence that processes exist, interact and are used in practice.
iso-27001-cl-4.4.failure_modes: Documents exist but no process owners or no interaction map.
iso-27001-cl-4.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates an ISMS designed in line with clause 4.4.
iso-27001-cl-4.4.scope_note: (SoA note) not used directly, but process interactions must support selected controls.

iso-27001-cl-5.1.title: Leadership and commitment
iso-27001-cl-5.1.requirement: Top management must lead and support the ISMS.
iso-27001-cl-5.1.auditor_intent: Auditors look for real ownership, not delegation in name only.
iso-27001-cl-5.1.pb_interpretation: Board/founders must approve scope, policy, objectives, resourcing and reviews; leadership must understand product-specific risks of support access, AI misuse, updates and customer trust.
iso-27001-cl-5.1.operational_evidence: Leadership interviews, evidence of decisions and resourcing.
iso-27001-cl-5.1.failure_modes: "Security belongs to IT"; no executive evidence.
iso-27001-cl-5.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox leadership governs the ISMS in line with clause 5.1.

iso-27001-cl-5.2.title: Policy
iso-27001-cl-5.2.requirement: Establish an information security policy appropriate to purpose, objectives, applicable requirements and continual improvement.
iso-27001-cl-5.2.auditor_intent: Audit verifies policy is approved, communicated, and current.
iso-27001-cl-5.2.pb_interpretation: Issue a top-level security policy plus topic-specific policies for access, SDLC, support, incident response, suppliers, privacy and acceptable AI use.
iso-27001-cl-5.2.operational_evidence: Evidence of communication and availability to relevant parties.
iso-27001-cl-5.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox has implemented policies consistent with clause 5.2.
iso-27001-cl-5.2.scope_note: (SoA note) selected controls should point to policy references.

iso-27001-cl-5.3.title: Organisational roles, responsibilities and authorities
iso-27001-cl-5.3.requirement: Assign and communicate roles relevant to information security, including ISMS conformance and reporting.
iso-27001-cl-5.3.auditor_intent: Audit verifies role clarity through staff interviews and reporting evidence.
iso-27001-cl-5.3.pb_interpretation: Define owner roles for ISMS lead, engineering, support, infrastructure, HR, legal, privacy, incident lead and customer-contact roles.
iso-27001-cl-5.3.operational_evidence: Staff interviews confirm role clarity; evidence of reporting to leadership.
iso-27001-cl-5.3.failure_modes: Shared inboxes; informal ownership; no escalation paths.
iso-27001-cl-5.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox has assigned security responsibilities in line with clause 5.3.
iso-27001-cl-5.3.scope_note: Distinguish PB roles from customer administrator duties.

iso-27001-cl-6.1.1.title: General
iso-27001-cl-6.1.1.requirement: Plan actions to address risks and opportunities from context and interested-party requirements.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.1.auditor_intent: Audit verifies planning items are integrated into ISMS processes, not parked in a register.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.1.pb_interpretation: Maintain an ISMS planning register that captures both risks and improvement opportunities, including product hardening, evidence automation and support-model improvements.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.1.operational_evidence: Evidence items are integrated into ISMS processes.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.1.failure_modes: Risk captured but no action integration.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.1.remediation: Link actions to owners and milestones.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses risk- and opportunity-based planning consistent with clause 6.1.1.

iso-27001-cl-6.1.2.title: Information security risk assessment
iso-27001-cl-6.1.2.requirement: Define and apply a repeatable risk-assessment process with criteria, risk owners, analysis and prioritisation.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.2.auditor_intent: Audit verifies live risk register with owners, scoring and review history.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.2.pb_interpretation: Use a documented method that assesses confidentiality, integrity and availability impacts for product, support, data flows, build systems and deployment patterns; include AI-specific failure modes and customer-site dependencies.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.2.operational_evidence: Live risk register with owners, scoring, review history.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.2.failure_modes: Inconsistent scoring; no owners; no re-assessment on change.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox assesses information-security risk in line with clause 6.1.2.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.2.scope_note: (SoA note) this row is the foundation for Annex A selection.

iso-27001-cl-6.1.3.title: Information security risk treatment
iso-27001-cl-6.1.3.requirement: Select treatment options, determine necessary controls, compare with Annex A, produce the SoA, create a treatment plan and obtain approvals.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.3.auditor_intent: Audit traces every selected control through risk → treatment → SoA → implementation evidence.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.3.pb_interpretation: Build a traceable chain: risk → treatment decision → control selection → SoA entry → implementation evidence → residual-risk acceptance. Add extra AI and product-security controls where Annex A alone is insufficient.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.3.operational_evidence: Evidence that selected controls are implemented or planned and exclusions are justified.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.3.cadence: Review with every significant risk or scope change
iso-27001-cl-6.1.3.failure_modes: Checklist SoA; exclusions without rationale; no residual-risk sign-off.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.3.remediation: Link every SoA line to risk treatment.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains an ISO/IEC 27001-aligned SoA and treatment plan.
iso-27001-cl-6.1.3.scope_note: Controls can be additional to Annex A where needed.

iso-27001-cl-6.2.title: Information security objectives and planning to achieve them
iso-27001-cl-6.2.requirement: Set measurable objectives, monitor them, communicate them and plan how to achieve them.
iso-27001-cl-6.2.auditor_intent: Audit verifies objectives are measurable, tracked, and reviewed by leadership.
iso-27001-cl-6.2.pb_interpretation: Use objectives such as privileged-access review completion, patch SLA adherence, backup restore success, incident-response timing, training completion and control-testing coverage.
iso-27001-cl-6.2.operational_evidence: Trend reports, dashboards, management review inputs.
iso-27001-cl-6.2.failure_modes: Vague goals; no metrics; no evaluation method.
iso-27001-cl-6.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox manages information-security objectives in line with clause 6.2.

iso-27001-cl-6.3.title: Planning of changes
iso-27001-cl-6.3.requirement: Changes to the ISMS must be planned.
iso-27001-cl-6.3.auditor_intent: Audit traces changes through pre-change review, security impact, approvals and follow-up.
iso-27001-cl-6.3.pb_interpretation: Apply formal change planning to scope changes, new support models, new integrations, customer telemetry options, new AI components, supplier changes and office/site changes.
iso-27001-cl-6.3.operational_evidence: Change records show pre-change review, security impact, approvals and follow-up.
iso-27001-cl-6.3.failure_modes: Informal changes; no security review; no unintended-consequence review.
iso-27001-cl-6.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox plans ISMS changes in line with clause 6.3.
iso-27001-cl-6.3.scope_note: Especially important for new deployments and support options.

iso-27001-cl-7.1.title: Resources
iso-27001-cl-7.1.requirement: Determine and provide resources needed for the ISMS.
iso-27001-cl-7.1.auditor_intent: Audit verifies capacity to deliver required controls.
iso-27001-cl-7.1.pb_interpretation: Resource budget for security tooling, staff, training, audits, penetration testing, backup testing and evidence management.
iso-27001-cl-7.1.operational_evidence: Evidence of capacity to deliver required controls.
iso-27001-cl-7.1.failure_modes: Unfunded controls; single points of failure.
iso-27001-cl-7.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox provides ISMS resources consistent with clause 7.1.

iso-27001-cl-7.2.title: Competence
iso-27001-cl-7.2.requirement: Ensure people affecting security performance are competent and retain evidence.
iso-27001-cl-7.2.auditor_intent: Audit samples competence evidence by role.
iso-27001-cl-7.2.pb_interpretation: Define competence needs for developers, support admins, incident responders, internal auditors and managers; include secure-development and on-prem deployment knowledge.
iso-27001-cl-7.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains role-based information-security competence in line with clause 7.2.

iso-27001-cl-7.3.title: Awareness
iso-27001-cl-7.3.requirement: People must understand the policy, their contribution, and implications of nonconformity.
iso-27001-cl-7.3.auditor_intent: Audit checks campaign completion, testing results, and observed behaviour.
iso-27001-cl-7.3.pb_interpretation: Run awareness tailored to engineering, support, sales and leadership; include phishing, local-hosting handling, remote-support boundaries and sensitive-customer scenarios.
iso-27001-cl-7.3.operational_evidence: Campaign completion, testing results, observed behaviour.
iso-27001-cl-7.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox runs security awareness consistent with clause 7.3.

iso-27001-cl-7.4.title: Communication
iso-27001-cl-7.4.requirement: Determine internal and external communications relevant to the ISMS.
iso-27001-cl-7.4.auditor_intent: Audit verifies communications happen in events and routine reviews.
iso-27001-cl-7.4.pb_interpretation: Set formal communications for incidents, customer advisories, vulnerability notifications, policy updates, supplier escalations and audit reporting.
iso-27001-cl-7.4.operational_evidence: Evidence of communications in events and routine reviews.
iso-27001-cl-7.4.failure_modes: No owner; no customer comms path.
iso-27001-cl-7.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox has defined ISMS communications in line with clause 7.4.

iso-27001-cl-7.5.1.title: Documented information — general
iso-27001-cl-7.5.1.requirement: The ISMS must include required documented information and other documentation necessary for effectiveness.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.1.auditor_intent: Audit checks required docs exist and are actually used.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.1.pb_interpretation: Maintain a controlled documentation set sized to the actual business, not a template bundle alone; include supportable records for on-prem deployments and customer hand-offs.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.1.operational_evidence: Evidence that required docs exist and are actually used.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains documented information consistent with clause 7.5.1.

iso-27001-cl-7.5.2.title: Creating and updating
iso-27001-cl-7.5.2.requirement: Documented information must be properly identified, formatted and approved.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.2.auditor_intent: Audit verifies documents are approved before issue.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.2.pb_interpretation: Apply version control, ownership, review and approval to all ISMS artefacts including customer-facing security docs and deployment runbooks.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.2.operational_evidence: Evidence that documents are approved before issue.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox controls document creation and update in line with clause 7.5.2.

iso-27001-cl-7.5.3.title: Control of documented information
iso-27001-cl-7.5.3.requirement: Documents must be available when needed and protected against loss, misuse, integrity issues and uncontrolled changes.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.3.auditor_intent: Audit checks access logs, version history and restore tests where relevant.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.3.pb_interpretation: Use access-controlled repositories, retention rules and archival for policies, runbooks, audit records, customer security packs and incident records.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.3.operational_evidence: Access logs, version history, restore tests where relevant.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.3.failure_modes: Shared-drive chaos; broken retention; uncontrolled external docs.
iso-27001-cl-7.5.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox controls documented information in line with clause 7.5.3.

iso-27001-cl-8.1.title: Operational planning and control
iso-27001-cl-8.1.requirement: Plan, implement and control processes, criteria, change effects and external providers.
iso-27001-cl-8.1.auditor_intent: Audit verifies controlled execution and change review.
iso-27001-cl-8.1.pb_interpretation: Define operational criteria for deployment, release, remote support, backups, access review, incident handling and supplier management; include controls for externally provided services used by the ISMS.
iso-27001-cl-8.1.operational_evidence: Evidence of controlled execution and change review.
iso-27001-cl-8.1.failure_modes: Criteria undefined; supplier controls weak; support shortcuts.
iso-27001-cl-8.1.remediation: Document control criteria and exception handling.
iso-27001-cl-8.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates controlled ISMS processes in line with clause 8.1.

iso-27001-cl-8.2.title: Information security risk assessment
iso-27001-cl-8.2.requirement: Perform risk assessments at planned intervals and on significant proposed or actual changes.
iso-27001-cl-8.2.auditor_intent: Audit verifies recent reassessments following real changes.
iso-27001-cl-8.2.pb_interpretation: Trigger reassessment for new integrations, new support channels, major releases, customer telemetry changes, new threat patterns and major supplier changes.
iso-27001-cl-8.2.operational_evidence: Evidence of recent reassessments following real changes.
iso-27001-cl-8.2.failure_modes: Annual-only review in a fast-changing product.
iso-27001-cl-8.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox re-performs risk assessment in line with clause 8.2.

iso-27001-cl-8.3.title: Information security risk treatment
iso-27001-cl-8.3.requirement: Implement and retain evidence of treatment results.
iso-27001-cl-8.3.auditor_intent: Audit traces tickets, config evidence, procedures, approvals, and implemented-control proofs.
iso-27001-cl-8.3.pb_interpretation: Track treatment actions to completion and evidence the implemented state of controls, not just the plan.
iso-27001-cl-8.3.operational_evidence: Tickets, config evidence, procedures, approvals, implemented-control proofs.
iso-27001-cl-8.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox implements risk treatment in line with clause 8.3.

iso-27001-cl-9.1.title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
iso-27001-cl-9.1.requirement: Decide what to measure, how, when, by whom, and evaluate security performance and ISMS effectiveness.
iso-27001-cl-9.1.auditor_intent: Audit verifies analysis happens, not just data collection.
iso-27001-cl-9.1.pb_interpretation: Define metrics for control operation and outcomes, not just activity volume; include patching, logging health, privileged reviews, training, restore tests and incident metrics.
iso-27001-cl-9.1.operational_evidence: Evidence of analysis, not just data collection.
iso-27001-cl-9.1.failure_modes: Vanity metrics; invalid methods; no reproducibility.
iso-27001-cl-9.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox monitors and evaluates ISMS effectiveness in line with clause 9.1.

iso-27001-cl-9.2.1.title: Internal audit — general
iso-27001-cl-9.2.1.requirement: Conduct internal audits to determine conformity to own requirements and the standard, and effectiveness.
iso-27001-cl-9.2.1.auditor_intent: Audit verifies completed audits and findings exist.
iso-27001-cl-9.2.1.pb_interpretation: Internal audit scope should cover governance plus sampled operational and Annex A controls across engineering, support and admin functions.
iso-27001-cl-9.2.1.operational_evidence: Evidence of completed audits and findings.
iso-27001-cl-9.2.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox conducts internal audits consistent with clause 9.2.1.

iso-27001-cl-9.2.2.title: Internal audit programme
iso-27001-cl-9.2.2.requirement: Maintain the audit programme, considering process importance and prior results; define criteria, scope and objective auditors.
iso-27001-cl-9.2.2.auditor_intent: Audit verifies schedule adherence, auditor competence and follow-through on findings.
iso-27001-cl-9.2.2.pb_interpretation: Maintain a rolling audit plan that covers clauses 4-10 every cycle and Annex A controls over time, weighted by risk and past findings.
iso-27001-cl-9.2.2.operational_evidence: Audit schedule adherence, auditor competence, follow-through on findings.
iso-27001-cl-9.2.2.failure_modes: No risk-based scheduling; auditors auditing themselves.
iso-27001-cl-9.2.2.remediation: Formal programme ownership and independence checks.
iso-27001-cl-9.2.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains an internal-audit programme in line with clause 9.2.2.

iso-27001-cl-9.3.1.title: Management review — general
iso-27001-cl-9.3.1.requirement: Top management must review the ISMS at planned intervals for continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.1.auditor_intent: Audit verifies top management participated and decisions were taken.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.1.pb_interpretation: Run a formal management review rather than an informal security chat; drive decisions, priorities and accountability.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.1.operational_evidence: Evidence that top management participated and decisions were taken.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.1.cadence: At least annual; quarterly is stronger for a growing vendor
iso-27001-cl-9.3.1.failure_modes: No formal review; no outputs; attendance gaps.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox performs management review in line with clause 9.3.1.

iso-27001-cl-9.3.2.title: Management review inputs
iso-27001-cl-9.3.2.requirement: Review prior actions, internal/external changes, interested-party changes, performance trends, audit results, feedback, risk results and improvement opportunities.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.2.auditor_intent: Audit verifies the input pack aligns with the clause's mandated inputs.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.2.pb_interpretation: Include scope changes, customer demands, support incidents, metrics, audit findings, supplier changes and product-risk changes.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.2.operational_evidence: Evidence pack aligns with the clause inputs.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.2.failure_modes: Missing interested-party changes or risk-treatment status.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's management review covers clause 9.3.2 inputs.

iso-27001-cl-9.3.3.title: Management review results
iso-27001-cl-9.3.3.requirement: Record decisions on improvement opportunities and changes needed.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.3.pb_interpretation: Capture decisions, owners, due dates and follow-up linkage into corrective action or project planning.
iso-27001-cl-9.3.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox records and tracks management-review outputs in line with clause 9.3.3.

iso-27001-cl-10.1.title: Continual improvement
iso-27001-cl-10.1.requirement: Continually improve the ISMS's suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.
iso-27001-cl-10.1.auditor_intent: Audit verifies improvements are selected, prioritised and completed.
iso-27001-cl-10.1.pb_interpretation: Treat ISO as an operational management system, not a once-off certification project; use findings, metrics and customer feedback to improve.
iso-27001-cl-10.1.operational_evidence: Evidence that improvements are selected, prioritised and completed.
iso-27001-cl-10.1.failure_modes: No improvement pipeline beyond corrective actions.
iso-27001-cl-10.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox continually improves the ISMS in line with clause 10.1.

iso-27001-cl-10.2.title: Nonconformity and corrective action
iso-27001-cl-10.2.requirement: React to nonconformities, correct them, identify causes, address recurrence risk and retain evidence.
iso-27001-cl-10.2.auditor_intent: Audit verifies closure, effectiveness review and systemic fixes.
iso-27001-cl-10.2.pb_interpretation: Use a CAPA process for audit findings, incidents, missed reviews, access failures, change failures and customer-reported control gaps.
iso-27001-cl-10.2.operational_evidence: Evidence of closure, effectiveness review and systemic fixes.
iso-27001-cl-10.2.failure_modes: Symptom fixes only; no root cause; no effectiveness check.
iso-27001-cl-10.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox manages corrective action in line with clause 10.2.

iso-27001-a-5.1.title: Policies for information security
iso-27001-a-5.1.objective: Set management direction; prevent ad hoc security decisions.
iso-27001-a-5.1.pb_interpretation: PB policy suite must cover product, support, SDLC, suppliers, privacy and AI use; customer local policy complements at site.
iso-27001-a-5.1.implementation: Top-level policy plus topic policies; approval and annual review; controlled repository.
iso-27001-a-5.1.external_safe_phrasing: Aligned to Annex A 5.1 within PrivateBox scope.

iso-27001-a-5.2.title: Information security roles and responsibilities
iso-27001-a-5.2.objective: Clarify who owns what; prevent control gaps.
iso-27001-a-5.2.pb_interpretation: Assign owners for ISMS, engineering, support, privacy, incidents and audits; customer admins remain separate roles.
iso-27001-a-5.2.external_safe_phrasing: Roles are defined against Annex A 5.2.

iso-27001-a-5.3.title: Segregation of duties
iso-27001-a-5.3.objective: Reduce fraud, error and override risk.
iso-27001-a-5.3.applies_rationale: Especially relevant to access, releases and support.
iso-27001-a-5.3.na_justification_template: Only if scope is tiny and conflicts truly absent.
iso-27001-a-5.3.pb_interpretation: Separate code approval, release, access grant, customer support escalation and evidence approval where practicable.
iso-27001-a-5.3.implementation: SoD standard; role design; access workflow.
iso-27001-a-5.3.evidence: Access matrix, approval chains, exception register.
iso-27001-a-5.3.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox applies segregation-of-duties controls aligned to 5.3.

iso-27001-a-5.4.title: Management responsibilities
iso-27001-a-5.4.objective: Ensure managers enforce policy; reduce "security by exception".
iso-27001-a-5.4.pb_interpretation: Managers must require secure behaviour in engineering, support and operations.
iso-27001-a-5.4.implementation: Manager responsibilities in policy and handbook.

iso-27001-a-5.5.title: Contact with authorities
iso-27001-a-5.5.na_justification_template: Only if no relevant authorities exist, which is unlikely.
iso-27001-a-5.5.pb_interpretation: Keep current contacts for regulators, law enforcement and incident escalation.
iso-27001-a-5.5.evidence: Contact list, exercise records, incident playbooks.
iso-27001-a-5.5.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains authority-contact arrangements aligned to 5.5.

iso-27001-a-5.6.title: Contact with special interest groups
iso-27001-a-5.6.objective: Improve awareness through peer groups and security communities.
iso-27001-a-5.6.applies_rationale: Strongly recommended for a security vendor.
iso-27001-a-5.6.na_justification_template: Only if deliberately not used and alternative threat intel exists.
iso-27001-a-5.6.pb_interpretation: Participate in relevant security, privacy and AI-security communities.
iso-27001-a-5.6.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox uses external security communities consistent with 5.6.

iso-27001-a-5.7.title: Threat intelligence
iso-27001-a-5.7.objective: Understand threat landscape; reduce blind spots.
iso-27001-a-5.7.na_justification_template: N/A hard to defend for an AI/security product.
iso-27001-a-5.7.pb_interpretation: Track threats relevant to on-prem appliances, remote support abuse, supply chain, ransomware, credential theft and AI misuse.
iso-27001-a-5.7.implementation: Threat-intel process; intake, triage, response links.
iso-27001-a-5.7.evidence: Feeds, advisories, triage records, resulting actions.
iso-27001-a-5.7.pitfall: Collecting intel with no action path.

iso-27001-a-5.8.title: Information security in project management
iso-27001-a-5.8.objective: Ensure projects include security from the outset.
iso-27001-a-5.8.na_justification_template: N/A only if no projects; not realistic.
iso-27001-a-5.8.pb_interpretation: Security gates for releases, integrations, deployments and major customer customisations.
iso-27001-a-5.8.evidence: Project artefacts, sign-offs, security review records.
iso-27001-a-5.8.external_safe_phrasing: Project security governance is aligned to 5.8.

iso-27001-a-5.9.title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
iso-27001-a-5.9.pb_interpretation: Maintain inventory of code repos, build systems, laptops, support tools, documentation, secrets stores, test data and customer-facing components.
iso-27001-a-5.9.implementation: Asset inventory standard and owner assignment.
iso-27001-a-5.9.remediation: Reconcile inventory to scope and risks.

iso-27001-a-5.10.title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
iso-27001-a-5.10.objective: Prevent misuse of assets and data.
iso-27001-a-5.10.pb_interpretation: Rules for laptops, repositories, customer data, removable media, shared admin accounts and support exports.

iso-27001-a-5.11.title: Return of assets
iso-27001-a-5.11.objective: Prevent retained access or data after exit/change.
iso-27001-a-5.11.pb_interpretation: Covers employees, contractors and loaned deployment/support assets.
iso-27001-a-5.11.evidence: Offboarding tickets, return logs, access revocation.
iso-27001-a-5.11.remediation: Link HR exit to IT workflow.

iso-27001-a-5.12.title: Classification of information
iso-27001-a-5.12.pb_interpretation: Classify product docs, source code, credentials, customer configs, support exports and legal records.
iso-27001-a-5.12.evidence: Labels in systems, staff awareness, sample artefacts.

iso-27001-a-5.13.title: Labelling of information
iso-27001-a-5.13.na_justification_template: Only where other handling mechanisms make labels unnecessary and justification is documented.
iso-27001-a-5.13.pb_interpretation: Use labels in documents, ticketing, repositories and exported files where useful.
iso-27001-a-5.13.evidence: Label samples, system configs, user guidance.

iso-27001-a-5.14.title: Information transfer
iso-27001-a-5.14.objective: Protect information in transit and exchange.
iso-27001-a-5.14.pb_interpretation: Covers customer file exchange, support logs, backup movement, email, ticket attachments and update packages.
iso-27001-a-5.14.implementation: Transfer policy, encryption rules, approved channels.
iso-27001-a-5.14.evidence: Transfer logs, encryption settings, procedure samples.
iso-27001-a-5.14.pitfall: Ad hoc transfers outside approved channels.
iso-27001-a-5.14.remediation: Formalise channels and disable risky ones.

iso-27001-a-5.15.title: Access control
iso-27001-a-5.15.pb_interpretation: Access model must cover product, code, support tooling, evidence stores and customer environments.

iso-27001-a-5.16.title: Identity management
iso-27001-a-5.16.pb_interpretation: Unique IDs for staff, contractors, service accounts and optional customer admin integrations.
iso-27001-a-5.16.evidence: IdP settings, account records, disablement logs.

iso-27001-a-5.17.title: Authentication information
iso-27001-a-5.17.pb_interpretation: Covers passwords, keys, tokens, API secrets, break-glass credentials.
iso-27001-a-5.17.evidence: Vault configs, MFA evidence, reset controls.

iso-27001-a-5.18.title: Access rights
iso-27001-a-5.18.objective: Ensure access is granted, reviewed and removed appropriately.
iso-27001-a-5.18.pb_interpretation: Formal approvals and recertification for admin, support and sensitive engineering access.

iso-27001-a-5.19.title: Information security in supplier relationships
iso-27001-a-5.19.pb_interpretation: Includes code hosting, ticketing, cloud SaaS, MSPs, device vendors, model/tool suppliers.
iso-27001-a-5.19.evidence: Supplier register, risk ratings, review records.

iso-27001-a-5.20.title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
iso-27001-a-5.20.pb_interpretation: Contracts should cover confidentiality, access, incident notice, deletion, audit rights and subcontracting.
iso-27001-a-5.20.implementation: Contract clause library; legal review workflow.

iso-27001-a-5.21.title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
iso-27001-a-5.21.na_justification_template: N/A hard to defend for software vendors.
iso-27001-a-5.21.pb_interpretation: Cover dependencies, libraries, build tooling, update signing, hardware components and third-party code.
iso-27001-a-5.21.evidence: Dependency inventories, review records, signing evidence.

iso-27001-a-5.22.title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier service
iso-27001-a-5.22.objective: Ensure supplier risk is managed over time.
iso-27001-a-5.22.pb_interpretation: Review key suppliers periodically and on material change or incident.
iso-27001-a-5.22.implementation: Supplier review schedule and change trigger.
iso-27001-a-5.22.external_safe_phrasing: Supplier monitoring and review align to 5.22.

iso-27001-a-5.23.title: Information security for use of cloud services
iso-27001-a-5.23.applies_rationale: On-prem product may still rely on cloud/SaaS in corporate scope.
iso-27001-a-5.23.na_justification_template: Justify N/A only if no in-scope cloud or SaaS is used.
iso-27001-a-5.23.pb_interpretation: Even a locally hosted product often still uses SaaS for code, tickets, email or CI; if so, this stays in scope.
iso-27001-a-5.23.evidence: SaaS inventory, approved-usage rules, exit considerations.
iso-27001-a-5.23.pitfall: Claiming on-prem means no cloud anywhere.
iso-27001-a-5.23.external_safe_phrasing: Where cloud/SaaS is used within scope, controls align to 5.23.

iso-27001-a-5.24.title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
iso-27001-a-5.24.pb_interpretation: Build playbooks for product vulnerability, credential theft, ransomware, customer-support breach, supply-chain issue and customer-hosted incident escalation.

iso-27001-a-5.25.title: Assessment and decision on information security events
iso-27001-a-5.25.pb_interpretation: Define what is an event vs incident and who decides escalation.

iso-27001-a-5.26.title: Response to information security incidents
iso-27001-a-5.26.pb_interpretation: Cover internal incidents and customer-site incidents where PB has a support role.
iso-27001-a-5.26.implementation: Incident response procedure and role matrix.

iso-27001-a-5.27.title: Learning from information security incidents
iso-27001-a-5.27.pb_interpretation: Feed lessons into SDLC, support restrictions, monitoring and training.

iso-27001-a-5.28.title: Collection of evidence
iso-27001-a-5.28.na_justification_template: Only if justified by narrow scope and external arrangements.
iso-27001-a-5.28.pb_interpretation: Define evidence preservation for logs, devices, tickets and customer-site escalation.

iso-27001-a-5.29.title: Information security during disruption
iso-27001-a-5.29.objective: Maintain security when operations are disrupted.
iso-27001-a-5.29.pb_interpretation: Ensure degraded operations, failover and manual workarounds keep minimum security.

iso-27001-a-5.30.title: ICT readiness for business continuity
iso-27001-a-5.30.objective: Ensure ICT can support continuity needs.
iso-27001-a-5.30.na_justification_template: Only if no ICT dependency, unrealistic here.
iso-27001-a-5.30.pb_interpretation: Define RTO/RPO for support systems, licensing, repos, update channels and any managed service used in the ISMS.
iso-27001-a-5.30.implementation: BC/DR standard, recovery objectives, test plan.

iso-27001-a-5.31.title: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
iso-27001-a-5.31.pb_interpretation: Maintain obligations register for POPIA, labour, contracts, IP, security commitments and sector-specific customer terms.

iso-27001-a-5.32.title: Intellectual property rights
iso-27001-a-5.32.pb_interpretation: Covers own code, open-source licences, customer material and third-party tools.
iso-27001-a-5.32.evidence: Licence reviews, code notices, contract terms.

iso-27001-a-5.33.title: Protection of records
iso-27001-a-5.33.pb_interpretation: Protect audit, HR, incident, access-review, change and contractual records.
iso-27001-a-5.33.evidence: Retention settings, archive controls, retrieval tests.

iso-27001-a-5.34.title: Privacy and protection of PII
iso-27001-a-5.34.applies_rationale: Usually applicable if PB staff can access personal data or process employee/customer data.
iso-27001-a-5.34.na_justification_template: Justify N/A only if scope truly excludes any PII processing or access.
iso-27001-a-5.34.pb_interpretation: Even on-prem, PB usually still processes employee data and may access customer personal data via support, logs or diagnostics.
iso-27001-a-5.34.implementation: Privacy standard, DPA handling, minimisation rules.
iso-27001-a-5.34.evidence: Data-flow maps, DPA terms, support-access controls.
iso-27001-a-5.34.external_safe_phrasing: Privacy controls are aligned to 5.34 where PrivateBox processes or can access PII.

iso-27001-a-5.35.title: Independent review of information security
iso-27001-a-5.35.pb_interpretation: Use internal audit independence, external assessments, penetration tests and management challenge.
iso-27001-a-5.35.evidence: Audit reports, pen tests, advisory reviews.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Periodic independent review with action closure.

iso-27001-a-5.36.title: Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security
iso-27001-a-5.36.pb_interpretation: Run compliance checks across engineering, support and operations against internal standards.
iso-27001-a-5.36.pitfall: Policy exists but no compliance check.

iso-27001-a-5.37.title: Documented operating procedures
iso-27001-a-5.37.pb_interpretation: Runbooks for deployment, patching, support access, key rotation, restore, evidence collection and incident handling.
iso-27001-a-5.37.evidence: Procedure samples, staff use evidence, review history.

iso-27001-a-6.1.title: Screening
iso-27001-a-6.1.na_justification_template: Only where legally inappropriate and alternative checks are defined.
iso-27001-a-6.1.pb_interpretation: Apply proportionate screening to staff and contractors with code, admin or customer-data access.
iso-27001-a-6.1.pitfall: Same screening for low and high risk roles.

iso-27001-a-6.2.title: Terms and conditions of employment
iso-27001-a-6.2.objective: Embed security obligations in employment terms.
iso-27001-a-6.2.pb_interpretation: Contracts should cover confidentiality, acceptable use and security duties.

iso-27001-a-6.3.title: Information security awareness, education and training
iso-27001-a-6.3.pb_interpretation: Role-based training for developers, support, leaders and auditors.

iso-27001-a-6.4.title: Disciplinary process
iso-27001-a-6.4.pb_interpretation: Define fair, lawful escalation for breaches of security policy.
iso-27001-a-6.4.remediation: Map policy breaches to HR process.

iso-27001-a-6.5.title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
iso-27001-a-6.5.objective: Prevent lingering obligations and access gaps.
iso-27001-a-6.5.pb_interpretation: Remind leavers and movers of continuing confidentiality and return duties.

iso-27001-a-6.6.title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
iso-27001-a-6.6.pb_interpretation: Use NDAs for staff, contractors and relevant partners; cover customer-confidential info and product IP.

iso-27001-a-6.7.title: Remote working
iso-27001-a-6.7.na_justification_template: Only if no remote work occurs.
iso-27001-a-6.7.pb_interpretation: Cover support staff, developers and executives using remote endpoints; define restrictions for customer data and admin access.
iso-27001-a-6.7.implementation: Remote-working standard; endpoint and VPN rules.
iso-27001-a-6.7.remediation: Define remote admin controls and exceptions.

iso-27001-a-6.8.title: Information security event reporting
iso-27001-a-6.8.objective: Ensure users report suspicious events quickly.
iso-27001-a-6.8.pb_interpretation: Provide clear reporting routes for staff and contractors, with special emphasis on support and engineering teams.
iso-27001-a-6.8.evidence: Report channels, ticket samples, campaign evidence.

iso-27001-a-7.1.title: Physical security perimeters
iso-27001-a-7.1.applies_rationale: Applies to PB premises and often customer hosting areas by contract.
iso-27001-a-7.1.na_justification_template: Only if scope excludes all physical facilities, which is unlikely.
iso-27001-a-7.1.pb_interpretation: PB controls its own workplaces and labs; customer controls dataroom perimeter; contract should state local-hosting expectations.
iso-27001-a-7.1.evidence: Office controls, customer-site requirements, visitor logs.
iso-27001-a-7.1.pitfall: Assuming customer site is "someone else's problem".
iso-27001-a-7.1.remediation: Define facility requirements for PB and customers.
iso-27001-a-7.1.external_safe_phrasing: Physical-perimeter controls are addressed with shared-responsibility logic aligned to 7.1.

iso-27001-a-7.2.title: Physical entry
iso-27001-a-7.2.pb_interpretation: PB premises need controlled entry; customer-hosted environments need local controls or contractual assurance.
iso-27001-a-7.2.evidence: Badge logs, visitor records, customer evidence if relied upon.
iso-27001-a-7.2.remediation: Require evidence for hosted customer sites.
iso-27001-a-7.2.external_safe_phrasing: Physical-entry controls align to 7.2 within scoped sites.

iso-27001-a-7.3.title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
iso-27001-a-7.3.pb_interpretation: PB offices, engineering areas and any storage or staging rooms must be secured; customer facilities addressed contractually where relevant.
iso-27001-a-7.3.implementation: Physical security standard and office rules.
iso-27001-a-7.3.evidence: Locks, room access rules, site walkthroughs.
iso-27001-a-7.3.pitfall: Sensitive work done in uncontrolled areas.
iso-27001-a-7.3.remediation: Harden sensitive areas and document rules.

iso-27001-a-7.4.title: Physical security monitoring
iso-27001-a-7.4.applies_rationale: Depends on site risk and security model.
iso-27001-a-7.4.na_justification_template: Only where monitoring is unnecessary and justified by low risk/alternative controls.
iso-27001-a-7.4.pb_interpretation: PB may rely on landlord/building monitoring or own CCTV; customer site monitoring is usually a customer control.
iso-27001-a-7.4.external_safe_phrasing: Physical monitoring is applied where risk justifies it, aligned to 7.4.

iso-27001-a-7.5.title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
iso-27001-a-7.5.objective: Reduce fire, flood, power and environmental risk.
iso-27001-a-7.5.pb_interpretation: PB addresses own premises and requires appropriate conditions for customer-hosted hardware.
iso-27001-a-7.5.evidence: Risk assessments, landlord/customer evidence, inspection records.

iso-27001-a-7.6.title: Working in secure areas
iso-27001-a-7.6.applies_rationale: Relevant if PB has secure areas or customer sites require them.
iso-27001-a-7.6.na_justification_template: If no secure areas exist and justified.
iso-27001-a-7.6.pb_interpretation: Applies to labs, storage or other restricted areas; customer-site secure-area rules must be followed where applicable.
iso-27001-a-7.6.external_safe_phrasing: Secure-area working aligns to 7.6 where such areas exist.

iso-27001-a-7.7.title: Clear desk and clear screen
iso-27001-a-7.7.pb_interpretation: Applies to PB workplaces and temporary support scenarios.
iso-27001-a-7.7.remediation: Start with awareness and spot checks.
iso-27001-a-7.7.external_safe_phrasing: Clear-desk and clear-screen controls align to 7.7.

iso-27001-a-7.8.title: Equipment siting and protection
iso-27001-a-7.8.objective: Protect equipment from damage or compromise.
iso-27001-a-7.8.pb_interpretation: PB devices and any staged/customer hardware must be placed securely and appropriately.

iso-27001-a-7.9.title: Security of assets off-premises
iso-27001-a-7.9.pb_interpretation: Covers laptops, removable media, loan hardware and transit to customer environments.
iso-27001-a-7.9.evidence: Asset logs, encryption evidence, courier controls.

iso-27001-a-7.10.title: Storage media
iso-27001-a-7.10.pb_interpretation: Covers USB, backup media, shipped media and any customer-returned storage.
iso-27001-a-7.10.remediation: Restrict removable media and track exceptions.

iso-27001-a-7.11.title: Supporting utilities
iso-27001-a-7.11.objective: Ensure power, cooling and utilities support security and continuity.
iso-27001-a-7.11.applies_rationale: Very relevant where PB controls facilities, otherwise customer/site assurance needed.
iso-27001-a-7.11.na_justification_template: If completely outside scope and justified in contracts and site model.
iso-27001-a-7.11.pb_interpretation: For customer-hosted appliances, this is usually CL-owned but should be specified in deployment requirements.
iso-27001-a-7.11.implementation: Site requirement checklist and continuity assumptions.
iso-27001-a-7.11.external_safe_phrasing: Supporting-utilities controls are handled through scoped shared responsibility aligned to 7.11.

iso-27001-a-7.12.title: Cabling security
iso-27001-a-7.12.na_justification_template: If no relevant in-scope cabling exposure exists.
iso-27001-a-7.12.pb_interpretation: Usually CL-owned in customer datarooms; PB should define required standard where product depends on it.

iso-27001-a-7.13.title: Equipment maintenance
iso-27001-a-7.13.pb_interpretation: PB-owned devices need secure maintenance; customer-hosted hardware maintenance responsibilities must be clear.
iso-27001-a-7.13.implementation: Maintenance standard and vendor handling rules.
iso-27001-a-7.13.evidence: Maintenance logs, vendor controls, sanitisation rules.

iso-27001-a-7.14.title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
iso-27001-a-7.14.objective: Prevent data leakage from retired equipment.
iso-27001-a-7.14.pb_interpretation: Applies to PB devices and any customer-returned or staging hardware; chain must be explicit.
iso-27001-a-7.14.implementation: Disposal/destruction standard and re-use sanitisation rules.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Evidence complete and linked to asset record.

iso-27001-a-7.14.remediation: Require disposal proof and register updates.
iso-27001-a-7.14.external_safe_phrasing: Secure disposal and re-use controls align to 7.14.

iso-27001-a-8.1.title: User endpoint devices
iso-27001-a-8.1.pb_interpretation: PB controls employee endpoints even if product is on-prem; customer endpoints are separate unless managed by PB.
iso-27001-a-8.1.evidence: Device inventory, config compliance, EDR coverage.

iso-27001-a-8.2.title: Privileged access rights
iso-27001-a-8.2.pb_interpretation: Covers servers, CI/CD, secrets vaults, support tools and customer environments where PB has admin access.
iso-27001-a-8.2.evidence: Admin list, approvals, session evidence, reviews.
iso-27001-a-8.2.remediation: Shrink admin set and add review cadence.

iso-27001-a-8.3.title: Information access restriction
iso-27001-a-8.3.objective: Restrict access to information by need.
iso-27001-a-8.3.pb_interpretation: Granular restrictions for code, configs, logs, customer artefacts and evidence stores.
iso-27001-a-8.3.implementation: Access matrices and system ACL rules.
iso-27001-a-8.3.remediation: Tighten groups and review high-risk stores.

iso-27001-a-8.4.title: Access to source code
iso-27001-a-8.4.pb_interpretation: Protect repos, branches and CI credentials; restrict contractor access.
iso-27001-a-8.4.remediation: Implement branch protection and review rules.

iso-27001-a-8.5.title: Secure authentication
iso-27001-a-8.5.pb_interpretation: MFA, strong auth and secure authenticator handling for admin and staff systems.
iso-27001-a-8.5.remediation: Enforce MFA on all admin paths.

iso-27001-a-8.6.title: Capacity management
iso-27001-a-8.6.pb_interpretation: Capacity planning for PB-operated systems and clear sizing guidance for customer-hosted deployments.
iso-27001-a-8.6.pitfall: Customer deployments undersized with no guidance.
iso-27001-a-8.6.remediation: Add sizing guidance and trend review.

iso-27001-a-8.7.title: Protection against malware
iso-27001-a-8.7.pb_interpretation: Endpoint and server protection for PB systems; deployment guidance for customer-managed hosts.

iso-27001-a-8.8.title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
iso-27001-a-8.8.pb_interpretation: Manage OS, libraries, containers, dependencies and appliance components; define customer responsibilities for local patching where applicable.
iso-27001-a-8.8.evidence: Scans, advisories, patch tickets, exception approvals.

iso-27001-a-8.9.title: Configuration management
iso-27001-a-8.9.pb_interpretation: Baselines for servers, endpoints, CI, secrets, containers, appliances and remote-support tooling.
iso-27001-a-8.9.implementation: Configuration baseline standard and drift control.

iso-27001-a-8.10.title: Information deletion
iso-27001-a-8.10.objective: Remove information securely when no longer needed.
iso-27001-a-8.10.pb_interpretation: Covers logs, tickets, temp files, backups, test datasets and returned equipment.
iso-27001-a-8.10.evidence: Deletion jobs, purge logs, procedure records.

iso-27001-a-8.11.title: Data masking
iso-27001-a-8.11.objective: Limit exposure of sensitive data, especially in non-production and support contexts.
iso-27001-a-8.11.applies_rationale: Highly relevant if support or test uses sensitive data.
iso-27001-a-8.11.na_justification_template: Only if sensitive data never enters non-production/support contexts.
iso-27001-a-8.11.pb_interpretation: Use masked or synthetic data for testing, demos, troubleshooting and analytics whenever possible.
iso-27001-a-8.11.evidence: Masking methods, dataset approvals, tool evidence.
iso-27001-a-8.11.external_safe_phrasing: Where sensitive data is handled, masking controls align to 8.11.

iso-27001-a-8.12.title: Data leakage prevention
iso-27001-a-8.12.na_justification_template: N/A hard to defend for a security-sensitive vendor.
iso-27001-a-8.12.pb_interpretation: DLP or equivalent controls for email, endpoints, cloud storage, repo secrets and support exports.
iso-27001-a-8.12.remediation: Start with critical channels and secrets.

iso-27001-a-8.13.title: Information backup
iso-27001-a-8.13.pb_interpretation: PB-backed-up systems need tested backups; for customer-hosted runtime, backup responsibility must be contractually clear.
iso-27001-a-8.13.implementation: Backup standard, schedule and test plan.
iso-27001-a-8.13.evidence: Backup reports, restore tests, exception logs.
iso-27001-a-8.13.remediation: Set restore test cadence and ownership.

iso-27001-a-8.14.title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
iso-27001-a-8.14.applies_rationale: Depends on scope and service commitments.
iso-27001-a-8.14.na_justification_template: If justified by scope, design and accepted risk.
iso-27001-a-8.14.pb_interpretation: PB should define where redundancy is required in its own systems and what the customer must provide for local deployments.
iso-27001-a-8.14.implementation: Resilience design standard and architecture requirements.
iso-27001-a-8.14.evidence: Architecture docs, failover tests, customer requirements.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Tested failover or accepted risk documented.

iso-27001-a-8.14.remediation: Document resilience assumptions by deployment model.
iso-27001-a-8.14.external_safe_phrasing: Redundancy controls are applied where required, aligned to 8.14.

iso-27001-a-8.15.title: Logging
iso-27001-a-8.15.pb_interpretation: Log authentication, admin actions, support access, key changes, release events and security-relevant system events.
iso-27001-a-8.15.implementation: Logging standard, retention and review rules.
iso-27001-a-8.15.evidence: SIEM/log configs, retained logs, review evidence.

iso-27001-a-8.16.title: Monitoring activities
iso-27001-a-8.16.pb_interpretation: Monitor high-risk admin, support and security events; define response workflow.
iso-27001-a-8.16.evidence: Alert rules, SOC/ops reviews, incident linkage.

iso-27001-a-8.17.title: Clock synchronisation
iso-27001-a-8.17.pb_interpretation: Ensure PB systems and supported deployments use consistent time sources.

iso-27001-a-8.18.title: Use of privileged utility programs
iso-27001-a-8.18.pb_interpretation: Control shell tools, debugging utilities, remote admin tools and emergency access utilities.

iso-27001-a-8.19.title: Installation of software on operational systems
iso-27001-a-8.19.pb_interpretation: Only approved software on PB-managed operational systems and support devices; define customer guidance where relevant.
iso-27001-a-8.19.evidence: Allow lists, package controls, change tickets.
iso-27001-a-8.19.pitfall: Local admin installing tools ad hoc.

iso-27001-a-8.20.title: Networks security
iso-27001-a-8.20.pb_interpretation: Covers PB corporate networks, build networks, admin access paths and any recommended customer network patterns for local hosting.

iso-27001-a-8.21.title: Security of network services
iso-27001-a-8.21.objective: Secure network services used by the organisation.
iso-27001-a-8.21.pb_interpretation: Validate VPN, DNS, remote access, proxies and provider-managed network services.
iso-27001-a-8.21.evidence: Service configs, supplier assurances, test results.

iso-27001-a-8.22.title: Segregation of networks
iso-27001-a-8.22.objective: Limit lateral movement and blast radius.
iso-27001-a-8.22.applies_rationale: Always needed for admin and build environments.
iso-27001-a-8.22.na_justification_template: Rarely defensible for admin and build environments.
iso-27001-a-8.22.pb_interpretation: Separate corporate, build, admin, management, test and customer-facing zones as appropriate.
iso-27001-a-8.22.pitfall: Management interfaces exposed in flat networks.

iso-27001-a-8.23.title: Web filtering
iso-27001-a-8.23.objective: Reduce web-borne threat and risky browsing.
iso-27001-a-8.23.applies_rationale: Useful for staff endpoints and support/admin systems.
iso-27001-a-8.23.na_justification_template: If justified by alternative egress controls and low exposure.
iso-27001-a-8.23.pb_interpretation: Apply safe-browsing controls especially on privileged endpoints.
iso-27001-a-8.23.external_safe_phrasing: Where deployed, web-filtering controls align to 8.23.

iso-27001-a-8.24.title: Use of cryptography
iso-27001-a-8.24.pb_interpretation: Define encryption in transit and at rest, key management, signing and certificate controls for product and corporate systems.
iso-27001-a-8.24.evidence: TLS settings, disk encryption, key rotation, signing evidence.
iso-27001-a-8.24.remediation: Document approved crypto and key ownership.

iso-27001-a-8.25.title: Secure development life cycle
iso-27001-a-8.25.pb_interpretation: Security requirements, reviews, testing and release gates across product and support tooling.
iso-27001-a-8.25.evidence: SDLC artefacts, review gates, issue tracking.
iso-27001-a-8.25.pitfall: Policy exists but no gate evidence.
iso-27001-a-8.25.remediation: Require security gates in workflow tooling.

iso-27001-a-8.26.title: Application security requirements
iso-27001-a-8.26.pb_interpretation: Capture auth, logging, secrets, tenancy, admin control, data handling and supportability requirements before build/change.
iso-27001-a-8.26.remediation: Add security requirement sections to stories/PRDs.

iso-27001-a-8.27.title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
iso-27001-a-8.27.pb_interpretation: Use principles for least privilege, compartmentalisation, secure defaults, tamper resistance and support-boundary minimisation.
iso-27001-a-8.27.evidence: Design docs, architecture reviews, exception records.
iso-27001-a-8.27.pitfall: Principles not applied to real designs.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Architecture decisions show them in use.

iso-27001-a-8.28.title: Secure coding
iso-27001-a-8.28.pb_interpretation: Secure coding standards for relevant languages and frameworks used by PrivateBox.
iso-27001-a-8.28.implementation: Secure coding standard and code-review expectations.
iso-27001-a-8.28.pitfall: Standard exists but reviewers ignore it.

iso-27001-a-8.29.title: Security testing in development and acceptance
iso-27001-a-8.29.objective: Validate that security requirements and controls work.
iso-27001-a-8.29.pb_interpretation: Use SAST, DAST, dependency scanning, configuration review and targeted manual testing; add acceptance criteria for major changes.
iso-27001-a-8.29.remediation: Formalise test gates and severity thresholds.

iso-27001-a-8.30.title: Outsourced development
iso-27001-a-8.30.applies_rationale: Applies if any third-party development is used.
iso-27001-a-8.30.na_justification_template: Only if no outsourced development exists in scope.
iso-27001-a-8.30.pb_interpretation: If contractors or partners write code, govern access, review, delivery and IP/security obligations.
iso-27001-a-8.30.evidence: Supplier reviews, repo access controls, deliverable assurance.
iso-27001-a-8.30.external_safe_phrasing: Where used, outsourced-development controls align to 8.30.

iso-27001-a-8.31.title: Separation of development, test and production environments
iso-27001-a-8.31.objective: Prevent unintended impact and leakage between environments.
iso-27001-a-8.31.na_justification_template: Rarely defensible for a product vendor.
iso-27001-a-8.31.pb_interpretation: Separate envs, accounts, secrets and data; for customer-hosted systems clearly distinguish PB testing from customer production.
iso-27001-a-8.31.implementation: Environment-separation standard and release pipeline rules.
iso-27001-a-8.31.remediation: Segregate accounts, secrets and data paths.

iso-27001-a-8.32.title: Change management
iso-27001-a-8.32.objective: Control system changes to reduce risk.
iso-27001-a-8.32.pb_interpretation: Formal change control for code, infra, support tooling, customer-impacting advisories and configuration baselines.
iso-27001-a-8.32.evidence: Change tickets, approvals, testing and rollback evidence.
iso-27001-a-8.32.remediation: Standardise change records and CAB logic.

iso-27001-a-8.33.title: Test information
iso-27001-a-8.33.pb_interpretation: Use synthetic or masked data; tightly control any real data used for testing/support reproduction.
iso-27001-a-8.33.evidence: Dataset approvals, masking evidence, access controls.

iso-27001-a-8.34.title: Protection of information systems during audit testing
iso-27001-a-8.34.objective: Ensure audit/assessment testing does not harm systems or data.
iso-27001-a-8.34.na_justification_template: Only if no such testing occurs in scope.
iso-27001-a-8.34.pb_interpretation: Applies to internal audits, pen tests, customer audits and security assessments on PB or customer-connected systems.
iso-27001-a-8.34.implementation: Safe-testing rules, approval and rollback plan.
iso-27001-a-8.34.evidence: Test plans, approvals, scope controls, post-test review.
iso-27001-a-8.34.pitfall: Test activity on production without controls.
iso-27001-a-8.34.external_safe_phrasing: Where audit testing occurs, controls align to 8.34.

iso-27701.full_name: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 — Privacy information management — Requirements and guidance

effective_dates.notes: Supersedes 2019 edition (withdrawn). Transition deadline October 2028 for existing 2019 certificate holders.

companion_standards.name: Code of practice for protection of PII in public clouds acting as PII processors
companion_standards.role: Becomes directly applicable when PB activates Teraco hosting (PB acts as PII processor in cloud).

companion_standards.name: Information security management systems — Requirements
companion_standards.role: Information security backbone. 27701 A.3 controls align to 27002:2022 themes; SoA can be combined.

companion_standards.name: Privacy framework
companion_standards.role: Privacy framework reference; 27701 maintains an updated mapping.

companion_standards.name: Requirements for bodies providing audit and certification of privacy information management systems
companion_standards.role: Certification-body scheme; shapes accredited auditor expectations. Not directly applicable to PB.

pb_application.why_chosen: Confidential-firm customer base (legal, financial, professional services) treats privacy management as a procurement gate. ISO 27701:2025 is the certifiable PIMS standard against which enterprise privacy claims can be tested. On-prem architecture reduces several classes of privacy risk (cross-border transfer, multi-tenant leakage, vendor-side telemetry exposure) but does NOT eliminate the need for a documented PIMS — PB the company still acts as controller (own corporate data) and processor (customer data via support, RAG, hosting).

on_prem.rationale: On-prem default: customer owns the runtime environment; PB acts as controller for own corporate/telemetry/lead data, and as processor for customer data when optional support, RAG ingestion, integration help, or update delivery touches client PII. Dual-role posture is the design choice; contracts and controls reflect both roles.

ai_factory.rationale: Future Teraco-hosted single-tenant scenario activates ISO 27018:2025 obligations directly. PB acts as processor in full; section-72 POPIA / GDPR Chapter V transfer rules become live considerations. Several scope decisions pending (DC partner, jurisdiction, CMK support, contract template) — see open questions.

top_risks: Marketing claiming 'ISO 27701 certified' before an accredited certificate is issued
Telemetry pipelines collecting customer content beyond what's contractually permitted (no-train commitment violations)
Standing support access undermining the on-prem privacy promise
Sub-processor register going stale as model API providers / monitoring vendors are added without notice
Backup support performed without verified deletion at end of engagement
Cross-border transfers to non-adequate jurisdictions without lawful basis (SCCs/POPIA s.72 evidence)
Treating PB as 'always processor' or 'always controller' rather than dual-role per activity

soa_guidance.principles: SoA covers Annex A.1 (controllers, ~31 controls), A.2 (processors, ~18 controls), and A.3 (shared security, 29 controls). When 27001:2022 is also held, A.3 SoA can be unified with the 27001 SoA — the 27701 A.3 controls are the 27002:2022 controls re-tagged with privacy lens.
soa_guidance.na_justification_rules: Apart from genuinely-N/A controls (e.g., joint controllership when never applicable), default is to apply. PIMS principle: if PB processes PII in any capacity, most controls apply. The common error is to mark processor controls N/A because 'we don't store customer data on our infra' — wrong, because support/telemetry/update flows still process PII on customer behalf.
soa_guidance.honesty_rules: Where a control is partially applied (e.g., subprocessor register exists but not yet public), document the gap and the closing plan. Auditors prefer honest 'in progress' to claimed-but- unevidenced 'fully applied'.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB ROPA covers controller-side processing (corp/telemetry/leads) and processor-side activities (support/RAG/hosting).
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer maintains its own ROPA covering use of PB and the data it processes through PB.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: PB provides processor-side ROPA outputs to customer for incorporation into customer's controller-side ROPA.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB establishes lawful basis for its own controller activities.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer establishes lawful basis for processing PII through PB.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: DPA records PB's processor scope; customer signs off lawful basis for its own use.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB fulfils DSARs against own controller-side data. PB assists customer for DSARs against client data.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer fulfils DSARs against its own data subjects.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: DPA defines assist SLAs and channel.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB conducts product-level DPIA per significant feature/scenario.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer conducts deployment-specific DPIA for its use case.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: PB provides DPIA template + product DPIA inputs to customer.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB maintains public sub-processor register; provides advance change notice.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer reviews changes; raises objections under DPA.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: DPA defines notice window and objection mechanism.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB documents transfers it controls (telemetry destinations, sub-processor locations).
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer documents transfers from its own use of PB.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Transfer register annexed to DPA where shared liability.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB IR plan covers product/support incidents; notifies customer per DPA SLA.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer IR plan covers deployment incidents; notifies PB if PB-side support involved.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Joint tabletop annually; notification SLA in DPA (e.g., <24h customer, <72h regulator).

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB documents crypto policy and KMS architecture; supports customer-managed keys.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer chooses key management posture (customer-managed vs provider-managed).
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: CMK option preferred for confidential firms; documented in deployment guide.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB-side logs (support sessions, telemetry, product diagnostics) — minimised, redacted, time-bounded.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer-side logs (prompts, outputs, RAG queries) — primary on-prem store.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Log schema documented; PII redaction by default; customer controls retention.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB annual privacy training for all staff; specialised training for support engineers.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer trains its own users on privacy-aware use of PB.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: PB provides deployer-side training pack.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB sets retention / deletion at end of engagement.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer instructs deletion or return of data at end of engagement.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: DPA defines deletion-with-certificate workflow.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB office security.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer datacentre / facility security (on-prem default).
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: When Teraco hosting active, Teraco's ISAE 3402 / PCI DSS attestations inherited; PB documents the inheritance.

evidence_families.name: Privacy governance charter
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Sets tone-from-the-top; required for Cl. 5.1 leadership commitment.

evidence_families.name: PIMS scope statement
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Cl. 4.3 — defines what is and is not in PIMS scope.

evidence_families.name: Internal privacy policy + public privacy notice
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Cl. 5.2 + Annex A.1.10 transparency.

evidence_families.name: Privacy risk methodology + register
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Cl. 6.1 — explicit privacy risk methodology required in 2025.

evidence_families.name: Records of Processing Activities (ROPA)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Annex A.1.8 + A.2.7 — Article 30 GDPR equivalent. Audit cornerstone.

evidence_families.name: Controller/Processor scenario matrix
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Demonstrates dual-role discipline; shows PB has thought through every processing flow.

evidence_families.name: DPA template (Article 28 + POPIA s.21 + AI no-train + transfers + audit + termination)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Annex A.1.6 + A.2.x — processor contracts required.

evidence_families.name: Public sub-processor register
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Annex A.2.16 — transparency to customer and customer's data subjects.

evidence_families.name: DSAR SOP (intake / identify / fulfil / log)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Annex A.1.11 + A.2.8 — data subject rights end-to-end.

evidence_families.name: Privacy incident response plan
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Annex A.3 incident management; GDPR Art 33-34 / POPIA s.22.

evidence_families.name: Support access SOP + PAM/JIT configuration
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Cornerstone of vendor-side privacy posture; failure mode is standing access.

evidence_families.name: Telemetry minimisation specification
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Demonstrates engineered minimisation, not just policy claim.

evidence_families.name: Update integrity standard (signed packages + SBOM)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Annex A.3 supply chain; supports integrity claims for on-prem update channel.

evidence_families.name: Backup & decommissioning SOP + deletion certificate template
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Annex A.2.10 — return/transfer/disposal at end of contract.

evidence_families.name: Data flow diagrams (per deployment mode)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Visualises dual-role per activity; backbone for DPIAs.

evidence_families.name: DPIA template + per-activity DPIAs
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Annex A.1.5; required for high-risk AI processing.

evidence_families.name: Cross-border transfer register
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Annex A.1.13/A.2.11; GDPR Ch. V; POPIA s.72.

evidence_families.name: Privacy training records + competence matrix
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Cl. 7.2/7.3 — competence and awareness.

evidence_families.name: Internal audit + management review pack
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Cl. 9.2/9.3 — required for certification.

evidence_families.name: Trust-pack language guide
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Prevents marketing overclaim that triggers regulator/customer pushback.

open_questions.question: Pursue accredited ISO 27701:2025 certification, and on what timeline?
candidate_answers: Yes — within 12 months (alongside or after 27001:2022 cert)
Defer until first qualified procurement demand
No — operate in alignment without seeking accredited cert
open_questions.notes: Drives investment levels in evidence quality, internal audit independence, and management review formality. Without a target cert date, there's no forcing function on evidence.

open_questions.question: Formal Privacy Lead/DPO designation — internal or outsourced?
candidate_answers: Internal Privacy Lead with outsourced fractional DPO
open_questions.notes: Required for procurement DDQs. Conflict-free reporting line to management is the discriminator.

open_questions.question: Is the 'no customer-content training' commitment engineering-true and contractually enforceable?
candidate_answers: Yes — engineering attestation + DPA clause + telemetry inspection on demand
Partial — DPA clause yes, engineering controls in flight
No — currently rely on customer trust without technical enforcement
open_questions.notes: The 'no-train' claim is the single most procurement-relevant differentiator for AI vendors. Vague claims here are a red flag in any DDQ.

open_questions.question: Customer-managed key (CMK) support — default or option?
candidate_answers: Vendor-managed default with CMK upgrade path
open_questions.notes: Confidential-firm customers (legal, finance) often require CMK. Drives KMS architecture decisions and customer-facing key-management UX.

open_questions.question: What goes on the public sub-processor register?
candidate_answers: All upstream model API providers + monitoring + support tooling vendors
Only those that touch customer PII (excluding vendor-internal tooling)
Defer publication until first accredited cert
open_questions.notes: Public sub-processor register is procurement-table-stakes. Stale or selectively-maintained registers fail DDQ scrutiny.

open_questions.question: When does Teraco hosting activate (triggers ISO 27018:2025)?
open_questions.notes: Teraco-hosted scenario activates 27018:2025 directly. POPIA s.72 + GDPR Ch. V transfer rules become live for non-SA customers hosted there.

open_questions.question: Which DC partner(s) for AI Factory deployments?
open_questions.notes: DC partner selection determines which 27018:2025 controls inherit-from-partner vs require PB-side implementation.

open_questions.question: Which legal jurisdiction(s) for AI Factory hosting?
open_questions.notes: Jurisdiction determines applicable transfer regimes (POPIA s.72 in SA, GDPR Ch. V in EU).

open_questions.question: What does the customer contract / DPA look like for AI Factory?
candidate_answers: Adapted on-prem template with hosting addendum
open_questions.notes: Hosting changes the DPA shape: subprocessor flow-down, data residency commitments, audit rights.

open_questions.question: Customer exit / data portability mechanism for AI Factory hosting?
candidate_answers: Encrypted data export + cryptographic disposal + signed certificate
Migration path back to on-prem deployment
open_questions.notes: Annex A.2.10 mandates return/deletion at end of contract; mechanism must be defined and verifiable.

scenarios.name: Pure on-prem, no remote support — vendor never touches client data
scenarios.description: PrivateBox is deployed on customer infrastructure with no support layers active. PB has no role for client data; PB is only a controller for its own corporate data.
scenarios.why_role_allocation: Vendor never accesses customer PII when no support layers active.
scenarios.contractual_implication: Customer-only DPA mostly N/A re client data; standard EULA + PB privacy notice for own data.
scenarios.required_clauses_or_terms: Standard EULA + privacy notice for PB-side data only.
scenarios.risk_if_misclassified: Treating PB as 'always processor' inflates obligations and complicates contracts.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: When deployed without optional support, PrivateBox does not process client data on the customer's behalf.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Scope statement should distinguish this baseline mode.

scenarios.name: On-prem with vendor update delivery
scenarios.description: Update channel is vendor-driven; metadata may flow back. PB acts as processor (limited) for any PII in update telemetry, controller for diagnostics where it sets purposes.
scenarios.why_role_allocation: Update channel is vendor-driven; metadata flow makes PB processor for any PII.
scenarios.contractual_implication: DPA needed if any PII in update telemetry.
scenarios.risk_if_misclassified: If treated as 'no role', any PII leak becomes silent breach.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: Updates are delivered via signed channels under documented terms.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Customer agreement covers update channel.
Update telemetry must not be repurposed for own use.
Update infra vendors registered as sub-processors.

scenarios.name: On-prem with telemetry
scenarios.description: Telemetry pipeline streams metadata back to PB. PB role depends on telemetry contents: controller for diagnostics PB defines purposes for, processor when customer content present.
scenarios.why_role_allocation: Depends on what telemetry contains and who decides purposes.
scenarios.risk_if_misclassified: Misclassifying as controller-only when content present is a breach exposure.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: Telemetry is minimised, configurable, and governed by dual-role terms.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Identify and document purposes for telemetry.
Lawful basis for diagnostic processing.
Customer instructions where telemetry contains customer data.
No repurposing of customer content.

scenarios.name: On-prem with support access
scenarios.description: PB engineer accesses customer environment to diagnose; sessions may include access to prompts, outputs, system parameters, customer data.
scenarios.why_role_allocation: Vendor processes customer data on instruction during support sessions.
scenarios.contractual_implication: DPA with explicit support-access terms; per-session approval.
scenarios.required_operational_control: Support access SOP; PAM logs; session recording.
scenarios.risk_if_misclassified: Standing access without DPA = unauthorised processing exposure.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: Support sessions are time-boxed, recorded, and approved per request.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Customer instructions cover support sessions.
Confidentiality undertakings for support engineers.
DSAR-assist obligations during support.
Notify on disclosure requests.

scenarios.name: Managed deployment support
scenarios.description: PB performs initial deployment configuration touching customer PII (e.g., directory sync, initial ingestion).
scenarios.why_role_allocation: PB performs configuration touching PII on customer instruction.
scenarios.risk_if_misclassified: Treating as 'consulting' with no DPA = silent breach.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: Managed deployment is performed under a processor agreement.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: DPA covers deployment scope.
Deletion at end of deployment engagement.

scenarios.name: Troubleshooting sessions involving customer data
scenarios.description: Live troubleshooting sessions where PB engineer interactively processes customer PII (reading logs, examining outputs).
scenarios.why_role_allocation: Direct PII processing on customer instruction during session.
scenarios.required_operational_control: Session log; deletion record; per-session approval.
scenarios.risk_if_misclassified: Logs retained beyond session purpose = retention violation.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: Troubleshooting sessions follow documented privacy controls.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Temp files and session captures must be deleted.

scenarios.name: Document ingestion / RAG setup support
scenarios.description: PB structures and ingests customer documents into RAG corpus; potentially involves sensitive PII at scale.
scenarios.why_role_allocation: PB processes customer documents on customer instruction; setup is processor activity.
scenarios.risk_if_misclassified: RAG corpus retained for own model improvement = no-train violation.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: RAG setup is performed under processor terms with no-train commitment.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Customer instructions define RAG corpus scope.
No-train commitment for RAG content.
Privacy by design in RAG architecture.

scenarios.name: Backup support
scenarios.description: PB performs backup operations on customer's behalf; storage and handling of backup data falls under PB's processor scope.
scenarios.why_role_allocation: PB stores/handles backups containing customer PII.
scenarios.risk_if_misclassified: Backups offshore without lawful basis = transfer violation.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: Backup support is governed by processor terms with regional storage options.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: DPA covers backup scope.
Backup deletion at end of contract.

scenarios.name: Integration / connector support
scenarios.description: PB designs and deploys integrations / connectors between PrivateBox and customer systems (CRM, email, file shares); connector design touches PII flow.
scenarios.why_role_allocation: PB designs PII flows on customer instruction; connector implementation is processor activity.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: Connectors are deployed after a privacy review.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Privacy by design in connector architecture.
Customer instructions define connector scope.

scenarios.name: Hosted update repository / model package distribution
scenarios.description: PB hosts an update / model-package repository serving customer deployments; metadata (deployment IDs, versions, last-checked timestamps) flows to PB.
scenarios.why_role_allocation: Processor for any PII in metadata; controller for repo-side telemetry purposes PB defines.
scenarios.risk_if_misclassified: Implicit cross-border transfers without lawful basis.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: Update repository operations are governed and disclosed.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Transfer basis for repo telemetry.
Disclose processing locations to customer.

scenarios.name: Future Teraco-hosted single-tenant deployment
scenarios.description: PB activates Teraco hosting; customer deploys into a single-tenant ringfenced footprint in Teraco DC. PB acts as processor in full; ISO 27018:2025 obligations attach directly. POPIA s.72 + GDPR Ch. V transfer rules become live for non-SA customers.
scenarios.why_role_allocation: PB operates the host (single-tenant) — full processor scope.
scenarios.required_clauses_or_terms: Customer-key option; data residency in SA; audit rights.
required_evidence: Teraco ISAE 3402 / PCI DSS attestation copies
scenarios.risk_if_misclassified: Mistreating as 'still on-prem' = inheriting wrong controls.
scenarios.sales_safe_explanation: Teraco-hosted PrivateBox operates as a processor with cloud-PII protections aligned to ISO/IEC 27018:2025.
scenarios.notes: Activates 27018:2025 (companion standard). Cross-references oq-27701-teraco-activation.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Hosting DPA covers entire processor scope.
Exit and portability mechanism critical.
POPIA s.72 + GDPR Ch. V transfer evidence.

requirements.title: Understanding the organization and its context
requirements.requirement: Determine internal and external issues including climate change, regulatory, technological context relevant to the PIMS.
requirements.pb_interpretation: On-prem AI for confidential firms is highly contextual. Document understanding of legal-firm/finance customer pressures, SA/Teraco context, AI evolution context, and climate change considerations.
requirements.operational_evidence: Cite specific regulations and risks; review minutes referencing context; reviewed by leadership ≥1×/year.
requirements.cadence: Annual + on regulatory or product change
requirements.failure_modes: Generic context unrelated to AI/privacy; no climate/AI references.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains a documented context analysis aligned with ISO/IEC 27701:2025 Clause 4.1.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
requirements.requirement: Identify stakeholders including PII principals; their needs/expectations; legal and contractual obligations.
