requirements.pb_interpretation: Customer firms (controllers); regulators (ICO, IR-SA, EDPB); data subjects (employees of client firms); partners; certifiers. Customer is itself an interested party as both controller and operator of the host environment.
requirements.operational_evidence: Register lists named parties and obligations; cross-checked with sales contract obligations.
requirements.failure_modes: Lists only commercial customers; missing data subjects/regulators.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains a documented interested-parties register including data subjects and regulators.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Determining the scope of the PIMS
requirements.requirement: Determine PIMS boundaries considering 4.1, 4.2, and processing of PII.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Critical scope decisions: include product engineering, support operations, update infrastructure, telemetry pipelines, optional Teraco footprint. Exclude internal-HR-only systems if not relevant. Scope statement modelled on Slack/Salesforce/Orbita exemplars; explicit dual-role (controller AND processor) declaration.
requirements.operational_evidence: Scope walkthrough with auditor; stable, justified, role-aware.
requirements.failure_modes: Scope too narrow (excludes support); too broad (includes everything but resourced for nothing).
requirements.external_safe_claim: Our PIMS covers PrivateBox product, support operations, update delivery and (when active) Teraco-hosted deployments.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Privacy information management system
requirements.requirement: Establish, implement, maintain, and continually improve the PIMS in accordance with the requirements of this document.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Foundational: PIMS framework integrating ISMS (where 27001 held), AIMS (where 42001 in scope). Not a paper-only management system.
requirements.operational_evidence: Internal audit verifies integration, not bolt-on.
requirements.failure_modes: Paper-only PIMS not integrated with operational reality.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a documented Privacy Information Management System.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Leadership and commitment
requirements.requirement: Top management to demonstrate commitment, integrate PIMS into business processes, allocate resources.
requirements.pb_interpretation: New 2025 emphasis: privacy is no longer subordinate to security. Leadership must approve on-prem privacy posture and shared-responsibility model.
requirements.operational_evidence: Walkthrough with CEO/CTO; visible board engagement on privacy.
requirements.failure_modes: Privacy delegated to IT only; leadership disengaged.
requirements.external_safe_claim: Top management has formally established and resourced a PIMS.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Privacy policy
requirements.requirement: Establish a privacy policy with objectives, commitment to legal requirements, and continual improvement.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Internal privacy policy + public privacy notice. Procurement reviewers ask for this first. Should explicitly describe on-prem-by-default and shared-responsibility.
requirements.operational_evidence: Compare published vs internal version; versioned, reviewed annually, signed by management.
requirements.failure_modes: Generic privacy policy lifted from a template.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains a documented privacy policy reviewed at least annually.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

source_refs.note: PIMS leadership commitment supports POPIA accountability principle.

requirements.title: Roles, responsibilities and authorities
requirements.requirement: Assign PIMS roles, including privacy lead/DPO equivalent.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Required for procurement DDQs. DPO not legally mandatory in all cases but practically essential for AI/PII vendor. Independent privacy lead with reporting line to management.
requirements.operational_evidence: Interview the role-holder; verify reporting line independence.
requirements.failure_modes: Privacy 'owned' by CTO with no separation.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox has appointed a privacy lead with documented authority.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

source_refs.note: PIMS roles overlap with POPIA Information Officer duties (ss 55-56).

requirements.title: Actions to address risks and opportunities (incl. privacy risk)
requirements.requirement: Privacy risk assessment and treatment, integrated with information security risk. New 2025 requirement: privacy risk now explicit, with a separate methodology where appropriate.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Risk register reflects on-prem-specific risks (support access, update channel, RAG ingestion, telemetry). Tied to Annex A controls. Updated quarterly.
requirements.operational_evidence: Sample risks → traced to controls; treatments evidenced.
requirements.failure_modes: Generic infosec risks rebadged as privacy risks.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a documented privacy risk methodology with a maintained register.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Privacy objectives and planning to achieve them
requirements.requirement: Establish measurable privacy objectives at relevant functions and levels.
requirements.pb_interpretation: KPIs include: zero unsanctioned cross-border transfers; DSAR support SLA; support-access auditability rate.
documented_evidence: Privacy objectives document with KPIs and targets
requirements.operational_evidence: Quarterly review report; track objective vs actual; trends improve.
requirements.failure_modes: Aspirational objectives with no measurement.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox tracks measurable privacy objectives at least quarterly.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Planning of changes
requirements.requirement: Plan PIMS changes in a controlled way.
requirements.pb_interpretation: New 2025 explicit clause. Especially relevant for adding Teraco hosting, new connectors, new model versions.
documented_evidence: Change management procedure including PIMS impact
requirements.operational_evidence: Sample changes with PIMS-impact assessment recorded.
requirements.failure_modes: New connector or hosting mode launched with no privacy impact assessment.
requirements.external_safe_claim: Privacy implications are assessed for material PIMS changes.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Resources
requirements.requirement: Provide resources for the establishment, implementation, maintenance and continual improvement of the PIMS.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Stable funding; budget line for privacy programme distinct from security.
requirements.operational_evidence: Compare budget to plan; stable funding visible.
requirements.failure_modes: Volunteer/best-effort privacy work.
requirements.external_safe_claim: Privacy programme is resourced as part of operating plan.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Competence
requirements.requirement: Privacy-relevant roles competent on the basis of education, training or experience.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Critical for support staff and engineering. Support engineers need privacy-aware training for customer-data exposure scenarios.
requirements.operational_evidence: Sampled training certificates; refresher every 12 months.
requirements.failure_modes: One-off generic training.
requirements.external_safe_claim: Personnel with privacy responsibilities are demonstrably competent.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

source_refs.note: PIA / DPIA cadence supports POPIA s 71 automated-decision impact analysis.
PIA process integrates POPIA s 19 security-safeguard analysis for personal information.

requirements.title: Awareness
requirements.requirement: All relevant personnel aware of the privacy policy, their contribution, and consequences.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Annual awareness program; ≥95% completion target.
requirements.operational_evidence: Random sampling; <80% completion = failure mode.
requirements.failure_modes: <80% completion.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains an annual privacy awareness program.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Communication
requirements.requirement: Determine internal and external communications relevant to the PIMS.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Should cover support-window incident comms to customers; templates tested in tabletop exercises.
requirements.operational_evidence: Drill exercise; templates tested.
requirements.failure_modes: No defined channel for breach communications.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains documented privacy communication procedures.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Documented information
requirements.requirement: Maintain control over policies/procedures (creation, update, retention, distribution).
requirements.pb_interpretation: Audit-ready always. Document register with version control and retention rules.
requirements.operational_evidence: Sample document with version history; old versions retrievable.
requirements.failure_modes: Sprawl in shared drives; uncontrolled documents.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates documented information control aligned with the PIMS.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Operational planning and control
requirements.requirement: Plan, implement, and control processes to meet PIMS requirements including privacy risk treatment.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Where most failures occur. Includes support-access, update-delivery, telemetry pipelines, RAG ingestion procedures.
documented_evidence: SOPs for each operational privacy process
requirements.operational_evidence: Sample SOP; ticket sample; walkthrough.
requirements.failure_modes: SOP exists but unused.
requirements.external_safe_claim: Privacy controls are embedded in operational procedures.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Privacy risk assessment (operational)
requirements.requirement: Privacy risk assessments for processing activities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Operational PIA per processing activity (RAG ingestion, support, telemetry).
requirements.operational_evidence: Sample PIA; each activity has a PIA.
requirements.failure_modes: Single org-wide PIA only.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox conducts privacy impact assessments for each processing activity.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Privacy risk treatment
requirements.requirement: Treat privacy risks per the treatment plan.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Treatments tied to SoA; tracked through to closure.
requirements.operational_evidence: Sample treatments; treatments completed on schedule.
requirements.failure_modes: Open treatments unaddressed for long periods.
requirements.external_safe_claim: Privacy risks are tracked through to treatment.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
requirements.requirement: Measure PIMS effectiveness including privacy KPIs.
requirements.pb_interpretation: KPIs: DSAR turnaround; support-access tickets without consent; telemetry minimisation rate. Trends visible and improving.
requirements.operational_evidence: Compare metrics over time; trends improve.
requirements.failure_modes: KPIs not collected or not reviewed.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains privacy KPIs and reviews trends.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

source_refs.note: PIMS performance monitoring covers POPIA s 19 security verification and s 22 incident detection.

requirements.title: Internal audit
requirements.requirement: Audit PIMS at planned intervals to determine PIMS conformity.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Required for certification. Independence between auditor and control owner essential.
requirements.operational_evidence: Independence check; findings closed.
requirements.failure_modes: Self-audit by control owner.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox conducts annual internal audits of the PIMS.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Management review
requirements.requirement: Top management review the PIMS at planned intervals.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Required for certification. Decisions documented in minutes.
requirements.operational_evidence: Walk inputs/outputs; decisions documented.
requirements.failure_modes: Skipped or rubber-stamped review.
requirements.external_safe_claim: Top management reviews the PIMS at least annually.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Continual improvement
requirements.requirement: Continually improve the suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the PIMS.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Improvement log tracks closed items; not stagnant CAPA.
requirements.operational_evidence: Sample items traced from finding to fix.
requirements.failure_modes: Stagnant CAPA list.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a continual improvement process for the PIMS.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Nonconformity and corrective action
requirements.requirement: Nonconformities identified, treated, root-cause analysed.
requirements.pb_interpretation: CAPA procedure with documented root-cause analysis.
requirements.operational_evidence: Sample NCs; closure timing; RCA visible.
requirements.failure_modes: NCs closed without RCA.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a documented nonconformity and corrective-action process.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

requirements.title: Identify and document purpose
requirements.objective: Identify, document, and justify purpose for processing PII.
requirements.applies_rationale: PB acts as controller for own corporate, telemetry, and lead data.
requirements.pb_interpretation: On-prem reduces purposes (no analytics-on-customer-content) but PB still has multiple controller-side purposes: telemetry, support metadata, leads, marketing comms. Each must have a documented purpose.
requirements.implementation: Documented purposes register; per data category list purpose, lawful basis, retention.
requirements.evidence: Purposes register v1.x; auditor samples 5 purposes and traces to controls.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Purposes tied to controls; reviewed annually; each entry traceable to lawful basis and retention rule.

requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Each processing activity has a documented purpose and lawful basis.

source_refs.note: Controller privacy notice supports POPIA s 17-18 openness and collection-notice obligations.
Privacy programme operationalises POPIA accountability.

requirements.title: Identify lawful basis
requirements.objective: Determine and document the lawful basis for each PII processing activity.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for each controller-side processing activity.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Customer's consent often inapplicable to vendor-side analytics; PB relies on contract performance or legitimate interest for support metadata, telemetry. LIA documented where legitimate interest used.
requirements.implementation: Lawful-basis register; per processing entry; LIA documented where applicable.
requirements.evidence: Register; sample entries; auditor cross-checks vs purpose register.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Lawful basis is determined and documented for each activity.

source_refs.note: Controller role assignment maps to POPIA Information Officer duties.

requirements.title: Determine when and how consent is obtained
requirements.objective: Where consent is the lawful basis, document procedures for obtaining and recording consent.
requirements.applies_rationale: Conditional on processing activities relying on consent.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Marketing communications and voluntary telemetry rely on consent. Consent must be granular, evidenced, and withdrawable.
requirements.implementation: Consent procedure; consent records system supporting withdrawal.
requirements.evidence: Consent records sample; verify withdrawal works.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Consent is captured, recorded and revocable.

requirements.title: Privacy impact assessment (PIA/DPIA)
requirements.objective: Conduct PIA/DPIA for high-risk processing activities.
requirements.applies_rationale: High-risk processing across multiple PB activities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI + sensitive client data + new technology implies DPIA likely required for most PB processing activities. PB conducts own DPIA per material activity AND provides DPIA template to assist customer.

role_applicability.processor_note: PB assists customer DPIA where customer is controller.

requirements.implementation: DPIA process and register; per-activity DPIAs.
requirements.evidence: DPIAs sampled; living documents updated on change.
requirements.pitfall: One-off generic DPIA used for all activities.

good_vs_audit_ready.good: DPIA template exists; DPIAs done for major activities.
good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Living DPIAs updated on change; tied to risk register; deployer-side DPIA aid template provided.

requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox conducts DPIAs for high-risk processing.

requirements.title: Contracts with PII processors
requirements.objective: Have contracts with processors meeting GDPR Art. 28 / POPIA s.21 equivalents.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for each processor PB engages.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB's own subprocessors (cloud, monitoring vendors, support tooling) governed by DPAs. Subprocessor register links to per-vendor DPAs.
requirements.implementation: Subprocessor register; signed DPAs aligned with Article 28(3) requirements.
requirements.evidence: DPA library; register; sample DPA terms.
requirements.pitfall: Missing or stale DPAs; weak flow-down clauses.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: All processors are governed by data-protection agreements.

crosswalk.note: Supplier relationships — security baseline; privacy adds Art 28 / s.21 specifics
Addressing security in supplier agreements — DPA is privacy-specific instance

requirements.title: Joint controllership
requirements.objective: Determine and document joint controller arrangements where applicable.
requirements.applies_rationale: Conditional on existence of joint controller arrangements.
requirements.na_justification_template: N/A where no joint-controller relationships exist after review.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Rarely PB; possible in specific integrations. Document if discovered.
requirements.pitfall: Unrecognised joint controllership in product integrations.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Joint controller arrangements documented when applicable.

requirements.title: Records related to processing PII (ROPA)
requirements.objective: Maintain records of processing activities equivalent to GDPR Art. 30.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for any processing of PII.
requirements.pb_interpretation: ROPA covers controller-side activities; updated quarterly.
requirements.evidence: ROPA sample; auditor traces an activity.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains records of processing activities.

requirements.title: Determining and fulfilling obligations to PII principals
requirements.objective: Identify and meet obligations to PII principals.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Tied to data subject rights process; obligations register.
requirements.implementation: Obligations register tied to rights process.
requirements.pitfall: Obligations identified but rights process not exercised.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Documented obligations to PII principals.

requirements.title: Determining information to be provided to PII principals
requirements.objective: Provide privacy notice meeting transparency obligations (GDPR Art 12-14 etc.).
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for transparency to data subjects.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Public privacy notice should clearly distinguish PB-as-controller scope vs PB-as-processor scope. Reviewed annually.
requirements.implementation: Public privacy notice published; layered (summary + detail).
requirements.pitfall: Misleading notice that conflates controller and processor scopes.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Provides a privacy notice covering controller-role processing.

requirements.title: Providing information; modifying purpose; access; correction; erasure; objection; portability; automated decisions
requirements.objective: End-to-end DSAR fulfilment and data subject rights.
requirements.applies_rationale: Statutory rights apply across processing activities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Critical for credibility. DSARs against PB-controller-side data go to PB; DSARs against client data go to customer (PB assists per A.2.8).
requirements.pitfall: No tooling; manual chase; missed timelines.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a documented DSAR fulfilment process.

requirements.title: Privacy by design and by default; collection limitation; minimisation; de-identification; deletion at end; temp files
requirements.objective: Architecture-level privacy protections.
requirements.pb_interpretation: On-prem helps significantly; reduces vendor exposure. Privacy-by-default still must be evidenced (default settings, telemetry minimisation, temp file handling, deletion at end of processing).
requirements.implementation: PbD design records; default settings docs; deletion runbook.
requirements.pitfall: PbD claimed but not evidenced in design records.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: PbD documented across SDLC; default settings evidenced; deletion runbook tested.

requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Privacy by design and default is evidenced in product design records.

crosswalk.note: Secure development lifecycle — PbD is the privacy lens of secure design
Secure system architecture and engineering principles

source_refs.note: PIA records cover POPIA s 71 automated-decision documentation.
PIA evidences POPIA accountability principle.

requirements.title: Identify basis for transfer of PII between jurisdictions
requirements.objective: Cross-border transfer basis (GDPR Ch. V; POPIA s.72).
requirements.applies_rationale: Live for SA clients touching global model APIs; for any cross-border telemetry.
requirements.pb_interpretation: On-prem in client country = no transfer. Teraco-SA = no transfer for SA-domiciled data. Outbound to non-adequate jurisdictions = SCCs/POPIA s.72 basis required.
requirements.implementation: Transfer register; SCCs/IDTAs/POPIA s.72 evidence per transfer.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Cross-border transfers are governed by documented legal bases.

crosswalk.note: Information transfer rules — privacy adds jurisdictional/legal basis layer

requirements.title: Countries / international organisations to which PII can be transferred
requirements.objective: Document recipients of PII transfers.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Recipient list annexed to privacy notice.
requirements.pitfall: Hidden recipients (e.g., new sub-processor not added).
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Recipients of PII transfers are disclosed.

requirements.title: Records of transfers
requirements.objective: Maintain a transfer log.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for accountability of cross-border transfers.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Live log of transfers with destination, basis, date.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Maintains transfer records.

requirements.title: Records of disclosures to third parties
requirements.objective: Maintain a disclosure log including law-enforcement requests.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Important for confidential firms. Law-enforcement requests reaching PB: narrow processor role limits scope, but disclosures still must be logged. Privileged-data escalation path critical.

role_applicability.processor_note: PB assists customer with disclosures of customer data per A.2.14.

requirements.implementation: Disclosure log; law-enforcement-request SOP with counsel-review step.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Maintains records of third-party disclosures.

crosswalk.note: Privacy and protection of PII — disclosure logging is a privacy-specific evidence

requirements.title: Customer agreement
requirements.objective: Process PII only on customer's documented instructions.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Cornerstone of processor relationship. Even though customer 'operates' the box, PB acts on customer instruction during support, telemetry, and any vendor-touch activity. Master DPA + per-engagement annexes covers this.
requirements.implementation: DPA / Customer Agreement defining instructions; annexes for specific engagements (deployment, support, RAG setup).
requirements.evidence: Signed DPA library; sampled DPAs verified.
requirements.pitfall: DPA missing or silent on AI specifics (no-train, RAG, support access).

good_vs_audit_ready.good: DPA template exists and is used.
good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: Master DPA + per-engagement annexes for all material processing flows; AI-specific clauses included.

requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Processes client PII only on documented customer instructions.

source_refs.note: Processor controls map to POPIA s 20-21 operator confidentiality and security.

requirements.title: Organization's purposes
requirements.objective: Processor does not process PII for its own purposes beyond the contract.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Critical for AI vendor: no model training on customer data. Telemetry must be designed to NOT capture customer content. The 'no-train' commitment must be engineering-true and contractually anchored.
requirements.implementation: Engineering attestation: no customer-data training; telemetry minimisation specification.
requirements.evidence: Telemetry spec; design doc; engineering attestations.
requirements.pitfall: Implicit/ambiguous re training; customer expected to trust without technical evidence.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Customer content is not used for model training.

source_refs.note: Processor role assignment supports POPIA operator-side accountability.

requirements.title: Marketing and advertising use
requirements.objective: No marketing or advertising use of PII unless explicit consent.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Policy explicitly prohibits.
requirements.implementation: Policy prohibiting; technical controls preventing use of customer data for marketing.
requirements.pitfall: Hidden marketing flags or inferred uses.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Customer PII is not used for marketing.

requirements.title: Infringing instructions
requirements.objective: Notify controller if instruction may infringe law.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Customers may request data export PB cannot legally action; documented escalation procedure.
requirements.implementation: Procedure to flag illegal instructions; counsel review step.
requirements.pitfall: No procedure; engineers comply without review.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Identifies and notifies customers of potentially infringing instructions.

requirements.title: Confidentiality undertakings
requirements.objective: Persons authorised to process PII committed to confidentiality.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for any persons accessing customer PII.
requirements.pb_interpretation: All support staff and contractors; NDAs and HR records evidence coverage.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: All personnel processing customer data are under confidentiality.

crosswalk.note: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements — same control with privacy emphasis

requirements.title: Records related to processing on behalf of customer (Art 30(2))
requirements.objective: Maintain processor records of processing.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Per-customer activity log; available to auditor.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Maintains processor records of processing.

requirements.title: Obligations to PII principals (assist controller)
requirements.objective: Assist customer with DSARs, breach notifications, DPIA cooperation.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required to support customer's controller-side obligations.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Critical. On-prem may give customer direct access for many DSARs, but PB still must assist when telemetry/support involved. SLA defined in DPA.
requirements.pitfall: Customer left to fend off DSARs alone.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Assists customers in fulfilling data-subject rights requests.

crosswalk.note: Privacy and protection of PII — assist obligations are operational instance

requirements.title: Temporary files
requirements.objective: Manage and delete temporary files containing PII.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required to prevent retention beyond purpose.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Telemetry, debug bundles, support session captures are common temp-file sources. Audit support-bundle / temp-file lifecycle.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Temporary files are subject to documented deletion procedures.

requirements.title: Return, transfer, disposal of PII
requirements.objective: At end of contract: delete or return PII; certify deletion.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required at end of every processor engagement.
requirements.pb_interpretation: On-prem: most data is already with customer. Vendor-side artefacts (telemetry, support bundles, backups, RAG corpora when PB-managed) must be deleted/returned with certificate.
requirements.pitfall: 'Yeah we deleted it' verbally without certificate.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Provides return/deletion of customer PII at end of service.

requirements.title: Basis for transfer of PII between jurisdictions (processor scope)
requirements.objective: Document lawful basis for processor-side cross-border transfers.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Per customer instruction; documented locations.
requirements.implementation: Annex with locations; per customer instruction.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Processor-side transfers are governed and disclosed.

requirements.title: Countries to which PII may be transferred (disclose to customer)
requirements.objective: Disclose to customer the countries where PII may be processed.
requirements.pb_interpretation: List of countries annexed to DPA.
requirements.implementation: List of countries; updated as sub-processors change.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Discloses countries of processing.

requirements.title: Records of disclosures (processor)
requirements.objective: Same as controller side: maintain disclosure log.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Live processor disclosure log.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Maintains processor disclosure records.

requirements.title: Notification of PII disclosure requests
requirements.objective: Notify controller of law-enforcement disclosure requests where lawful.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required to allow controller to take appropriate action.
requirements.pb_interpretation: LE-request SOP with counsel review; customer notified per DPA terms.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Customers are notified of disclosure requests where lawful.

requirements.title: Legally binding PII disclosures
requirements.objective: Comply only when legally binding; counsel review.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Legal-review step before any compelled disclosure.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Legally binding disclosure requests are reviewed by counsel.

requirements.title: Disclosure of subcontractors
requirements.objective: Disclose sub-processors before use; allow customer to consent or object.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Critical for AI vendor (model API providers, monitoring vendors). Pure on-prem with no remote support → minimal sub-processors. Once telemetry/support added → register grows. Public sub-processor register with change-notice channel.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Maintains a published subprocessor register with change notifications.

crosswalk.note: Supplier relationships — sub-processor register is privacy operationalisation
Managing security in the ICT supply chain

requirements.title: Engagement of a subcontractor (flow-down obligations)
requirements.objective: Same data-protection obligations flow down to sub-processors.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Flow-down clauses in sub-processor DPAs are equivalent to customer DPAs.
requirements.pitfall: Weak flow-down (e.g., processor accepts customer DPA but flows down only generic clauses).
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Subprocessor obligations equivalent to customer DPAs.

crosswalk.note: Addressing security in supplier agreements — DPA flow-down is the privacy form

requirements.title: Change of subcontractor (notice and objection)
requirements.objective: Notify customer of sub-processor changes; allow objection per DPA.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Defined notice window in DPA; objection mechanism.
requirements.implementation: Change-notice procedure; defined window (e.g., 30 days advance notice).
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Subprocessor changes are notified in advance.

requirements.title: Threat intelligence
requirements.objective: Collect and use threat intelligence to inform privacy and security risk.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Privacy-aware threat intel feed; integrated with privacy risk register.
requirements.implementation: Threat-intel feed; integration with risk register.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Threat intelligence feeds privacy risk.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

requirements.title: Identity management & privileged access
requirements.objective: Strong authentication, privileged-access management for support roles.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for any vendor-side access to customer environments.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Cornerstone for support-access risk. Support engineers' access to client environments must be MFA + JIT + recorded. PAM logs evidence each session.
requirements.implementation: JIT/PAM for support; SSO; MFA; session recording.

good_vs_audit_ready.audit_ready: JIT credential issuance; per-session approval; session recording; quarterly access review.

requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Vendor support access is governed by privileged-access management.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

requirements.title: Information transfer security
requirements.objective: Secure mechanisms for information transfer.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for any vendor↔customer data transfer.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Backup support, log shipping, telemetry — all must use encrypted channels with allow- listed destinations. Customer-controllable.
requirements.implementation: Encrypted channels; signed packages; allow-listed destinations.
requirements.pitfall: Telemetry over plain HTTP; unsigned updates.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Vendor↔customer transfers are encrypted and integrity-protected.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

requirements.title: Logging & monitoring
requirements.objective: Privacy-aware logging.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for accountability and incident response.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Critical for support-access auditability. Customer keeps primary logs on-prem; PB-side logs minimised and PII-redacted by default.
requirements.implementation: Logging specification including PII minimisation and redaction defaults.
requirements.pitfall: Logs include raw client content (e.g., prompts, outputs).
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Logs are designed to minimise PII while retaining auditability.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

requirements.title: Cryptography (incl. KMS)
requirements.objective: Cryptographic protection of PII.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for any PII at rest or in transit.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Essential for confidential firms. Customer-managed keys preferred; PB documents key management options (CMK vs vendor-managed) in deployment guide.
requirements.implementation: Crypto policy; KMS architecture; customer-managed keys offered.
requirements.pitfall: Vendor-controlled keys forced; no CMK option.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Supports customer-managed encryption keys.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

requirements.title: Secure development (SDL incl. PbD)
requirements.objective: Privacy by design embedded in SDLC.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required to evidence PbD architectural posture.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Tied to A.1.12. PbD checklist in PR template; privacy review per material change.
requirements.implementation: PbD checklist in PR template; SDLC documentation.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Privacy by design is embedded in the SDLC.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

requirements.title: Backup integrity & access
requirements.objective: Backup operations protect PII.
requirements.pb_interpretation: When PB performs backups (optional service), full A.2 processor obligations attach. Encrypted backups; tested restore.
requirements.cadence: Per backup cycle + annual restore test
requirements.pitfall: Untested backups; backups offshore without basis.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Backup operations follow documented privacy-preserving procedures.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

requirements.title: Privacy incident management
requirements.objective: Privacy incident response capability.
requirements.applies_rationale: Required for accountability and regulatory compliance.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Privacy track within IR plan; notification SLA in DPAs (e.g., <24h customer, <72h regulator); coordination with customer.
requirements.implementation: IR plan with privacy track; notification SLAs in DPAs.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Privacy incidents trigger documented notification SLAs.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

requirements.title: Supplier / cloud security
requirements.objective: Third-party / cloud security with privacy lens.
requirements.pb_interpretation: All optional support layers introduce supplier dependencies; subject to privacy-aware due diligence.
requirements.pitfall: Stale supplier register; no privacy review on renewal.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Suppliers are subject to privacy-aware due diligence.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

requirements.title: Physical security
requirements.objective: Physical security of PII processing facilities.
requirements.applies_rationale: Customer-owned in on-prem default; inherited from host in Teraco scenario.
requirements.pb_interpretation: On-prem default: less directly applicable (customer-owned facility). Teraco scenario: inherits Teraco's ISAE 3402 / PCI DSS attestations; PB documents the inheritance.
requirements.implementation: Physical-security mapping document; Teraco compliance pack inheritance for hosted scenario.
requirements.pitfall: Asserted but not documented; claiming Teraco's certifications as PB's own.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Physical security relies on customer-owned facility or Teraco-attested colocation.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

requirements.title: Compliance / records (legal compliance)
requirements.objective: Track legal and regulatory compliance obligations.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Compliance register tracking GDPR, POPIA, AI Act, sectoral regulations.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: Legal/regulatory obligations are tracked.

crosswalk.note: ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

iso-27701-cl-4.1.title: Understanding the organization and its context
iso-27701-cl-4.1.requirement: Determine internal and external issues including climate change, regulatory, technological context relevant to the PIMS.
iso-27701-cl-4.1.pb_interpretation: On-prem AI for confidential firms is highly contextual. Document understanding of legal-firm/finance customer pressures, SA/Teraco context, AI evolution context, and climate change considerations.
iso-27701-cl-4.1.operational_evidence: Cite specific regulations and risks; review minutes referencing context; reviewed by leadership ≥1×/year.
iso-27701-cl-4.1.cadence: Annual + on regulatory or product change
iso-27701-cl-4.1.failure_modes: Generic context unrelated to AI/privacy; no climate/AI references.
iso-27701-cl-4.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains a documented context analysis aligned with ISO/IEC 27701:2025 Clause 4.1.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

iso-27701-cl-4.2.title: Understanding the needs and expectations of interested parties
iso-27701-cl-4.2.requirement: Identify stakeholders including PII principals; their needs/expectations; legal and contractual obligations.
iso-27701-cl-4.2.pb_interpretation: Customer firms (controllers); regulators (ICO, IR-SA, EDPB); data subjects (employees of client firms); partners; certifiers. Customer is itself an interested party as both controller and operator of the host environment.
iso-27701-cl-4.2.operational_evidence: Register lists named parties and obligations; cross-checked with sales contract obligations.
iso-27701-cl-4.2.failure_modes: Lists only commercial customers; missing data subjects/regulators.
iso-27701-cl-4.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains a documented interested-parties register including data subjects and regulators.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

iso-27701-cl-4.3.title: Determining the scope of the PIMS
iso-27701-cl-4.3.requirement: Determine PIMS boundaries considering 4.1, 4.2, and processing of PII.
iso-27701-cl-4.3.pb_interpretation: Critical scope decisions: include product engineering, support operations, update infrastructure, telemetry pipelines, optional Teraco footprint. Exclude internal-HR-only systems if not relevant. Scope statement modelled on Slack/Salesforce/Orbita exemplars; explicit dual-role (controller AND processor) declaration.
iso-27701-cl-4.3.operational_evidence: Scope walkthrough with auditor; stable, justified, role-aware.
iso-27701-cl-4.3.failure_modes: Scope too narrow (excludes support); too broad (includes everything but resourced for nothing).
iso-27701-cl-4.3.external_safe_claim: Our PIMS covers PrivateBox product, support operations, update delivery and (when active) Teraco-hosted deployments.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

iso-27701-cl-4.4.title: Privacy information management system
iso-27701-cl-4.4.requirement: Establish, implement, maintain, and continually improve the PIMS in accordance with the requirements of this document.
iso-27701-cl-4.4.pb_interpretation: Foundational: PIMS framework integrating ISMS (where 27001 held), AIMS (where 42001 in scope). Not a paper-only management system.
iso-27701-cl-4.4.operational_evidence: Internal audit verifies integration, not bolt-on.
iso-27701-cl-4.4.failure_modes: Paper-only PIMS not integrated with operational reality.
iso-27701-cl-4.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a documented Privacy Information Management System.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

iso-27701-cl-5.1.title: Leadership and commitment
iso-27701-cl-5.1.requirement: Top management to demonstrate commitment, integrate PIMS into business processes, allocate resources.
iso-27701-cl-5.1.pb_interpretation: New 2025 emphasis: privacy is no longer subordinate to security. Leadership must approve on-prem privacy posture and shared-responsibility model.
iso-27701-cl-5.1.operational_evidence: Walkthrough with CEO/CTO; visible board engagement on privacy.
iso-27701-cl-5.1.failure_modes: Privacy delegated to IT only; leadership disengaged.
iso-27701-cl-5.1.external_safe_claim: Top management has formally established and resourced a PIMS.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

iso-27701-cl-5.2.title: Privacy policy
iso-27701-cl-5.2.requirement: Establish a privacy policy with objectives, commitment to legal requirements, and continual improvement.
iso-27701-cl-5.2.pb_interpretation: Internal privacy policy + public privacy notice. Procurement reviewers ask for this first. Should explicitly describe on-prem-by-default and shared-responsibility.
iso-27701-cl-5.2.operational_evidence: Compare published vs internal version; versioned, reviewed annually, signed by management.
iso-27701-cl-5.2.failure_modes: Generic privacy policy lifted from a template.
iso-27701-cl-5.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains a documented privacy policy reviewed at least annually.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

source_refs.note: PIMS leadership commitment supports POPIA accountability principle.

iso-27701-cl-5.3.title: Roles, responsibilities and authorities
iso-27701-cl-5.3.requirement: Assign PIMS roles, including privacy lead/DPO equivalent.
iso-27701-cl-5.3.pb_interpretation: Required for procurement DDQs. DPO not legally mandatory in all cases but practically essential for AI/PII vendor. Independent privacy lead with reporting line to management.
iso-27701-cl-5.3.operational_evidence: Interview the role-holder; verify reporting line independence.
iso-27701-cl-5.3.failure_modes: Privacy 'owned' by CTO with no separation.
iso-27701-cl-5.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox has appointed a privacy lead with documented authority.

crosswalk.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

source_refs.note: PIMS roles overlap with POPIA Information Officer duties (ss 55-56).

iso-27701-cl-6.1.title: Actions to address risks and opportunities (incl. privacy risk)
iso-27701-cl-6.1.requirement: Privacy risk assessment and treatment, integrated with information security risk. New 2025 requirement: privacy risk now explicit, with a separate methodology where appropriate.
iso-27701-cl-6.1.pb_interpretation: Risk register reflects on-prem-specific risks (support access, update channel, RAG ingestion, telemetry). Tied to Annex A controls. Updated quarterly.
iso-27701-cl-6.1.operational_evidence: Sample risks → traced to controls; treatments evidenced.
iso-27701-cl-6.1.failure_modes: Generic infosec risks rebadged as privacy risks.
iso-27701-cl-6.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a documented privacy risk methodology with a maintained register.

iso-27701-cl-6.2.title: Privacy objectives and planning to achieve them
iso-27701-cl-6.2.requirement: Establish measurable privacy objectives at relevant functions and levels.
iso-27701-cl-6.2.pb_interpretation: KPIs include: zero unsanctioned cross-border transfers; DSAR support SLA; support-access auditability rate.
documented_evidence: Privacy objectives document with KPIs and targets
iso-27701-cl-6.2.operational_evidence: Quarterly review report; track objective vs actual; trends improve.
iso-27701-cl-6.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox tracks measurable privacy objectives at least quarterly.

iso-27701-cl-6.3.title: Planning of changes

documented_evidence: Change management procedure including PIMS impact
iso-27701-cl-6.3.operational_evidence: Sample changes with PIMS-impact assessment recorded.
iso-27701-cl-6.3.failure_modes: New connector or hosting mode launched with no privacy impact assessment.
iso-27701-cl-6.3.external_safe_claim: Privacy implications are assessed for material PIMS changes.

iso-27701-cl-7.1.operational_evidence: Compare budget to plan; stable funding visible.
iso-27701-cl-7.1.external_safe_claim: Privacy programme is resourced as part of operating plan.
