iso-42001-cl-7.5.pb_interpretation: Single document-management discipline across ISMS / PIMS / AIMS. Version control, date, owner, retention, location index, accessibility for auditors. ISO/IEC 42006 specifically calls this out as a sampling target.
iso-42001-cl-7.5.operational_evidence: Random doc lookup completes within sampling expectation; versions trace through change history.
iso-42001-cl-7.5.failure_modes: Untracked SharePoint sprawl; missing retention; outdated documents in active use.
iso-42001-cl-7.5.external_safe_claim: Documented-information control is integrated across the management system family.

crosswalk.note: 27001 splits 7.5 into general / creation-update / control; 42001 collapses to a single clause.
HLS-aligned.

iso-42001-cl-8.1.title: Operational planning and control
iso-42001-cl-8.1.requirement: Plan, implement and control the processes needed to meet AIMS requirements; establish criteria; keep documented information to confirm processes carried out as planned; control planned changes and review unintended changes.
iso-42001-cl-8.1.auditor_intent: Auditor expects to see operational SOPs aligned with AIMS planning, with documented adherence evidence sampled.
iso-42001-cl-8.1.pb_interpretation: Operational planning covers the full lifecycle (ideation → retirement) per source §8. SOPs for each lifecycle stage; criteria documented; adherence sampled.
iso-42001-cl-8.1.operational_evidence: Recent system releases trace through SOPs with documented adherence.
iso-42001-cl-8.1.failure_modes: SOPs exist on paper; actual operations diverge; adherence not sampled.
iso-42001-cl-8.1.external_safe_claim: AI operations are planned and controlled under documented processes covering the full system lifecycle.

crosswalk.note: HLS-aligned.
HLS-aligned.
Operational planning satisfies the operational portion of Art. 9 risk-management for high-risk AI.
NIST AI RMF MANAGE 2.2 mechanisms sustain AI system value.

iso-42001-cl-8.2.title: AI risk assessment (operational)
iso-42001-cl-8.2.requirement: Perform AI risk assessments at planned intervals and on significant change.
iso-42001-cl-8.2.auditor_intent: Auditor checks risk assessments are alive — quarterly cadence at minimum, refreshed on change.
iso-42001-cl-8.2.pb_interpretation: Quarterly cadence; per-system register kept current; major changes (model swap, regulatory update, sector expansion) trigger immediate re-assessment.
iso-42001-cl-8.2.operational_evidence: Risk register dates show currency; recent change events trace to re-assessment records.
iso-42001-cl-8.2.failure_modes: Annual-only risk assessment; no link to change events; static register.
iso-42001-cl-8.2.external_safe_claim: AI risks are assessed quarterly and on every significant change, in line with Clause 8.2.

crosswalk.note: HLS-aligned; AI risk extension to ISMS operational risk assessment.
Continuous risk-management process under Art. 9 maps to AIMS Clause 8.2 cadence.

iso-42001-cl-8.3.title: AI risk treatment (operational)
iso-42001-cl-8.3.requirement: Implement the AI risk treatment plan.
iso-42001-cl-8.3.auditor_intent: Auditor checks treatments are not just plans on paper — operational evidence shows controls in place.
iso-42001-cl-8.3.pb_interpretation: Treatments traced from risk register → SoA → operational SOP → live evidence. Closure of risk treatment items is recorded.
iso-42001-cl-8.3.operational_evidence: Recent risks trace from register through SoA to live control evidence.
iso-42001-cl-8.3.failure_modes: Treatment plan exists but no implementation evidence; risks open forever.
iso-42001-cl-8.3.external_safe_claim: AI risk treatments are implemented and traceable from register to live evidence.

crosswalk.note: HLS-aligned.

iso-42001-cl-8.4.title: AI system impact assessment (operational)
iso-42001-cl-8.4.requirement: Perform AI system impact assessments at planned intervals and on significant change. Pre-deployment AISIA is mandatory before any new system or material upgrade.
iso-42001-cl-8.4.auditor_intent: Auditor verifies pre-deployment AISIA was completed for every recent release; cadence on existing systems is held.
iso-42001-cl-8.4.pb_interpretation: Pre-deployment gate (A.6.2.5) blocks any release without current AISIA. Cadence: scheduled review plus on-significant-change. AISIA deferred is a fail; AISIA late is a finding.
iso-42001-cl-8.4.companion_note: AISIA conducted using the 42005 methodology and template per Clause 6.1.4.
iso-42001-cl-8.4.operational_evidence: Every recent release links to a current AISIA approved before go-live.
iso-42001-cl-8.4.cadence: Pre-deployment + scheduled review + on significant change
iso-42001-cl-8.4.failure_modes: AISIA done as a tickbox; foreseeable-misuse omitted; AISIA after deployment.
iso-42001-cl-8.4.external_safe_claim: AISIA is conducted before every deployment and at scheduled intervals during operation, in line with ISO/IEC 42005:2025.

crosswalk.note: PIMS DPIA cadence is structurally adjacent; integrated AISIA + DPIA template recommended.
Continuous AI Act Art. 9 risk + Art. 27 FRIA align with operational AISIA cadence.
NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.4 production monitoring of functionality and behaviour.

iso-42001-cl-9.1.title: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation
iso-42001-cl-9.1.requirement: Determine what to monitor, how to measure, when, by whom, and how to analyse and evaluate AIMS performance and effectiveness.
iso-42001-cl-9.1.auditor_intent: Auditor expects a KPI dashboard with thresholds, monitoring records, evaluation cadence, and analysis findings reported to management review.
iso-42001-cl-9.1.pb_interpretation: KPI dashboard covers model performance drift, fairness/bias indicators, incident counts, support- ticket categorisation, concern-reporting volumes, SoA implementation health. Thresholds defined; runbooks for breach. EU AI Act Art. 72 post-market monitoring satisfied via this clause.
iso-42001-cl-9.1.operational_evidence: Live dashboard accessible; trends sampled at management review; threshold breaches trigger documented response.
iso-42001-cl-9.1.failure_modes: Dashboard exists but unmonitored; no thresholds; drift detection lagging.
iso-42001-cl-9.1.external_safe_claim: Continuous AIMS monitoring with documented KPIs, thresholds and trend analysis.

crosswalk.note: HLS-aligned; AIMS adds AI-specific KPIs (drift, bias, fairness).
HLS-aligned.
EU AI Act Art. 72 post-market monitoring satisfied through AIMS Clause 9.1 + 8.4.
AIMS monitoring includes POPIA s 19 security-safeguard verification and s 22 incident-detection cadence.
NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.4 + MANAGE 4.1 post-deployment monitoring.

iso-42001-cl-9.2.title: Internal audit
iso-42001-cl-9.2.requirement: Conduct internal audits at planned intervals; impartial auditors; report to relevant management; track corrective actions.
iso-42001-cl-9.2.auditor_intent: Auditor checks audit team is independent of the AIMS team (ISO/IEC 42006 emphasis); findings are tracked through closure.
iso-42001-cl-9.2.pb_interpretation: Internal audit programme conducted by auditors not reporting into AI Gov. External audit advisor may augment. Findings tracked in NC/CAPA log; closure verified.
iso-42001-cl-9.2.operational_evidence: Most recent audit report available; findings closure trace.
iso-42001-cl-9.2.cadence: At least annual; more often during early maturity
iso-42001-cl-9.2.failure_modes: Same team auditing itself; findings open forever; programme exists but no audits conducted.
iso-42001-cl-9.2.external_safe_claim: Independent internal audit programme covers the AIMS at least annually.

crosswalk.note: 27001 splits into programme + execution; 42001 collapses to single clause.
HLS-aligned.
NIST AI RMF MEASURE 1.3 independent assessors involved.

iso-42001-cl-9.3.title: Management review
iso-42001-cl-9.3.requirement: Top management reviews the AIMS at planned intervals; inputs include audit results, KPI data, nonconformities, status of actions, changes in external/internal issues, feedback from interested parties, and opportunities for continual improvement; outputs include decisions, resource changes and improvement opportunities.
iso-42001-cl-9.3.auditor_intent: Auditor expects management review minutes covering all required inputs and outputs, signed by top management, with traceable follow-through on decisions.
iso-42001-cl-9.3.pb_interpretation: Annual at minimum; more often during early maturity. Standardised inputs/outputs template; minutes distributed; decisions tracked.
iso-42001-cl-9.3.operational_evidence: Most recent management review covered all inputs; decisions trace forward.
iso-42001-cl-9.3.cadence: At least annual; more often during early maturity
iso-42001-cl-9.3.failure_modes: Lunch meeting with no minutes; inputs partial; decisions not tracked.
iso-42001-cl-9.3.external_safe_claim: Documented management review of the AIMS conducted at least annually with full inputs and outputs.

crosswalk.note: 27001 splits into general / inputs / outputs; 42001 collapses to single clause.
HLS-aligned.

iso-42001-cl-10.1.title: Continual improvement
iso-42001-cl-10.1.requirement: Continually improve the suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the AIMS.
iso-42001-cl-10.1.auditor_intent: Auditor expects improvement records, not just policy statements — visible improvement actions over the audit period.
iso-42001-cl-10.1.pb_interpretation: Improvement actions logged with rationale, owner, target date, and outcome. Source includes audit findings, KPI trends, incident lessons-learned, customer feedback.
iso-42001-cl-10.1.operational_evidence: Recent improvement actions trace to source (finding / KPI / incident / feedback) and to outcome.
iso-42001-cl-10.1.failure_modes: Continual improvement reduced to "we'll do better"; no visible actions logged.
iso-42001-cl-10.1.external_safe_claim: Continual improvement of the AIMS is documented through a register of actions traceable to source evidence.

crosswalk.note: HLS-aligned.
HLS-aligned.

iso-42001-cl-10.2.title: Nonconformity and corrective action
iso-42001-cl-10.2.requirement: React to nonconformities; evaluate cause; implement actions; review effectiveness; update AIMS if needed; retain documented information of nature, actions and results.
iso-42001-cl-10.2.auditor_intent: Auditor expects each NC to have cause analysis, action, effectiveness review, and closure date — not just an open ticket.
iso-42001-cl-10.2.pb_interpretation: NC / CAPA log; each entry: cause, action, effectiveness review, closure date. Trend analysis at management review. EU AI Act Art. 73 serious-incident workflow integrated.
iso-42001-cl-10.2.operational_evidence: Random NC sampling shows cause analysis, effective action, closure date.
iso-42001-cl-10.2.failure_modes: NC log records issues but never closes them; cause analysis is "we forgot"; effectiveness review skipped.
iso-42001-cl-10.2.external_safe_claim: Nonconformities are tracked through structured cause analysis, corrective action and effectiveness review under documented procedure.

crosswalk.note: HLS-aligned.
HLS-aligned.
NIST AI RMF MANAGE 4.3 incident communication and recovery.

iso-42001-a-2.2.title: AI Policy
iso-42001-a-2.2.objective: Documented AI policy informed by business strategy, values, risk environment and legal requirements, with principles, scope, prohibited uses and an exceptions process.
iso-42001-a-2.2.pb_interpretation: Mandatory. PrivateBox AI Policy v1.x sets principles, scope, prohibited uses, exceptions handling. Linked tightly to clause 5.2. Policy is signed PDF; dissemination logged; staff sample test in auditor interview must succeed.
iso-42001-a-2.2.operational_evidence: Random employee interview confirms understanding of core principles.
iso-42001-a-2.2.failure_modes: Boilerplate not tailored to PB; principles unmemorable; exceptions handled informally without procedure.
iso-42001-a-2.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates under a documented AI Policy aligned to ISO/IEC 42001 A.2.2.

crosswalk.note: AI Policy parallels InfoSec Policy under 27001 A.5.1 — single integrated policy stack.
PIMS controllers privacy notice / policy; cross-policy alignment required.
AI Policy aligned to POPIA accountability principle; sales / marketing red-flag list reduces s 69 (direct marketing) and external-claim exposure.
NIST AI RMF GOV 1.2 trustworthy-AI policy framework.

iso-42001-a-2.3.title: Alignment with other organisational policies
iso-42001-a-2.3.objective: Document alignment of the AI Policy with other relevant organisational policies (information security, privacy, HR, procurement, ethics).
iso-42001-a-2.3.pb_interpretation: Cross-policy alignment map showing intersections with InfoSec (27001), Privacy (27701/POPIA/GDPR), HR, Procurement, Ethics. AI Policy must not contradict the privacy policy on retention or any other shared topic.
iso-42001-a-2.3.operational_evidence: Sample alignment review records reflect intersection assessments.
iso-42001-a-2.3.failure_modes: AI Policy contradicts privacy policy on retention; intersection not mapped; conflicts surface in operations rather than in alignment review.
iso-42001-a-2.3.external_safe_phrasing: AI Policy alignment with privacy, information security, HR and procurement policies is explicitly maintained.

crosswalk.note: Alignment integrates with 27001 InfoSec policy.
Alignment integrates with PIMS privacy notice / policy.
AI Policy must align with PB privacy notice / PAIA Manual under POPIA s 17-18 openness.

iso-42001-a-2.4.title: Review of the AI policy
iso-42001-a-2.4.objective: Maintain a review process for the AI Policy with named owner, scheduled reviews and trigger-based reviews.
iso-42001-a-2.4.pb_interpretation: Named owner is AI Governance Lead. Scheduled annual review; trigger-based reviews on regulatory change, material incident, or new framework adoption. Review log maintained.
iso-42001-a-2.4.operational_evidence: Auditor reads review log and review minutes; cadence held over the audit period.
iso-42001-a-2.4.failure_modes: Review owner unnamed; reviews skipped; trigger criteria unclear.
iso-42001-a-2.4.external_safe_phrasing: The AI Policy is reviewed at least annually and on defined triggers under named ownership.

crosswalk.note: 27001 policy review pattern.

iso-42001-a-3.2.title: AI roles and responsibilities
iso-42001-a-3.2.objective: Define and communicate AI roles and responsibilities across the organisation.
iso-42001-a-3.2.pb_interpretation: Linked to clause 5.3. AI RACI; org chart with AI roles. Same person may NOT own development AND oversight (Annex A.3.2 failure pattern). AI Governance Committee with executive representation.
documented_evidence: Org chart with named AI roles
iso-42001-a-3.2.operational_evidence: Each AI role has a named individual who can speak to their responsibility in interview.
iso-42001-a-3.2.failure_modes: Same person owns development and oversight; titles exist but authority does not; committee meets irregularly.
iso-42001-a-3.2.external_safe_phrasing: Cross-functional AI governance with named owners and committee oversight per ISO/IEC 42001 A.3.2.

crosswalk.note: 27001 segregation of duties / roles overlay AI roles.
PIMS information officer role overlaps.
AI Governance roles overlap with POPIA Information Officer duties; cross-engagement on incidents and data-subject requests.
NIST AI RMF GOV 2.1 roles, responsibilities and communication lines.

iso-42001-a-3.3.title: Reporting of concerns
iso-42001-a-3.3.objective: Provide a non-retaliatory mechanism for staff to raise AI-related concerns.
iso-42001-a-3.3.pb_interpretation: Whistleblower-equivalent for AI concerns. Documented SOP; concern log; staff training. Test: random employee asked "How would you raise an AI concern?" — must answer with the documented channel.
documented_evidence: Staff training records on the channel
iso-42001-a-3.3.operational_evidence: Random employee interview confirms knowledge of channel and non-retaliation.
iso-42001-a-3.3.cadence: Annual review of SOP + continuous concern intake
iso-42001-a-3.3.failure_modes: Channel exists on paper only; staff don't know how to raise concerns; concerns dismissed without record.
iso-42001-a-3.3.external_safe_phrasing: Documented non-retaliatory AI concerns channel available to all PrivateBox staff.

crosswalk.note: Management responsibilities; concern channel parallels infosec concern reporting.
Concern channel supports POPIA accountability principle; concerns about AI handling of personal information escalate via this route.

iso-42001-a-4.2.title: Resource documentation
iso-42001-a-4.2.objective: Document resources required for AI systems across their lifecycle.
iso-42001-a-4.2.pb_interpretation: General resource catalogue per AI system in scope. Cross-references the more specific A.4.3-4.6 controls for data, tooling, system/computing, human resources.
iso-42001-a-4.2.operational_evidence: Resource catalogue current with deployed inventory.
iso-42001-a-4.2.failure_modes: Catalogue exists but stale; resource categories incomplete.
iso-42001-a-4.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox documents the resources required by each AI system in scope.

crosswalk.note: Asset inventory; AI resource catalogue extends to data and tooling.

iso-42001-a-4.3.title: Data resources
iso-42001-a-4.3.objective: Document data resources used by AI systems — provenance, categories (train/val/test/production), labelling, quality, retention, known biases and rights.
iso-42001-a-4.3.pb_interpretation: PrivateBox documents ITS training/eval data and the structure expected of customer data; customer data itself remains customer-owned. Datasheets per dataset. Critical control — provenance is a top auditor finding pattern.
iso-42001-a-4.3.operational_evidence: Each in-scope AI system traces its data resources to documented datasheets.
iso-42001-a-4.3.failure_modes: Training-data provenance undocumented; datasheets exist for new datasets but not legacy; rights not surfaced; bias signals not captured.
iso-42001-a-4.3.external_safe_phrasing: Datasheets for development and evaluation data, including provenance, rights and known biases.

crosswalk.note: Data classification; AI datasets need provenance + rights overlay.
Data governance for high-risk AI under Art. 10.
NIST AI RMF GOV 1.6 AI system / model inventory.

iso-42001-a-4.4.title: Tooling resources
iso-42001-a-4.4.objective: Document tooling resources used in AI development and operation — frameworks, evaluation libraries, guardrail tools, prompt templates, RAG stack.
iso-42001-a-4.4.pb_interpretation: Tooling register with versions, licences, vulnerability disclosure references. Vendored model libraries, eval harnesses, guardrail middleware all in scope.
iso-42001-a-4.4.operational_evidence: Tooling register reconciles to the build environment.
iso-42001-a-4.4.failure_modes: Tooling register stale; licence violations slip in; experimental libraries in production code.
iso-42001-a-4.4.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains a documented register of AI tooling resources with versions and licences.

crosswalk.note: Tooling is part of the asset inventory.

iso-42001-a-4.5.title: System and computing resources
iso-42001-a-4.5.objective: Document and govern system and computing resources used by AI systems — hardware, hosting type, processing/storage capacity, environmental impact.
iso-42001-a-4.5.pb_interpretation: Critical because deployment target is customer hardware (on-prem) or partner-DC ringfenced infrastructure (AI Factory). Document minimum-spec sheet, GPU sizing guidance, energy / environmental disclosure. Annex C.2.4 environmental impact applies.
iso-42001-a-4.5.operational_evidence: Customer deployments meet documented minimum spec; environmental estimates published.
iso-42001-a-4.5.failure_modes: No spec sheet; hardware lifecycle ignored; environmental impact not estimated.
iso-42001-a-4.5.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox documents the system and computing resources required for AI deployment, including environmental footprint guidance.

crosswalk.note: Asset inventory includes computing resources.

iso-42001-a-4.6.title: Human resources
iso-42001-a-4.6.objective: Document and govern human resources for AI systems — skill matrix, diversity considerations for teams handling sensitive data domains.
iso-42001-a-4.6.pb_interpretation: Skill matrix with diversity considerations for sector teams (e.g., clinical advisors for healthcare data; legal SMEs for legal). Annex C.2.2 (AI expertise) applies. Cross-link to clause 7.2 competence.
documented_evidence: Skill matrix per role and sector
Diversity / SME engagement records for sensitive sectors
iso-42001-a-4.6.operational_evidence: Sector teams include named SMEs; skill gaps tracked and resourced.
iso-42001-a-4.6.failure_modes: "Pure tech team" with no clinical / legal / financial SME involvement; diversity considerations absent.
iso-42001-a-4.6.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains a sector-aware AI skills matrix that includes domain SME engagement.

crosswalk.note: Asset inventory; HR skill matrix is the people dimension.

iso-42001-a-5.2.title: AI system impact assessment process
iso-42001-a-5.2.objective: Maintain a documented, repeatable AISIA methodology applicable across AI systems in scope.
iso-42001-a-5.2.pb_interpretation: Mandatory. Cannot be excluded from SoA even by orgs using only third-party models. AISIA methodology aligned with ISO/IEC 42005:2025 covering 10 elements; 42005 Annex E template operationalised.
iso-42001-a-5.2.companion_note: Methodology must align to ISO/IEC 42005:2025 process structure.
iso-42001-a-5.2.operational_evidence: Methodology applied per AI system; deviations documented.
iso-42001-a-5.2.failure_modes: Methodology hand-wave; no thresholds; same template for every system regardless of risk.
iso-42001-a-5.2.external_safe_phrasing: AISIA methodology aligned with ISO/IEC 42005:2025, applied to every AI system in scope.

crosswalk.note: PIMS PIA / DPIA structurally adjacent — integrated AISIA + DPIA recommended.
EU AI Act Art. 9 risk + Art. 27 FRIA align with AISIA methodology.
AISIA methodology covers POPIA s 71 automated decision-making analysis where AI makes consequential decisions.
NIST AI RMF MAP 5.1 impact assessment methodology.

iso-42001-a-5.3.title: Documentation of AI system impact assessments
iso-42001-a-5.3.pb_interpretation: Per-system AISIA records library. Each record traceable to methodology, dated, approved. Foreseeable-misuse section completed.
iso-42001-a-5.3.operational_evidence: Each in-scope AI system has a current AISIA approved before go-live.
iso-42001-a-5.3.cadence: Pre-deployment + scheduled review + on material change
iso-42001-a-5.3.failure_modes: AISIA records inaccessible; foreseeable-misuse omitted; AISIA done after deployment.
iso-42001-a-5.3.external_safe_phrasing: AISIA records are maintained per AI system, including foreseeable-misuse coverage.

crosswalk.note: PIA records; integrated AISIA + DPIA template.
AI Act FRIA records align.

iso-42001-a-5.4.title: Assessing AI system impact on individuals or groups
iso-42001-a-5.4.objective: Within the AISIA, assess impacts on individuals or groups (fairness, privacy, safety, autonomy, employment).
iso-42001-a-5.4.pb_interpretation: Sector-criticality drives depth: healthcare → patient safety; legal → access to justice and privilege; finance → fair lending and POPIA s.71 automated decision-making. Harm/benefit taxonomy (42005 Annex C) applied.
iso-42001-a-5.4.operational_evidence: Sample AISIA for a sector use-case shows individual / group impact section completed.
iso-42001-a-5.4.failure_modes: Selective use of taxonomy; sector-specific impacts ignored; vulnerable-population impacts omitted.
iso-42001-a-5.4.external_safe_phrasing: AISIA covers impacts on individuals and groups using the ISO/IEC 42005 harm/benefit taxonomy.

crosswalk.note: Individual privacy impact assessed in DPIA.
FRIA covers fundamental-rights impacts on individuals.
Individual / group impact analysis covers special-PI sensitivity (POPIA ss 26-33) where AI processes health, biometric, or other special data.
Children's data impact analysis (POPIA ss 34-35) where AI processes children's personal information.

iso-42001-a-5.5.title: Assessing societal impacts
iso-42001-a-5.5.objective: Within the AISIA, assess societal impacts (public trust, environment, democratic processes, employment).
iso-42001-a-5.5.pb_interpretation: Broader-than-individual impacts. Annex C.2.4 environmental impact, C.2.11 transparency, public trust considerations.
iso-42001-a-5.5.failure_modes: Societal-impact section is a placeholder; environmental impact ignored; trust/transparency unaddressed.
iso-42001-a-5.5.external_safe_phrasing: AISIA includes societal-impact analysis covering public trust, environmental and broader socioeconomic considerations.

crosswalk.note: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment includes societal impacts.

iso-42001-a-6.1.1.title: Objectives for responsible development of AI systems
iso-42001-a-6.1.1.objective: Translate AI Policy and risk management decisions into engineering objectives for development.
iso-42001-a-6.1.1.pb_interpretation: Engineering-level objectives derived from policy: fairness, transparency, robustness, security, privacy, accountability targets per release. Translated from policy to specs.
iso-42001-a-6.1.1.operational_evidence: Per-release docs reference the engineering objectives.
iso-42001-a-6.1.1.failure_modes: Policy translated as platitudes; engineering objectives not measurable; release docs ignore objectives.
iso-42001-a-6.1.1.external_safe_phrasing: Engineering objectives for responsible AI development are derived from PrivateBox AI Policy.

crosswalk.note: Secure development lifecycle policy.
Risk-management requirements feed development objectives.

iso-42001-a-6.1.2.title: Processes for responsible design and development of AI systems
iso-42001-a-6.1.2.objective: Establish SDLC processes that integrate fairness, transparency, robustness, security and privacy by design.
iso-42001-a-6.1.2.pb_interpretation: SDLC integrating NIST SP 800-218A (AI overlay on SSDF). Threat modelling extends to AI-specific threats (prompt injection, training-data poisoning, model extraction). Privacy/safety/fairness by design rather than retro-fit.
iso-42001-a-6.1.2.operational_evidence: Sample release shows AI-specific design steps in commit history and review records.
iso-42001-a-6.1.2.failure_modes: "Just like normal code"; retro-fitted privacy/safety/fairness; AI-specific threats absent from threat model.
iso-42001-a-6.1.2.external_safe_phrasing: AI design and development integrates AI-specific overlays on PrivateBox's secure SDLC.

crosswalk.note: Secure SDLC + secure architecture + secure coding practices.
Risk-management and accuracy/robustness obligations under EU AI Act.

iso-42001-a-6.1.3.title: Documentation of AI system development
iso-42001-a-6.1.3.objective: Maintain documented records of AI system development — design specifications, decisions, test results, sign-offs.
iso-42001-a-6.1.3.pb_interpretation: AI equivalent of model card + system card + datasheet. Per-system / per-release documentation bundle. Auditable build pipeline.
iso-42001-a-6.1.3.operational_evidence: Recent releases have full development bundles; auditor can sample any release.
iso-42001-a-6.1.3.failure_modes: Model cards exist for marquee models but not minor ones; sign-offs verbal; test results unsigned.
iso-42001-a-6.1.3.external_safe_phrasing: Per-release model and system cards, test records and sign-offs are maintained for every AI system in scope.

crosswalk.note: Secure coding + security testing in development.
Technical documentation for high-risk AI under Annex IV.
NIST AI RMF GOV 4.2 risks and impacts documented.

iso-42001-a-6.1.4.title: AI system technical documentation regarding bias
iso-42001-a-6.1.4.objective: Address bias in AI systems through documented methodology, baselines, and retest cadence.
iso-42001-a-6.1.4.pb_interpretation: Awareness statement is NOT a methodology. Defined criteria, baselines, retest cadence per release and on drift trigger. Sector-specific fairness criteria (hiring, credit, healthcare).
iso-42001-a-6.1.4.operational_evidence: Recent release has bias evaluation against baselines; drift detection reports current.
iso-42001-a-6.1.4.failure_modes: Awareness statement instead of method; baselines not maintained; sector criteria absent.
iso-42001-a-6.1.4.external_safe_phrasing: Bias is assessed pre-release and during operation against documented criteria with sector-specific fairness considerations.

crosswalk.note: Data governance and bias mitigation obligations under Art. 10 for high-risk AI.
Bias methodology supports POPIA s 71 substantial-effect analysis for automated decisions.
NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.11 fairness and bias evaluation.

iso-42001-a-6.1.5.title: AI system robustness and verification & validation
iso-42001-a-6.1.5.objective: Establish robustness, verification and validation including adversarial testing, edge cases and red-teaming.
iso-42001-a-6.1.5.pb_interpretation: Functional-only testing fails. Adversarial testing (prompt injection, jailbreak, extraction attempts), edge cases, red-teaming, drift testing. Test depth proportional to risk tier.
documented_evidence: Test plans (functional + adversarial + edge + drift + fairness + security)
iso-42001-a-6.1.5.operational_evidence: Recent releases show full V&V evidence; risk-proportionate depth held.
iso-42001-a-6.1.5.failure_modes: Functional-only tests; adversarial tests skipped for "low-risk" use cases; red-team findings not closed.
iso-42001-a-6.1.5.external_safe_phrasing: Pre-release V&V against documented criteria including adversarial and red-team testing.

crosswalk.note: Security testing in development.
Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity obligations for high-risk AI.
NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.5 validity and reliability.
NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.7 AI security and resilience evaluation.
NIST 218A adversarial / prompt-injection testing.

iso-42001-a-6.2.1.title: AI system operational concept
iso-42001-a-6.2.1.objective: Document the operational concept — how the AI system is meant to run in the customer environment.
iso-42001-a-6.2.1.pb_interpretation: Operational concept covers intended uses, environmental assumptions, prerequisites, configurations, integration points. Becomes the baseline for the customer's own ops.
documented_evidence: Operational concept document per AI system
iso-42001-a-6.2.1.operational_evidence: Customer-facing ops docs trace to operational concept.
iso-42001-a-6.2.1.failure_modes: Operational concept is engineering-side only; customer ops docs disconnected.
iso-42001-a-6.2.1.external_safe_phrasing: Documented operational concept covers intended uses, prerequisites and integration assumptions.

crosswalk.note: SDLC operational handover.

iso-42001-a-6.2.2.title: AI system testing
iso-42001-a-6.2.2.objective: Pre-deployment testing depth proportional to risk.
iso-42001-a-6.2.2.pb_interpretation: Test plans per system, signed; test results signed; pre-go-live test verification by Change Board.
iso-42001-a-6.2.2.operational_evidence: Recent releases sampled show signed test plans and results.
iso-42001-a-6.2.2.failure_modes: Test depth not proportional to risk; sign-off verbal; test plans missing for low-priority systems.
iso-42001-a-6.2.2.external_safe_phrasing: Pre-deployment testing against documented plans with depth proportional to risk.

crosswalk.note: Security testing in development.
Accuracy and robustness testing obligations.

iso-42001-a-6.2.3.title: Human oversight of AI systems
iso-42001-a-6.2.3.objective: Provide genuine human oversight including override authority, accuracy/consistency monitoring, concern channel, and automated-decision-appropriateness assessment per use case.
iso-42001-a-6.2.3.pb_interpretation: Genuine override authority — not rubber-stamp. Sector-critical defaults to "human-in-the-loop" for legal / finance / healthcare consequential outputs. Per-system oversight design covering where humans-in-the-loop sit, what they can stop, escalation paths.
iso-42001-a-6.2.3.operational_evidence: Sample customer deployment shows oversight design exists and is operational.
iso-42001-a-6.2.3.cadence: Annual SOP review + per system release
iso-42001-a-6.2.3.failure_modes: Rubber-stamp oversight; oversight design but no override authority; legal/finance/healthcare deployments lack human-in-the-loop.
iso-42001-a-6.2.3.external_safe_phrasing: Genuine human oversight per ISO/IEC 42001 A.6.2.3, with sector-specific defaults for high-stakes use cases.

crosswalk.note: Human oversight obligations for high-risk AI under Art. 14.
Human oversight is the primary safeguard for POPIA s 71 automated decision-making in consequential workflows.
NIST AI RMF MAP 3.5 human oversight processes.
NIST AI RMF GOV 3.2 human-AI configuration and oversight roles.

iso-42001-a-6.2.4.title: AI system event logs
iso-42001-a-6.2.4.objective: Log AI system events — time, date, inputs (or hashes), outputs, anomalies — with defined retention.
iso-42001-a-6.2.4.pb_interpretation: Logs are CUSTOMER-OWNED by default. PrivateBox defines schema and retention guidance; only sees what telemetry contracts permit. Schema supports auditor reconstruction of events.
documented_evidence: AI Event-Logging Standard (schema + retention + integrity + customer-control)
iso-42001-a-6.2.4.operational_evidence: Logs in customer deployments meet the standard; retention held; integrity guarantees maintained.
iso-42001-a-6.2.4.failure_modes: Logs in customer environment without published spec; retention undefined; integrity unprotected; PB-side retention unbounded.
iso-42001-a-6.2.4.external_safe_phrasing: Auditable AI event-logging standard defining schema, retention and integrity expectations, customer-controlled by default.

crosswalk.note: 27001 logging / monitoring / clock sync controls.
Record-keeping for high-risk AI under Art. 12.
AI event logs subject to POPIA s 14 retention rules; logs must not be retained longer than necessary.
Event logging supports POPIA s 19 security-safeguard verification and s 22 incident investigation.
NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.4 production monitoring including event logs.

iso-42001-a-6.2.5.title: AI system deployment
iso-42001-a-6.2.5.objective: Document deployment plans and require pre-go-live verification — impact assessment, testing, oversight, ops docs all complete and signed.
iso-42001-a-6.2.5.pb_interpretation: Pre-deployment gate enforced by Change Board. Checklist: AISIA signed, testing signed, oversight in place, ops documentation complete. Verbal sign-off fails the gate.
iso-42001-a-6.2.5.operational_evidence: Recent deployments traced through Change Board records.
iso-42001-a-6.2.5.failure_modes: Verbal sign-off; checklist items skipped; deployment without current AISIA.
iso-42001-a-6.2.5.external_safe_phrasing: Documented pre-deployment approval gate with signed verification of impact assessment, testing, oversight and operational documentation.

crosswalk.note: Separation of dev/test/production environments.
NIST AI RMF MANAGE 1.1 go / no-go decision.

iso-42001-a-7.2.title: Data for development and enhancement of AI systems
iso-42001-a-7.2.objective: Govern operational data — quality, provenance, security, transparency — used in AI development and enhancement.
iso-42001-a-7.2.pb_interpretation: Operational data governance frame. Cross-references A.4.3 (data resources). For PB: training/eval data is PB-owned; customer corpora are customer-owned but governed under PB's framework when PB advises on RAG setup.
iso-42001-a-7.2.operational_evidence: Sample dataset traces through governance framework end-to-end.
iso-42001-a-7.2.failure_modes: Provenance partial; quality criteria implicit; transparency on data sources missing.
iso-42001-a-7.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a documented data governance framework covering quality, provenance, security and transparency.

crosswalk.note: Data classification.
Data governance for high-risk AI.
Data governance frame applies POPIA processing-limitation conditions (ss 9-12) — minimisation, lawful basis, direct collection.
NIST AI RMF MAP 4.1 component risks including data.

iso-42001-a-7.3.title: Acquisition of data
iso-42001-a-7.3.objective: Document data acquisition — categories, quantities, sources, demographics, prior handling, rights (PII, copyright), labelling metadata, provenance.
iso-42001-a-7.3.pb_interpretation: Per-acquisition record. Particularly important for open-weight model training data (Common Crawl, LAION, etc. — copyright/PII hot-spots). Customer-corpus acquisitions documented when PB advises.
iso-42001-a-7.3.operational_evidence: Sample acquisition has full record; rights cleared before use.
iso-42001-a-7.3.failure_modes: Acquisition records skipped for "public" data; rights not cleared; demographics undocumented.
iso-42001-a-7.3.external_safe_phrasing: Data acquisitions are documented with provenance, rights and demographic metadata.

crosswalk.note: Data governance / acquisition obligations.
Data acquisition documentation supports POPIA s 11 lawful-basis justification and s 12 direct-collection rules.

iso-42001-a-7.4.title: Quality of data for AI systems
iso-42001-a-7.4.objective: Define data quality criteria, measurement methods and remediation paths.
iso-42001-a-7.4.pb_interpretation: Quality criteria documented and measurable. Per-dataset quality reports; remediation procedures for sub-threshold data. Customer corpus advisories include quality gates.
documented_evidence: Data quality criteria per dataset class
iso-42001-a-7.4.operational_evidence: Sample dataset has current quality report; remediation actions trace.
iso-42001-a-7.4.failure_modes: Quality criteria implicit; thresholds undefined; remediation ad-hoc.
iso-42001-a-7.4.external_safe_phrasing: Data quality criteria and remediation procedures are documented per dataset class.

crosswalk.note: PIMS data minimisation / quality overlap.
Data quality obligations for high-risk AI.
Data quality criteria operationalise POPIA s 16 information-quality condition.

iso-42001-a-7.5.title: Processing of personal information in AI systems
iso-42001-a-7.5.objective: Govern processing of personal information by AI systems — integration with POPIA / GDPR / UK GDPR / HIPAA-equivalent.
iso-42001-a-7.5.pb_interpretation: PB defaults to "no customer PII processing in PB environment" with technical evidence; processing remains in customer's environment. When PB telemetry or support access touches PII, it is exception-only with case-by-case authorisation.
iso-42001-a-7.5.operational_evidence: Sample telemetry confirms PII-free; sample support session confirms case-by-case authorisation for PII access.
iso-42001-a-7.5.cadence: Annual + per change to data flows
iso-42001-a-7.5.failure_modes: Telemetry leaks PII; standing support access; PII processing not flowed; DPIA missing for AI-with-PII.
iso-42001-a-7.5.external_safe_phrasing: By default, PrivateBox does not process customer personal information in its environment; exceptions are case-by-case, customer-authorised and logged.

crosswalk.note: PIMS controllers + processors PII processing controls; full PIMS overlay applies.
Special categories of personal data under Art. 10(5).
PII processing in AI subject to POPIA processing-limitation conditions (ss 9-12).
PII processing in AI subject to POPIA s 19 security safeguards and s 20-21 operator confidentiality.
Special-PI handling in AI subject to POPIA ss 26-33 enhanced-control regime.
NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.10 privacy risk examination.

iso-42001-a-8.2.title: System characteristics and documentation
iso-42001-a-8.2.objective: Document AI system characteristics — intended use, capabilities, limitations, conditions of non-use.
iso-42001-a-8.2.pb_interpretation: Customer-facing transparency pack including system cards, capabilities, limitations, conditions of non-use, change log. Sales-driven inflation is a typical risk; legal review before publication.
documented_evidence: Transparency / User Information Pack per release
iso-42001-a-8.2.operational_evidence: Recent releases have current transparency pack accessible to customers.
iso-42001-a-8.2.failure_modes: Sales-driven inflation; conditions of non-use absent; capability claims unbacked by V&V.
iso-42001-a-8.2.external_safe_phrasing: Customer-facing transparency pack documenting capabilities, limitations and conditions of non-use per release.

crosswalk.note: Privacy and protection of personal information; transparency overlap.
Transparency and provision of information for high-risk AI under Art. 13.
System characteristics documentation feeds POPIA s 18 collection-notice obligations for AI deployments.
NIST AI RMF GOV 4.2 system / model card documentation.

iso-42001-a-8.3.title: External reporting / disclosure when users interact with AI
iso-42001-a-8.3.objective: Disclose when users interact with AI; align with EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency obligations.
iso-42001-a-8.3.pb_interpretation: Customer-facing UI/UX components disclose AI interaction. Transparency aligned with EU AI Act Art. 50 (deepfake / synthetic content / AI-system interaction).
iso-42001-a-8.3.failure_modes: Disclosure absent in some UI surfaces; deepfake/synthetic-content marking missing; legal review skipped.
iso-42001-a-8.3.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox surfaces disclose AI-interaction in line with EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency obligations.

crosswalk.note: Transparency obligations for AI providers and deployers.

iso-42001-a-8.4.title: Communication of incidents
iso-42001-a-8.4.objective: Maintain incident-notification procedures with timelines aligned to applicable regulation.
iso-42001-a-8.4.pb_interpretation: Notification SOPs: EU AI Act Art. 73 (15-day serious-incident); POPIA s.22 breach notification where personal info is involved (72-hour standard pattern). Customer-facing incident notification template.
iso-42001-a-8.4.operational_evidence: Past incidents trace through notification timelines.
iso-42001-a-8.4.failure_modes: Late notification; SOP exists but not exercised; customer notification template absent.
iso-42001-a-8.4.external_safe_phrasing: Documented incident-notification procedures aligned to EU AI Act Art. 73 and POPIA s.22 timelines.

crosswalk.note: Privacy notification overlap.
Serious-incident reporting obligations for high-risk AI (15 days).
Incident communication includes POPIA s 22 breach notification (operator notifies RP immediately; RP notifies Regulator + data subjects).
NIST AI RMF MANAGE 4.3 incident communication.

iso-42001-a-8.5.title: Information for interested parties
iso-42001-a-8.5.objective: Provide interested parties with information about limitations, known biases and edge cases.
iso-42001-a-8.5.pb_interpretation: Goes beyond capability claims — actively communicates limitations, known biases, edge cases. Procurement responses provide depth that sales pages don't.
iso-42001-a-8.5.operational_evidence: Procurement responses sampled show comprehensive limitation coverage.
iso-42001-a-8.5.failure_modes: Limitations omitted from public surfaces; known biases redacted; edge cases not surfaced.
iso-42001-a-8.5.external_safe_phrasing: Documented limitations, known biases and edge cases are communicated to procurement and interested parties.

crosswalk.note: Transparency obligations include limitations disclosure.

iso-42001-a-8.6.title: Communication of changes
iso-42001-a-8.6.objective: Communicate material changes in AI systems to affected parties.
iso-42001-a-8.6.pb_interpretation: Customer change-notification pipeline. Material changes trigger notification with sufficient lead time for customer change-management.
iso-42001-a-8.6.operational_evidence: Recent material changes notified per SOP; customer ack records held.
iso-42001-a-8.6.failure_modes: Notification skipped for "minor" model swaps that materially change behaviour; lead time too short for customer change-management.
iso-42001-a-8.6.external_safe_phrasing: Material changes to PrivateBox AI systems are communicated to customers under documented SOP with sufficient lead time.

crosswalk.note: Transparency includes change notification to deployers.

iso-42001-a-9.2.title: Processes for responsible use of AI systems
iso-42001-a-9.2.objective: Establish internal processes that guide responsible use of AI systems.
iso-42001-a-9.2.pb_interpretation: Internal Acceptable Use Policy for PrivateBox staff and contractors. Covers PB-internal use of AI tooling for development, support, customer engagement.
iso-42001-a-9.2.operational_evidence: Random staff sampling confirms knowledge of acceptable-use scope.
iso-42001-a-9.2.failure_modes: Acceptable use undefined for new tools; staff use AI for sensitive purposes outside policy.
iso-42001-a-9.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a documented internal acceptable-use policy for AI systems.

crosswalk.note: Acceptable use of information and assets.

iso-42001-a-9.3.title: Objectives for responsible use of AI systems
iso-42001-a-9.3.objective: Translate responsible-use principles into measurable objectives.
iso-42001-a-9.3.pb_interpretation: Measurable objectives for responsible internal AI use — e.g., 100% of customer-data interactions via approved tooling; bias reports reviewed before sales deployment.
iso-42001-a-9.3.operational_evidence: Objectives traced to KPIs and reviewed at management review.
iso-42001-a-9.3.external_safe_phrasing: Responsible-use objectives are measurable and tracked through management review.

crosswalk.note: Acceptable use objectives.

iso-42001-a-9.4.title: Intended use of the AI system
iso-42001-a-9.4.objective: Operational safeguards prevent out-of-scope use; user training and guidance support intended use.
iso-42001-a-9.4.pb_interpretation: Critical for legal/finance/healthcare to scope what PrivateBox is NOT used for (e.g., not the sole basis for clinical, credit, or legal-advice decisions). Customer-facing intended-use register with prohibitions.
iso-42001-a-9.4.operational_evidence: Customer deployment artefacts show intended-use scope; staff training current.
iso-42001-a-9.4.failure_modes: Intended-use undefined; customer deployments drift outside scope without flag; sector-specific prohibitions absent.
iso-42001-a-9.4.external_safe_phrasing: Documented intended and prohibited uses per AI system, with sector-specific guidance for high-stakes domains.

crosswalk.note: Acceptable use + asset return cover use scope.
Deployer obligations under Art. 26 + transparency under Art. 50.
Intended-use enforcement supports POPIA s 11 lawful-purpose limitation.
Intended-use scope flags AI workflows that may trigger POPIA s 71 substantial-effect analysis.
NIST AI RMF MAP 3.3 targeted application scope.

iso-42001-a-10.2.title: Allocating responsibilities (suppliers and partners)
iso-42001-a-10.2.objective: Allocate AI-related responsibilities across the supply chain — model providers, partners, upstream data sources.
iso-42001-a-10.2.pb_interpretation: Cannot be excluded from SoA. Due diligence on model providers (open-weight: Meta, Mistral, etc.; proprietary: Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.). Contractual AI requirements; ongoing oversight; model-card review. "Pulled-from-Hugging-Face" model adoption without DD is a known finding pattern.
iso-42001-a-10.2.operational_evidence: Sample model approval traces from DD through approval to deployment.
iso-42001-a-10.2.failure_modes: Open-weight models adopted without DD; supplier review absent; contractual AI clauses missing from MSAs; partner DC supplier obligations undefined.
iso-42001-a-10.2.external_safe_phrasing: Documented supplier and model-vendor due-diligence programme covering open-weight and proprietary models.

crosswalk.note: Supplier relationships, ICT supply chain, monitoring of supplier services.
Responsibilities along the AI value chain under Art. 25.
Supplier due diligence covers POPIA s 20-21 operator obligations; sub-processor authorisation under s 21.
NIST AI RMF GOV 6.1 third-party AI / data / IP / supply-chain risk.
NIST 218A third-party model governance.

iso-42001-a-10.3.title: Suppliers
iso-42001-a-10.3.objective: Govern the supplier-side of AI relationships — DD, contractual obligations, ongoing oversight.
iso-42001-a-10.3.pb_interpretation: Supplier programme covering model providers, hosting partners, RAG / data acquisition vendors, open-source library providers (with appropriate scope per supplier risk).
iso-42001-a-10.3.operational_evidence: Supplier register sampled traces to current contracts and review records.
iso-42001-a-10.3.failure_modes: Open-source library providers omitted from supplier programme; reviews skipped.
iso-42001-a-10.3.external_safe_phrasing: Supplier programme covers AI-relevant vendors with appropriate due-diligence depth.

crosswalk.note: Supplier relationships and contractual InfoSec.

iso-42001-a-10.4.title: Customers
iso-42001-a-10.4.objective: Govern customer-side AI relationships — intended use, prohibited use, oversight expectations, change notifications, EULA / MSA AI clauses.
iso-42001-a-10.4.pb_interpretation: Customer-facing AI clauses in MSA / EULA. Customer Shared-Responsibility Matrix (productised version of source §7). Customer enablement pack. Sales staff trained on red-flag claim list.
iso-42001-a-10.4.operational_evidence: Recent customer onboarding sampled shows AI clauses signed, matrix delivered, enablement complete.
iso-42001-a-10.4.cadence: Per customer onboarding + per material change
iso-42001-a-10.4.failure_modes: AI clauses absent from MSA; shared-responsibility matrix verbal; customer enablement skipped.
iso-42001-a-10.4.external_safe_phrasing: Documented customer-facing AI obligations covering intended use, prohibited use, oversight expectations and change notifications.

popia.full_name: Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013 (Act No. 4 of 2013)

effective_dates.milestone: POPIA full commencement (1-year transitional period ended)

pb_application.why_chosen: POPIA is the binding South African statutory baseline for processing personal information. PrivateBox targets SA legal, finance and healthcare buyers as primary market — every procurement engagement asks about POPIA posture. Unlike ISO 42001 (alignment standard), POPIA is law: enforcement notices, fines and civil remedies are real and visible (Dis-Chem enforcement notice; Department of Basic Education infringement notice). PrivateBox carries dual POPIA roles — responsible party for its own corporate processing (website, CRM, billing, staff, marketing) and operator when handling customer personal information under contract (support, telemetry, managed services). On-prem architecture materially reduces operator-scenario exposure but does NOT remove POPIA duties; "on-prem = compliant" is a red-flag claim. PB's external position: "designed in alignment with POPIA, with explicit responsible-party / operator role allocation per deployment scenario."

on_prem.rationale: PB's responsible-party duties for its own corporate processing apply regardless of customer deployment model. PB's operator duties activate per scenario (Table 3 in source pack): pure on-prem with no remote support has minimal operator exposure; on-prem with telemetry / support access / managed services creates operator scenarios with full ss 19-22 duties. Default contractual posture: customer is responsible party for in-environment processing; PB is operator only for explicitly-scoped service layers. The dual-role schedule is published as part of the Customer Shared-Responsibility Matrix.

ai_factory.rationale: AI Factory ringfenced multi-tenant deployment in a partner DC introduces additional operator and sub-processor relationships. Cross-border concerns (s 72) activate if the partner DC is offshore. Until partner DC is selected (oq-partner-dc-selection), specific operator-chain documentation (s 21 sub-operator notification, s 22 incident notification flow, transfer register under s 72) is incomplete. AI Factory POPIA scope is provisional pending partner-DC decision.

top_risks: Misclassification of role — assuming "software vendor only / on-prem means out of scope" when optional services (telemetry, support access, backups, RAG ingestion assistance) make PB an operator under s 20-21. Documented role allocation per deployment scenario is the primary control.

Hidden secondary use of customer data for vendor analytics or model training (s 15). High-likelihood challenge from buyers. Mitigation: contractual prohibition + technical separation + no-vendor-training default in DPA.
Telemetry leakage of personal information (ss 9-12, 17-18, 19). Telemetry pipeline must have a field-level data dictionary, default-off for customer content, opt-in for operational metadata.
Standing or undocumented support access (s 20-21). Just-in-time, customer-approved, fully logged sessions are mandatory; ad-hoc access via Slack / WhatsApp / screen-photos is a finding pattern.

Late or partial breach notification (s 22). PB-as-operator must notify customer-as-responsible-party "immediately"; customer then has the s 22 statutory notification duty to Regulator + data subjects. Joint-response tabletop exercise is the maturity indicator.
Cross-border support transferring personal information offshore (s 72) without adequacy / binding agreements / consent. Even "temporary support access" by foreign engineer is a transfer.

Information Officer designated but not registered with the Regulator (s 55-56 + Regulation 4). Registration is a public filing; auditors and clients verify it. Active operating cadence (training, awareness, framework review) is the additional bar.

Special / children's data deployments (ss 26-35) without enhanced controls profile and lawful-basis documentation. Pharmacy / healthcare / education sectors are high-likelihood PB customer deployments — generic deployment templates fail in these sectors.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB's own RP duties for corporate processing — website, CRM, billing, staff, marketing.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer's RP duties for their AI use cases inside their environment.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Two parallel RP postures; one trust interface (DPA / MSA).

shared_responsibility_model.area: Operator (processor) duties when PB processes customer data
shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: ss 20-21 operator duties — confidentiality, security same-as-RP, immediate breach notice to RP.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: RP-side oversight of PB-as-operator including DD and contractual flow-down.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Activates only for scenarios where PB sees / handles customer personal information.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB designates and registers its own IO with the Information Regulator.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer designates its own IO; routes data-subject requests via PB only when contractually agreed.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Two IOs — distinct legal duties, occasional cross-engagement on incidents.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PrivateBox's own privacy notice, PAIA manual, marketing notice.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer's deployment-specific notice to its end-users.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: PB provides deployment-notice template inputs; customer adapts to its context.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: Product hardening, signed updates, support-access controls, telemetry boundary, vendor-side stores.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Environment-level security (network, physical, endpoint, identity).
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: PB ships a security baseline and minimum-spec sheet; customer operates above that baseline.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB-as-operator notifies customer-as-RP "immediately" on suspected unauthorised access.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: RP statutory notification duty to Regulator + data subjects (within "as soon as reasonably possible").
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Joint IR plan + escalation contacts; tabletop exercise per year.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: PB-side records (PB website, billing, support tickets, marketing) — PB answers directly.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer-side records (in-deployment data, conversations, RAG content) — customer answers.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: PB tooling supports search / export / deletion; routing matrix in MSA.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: Telemetry pipeline; data dictionary; PII-free statement; opt-in / opt-out controls.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer accepts / declines telemetry per opt-in; configures telemetry scope in product.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Telemetry crosses trust boundary — most-tested control by procurement.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: Just-in-time access workflow; session recording; access reviews; deletion of copied artefacts.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Per-session approval; incident-specific authorisation.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Standing access is forbidden — POPIA-relevant operator scenario.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: Ingestion framework, quality gates, sensitive-data handling controls, ingestion SOP.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer owns the corpus, content rights, end-of-life data handling.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: PB advisory engagement may briefly process customer documents — operator scenario.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: Backup infrastructure (when vendor-managed) — encryption, location, retention, customer-managed keys.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer authorises and configures backups; receives restore approval authority.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Backups are operator-scenario data flows; cross-border issue if backup location is offshore.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: Foreign-touchpoint review, transfer register, sub-processor list.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer notice + consent for cross-border transfer where required.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Section 72 transfer rules apply; offshore engineer access is a transfer.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: Consent records; opt-out workflow; suppression lists.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: N/A (customer's marketing is customer's responsibility).
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Section 69 applies to PB's B2B marketing.
