CEO + at least one other executive on the board (Practice 30). Diversity across competencies, age, culture, race, gender (Practice 31). Staggered rotation (Practice 32). Succession plan (Practice 33). Skills matrix. Independence assessed against the nine Practice 42 factors. Background checks and verified qualifications for nominees (Practice 37c).
documented_evidence: Per-NED independence assessment memo (against Practice 42 nine factors)
requirements.operational_evidence: Independence rationale documented for any director near the line; nine-year tenure reviewed annually.
requirements.failure_modes: Founder-controlled board with no independents; cosmetic NED who is in fact a customer/supplier; "independence by assertion".
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's board composition is structured to deliver the balance of competencies, diversity and independence contemplated by King V Principle 5, applied proportionately to a scaling private technology company.

crosswalk.note: Roles, responsibilities and authorities under ISMS map to board composition discipline.
PIMS roles analogue.
AIMS roles analogue.
Information Officer designation under POPIA overlaps with King V board composition discipline.

requirements.title: Committees of the governing body
requirements.requirement: Arrangements for delegation to committees and individuals promote the objective and effective discharge of the governing body's obligations.
requirements.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects committees with written TORs, at least 3 members, balanced; combined committee architecture proportionate at smaller scale; statutory S&E committee triggered by PI Score.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Proportionate model — combined Audit/Risk/Technology Committee (one TOR, 3 members, majority NED, at least one independent NED, chaired by independent NED who is not Chair of board); combined Social/Ethics/Remuneration Committee (incorporates corporate citizenship and remuneration governance); optional AI Oversight Sub-committee or working group reporting in. PI Score crossing triggers mandatory statutory S&E committee under Companies Act s 72(4) and Regulation 43.
requirements.operational_evidence: Each committee has documented terms of reference, named members, attendance records, and chair reports to board.
requirements.failure_modes: Chair of board chairing the audit committee (Practice 49a prohibits); CEO sitting on audit/remuneration/nominations as a member (Practice 78 prohibits, may attend by invitation).
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a proportionate committee architecture aligned to King V Principle 6, including a combined audit, risk and technology committee and a social, ethics and remuneration committee.

crosswalk.note: ISMS management-review committees overlap with King V committee discipline.
PIMS management review analogue.
AIMS management review analogue.

requirements.title: Appointment and delegation to management
requirements.requirement: Arrangements for appointment of and delegation to management promote operational effectiveness and ensure that the respective roles and decision-making powers of the governing body and management are clearly defined.
requirements.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects CEO formally appointed, not on audit/remuneration/nominations (Practice 78), board-approved Delegation of Authority framework, CEO succession plan, access to professional corporate-governance services.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Founder/CEO must not also chair (Practice 48); 3-year cooling-off applies before retired CEO can chair. CEO formally appointed (Practice 76). Board-approved Delegation of Authority framework (Practice 82). CEO succession plan (Practice 79). Cosec or equivalent provides independent corporate-governance services (Practices 84-90).
requirements.operational_evidence: DoA framework demonstrably operates — recent material decisions trace through documented authority.
requirements.failure_modes: Founder retains undocumented decision-making power; no cosec equivalent; CEO succession plan absent.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a board-approved delegation-of-authority framework and CEO succession discipline aligned with King V Principle 7.

crosswalk.note: Roles and authorities under ISMS overlap with King V management delegation.
AIMS roles analogue.

requirements.title: Risk
requirements.requirement: The governing body governs risk in a way that enables the organisation to sustain and optimise its strategy and objectives — opportunity-aware, integrated, iterative, resilient.
requirements.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects approved risk policy, documented risk appetite and tolerance, integrated risk register, BCM with testing, iterative change-responsive process, periodic assurance.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Cyber, AI model, supplier, business-continuity, key-person, regulatory, and reputational risks are the dominant cluster. Model drift, prompt-injection, supply-chain compromise of foundation models / model weights / containers, telemetry channel abuse, on-prem update channel integrity, key-person dependency on founder.
documented_evidence: Enterprise Risk Register (cyber, AI, supplier, key-person, BCM, regulatory, commercial sub-registers)
requirements.operational_evidence: Risk register reviewed quarterly; appetite breaches escalated; BCM exercised annually.
requirements.cadence: Quarterly register review + annual appetite review + annual BCM exercise
requirements.failure_modes: Separate cyber/AI/privacy registers with no integration; treating ISO 27001 as a substitute for board-level risk governance; static appetite that never changes.
requirements.external_safe_claim: Risk governance at PrivateBox is structured to deliver the outcomes contemplated by King V Principle 8, with explicit treatment of cyber, AI model, supplier, and business-continuity risks.

crosswalk.note: ISMS risk framework feeds King V P8 enterprise risk view.
PIMS risk analogue.
AIMS risk methodology feeds King V P8 enterprise risk view.
POPIA security safeguards (s 19) are a sub-register of enterprise risk under King V P8.
EU AI Act risk-management for high-risk AI feeds King V P8 enterprise risk.

requirements.title: Compliance
requirements.requirement: The governing body governs compliance with applicable laws and adopted policies, non-binding rules, codes and standards in a way that promotes ethics and responsible corporate citizenship.
requirements.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects board-approved compliance framework, obligations register naming binding and adopted standards, regulatory horizon scanning.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Obligations register covers POPIA, Cybercrimes Act, ECTA, Companies Act, EU AI Act extra- territorial reach, sector pass-through obligations. Adopted non-binding standards explicitly listed (ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 42001, NIST AI RMF, NIST SSDF, EU AI Act voluntary GPAI code, King V itself). Regulatory horizon scanning (Practice 98d). Periodic assurance on compliance function (Practice 99).
requirements.operational_evidence: Regulatory changes trace from horizon scan to register update to control adjustment.
requirements.failure_modes: Confusing King V (voluntary) with binding obligations; obligations register stale; no horizon scanning.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's compliance framework is governed at board level and addresses both binding obligations and adopted non-binding standards in alignment with King V Principle 9.

crosswalk.note: Legal / regulatory / contractual compliance under ISMS; privacy and PII.
POPIA accountability principle underpins compliance governance.

requirements.title: Data, information and technology
requirements.requirement: The governing body governs data, information and technology in a way that enables the organisation to sustain and optimise its strategy and objectives — including emerging-technology risk and AI governance under Practice 109c covering ethics, human centricity, accountability, transparency, explainability, security, privacy, fairness, trustworthiness, and human oversight commensurate with risk.
requirements.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects board-approved data and technology policies, AI governance under Practice 109c, cyber dashboard, privacy programme, third-party assurance. This is the King V flagship update versus King IV — the AI governance limb is the highest-leverage clause.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Highest-leverage principle for PrivateBox. Data and Information branch (Practices 100-104): lifecycle controls, classification, CIA, privacy, third-party / cross-border risk, periodic assurance. Technology branch (Practices 105-110): legal compliance, resilience, benefit realisation, responsible disposal, effective cybersecurity, outsourced/third-party tech assurance. Emerging tech / AI checklist (109c): AI ethics, human centricity, accountability, transparency, explainability, security, privacy, fairness, trustworthiness; clear accountability for AI decisions/actions/outputs/outcomes; human oversight and override mechanisms commensurate with risk. On-prem architecture earns its keep here — policy explicitly states telemetry / update / support / connector channels, what data they touch, what consent and control client has, and how human override works for AI outputs.
documented_evidence: Privacy Governance Policy + POPIA Programme + IO designation
AI Governance Policy + AI Ethics Statement
AI System Cards per production model
AI Oversight Working Group charter + minutes
requirements.operational_evidence: Board pack includes cyber dashboard, AI risk dashboard, privacy dashboard; AI System Cards trace to production deployments; human-override mechanisms tested.
requirements.cadence: Annual policy review + per release / per AI change
requirements.failure_modes: AI deployed before policy in place; "human in the loop" as decoration; telemetry treated as "metadata not personal" without analysis; CISO reporting only annually; cyber treated as IT; foundation-model provider treated as untouchable.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox governs data, information, technology and AI through board-approved policies designed to deliver the human-oversight, accountability, transparency, explainability, security, privacy, fairness and trustworthiness expectations of King V Principle 10.

crosswalk.note: ISMS asset inventory + data classification + privacy overlay.
PIMS PIA / records underpin King V P10 data branch.
AIMS AISIA + human oversight + bias methodology operationalise Practice 109c.
POPIA processing limitation, security safeguards, s 71 automated decisions feed P10.
EU AI Act high-risk articles align with Practice 109c values and oversight requirements.

requirements.title: Remuneration
requirements.requirement: The organisation remunerates fairly, responsibly and transparently to promote sustainable value creation. King V is less prescriptive than King IV, deferring to s 30(4) Companies Act and Companies Amendment Act 16 of 2024 (not yet in force at time of authoring).
requirements.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects board-approved remuneration policy, fair-and-responsible test between executive and overall employee remuneration, malus/clawback for variable pay.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Board-approved remuneration policy (Practice 113). Fair-and-responsible test between executive and overall employee remuneration (Practice 114b). Malus/clawback for variable pay (Practice 114e). NED fee policy submitted to shareholders if applicable (Practice 115). Shareholder engagement on remuneration (Practice 116). For entities required to have a S&E committee, non- binding advisory votes on policy (every 3 years or material change) and on implementation report (annual) (Practice 118).
documented_evidence: Malus / clawback clauses for variable pay
requirements.operational_evidence: Remuneration decisions trace through documented policy; fair-and-responsible test recalculated annually.
requirements.failure_modes: Founder-set salaries with no committee discipline; absence of malus/clawback for AI-misuse-linked variable pay; no fair-and-responsible analysis.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a fair, responsible and transparent remuneration framework aligned to King V Principle 11.

requirements.title: Assurance
requirements.requirement: Assurance functions and services promote an effective internal control environment and safeguard the integrity of external reports through combined assurance across line management, specialist functions, internal audit, external audit, specialist providers and regulators.
requirements.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects combined assurance map covering all significant risks × assurance providers × cadence; internal audit charter (co-sourced acceptable for proportionate scale); external IA quality review every 5 years.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Combined assurance map covers ISO 27001/27701 surveillance, NIST AI RMF self-assessment, SOC 2 Type II (if pursued), penetration testing, internal financial controls review, statutory audit (when triggered), product-level red-team / model-evaluation reports. For each external report state level of assurance and criteria (Practice 126). Internal audit charter and risk-based plan (Practices 129-130) — may be co-sourced (Practice 131c). External independent review of internal audit at least once every 5 years (Practice 131f).
requirements.operational_evidence: Assurance providers operate per cadence; remediation tracked through closure; map refreshed annually.
requirements.cadence: Annual map review + per assurance cycle
requirements.failure_modes: Audit fatigue with no remediation; same team auditing itself; gaps in coverage of AI risk; external IA quality review skipped.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a combined assurance model designed to support effective internal controls and the integrity of external reports in alignment with King V Principle 12.

crosswalk.note: ISMS internal audit feeds King V P12 combined assurance.
PIMS internal audit analogue.
AIMS internal audit analogue.

requirements.title: Stakeholders
requirements.requirement: The governing body adopts a stakeholder-inclusive approach in the execution of its duties in the long-term best interests of the organisation within its economic, social and environmental context, upholding Ubuntu-Botho.
requirements.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects stakeholder map, engagement framework, dispute-resolution mechanism, periodic assurance on stakeholder management; for organisations with shareholders, AGM cadence and accessibility.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Stakeholder map covers clients (especially regulated-sector procurement and CISO/DPO functions), client end-users (data subjects), regulators (Information Regulator, sector regulators), sub-processors and model / foundation-model providers, employees, founders/investors, community/society. Board-approved stakeholder engagement framework (Practice 134). Stakeholder methodology (135a). Risk/opportunity analysis from stakeholder interests (135b). Dispute resolution (135c). Periodic assurance on stakeholder management (136). For PrivateBox, shareholder engagement scales to investor relations and material customer relationship management. Group governance framework if subsidiaries exist (Practices 145-150).
documented_evidence: AI Transparency Disclosure pack for clients
requirements.operational_evidence: Quarterly stakeholder report to board; documented client / customer engagement minutes; published governance section on website.
requirements.cadence: Annual framework review + continuous engagement log
requirements.failure_modes: "Marketing trust" without substance; opaque telemetry; missing AI transparency; founder-only stakeholder relationships with no institutional memory.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies a stakeholder-inclusive approach to governance, designed to support the King V outcomes of legitimacy and sustainable value creation.

crosswalk.note: ISMS interested-parties identification underpins King V stakeholder framework.
PIMS interested-parties analogue.
AIMS interested-parties analogue.
POPIA data subject participation underpins stakeholder rights handling.

requirements.title: Board Charter
requirements.objective: The governing body approves and periodically reviews a Board Charter setting out its roles, responsibilities, membership, conduct, meeting procedures, reserved matters, and delegation.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PrivateBox Board Charter v1.0+ is King V-mapped with PrivateBox-specific reserved matters including: approval of new sub-processor, approval of foundation-model change, approval of telemetry policy change, approval of AI Governance Policy, approval of King V Disclosure Framework publication.
requirements.operational_evidence: Charter referenced in board minutes; reserved matters traced to approval decisions.
requirements.failure_modes: Template charter with no PrivateBox-specific reserved matters; charter approved but not lived.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: The PrivateBox Board operates under a King V-mapped Board Charter with explicit reserved-matters discipline for sub-processor approvals, AI changes and telemetry policy.

crosswalk.note: ISMS leadership commitment instantiates through board-level chartering.

requirements.title: Board evaluation cadence
requirements.objective: The governing body undertakes formal performance evaluation of the board, committees, chair and individuals at least every two years, with methodology approved and external facilitation considered.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Biennial internal evaluation; externally facilitated evaluation expected at maturity (Series B onwards). Methodology approved by board / nominations. Evaluation report and action plan maintained.
requirements.operational_evidence: Most recent evaluation traceable; actions closed through subsequent agenda items.
requirements.cadence: At least every 2 years; externally facilitated at maturity
requirements.failure_modes: Evaluation done but no follow-up; methodology never reviewed; external facilitation skipped at maturity.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox conducts formal board, committee, chair and director performance evaluations at least every two years.

requirements.title: Code of Ethics
requirements.objective: The governing body approves a Code of Ethics / Code of Conduct addressing values, ethical standards, anti-corruption / anti-fraud / anti-money-laundering programmes, and the organisation's expectations of conduct.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Includes explicit AI Ethics Statement (linked to Principle 10 109c values). Anti-corruption programme covers gifts, hospitality, facilitation payments. Built into hiring, performance, and supplier onboarding.
requirements.operational_evidence: Code referenced in employment contracts and supplier onboarding; training completion sampled.
requirements.failure_modes: Code on the wall but not lived; ethics confused with compliance.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a board-approved Code of Ethics including an AI Ethics Statement and anti-corruption programmes.

crosswalk.note: Management responsibilities under ISMS overlap with code-of-ethics governance.
AI Policy operationalises the AI-specific portion of Practice 12.

requirements.title: Protected whistleblowing channel
requirements.objective: The governing body ensures the existence of a protected whistleblowing channel that is independent and accessible to internal and external whistleblowers.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Independent third-party provider preferred. Anonymity and protection from retaliation enforced. Periodic stats to S&E committee (anonymised). Channel covers ethics matters, fraud, AI misuse concerns.
requirements.operational_evidence: Trace a reported ethics matter from intake to closure; non-retaliation enforced.
requirements.cadence: Continuous channel + quarterly stats to S&E
requirements.failure_modes: Channel routes to the implicated party; no independence; staff don't know how to raise concerns.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates an independent, protected whistleblowing channel with periodic reporting to the Social and Ethics function.

crosswalk.note: AIMS reporting of concerns is the AI-specific instantiation of King V protected whistleblowing.

requirements.title: King V Disclosure Framework publication
requirements.objective: The governing body approves and publishes on the website an annual King V Governance Disclosure structured in accordance with the King V Disclosure Framework, including a concluding statement on the four governance outcomes.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Mandatory for any organisation claiming application of King V. PrivateBox publishes an annual King V Governance Disclosure on its website. Concluding statement on Ethical Culture, Performance and Value Creation, Conformance and Prudent Control, Legitimacy. Companion Trust & Governance Pack NDA-gated for procurement diligence.
requirements.operational_evidence: Disclosure accessible on website; board minute approving each annual edition.
requirements.failure_modes: Disclosure absent from website; copy-paste concluding statement with no evidence trail; marketing copy posing as disclosure.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox publishes an annual King V Governance Disclosure structured in accordance with the King V Disclosure Framework.

crosswalk.note: POPIA s 17-18 openness condition; external disclosure overlap.

requirements.title: Majority non-executive directors
requirements.objective: The governing body has a majority of non-executive members, most of whom are independent (assessed holistically against Practice 42).
requirements.pb_interpretation: Target: at least one independent NED at minimum, scaling to majority pre-IPO. Founder-led — must consciously over-correct for independence.
requirements.operational_evidence: Composition reviewed at least annually; rationale documented for any director near the independence line.
requirements.failure_modes: Founder-controlled board with no independents; cosmetic NEDs in fact customers/suppliers.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's board composition includes independent non-executive representation appropriate to its stage and scale, with documented trajectory toward majority NED.

crosswalk.note: Roles and authorities under ISMS overlap with NED composition discipline.

requirements.title: Holistic independence assessment
requirements.objective: The governing body assesses each non-executive director's independence holistically against the nine factors set out in Practice 42 (significant funder; securities-significance; former-executive within 3 years; former-audit-team within 3 years; significant supplier/customer; related-party; tenure exceeding 9 years; performance-contingent remuneration; other reasonable factors).
requirements.pb_interpretation: Documented per-NED independence assessment memo, signed by board, refreshed annually. Tenure exceeding 9 years assessed annually with explicit rationale. "Independence by assertion" is a common King V transition failure.
requirements.operational_evidence: Assessment memo references all nine factors; signed by board.
requirements.failure_modes: Ticking only the ">9 yrs" box; independence by assertion without nine-factor analysis.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox assesses non-executive director independence holistically against the King V Practice 42 factors with documented per-director rationale.

requirements.title: Chair and Lead Independent NED
requirements.objective: The chair is an independent non-executive director; where this is not the case (e.g., founder-CEO is Chair), a Lead Independent NED is appointed as compensating measure.
requirements.pb_interpretation: For PrivateBox, until an independent Chair is appointed, the Lead Independent NED designation is the mandatory compensating measure. Lead Independent leads board in Chair's absence/conflict, leads Chair evaluation, acts as sounding board.
requirements.operational_evidence: Lead Independent operates with actual authority; trace NED-only session attendance.
requirements.failure_modes: Founder/CEO dual-hatting as Chair with no Lead Independent; Lead Independent title without authority.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's board operates with either an independent Chair or a Lead Independent NED designation in accordance with King V Practice 45.

requirements.title: Audit committee scope and oversight
requirements.objective: The audit committee oversees external assurance, internal financial controls, the CFO and finance function, external reports, internal audit, combined assurance, and risk where combined.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Combined Audit/Risk/Technology Committee TOR. Minimum 4 meetings per year at maturity. At least one independent NED chairing. Practice 49a — Chair of board does not chair audit committee.
documented_evidence: CFO / CISO / DPO / Head of AI dashboards
requirements.operational_evidence: Auditor / external review can trace from external audit findings through committee minutes to closure.
requirements.cadence: Minimum 4 meetings per year at maturity
requirements.failure_modes: Audit dominance with risk and technology underweighted; no independent NED chair; Chair-of-board chairing audit committee.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's combined Audit/Risk/Technology Committee oversees assurance, internal financial controls, cyber, privacy and AI in alignment with King V Practice 59.

crosswalk.note: ISMS internal audit governance reaches audit committee oversight.

requirements.title: Social and Ethics committee scope
requirements.objective: The social and ethics committee oversees ethics, corporate citizenship, impact on the six capitals, stakeholder relationships, and (where the entity is required to have a statutory S&E committee under Companies Act s 72(4) and Regulation 43) the statutory monitoring and reporting matters.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Combined Social/Ethics/Remuneration Committee TOR while proportionate; converts to standalone statutory S&E committee when PI Score triggers Regulation 43. At least one independent NED member. Minimum 2 meetings per year.
requirements.operational_evidence: Committee meets at cadence; ethics, citizenship, stakeholder topics traced through minutes.
requirements.cadence: Minimum 2 meetings per year + statutory cycle when triggered
requirements.failure_modes: Box-tick S&E with no real ethics work; remuneration set without policy; no independent NED member.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's Social, Ethics and Remuneration Committee oversees ethics, corporate citizenship and stakeholders in alignment with King V Practice 74, pre-built to convert to statutory S&E committee on PI Score trigger.

requirements.title: Delegation of Authority Framework
requirements.objective: The governing body approves a Delegation of Authority Framework setting out the matrix of reserved vs delegated decisions with monetary, contractual, hiring, sub-processor, security exception, and AI-deployment thresholds.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PrivateBox DoA Framework explicitly thresholds: sub-processor onboarding, foundation-model selection, telemetry policy change, security exception approval, AI deployment / model change.
documented_evidence: Recent material decisions traced through DoA
requirements.operational_evidence: Material decisions trace through documented authority; no founder-only approvals on DoA-reserved items.
requirements.failure_modes: Founder retains undocumented authority; DoA exists but not enforced.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a board-approved Delegation of Authority Framework covering monetary, contractual, sub-processor and AI deployment thresholds.

crosswalk.note: Authorities and responsibilities under ISMS overlap with DoA framework.

requirements.title: Company Secretary / governance support
requirements.objective: The governing body has access to a company secretary or equivalent professional corporate- governance service that is arms-length from management.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Cosec-as-a-service is proportionate for PrivateBox's current stage. Transition to in-house at scale. Statutory filings, minute integrity, governance counsel.
requirements.operational_evidence: Statutory filings up to date; minute integrity demonstrable.
requirements.failure_modes: Cosec function blended with management role of an executive; missing statutory filings.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's governing body has access to professional corporate-governance services arms-length from management in alignment with King V Practice 84.

requirements.title: Risk Management Policy
requirements.objective: The governing body approves a Risk Management Policy and frameworks covering enterprise risk identification, assessment, treatment, monitoring and reporting.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Risk Management Policy covers cyber, AI, supplier, key-person, BCM, regulatory, commercial sub-registers. Integration with ISMS / PIMS / AIMS risk methodologies.
requirements.operational_evidence: Policy referenced in risk register entries; cadence held.
requirements.failure_modes: Policy stale; not integrated with ISMS/PIMS/AIMS risk methodologies.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a board-approved Risk Management Policy and framework integrated with its ISMS, PIMS and AIMS risk methodologies in alignment with King V Practice 92.

crosswalk.note: ISMS risk methodology feeds enterprise risk policy.
AIMS risk methodology feeds enterprise risk policy.

requirements.title: Risk register, appetite and BCM
requirements.objective: The governing body approves a documented risk appetite and tolerance, maintains a risk register covering economic / social / environmental context, and ensures business-continuity and resilience arrangements with periodic testing.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Quarterly register review; annual appetite review; annual BCM test report. Key-person sub- register for founder dependency. Iterative, change-responsive.
requirements.operational_evidence: Recent appetite breaches escalated; recent BCM exercise completed within cadence.
requirements.cadence: Quarterly register + annual appetite + annual BCM
requirements.failure_modes: Static register; no link to change events; BCM untested; key-person risk ignored.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains a documented risk appetite, integrated risk register and tested business- continuity arrangements in alignment with King V Practice 93.

crosswalk.note: ISMS risk register pattern.
AIMS risk register pattern.

requirements.title: Compliance universe / obligations register
requirements.objective: The governing body maintains an obligations register linking each obligation to a control owner, covering binding laws, regulator obligations of clients that pass through, adopted non-binding standards, and regulatory horizon scanning.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Obligations register: POPIA, Cybercrimes Act, ECTA, Companies Act, sector pass-through, EU AI Act extra-territorial reach. Adopted standards: ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 42001, NIST AI RMF, NIST SSDF, EU AI Act voluntary GPAI code, King V. Horizon-scanning log.
requirements.operational_evidence: Regulatory changes trace from horizon scan to register update to control adjustment.
requirements.failure_modes: Register stale; horizon scanning informal; King V (voluntary) confused with binding obligations.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains a documented compliance universe covering binding obligations, sector pass- through, and adopted non-binding standards in alignment with King V Practice 98.

crosswalk.note: Legal / regulatory / contractual compliance under ISMS.

requirements.title: Data and information lifecycle
requirements.objective: The governing body approves data and information policies covering acquisition, creation, use, dissemination, disposal, ethical/responsible management, legal compliance, classification of sensitive data, confidentiality, integrity, availability, privacy, data quality, and third-party / cross-border data risk management.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Data classification scheme; data flow map; retention schedule; disposal SOP; cross-border / sub-processor register. Telemetry data dictionary; PII handling default-off. Linked to ISMS A.5.9-5.13/A.5.34, PIMS 6.x/7.x, POPIA s 13-22, EU AI Act Art. 10.
requirements.operational_evidence: Recent data classification decisions traced; retention reviewed; sub-processor list current.
requirements.failure_modes: Telemetry data treated as "metadata not personal" without analysis; retention undocumented.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox governs data and information through board-approved policies covering lifecycle, classification, privacy and third-party risk in alignment with King V Practice 103.

crosswalk.note: Data classification.
PIMS data accuracy / quality.
AIMS data for AI systems.
POPIA processing limitation + purpose specification + retention.

requirements.title: Technology governance
requirements.objective: The governing body approves technology policies covering legal compliance, resilience / disaster recovery / testing, benefit realisation vs investment, responsible disposal, effective cybersecurity, and outsourced / third-party tech assurance.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Cybersecurity policy + cyber dashboard + IR plan linked to ISO 27001 and NIST CSF. Sub-processor register with SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / DPA evidence. Foundation-model provider terms reviewed.
requirements.operational_evidence: Cyber dashboard reviewed quarterly; sub-processor reviews current; resilience tested annually.
requirements.failure_modes: CISO reporting only annually; cyber treated as IT; foundation-model provider treated as untouchable.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's technology governance — including cybersecurity, resilience, and third-party assurance — is board-overseen in alignment with King V Practice 108.

crosswalk.note: Secure SDLC + secure architecture + secure coding.
AIMS responsible design and development processes.

requirements.title: AI governance (the King V flagship)
requirements.objective: The governing body ensures that AI systems adhere to ethics, human centricity, accountability, transparency, explainability, security, privacy, fairness and trustworthiness; that clear accountability is established for AI decisions, actions, outputs and outcomes; and that human oversight and override mechanisms commensurate with risk are implemented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Defining battleground for PrivateBox. AI inventory; AI risk classification (low / limited / high / prohibited per EU AI Act); AI System Cards; pre-deployment review; model eval / red-team; human-override SOP; training-data provenance; output traceability; bias monitoring; AI incident process; user disclosure when AI is in use. AI Oversight Working Group reports into Audit/Risk/ Tech committee.
documented_evidence: AI System Cards per production model
AI Oversight Working Group charter + minutes
requirements.operational_evidence: Board pack includes AI risk dashboard, model changes, incidents; AI System Cards trace to production; human-override tested.
requirements.cadence: Annual policy review + per AI release / change
requirements.failure_modes: AI deployed before policy in place; "human in the loop" as decoration; AI ethics treated as marketing.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's AI governance is overseen by the board through a board-approved AI policy that operationalises the King V Practice 109c values, requires human oversight commensurate with risk, and is supported by AI System Cards for all production models.

crosswalk.note: AIMS AISIA + impact assessment + bias methodology + human oversight operationalise Practice 109c.
POPIA s 71 automated decision-making is the binding underpinning for human-override requirement.
EU AI Act risk-mgmt + transparency + human oversight + accuracy/robustness align with Practice 109c values.

requirements.title: Periodic assurance on technology effectiveness, compliance and ethics
requirements.objective: The governing body ensures that periodic assurance is obtained over technology effectiveness, compliance with legal and regulatory requirements, and adherence to organisational ethics in technology use.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Assurance providers: ISO 27001 surveillance audits, ISO 27701 audits, NIST AI RMF self- assessment, pen tests, model red-teams, internal audit. Combined assurance map covers technology risk.
requirements.operational_evidence: Assurance providers operate at cadence; findings closed.
requirements.failure_modes: Gaps in coverage of AI risk; assurance providers unmapped.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox obtains periodic assurance over technology effectiveness, compliance and ethics from a documented set of assurance providers in alignment with King V Practice 110.

crosswalk.note: ISMS internal audit feeds technology assurance.
AIMS internal audit feeds technology assurance.

requirements.title: Fair-and-responsible remuneration with malus/clawback
requirements.objective: The remuneration policy operationalises a fair-and-responsible test between executive and overall employee remuneration; variable pay arrangements include malus and clawback provisions; non- binding advisory votes are held where applicable.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Fair-and-responsible analysis recalculated annually. Malus / clawback specifically covers AI-misuse-linked variable pay. NED fees policy submitted to shareholders where applicable.
documented_evidence: Remuneration Policy with malus / clawback clauses
requirements.operational_evidence: Variable pay traces through documented policy; malus / clawback enforceable.
requirements.failure_modes: Founder-set salaries with no committee discipline; absence of malus / clawback; missing fair-and- responsible analysis.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's remuneration policy includes fair-and-responsible analysis and malus / clawback for variable pay in alignment with King V Practice 114.

requirements.title: Combined Assurance Map
requirements.objective: The governing body approves an explicit Combined Assurance Map identifying assurance providers per significant risk, level of assurance, and cadence.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Map covers ISO 27001/27701 surveillance, NIST AI RMF self-assessment, SOC 2 Type II (when pursued), penetration testing, internal financial controls review, statutory audit (when triggered), product-level red-team / model-evaluation reports.
requirements.operational_evidence: Map reconciles to live engagements; gaps highlighted and remediated.
requirements.failure_modes: Gaps in coverage; audit fatigue; remediation not tracked.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a documented Combined Assurance Map covering significant risks across line management, specialist functions, internal audit, and external assurance in alignment with King V Practice 121.

crosswalk.note: ISMS audit execution mapped via combined assurance.
AIMS audit execution mapped via combined assurance.

requirements.title: Internal Audit Charter
requirements.objective: The governing body approves an Internal Audit Charter setting out the role of internal audit, its place in combined assurance, scope, authority, independence, and reporting line. For a scaling firm, co-sourced or outsourced arrangements with a named accountable executive are acceptable (Practice 131c).
requirements.pb_interpretation: Co-sourced internal audit acceptable. Named accountable executive (e.g., COO or CFO with cosec support). Risk-based plan reviewed annually. External independent quality review at least once every 5 years (Practice 131f).
requirements.operational_evidence: IA plan executed; findings tracked through closure; quality review completed within cadence.
requirements.failure_modes: Same team auditing itself; external quality review skipped at 5-year mark.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates an Internal Audit function under a board-approved charter, co-sourced where proportionate, with external quality review at the King V Practice 131f cadence.

crosswalk.note: ISMS internal audit programme feeds King V internal audit.
AIMS internal audit programme feeds King V internal audit.

requirements.title: Stakeholder Engagement Framework
requirements.objective: The governing body approves a Stakeholder Engagement Framework setting out methodology, risk / opportunity analysis from stakeholder interests, dispute-resolution mechanisms, and periodic assurance on stakeholder management.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PrivateBox Stakeholder Engagement Framework covers clients (especially regulated-sector procurement and CISO/DPO functions), client end-users (data subjects), regulators (Information Regulator, sector regulators), sub-processors and model providers, employees, founders/investors, community/society.
requirements.operational_evidence: Framework reviewed annually; engagement log current; stakeholder feedback feeds strategy.
requirements.cadence: Annual framework review + continuous engagement log
requirements.failure_modes: Founder-only stakeholder relationships with no institutional memory; no dispute resolution.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a documented Stakeholder Engagement Framework covering clients, regulators, employees, suppliers and community in alignment with King V Practice 134.

crosswalk.note: ISMS interested-parties identification feeds stakeholder framework.
AIMS interested-parties identification feeds stakeholder framework.

king-v-p-1.title: Leadership
king-v-p-1.requirement: The governing body leads ethically and effectively as the focal point of corporate governance in the organisation, embodying tone-from-the-top through integrity, competence, responsibility, accountability, fairness and transparency.
king-v-p-1.auditor_intent: Adviser / procurement / investor reviewer checks that founder-led culture is balanced with collective board accountability and that the board operates under a written charter.
king-v-p-1.pb_interpretation: Founder-led culture must be balanced with collective board accountability. Board operates under a written charter (Practice 4) reviewed annually. Each director cultivates the six characteristics (Practice 1) with declared interests at least annually and at every meeting (Practices 39-40). Performance evaluation at least every two years (Practices 6-9).
king-v-p-1.operational_evidence: Directors can describe the charter and how it was last reviewed; evaluation actions tracked.
king-v-p-1.failure_modes: Founder/CEO acting as both Chair and CEO without Lead Independent designation; charter copied from a template and not lived.
king-v-p-1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's governing body operates under a board charter and director conduct expectations aligned with King V Principle 1.

crosswalk.note: ISMS leadership maps to King V leadership at organisational level.
PIMS leadership analogue.
AIMS leadership analogue.
POPIA accountability principle is the statutory underpinning King V Principle 1 instantiates.

king-v-p-2.title: Ethics
king-v-p-2.requirement: The governing body governs the ethics of the organisation in a way that enables an ethical culture and responsible corporate citizenship.
king-v-p-2.auditor_intent: Procurement / investor reviewer tests that ethics is governed (board-approved code, training cadence, whistleblower channel, sanctions log) rather than assumed.
king-v-p-2.pb_interpretation: Critical because clients trust PrivateBox not to misuse data. Board approves ethics policy / code of conduct (Practice 12), AI ethics statement (linked to Principle 10 AI values), anti-corruption / anti-fraud / anti-money-laundering programmes (Practice 12c), protected whistleblowing channel (Practice 13d). Ethics built into hiring, performance, supplier onboarding.
king-v-p-2.operational_evidence: Trace a reported ethics matter from intake to closure; periodic assurance on ethics effectiveness (Practice 14).
king-v-p-2.failure_modes: Ethics conflated with compliance; whistleblower channel that routes to the implicated party; "ethics" treated as a poster on the wall.
king-v-p-2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's ethics framework, anti-corruption controls and protected whistleblowing process are governed by the board in accordance with King V Principle 2.

crosswalk.note: Management responsibilities; ethics overlay.
AI Policy operationalises the AI-specific portion of King V ethics.
POPIA accountability principle reaches ethics governance.

king-v-p-3.group_name: Strategy, performance and sustainable value creation
king-v-p-3.title: Strategy, performance and sustainable value creation
king-v-p-3.requirement: The governing body ensures that the organisation's purpose, strategy and business model support performance that creates sustainable value within the organisation's economic, social and environmental context.
king-v-p-3.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects board-approved strategy with SMART KPIs, integrated-thinking framing across the six capitals, and explicit negative-impact mitigation.
king-v-p-3.pb_interpretation: Articulate purpose, business model, strategy and KPIs. Tie product roadmap to sustainable client outcomes — data sovereignty, on-prem assurance, lower regulatory risk for clients. Apply integrated thinking to financial, intellectual, human, social/relationship, natural (energy footprint), manufactured capitals.
king-v-p-3.operational_evidence: Board minute approving strategy and budget; quarterly KPI review; founder/CEO performance review against strategic targets.
king-v-p-3.failure_modes: Performance reduced to ARR; no link to systems value; strategy not refreshed; sustainability ignored on assumption that "we're a small tech company".
king-v-p-3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's strategy is approved and overseen by the governing body using integrated thinking aligned with King V Principle 3.

crosswalk.note: ISMS objectives integrated with strategy.
AIMS objectives integrated with strategy.

king-v-p-4.title: Reporting
king-v-p-4.requirement: The governing body ensures that external reports enable stakeholders to make informed assessments of how the organisation creates, preserves and erodes value, applying double-materiality concepts and integrity controls over reports.
king-v-p-4.auditor_intent: Procurement / investor reviewer expects King V Disclosure Framework published on website with a concluding statement on the four outcomes; integrated narrative across strategy / risk / AI / security / privacy.
king-v-p-4.pb_interpretation: Even as a private company, the trust pack, security disclosures, AI factsheets and governance section function as external reports. Board approves the suite (Practice 25b). Annual integrated report or proportionate equivalent. King V Disclosure Framework published on the website (Practice 27c). Double-materiality applied (Practice 26).
documented_evidence: Board approval minutes for each external report
king-v-p-4.operational_evidence: Each external report has a board approval minute and accessible publication record; concluding statement on outcomes signed off.
king-v-p-4.failure_modes: Marketing copy mistaken for governance disclosure; King V Disclosure Framework absent (mandatory failure); concluding statement copied without evidence trail.
king-v-p-4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox publishes a governance disclosure structured in accordance with the King V Disclosure Framework.

crosswalk.note: Privacy / external disclosure overlap.
PIMS controllers transparency / notice obligation overlap.
POPIA s 17-18 openness condition; external disclosure obligations.

king-v-p-5.title: Composition of the governing body
king-v-p-5.requirement: The governing body ensures that its composition is balanced with respect to the mix of competencies, diversity and independence appropriate to the organisation's purpose and context.
king-v-p-5.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects majority NED, most independent, diverse, with independence holistically assessed against Practice 42 nine factors.
king-v-p-5.pb_interpretation: Founder-led — must consciously over-correct for independence. Target at least one independent NED at minimum, scaling to majority pre-IPO. CEO + at least one other executive on the board (Practice 30). Diversity across competencies, age, culture, race, gender (Practice 31). Staggered rotation (Practice 32). Succession plan (Practice 33). Skills matrix. Independence assessed against the nine Practice 42 factors. Background checks and verified qualifications for nominees (Practice 37c).
documented_evidence: Per-NED independence assessment memo (against Practice 42 nine factors)
king-v-p-5.operational_evidence: Independence rationale documented for any director near the line; nine-year tenure reviewed annually.
king-v-p-5.failure_modes: Founder-controlled board with no independents; cosmetic NED who is in fact a customer/supplier; "independence by assertion".
king-v-p-5.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's board composition is structured to deliver the balance of competencies, diversity and independence contemplated by King V Principle 5, applied proportionately to a scaling private technology company.

crosswalk.note: Roles, responsibilities and authorities under ISMS map to board composition discipline.
PIMS roles analogue.
AIMS roles analogue.
Information Officer designation under POPIA overlaps with King V board composition discipline.

king-v-p-6.title: Committees of the governing body
king-v-p-6.requirement: Arrangements for delegation to committees and individuals promote the objective and effective discharge of the governing body's obligations.
king-v-p-6.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects committees with written TORs, at least 3 members, balanced; combined committee architecture proportionate at smaller scale; statutory S&E committee triggered by PI Score.
king-v-p-6.pb_interpretation: Proportionate model — combined Audit/Risk/Technology Committee (one TOR, 3 members, majority NED, at least one independent NED, chaired by independent NED who is not Chair of board); combined Social/Ethics/Remuneration Committee (incorporates corporate citizenship and remuneration governance); optional AI Oversight Sub-committee or working group reporting in. PI Score crossing triggers mandatory statutory S&E committee under Companies Act s 72(4) and Regulation 43.
king-v-p-6.operational_evidence: Each committee has documented terms of reference, named members, attendance records, and chair reports to board.
king-v-p-6.failure_modes: Chair of board chairing the audit committee (Practice 49a prohibits); CEO sitting on audit/remuneration/nominations as a member (Practice 78 prohibits, may attend by invitation).
king-v-p-6.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a proportionate committee architecture aligned to King V Principle 6, including a combined audit, risk and technology committee and a social, ethics and remuneration committee.

crosswalk.note: ISMS management-review committees overlap with King V committee discipline.
PIMS management review analogue.
AIMS management review analogue.

king-v-p-7.title: Appointment and delegation to management
king-v-p-7.requirement: Arrangements for appointment of and delegation to management promote operational effectiveness and ensure that the respective roles and decision-making powers of the governing body and management are clearly defined.
king-v-p-7.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects CEO formally appointed, not on audit/remuneration/nominations (Practice 78), board-approved Delegation of Authority framework, CEO succession plan, access to professional corporate-governance services.
king-v-p-7.pb_interpretation: Founder/CEO must not also chair (Practice 48); 3-year cooling-off applies before retired CEO can chair. CEO formally appointed (Practice 76). Board-approved Delegation of Authority framework (Practice 82). CEO succession plan (Practice 79). Cosec or equivalent provides independent corporate-governance services (Practices 84-90).
king-v-p-7.operational_evidence: DoA framework demonstrably operates — recent material decisions trace through documented authority.
king-v-p-7.failure_modes: Founder retains undocumented decision-making power; no cosec equivalent; CEO succession plan absent.
king-v-p-7.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a board-approved delegation-of-authority framework and CEO succession discipline aligned with King V Principle 7.

crosswalk.note: Roles and authorities under ISMS overlap with King V management delegation.
AIMS roles analogue.

king-v-p-8.title: Risk
king-v-p-8.requirement: The governing body governs risk in a way that enables the organisation to sustain and optimise its strategy and objectives — opportunity-aware, integrated, iterative, resilient.
king-v-p-8.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects approved risk policy, documented risk appetite and tolerance, integrated risk register, BCM with testing, iterative change-responsive process, periodic assurance.
king-v-p-8.pb_interpretation: Cyber, AI model, supplier, business-continuity, key-person, regulatory, and reputational risks are the dominant cluster. Model drift, prompt-injection, supply-chain compromise of foundation models / model weights / containers, telemetry channel abuse, on-prem update channel integrity, key-person dependency on founder.
documented_evidence: Enterprise Risk Register (cyber, AI, supplier, key-person, BCM, regulatory, commercial sub-registers)
king-v-p-8.operational_evidence: Risk register reviewed quarterly; appetite breaches escalated; BCM exercised annually.
king-v-p-8.cadence: Quarterly register review + annual appetite review + annual BCM exercise
king-v-p-8.failure_modes: Separate cyber/AI/privacy registers with no integration; treating ISO 27001 as a substitute for board-level risk governance; static appetite that never changes.
king-v-p-8.external_safe_claim: Risk governance at PrivateBox is structured to deliver the outcomes contemplated by King V Principle 8, with explicit treatment of cyber, AI model, supplier, and business-continuity risks.

crosswalk.note: ISMS risk framework feeds King V P8 enterprise risk view.
PIMS risk analogue.
AIMS risk methodology feeds King V P8 enterprise risk view.
POPIA security safeguards (s 19) are a sub-register of enterprise risk under King V P8.
EU AI Act risk-management for high-risk AI feeds King V P8 enterprise risk.

king-v-p-9.title: Compliance
king-v-p-9.requirement: The governing body governs compliance with applicable laws and adopted policies, non-binding rules, codes and standards in a way that promotes ethics and responsible corporate citizenship.
king-v-p-9.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects board-approved compliance framework, obligations register naming binding and adopted standards, regulatory horizon scanning.
king-v-p-9.pb_interpretation: Obligations register covers POPIA, Cybercrimes Act, ECTA, Companies Act, EU AI Act extra- territorial reach, sector pass-through obligations. Adopted non-binding standards explicitly listed (ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 42001, NIST AI RMF, NIST SSDF, EU AI Act voluntary GPAI code, King V itself). Regulatory horizon scanning (Practice 98d). Periodic assurance on compliance function (Practice 99).
king-v-p-9.operational_evidence: Regulatory changes trace from horizon scan to register update to control adjustment.
king-v-p-9.failure_modes: Confusing King V (voluntary) with binding obligations; obligations register stale; no horizon scanning.
king-v-p-9.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's compliance framework is governed at board level and addresses both binding obligations and adopted non-binding standards in alignment with King V Principle 9.

crosswalk.note: Legal / regulatory / contractual compliance under ISMS; privacy and PII.
POPIA accountability principle underpins compliance governance.

king-v-p-10.title: Data, information and technology
king-v-p-10.requirement: The governing body governs data, information and technology in a way that enables the organisation to sustain and optimise its strategy and objectives — including emerging-technology risk and AI governance under Practice 109c covering ethics, human centricity, accountability, transparency, explainability, security, privacy, fairness, trustworthiness, and human oversight commensurate with risk.
king-v-p-10.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects board-approved data and technology policies, AI governance under Practice 109c, cyber dashboard, privacy programme, third-party assurance. This is the King V flagship update versus King IV — the AI governance limb is the highest-leverage clause.
king-v-p-10.pb_interpretation: Highest-leverage principle for PrivateBox. Data and Information branch (Practices 100-104): lifecycle controls, classification, CIA, privacy, third-party / cross-border risk, periodic assurance. Technology branch (Practices 105-110): legal compliance, resilience, benefit realisation, responsible disposal, effective cybersecurity, outsourced/third-party tech assurance. Emerging tech / AI checklist (109c): AI ethics, human centricity, accountability, transparency, explainability, security, privacy, fairness, trustworthiness; clear accountability for AI decisions/actions/outputs/outcomes; human oversight and override mechanisms commensurate with risk. On-prem architecture earns its keep here — policy explicitly states telemetry / update / support / connector channels, what data they touch, what consent and control client has, and how human override works for AI outputs.
documented_evidence: Privacy Governance Policy + POPIA Programme + IO designation
AI Governance Policy + AI Ethics Statement
AI System Cards per production model
AI Oversight Working Group charter + minutes
king-v-p-10.operational_evidence: Board pack includes cyber dashboard, AI risk dashboard, privacy dashboard; AI System Cards trace to production deployments; human-override mechanisms tested.
king-v-p-10.cadence: Annual policy review + per release / per AI change
king-v-p-10.failure_modes: AI deployed before policy in place; "human in the loop" as decoration; telemetry treated as "metadata not personal" without analysis; CISO reporting only annually; cyber treated as IT; foundation-model provider treated as untouchable.
king-v-p-10.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox governs data, information, technology and AI through board-approved policies designed to deliver the human-oversight, accountability, transparency, explainability, security, privacy, fairness and trustworthiness expectations of King V Principle 10.

crosswalk.note: ISMS asset inventory + data classification + privacy overlay.
PIMS PIA / records underpin King V P10 data branch.
AIMS AISIA + human oversight + bias methodology operationalise Practice 109c.
POPIA processing limitation, security safeguards, s 71 automated decisions feed P10.
EU AI Act high-risk articles align with Practice 109c values and oversight requirements.

king-v-p-11.title: Remuneration
king-v-p-11.requirement: The organisation remunerates fairly, responsibly and transparently to promote sustainable value creation. King V is less prescriptive than King IV, deferring to s 30(4) Companies Act and Companies Amendment Act 16 of 2024 (not yet in force at time of authoring).
king-v-p-11.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects board-approved remuneration policy, fair-and-responsible test between executive and overall employee remuneration, malus/clawback for variable pay.
king-v-p-11.pb_interpretation: Board-approved remuneration policy (Practice 113). Fair-and-responsible test between executive and overall employee remuneration (Practice 114b). Malus/clawback for variable pay (Practice 114e). NED fee policy submitted to shareholders if applicable (Practice 115). Shareholder engagement on remuneration (Practice 116). For entities required to have a S&E committee, non- binding advisory votes on policy (every 3 years or material change) and on implementation report (annual) (Practice 118).
documented_evidence: Malus / clawback clauses for variable pay
king-v-p-11.operational_evidence: Remuneration decisions trace through documented policy; fair-and-responsible test recalculated annually.
king-v-p-11.failure_modes: Founder-set salaries with no committee discipline; absence of malus/clawback for AI-misuse-linked variable pay; no fair-and-responsible analysis.
king-v-p-11.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a fair, responsible and transparent remuneration framework aligned to King V Principle 11.

king-v-p-12.title: Assurance
king-v-p-12.requirement: Assurance functions and services promote an effective internal control environment and safeguard the integrity of external reports through combined assurance across line management, specialist functions, internal audit, external audit, specialist providers and regulators.
king-v-p-12.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects combined assurance map covering all significant risks × assurance providers × cadence; internal audit charter (co-sourced acceptable for proportionate scale); external IA quality review every 5 years.
king-v-p-12.pb_interpretation: Combined assurance map covers ISO 27001/27701 surveillance, NIST AI RMF self-assessment, SOC 2 Type II (if pursued), penetration testing, internal financial controls review, statutory audit (when triggered), product-level red-team / model-evaluation reports. For each external report state level of assurance and criteria (Practice 126). Internal audit charter and risk-based plan (Practices 129-130) — may be co-sourced (Practice 131c). External independent review of internal audit at least once every 5 years (Practice 131f).
king-v-p-12.operational_evidence: Assurance providers operate per cadence; remediation tracked through closure; map refreshed annually.
king-v-p-12.cadence: Annual map review + per assurance cycle
king-v-p-12.failure_modes: Audit fatigue with no remediation; same team auditing itself; gaps in coverage of AI risk; external IA quality review skipped.
king-v-p-12.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a combined assurance model designed to support effective internal controls and the integrity of external reports in alignment with King V Principle 12.

crosswalk.note: ISMS internal audit feeds King V P12 combined assurance.
PIMS internal audit analogue.
AIMS internal audit analogue.

king-v-p-13.title: Stakeholders
king-v-p-13.requirement: The governing body adopts a stakeholder-inclusive approach in the execution of its duties in the long-term best interests of the organisation within its economic, social and environmental context, upholding Ubuntu-Botho.
king-v-p-13.auditor_intent: Reviewer expects stakeholder map, engagement framework, dispute-resolution mechanism, periodic assurance on stakeholder management; for organisations with shareholders, AGM cadence and accessibility.
king-v-p-13.pb_interpretation: Stakeholder map covers clients (especially regulated-sector procurement and CISO/DPO functions), client end-users (data subjects), regulators (Information Regulator, sector regulators), sub-processors and model / foundation-model providers, employees, founders/investors, community/society. Board-approved stakeholder engagement framework (Practice 134). Stakeholder methodology (135a). Risk/opportunity analysis from stakeholder interests (135b). Dispute resolution (135c). Periodic assurance on stakeholder management (136). For PrivateBox, shareholder engagement scales to investor relations and material customer relationship management. Group governance framework if subsidiaries exist (Practices 145-150).
documented_evidence: AI Transparency Disclosure pack for clients
king-v-p-13.operational_evidence: Quarterly stakeholder report to board; documented client / customer engagement minutes; published governance section on website.
king-v-p-13.cadence: Annual framework review + continuous engagement log
king-v-p-13.failure_modes: "Marketing trust" without substance; opaque telemetry; missing AI transparency; founder-only stakeholder relationships with no institutional memory.
king-v-p-13.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies a stakeholder-inclusive approach to governance, designed to support the King V outcomes of legitimacy and sustainable value creation.

crosswalk.note: ISMS interested-parties identification underpins King V stakeholder framework.
PIMS interested-parties analogue.
AIMS interested-parties analogue.
POPIA data subject participation underpins stakeholder rights handling.

king-v-rp-4.title: Board Charter
king-v-rp-4.objective: The governing body approves and periodically reviews a Board Charter setting out its roles, responsibilities, membership, conduct, meeting procedures, reserved matters, and delegation.
king-v-rp-4.pb_interpretation: PrivateBox Board Charter v1.0+ is King V-mapped with PrivateBox-specific reserved matters including: approval of new sub-processor, approval of foundation-model change, approval of telemetry policy change, approval of AI Governance Policy, approval of King V Disclosure Framework publication.
king-v-rp-4.operational_evidence: Charter referenced in board minutes; reserved matters traced to approval decisions.
king-v-rp-4.failure_modes: Template charter with no PrivateBox-specific reserved matters; charter approved but not lived.
king-v-rp-4.external_safe_phrasing: The PrivateBox Board operates under a King V-mapped Board Charter with explicit reserved-matters discipline for sub-processor approvals, AI changes and telemetry policy.

crosswalk.note: ISMS leadership commitment instantiates through board-level chartering.

king-v-rp-8.title: Board evaluation cadence
king-v-rp-8.objective: The governing body undertakes formal performance evaluation of the board, committees, chair and individuals at least every two years, with methodology approved and external facilitation considered.
king-v-rp-8.pb_interpretation: Biennial internal evaluation; externally facilitated evaluation expected at maturity (Series B onwards). Methodology approved by board / nominations. Evaluation report and action plan maintained.
king-v-rp-8.operational_evidence: Most recent evaluation traceable; actions closed through subsequent agenda items.
king-v-rp-8.cadence: At least every 2 years; externally facilitated at maturity
king-v-rp-8.failure_modes: Evaluation done but no follow-up; methodology never reviewed; external facilitation skipped at maturity.
king-v-rp-8.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox conducts formal board, committee, chair and director performance evaluations at least every two years.

king-v-rp-12.title: Code of Ethics
king-v-rp-12.objective: The governing body approves a Code of Ethics / Code of Conduct addressing values, ethical standards, anti-corruption / anti-fraud / anti-money-laundering programmes, and the organisation's expectations of conduct.
king-v-rp-12.pb_interpretation: Includes explicit AI Ethics Statement (linked to Principle 10 109c values). Anti-corruption programme covers gifts, hospitality, facilitation payments. Built into hiring, performance, and supplier onboarding.
king-v-rp-12.operational_evidence: Code referenced in employment contracts and supplier onboarding; training completion sampled.
king-v-rp-12.failure_modes: Code on the wall but not lived; ethics confused with compliance.
king-v-rp-12.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a board-approved Code of Ethics including an AI Ethics Statement and anti-corruption programmes.

crosswalk.note: Management responsibilities under ISMS overlap with code-of-ethics governance.
AI Policy operationalises the AI-specific portion of Practice 12.

king-v-rp-13.title: Protected whistleblowing channel
king-v-rp-13.objective: The governing body ensures the existence of a protected whistleblowing channel that is independent and accessible to internal and external whistleblowers.
king-v-rp-13.pb_interpretation: Independent third-party provider preferred. Anonymity and protection from retaliation enforced. Periodic stats to S&E committee (anonymised). Channel covers ethics matters, fraud, AI misuse concerns.
king-v-rp-13.operational_evidence: Trace a reported ethics matter from intake to closure; non-retaliation enforced.
king-v-rp-13.cadence: Continuous channel + quarterly stats to S&E
king-v-rp-13.failure_modes: Channel routes to the implicated party; no independence; staff don't know how to raise concerns.
king-v-rp-13.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates an independent, protected whistleblowing channel with periodic reporting to the Social and Ethics function.

crosswalk.note: AIMS reporting of concerns is the AI-specific instantiation of King V protected whistleblowing.

king-v-rp-27.title: King V Disclosure Framework publication
king-v-rp-27.objective: The governing body approves and publishes on the website an annual King V Governance Disclosure structured in accordance with the King V Disclosure Framework, including a concluding statement on the four governance outcomes.
king-v-rp-27.pb_interpretation: Mandatory for any organisation claiming application of King V. PrivateBox publishes an annual King V Governance Disclosure on its website. Concluding statement on Ethical Culture, Performance and Value Creation, Conformance and Prudent Control, Legitimacy. Companion Trust & Governance Pack NDA-gated for procurement diligence.
king-v-rp-27.operational_evidence: Disclosure accessible on website; board minute approving each annual edition.
king-v-rp-27.failure_modes: Disclosure absent from website; copy-paste concluding statement with no evidence trail; marketing copy posing as disclosure.
king-v-rp-27.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox publishes an annual King V Governance Disclosure structured in accordance with the King V Disclosure Framework.

crosswalk.note: POPIA s 17-18 openness condition; external disclosure overlap.

king-v-rp-29.title: Majority non-executive directors
king-v-rp-29.objective: The governing body has a majority of non-executive members, most of whom are independent (assessed holistically against Practice 42).
king-v-rp-29.pb_interpretation: Target: at least one independent NED at minimum, scaling to majority pre-IPO. Founder-led — must consciously over-correct for independence.
king-v-rp-29.operational_evidence: Composition reviewed at least annually; rationale documented for any director near the independence line.
king-v-rp-29.failure_modes: Founder-controlled board with no independents; cosmetic NEDs in fact customers/suppliers.
king-v-rp-29.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's board composition includes independent non-executive representation appropriate to its stage and scale, with documented trajectory toward majority NED.

crosswalk.note: Roles and authorities under ISMS overlap with NED composition discipline.

king-v-rp-42.title: Holistic independence assessment
king-v-rp-42.objective: The governing body assesses each non-executive director's independence holistically against the nine factors set out in Practice 42 (significant funder; securities-significance; former-executive within 3 years; former-audit-team within 3 years; significant supplier/customer; related-party; tenure exceeding 9 years; performance-contingent remuneration; other reasonable factors).
king-v-rp-42.pb_interpretation: Documented per-NED independence assessment memo, signed by board, refreshed annually. Tenure exceeding 9 years assessed annually with explicit rationale. "Independence by assertion" is a common King V transition failure.
king-v-rp-42.operational_evidence: Assessment memo references all nine factors; signed by board.
king-v-rp-42.failure_modes: Ticking only the ">9 yrs" box; independence by assertion without nine-factor analysis.
king-v-rp-42.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox assesses non-executive director independence holistically against the King V Practice 42 factors with documented per-director rationale.

king-v-rp-45.title: Chair and Lead Independent NED
king-v-rp-45.objective: The chair is an independent non-executive director; where this is not the case (e.g., founder-CEO is Chair), a Lead Independent NED is appointed as compensating measure.
king-v-rp-45.pb_interpretation: For PrivateBox, until an independent Chair is appointed, the Lead Independent NED designation is the mandatory compensating measure. Lead Independent leads board in Chair's absence/conflict, leads Chair evaluation, acts as sounding board.
king-v-rp-45.operational_evidence: Lead Independent operates with actual authority; trace NED-only session attendance.
king-v-rp-45.failure_modes: Founder/CEO dual-hatting as Chair with no Lead Independent; Lead Independent title without authority.
king-v-rp-45.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's board operates with either an independent Chair or a Lead Independent NED designation in accordance with King V Practice 45.

king-v-rp-59.title: Audit committee scope and oversight
king-v-rp-59.objective: The audit committee oversees external assurance, internal financial controls, the CFO and finance function, external reports, internal audit, combined assurance, and risk where combined.
king-v-rp-59.pb_interpretation: Combined Audit/Risk/Technology Committee TOR. Minimum 4 meetings per year at maturity. At least one independent NED chairing. Practice 49a — Chair of board does not chair audit committee.
documented_evidence: CFO / CISO / DPO / Head of AI dashboards
king-v-rp-59.operational_evidence: Auditor / external review can trace from external audit findings through committee minutes to closure.
king-v-rp-59.cadence: Minimum 4 meetings per year at maturity
king-v-rp-59.failure_modes: Audit dominance with risk and technology underweighted; no independent NED chair; Chair-of-board chairing audit committee.
king-v-rp-59.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's combined Audit/Risk/Technology Committee oversees assurance, internal financial controls, cyber, privacy and AI in alignment with King V Practice 59.

crosswalk.note: ISMS internal audit governance reaches audit committee oversight.

king-v-rp-74.title: Social and Ethics committee scope
king-v-rp-74.objective: The social and ethics committee oversees ethics, corporate citizenship, impact on the six capitals, stakeholder relationships, and (where the entity is required to have a statutory S&E committee under Companies Act s 72(4) and Regulation 43) the statutory monitoring and reporting matters.
king-v-rp-74.pb_interpretation: Combined Social/Ethics/Remuneration Committee TOR while proportionate; converts to standalone statutory S&E committee when PI Score triggers Regulation 43. At least one independent NED member. Minimum 2 meetings per year.
king-v-rp-74.operational_evidence: Committee meets at cadence; ethics, citizenship, stakeholder topics traced through minutes.
king-v-rp-74.cadence: Minimum 2 meetings per year + statutory cycle when triggered
king-v-rp-74.failure_modes: Box-tick S&E with no real ethics work; remuneration set without policy; no independent NED member.
king-v-rp-74.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's Social, Ethics and Remuneration Committee oversees ethics, corporate citizenship and stakeholders in alignment with King V Practice 74, pre-built to convert to statutory S&E committee on PI Score trigger.

king-v-rp-82.title: Delegation of Authority Framework
king-v-rp-82.objective: The governing body approves a Delegation of Authority Framework setting out the matrix of reserved vs delegated decisions with monetary, contractual, hiring, sub-processor, security exception, and AI-deployment thresholds.
king-v-rp-82.pb_interpretation: PrivateBox DoA Framework explicitly thresholds: sub-processor onboarding, foundation-model selection, telemetry policy change, security exception approval, AI deployment / model change.
documented_evidence: Recent material decisions traced through DoA
king-v-rp-82.operational_evidence: Material decisions trace through documented authority; no founder-only approvals on DoA-reserved items.
king-v-rp-82.failure_modes: Founder retains undocumented authority; DoA exists but not enforced.
king-v-rp-82.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a board-approved Delegation of Authority Framework covering monetary, contractual, sub-processor and AI deployment thresholds.

crosswalk.note: Authorities and responsibilities under ISMS overlap with DoA framework.

king-v-rp-84.title: Company Secretary / governance support
king-v-rp-84.objective: The governing body has access to a company secretary or equivalent professional corporate- governance service that is arms-length from management.
king-v-rp-84.pb_interpretation: Cosec-as-a-service is proportionate for PrivateBox's current stage. Transition to in-house at scale. Statutory filings, minute integrity, governance counsel.
king-v-rp-84.operational_evidence: Statutory filings up to date; minute integrity demonstrable.
king-v-rp-84.failure_modes: Cosec function blended with management role of an executive; missing statutory filings.
king-v-rp-84.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox's governing body has access to professional corporate-governance services arms-length from management in alignment with King V Practice 84.

king-v-rp-92.title: Risk Management Policy
king-v-rp-92.objective: The governing body approves a Risk Management Policy and frameworks covering enterprise risk identification, assessment, treatment, monitoring and reporting.
king-v-rp-92.pb_interpretation: Risk Management Policy covers cyber, AI, supplier, key-person, BCM, regulatory, commercial sub-registers. Integration with ISMS / PIMS / AIMS risk methodologies.
king-v-rp-92.operational_evidence: Policy referenced in risk register entries; cadence held.
king-v-rp-92.failure_modes: Policy stale; not integrated with ISMS/PIMS/AIMS risk methodologies.
king-v-rp-92.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a board-approved Risk Management Policy and framework integrated with its ISMS, PIMS and AIMS risk methodologies in alignment with King V Practice 92.

crosswalk.note: ISMS risk methodology feeds enterprise risk policy.
AIMS risk methodology feeds enterprise risk policy.

king-v-rp-93.title: Risk register, appetite and BCM
king-v-rp-93.objective: The governing body approves a documented risk appetite and tolerance, maintains a risk register covering economic / social / environmental context, and ensures business-continuity and resilience arrangements with periodic testing.
king-v-rp-93.pb_interpretation: Quarterly register review; annual appetite review; annual BCM test report. Key-person sub- register for founder dependency. Iterative, change-responsive.
king-v-rp-93.operational_evidence: Recent appetite breaches escalated; recent BCM exercise completed within cadence.
king-v-rp-93.cadence: Quarterly register + annual appetite + annual BCM
king-v-rp-93.failure_modes: Static register; no link to change events; BCM untested; key-person risk ignored.
king-v-rp-93.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains a documented risk appetite, integrated risk register and tested business- continuity arrangements in alignment with King V Practice 93.

crosswalk.note: ISMS risk register pattern.
AIMS risk register pattern.

king-v-rp-98.title: Compliance universe / obligations register
