requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox governs third-party and incident-related privacy obligations for in-scope personal information.

crosswalk.note: POPIA security safeguards (s 22 breach + s 20-21 operator).

requirements.title: Personal information quality
requirements.requirement: The entity collects and maintains accurate, up-to-date, complete, and relevant personal information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Define necessary PI fields only; correction workflow for account / support records. Usually narrow in system scope for on-prem vendors.
requirements.type_i_evidence: Quality rules; correction workflow.
requirements.type_ii_evidence: Correction records across the period.
requirements.failure_modes: Bad data persists without correction path.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains data-quality controls for in-scope personal information.

crosswalk.note: POPIA information quality.

requirements.title: Privacy inquiries, complaints, and enforcement
requirements.requirement: The entity implements a process for receiving, addressing, resolving, and communicating the resolution of inquiries, complaints, and disputes from data subjects and others, and monitors compliance with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Intake path, escalation, and remediation for privacy issues. Relevant if Privacy is in scope or if procurement requires a privacy-operating model.
requirements.type_i_evidence: Complaint process; escalation and compliance monitoring.
requirements.type_ii_evidence: Complaint log across the period; closure evidence.
requirements.failure_modes: Privacy issue handling ad hoc; complaints unresolved.
requirements.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains processes for privacy inquiries and remediation where applicable.

crosswalk.note: POPIA data subject participation including complaints.

soc-2-cc-1.1.title: Integrity and ethical values
soc-2-cc-1.1.requirement: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values through standards of conduct that are communicated, with deviations addressed.
soc-2-cc-1.1.pb_interpretation: Board-approved code of conduct; security expectations for staff and contractors; disciplinary process. Small-company governance acceptable if oversight is real and evidenced.
soc-2-cc-1.1.type_i_evidence: Approved policies; signed acknowledgements; issue log demonstrating the disciplinary path exists and has been used.
soc-2-cc-1.1.type_ii_evidence: Onboarding attestations across the period; annual refresh records; event-driven enforcement records.
soc-2-cc-1.1.failure_modes: Missing attestations; inconsistent enforcement; unapproved policy; no contractor scope.
soc-2-cc-1.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains documented ethical and security conduct standards aligned to SOC 2 CC1.1.

crosswalk.note: ISMS management responsibilities; ethical-conduct overlay.
King V Principle 2 ethics + Practice 12 code of ethics.

soc-2-cc-1.2.title: Oversight independence
soc-2-cc-1.2.requirement: The board of directors (or equivalent oversight body) demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.
soc-2-cc-1.2.pb_interpretation: Oversight body or formal leadership forum with security agenda. Founder-led firms still need demonstrable oversight even if not a public-company board. King V independent NED appointment strengthens this.
soc-2-cc-1.2.type_ii_evidence: Minutes across the period showing actual challenge and follow-up; quarterly or more frequent meetings.
soc-2-cc-1.2.failure_modes: Security oversight only informal; meetings held but no challenge or action.
soc-2-cc-1.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox has documented oversight of its control environment.

crosswalk.note: ISMS leadership and commitment.
King V Principle 1 leadership.

soc-2-cc-1.3.title: Structure, authority and responsibility
soc-2-cc-1.3.requirement: Management, with board oversight, establishes structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in pursuit of objectives.
soc-2-cc-1.3.pb_interpretation: Org chart and responsibility matrix covering product, ops, security, support. Include remote support authority and emergency powers explicitly.
soc-2-cc-1.3.type_i_evidence: Org chart and role descriptions; RACI covering support and security.
soc-2-cc-1.3.type_ii_evidence: Approved org updates across the period; delegated approvals samples.
soc-2-cc-1.3.cadence: As changed; at least annual review
soc-2-cc-1.3.failure_modes: Ambiguous authority; no owner list; outdated org chart; approval by wrong person.
soc-2-cc-1.3.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox defines security and operational responsibilities clearly.

crosswalk.note: ISMS roles, responsibilities and authorities.
King V composition + Delegation of Authority Framework.

soc-2-cc-1.4.title: Competence and staffing
soc-2-cc-1.4.requirement: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.
soc-2-cc-1.4.pb_interpretation: Formal hiring criteria; onboarding checklist; annual security education. Local-hosted deployments still require competent support and deployment staff.
soc-2-cc-1.4.type_i_evidence: Hiring checklist; training plan; role-based training mapping.
soc-2-cc-1.4.type_ii_evidence: Training logs across the period; onboarding records sampled by hire; annual training completion.
soc-2-cc-1.4.failure_modes: No role-based training; missed training; incomplete onboarding.
soc-2-cc-1.4.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox aligns staffing and training to secure service delivery.

crosswalk.note: ISMS competence.
AIMS competence; AI-specific skills.

soc-2-cc-1.5.title: Accountability
soc-2-cc-1.5.requirement: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities in pursuit of objectives.
soc-2-cc-1.5.pb_interpretation: Security duties tied to performance reviews and policy attestations. If duties are informal, evidence usually weakens quickly.
soc-2-cc-1.5.type_ii_evidence: Reviews completed across the period; sanctions log; policy attestations refreshed.
soc-2-cc-1.5.failure_modes: Policies without enforcement path; reviews omitted; no action on breaches.
soc-2-cc-1.5.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox assigns accountability for control execution.

crosswalk.note: ISMS roles and accountability.

soc-2-cc-2.1.title: Quality information
soc-2-cc-2.1.requirement: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal control.
soc-2-cc-2.1.pb_interpretation: Key control data sources defined: asset lists, user lists, release records, incidents. For customer-hosted estates, distinguish PB-known data from customer-only data.
soc-2-cc-2.1.type_i_evidence: Inventory governance documentation; data owners named.
soc-2-cc-2.1.type_ii_evidence: Current inventories maintained across the period; reconciliations.
soc-2-cc-2.1.failure_modes: Stale or conflicting inventories; local estates create inventory gaps.
soc-2-cc-2.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains quality operational and security information.

crosswalk.note: ISMS asset inventory + data classification.

soc-2-cc-2.2.title: Internal communication
soc-2-cc-2.2.requirement: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.
soc-2-cc-2.2.pb_interpretation: Formal channels for policy publication and change communication. Evidence should show both publication and receipt.
soc-2-cc-2.2.type_i_evidence: Policy portal / publication record; change-notification process.
soc-2-cc-2.2.type_ii_evidence: Acknowledgements across the period; change notices; meeting notes.
soc-2-cc-2.2.failure_modes: "Available on request" only; messages posted but not acknowledged.
soc-2-cc-2.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox communicates security and operational duties internally.

crosswalk.note: ISMS communication.

soc-2-cc-2.3.title: External communication
soc-2-cc-2.3.requirement: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal control.
soc-2-cc-2.3.pb_interpretation: Critical for shared-responsibility clarity. Customer-facing documentation for local responsibilities, support paths, and incident routing. Vague promises and missing customer duties are the central failure modes.
soc-2-cc-2.3.type_i_evidence: Security overview; support terms; shared-responsibility matrix template.
soc-2-cc-2.3.type_ii_evidence: Customer documentation versions across the period; contract clauses; notifications sent.
soc-2-cc-2.3.failure_modes: Vague promises; missing customer duties; customer docs inconsistent with support reality.
soc-2-cc-2.3.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox provides clear security and responsibility information to customers.

crosswalk.note: ISMS privacy / external disclosure overlap.
PIMS notice to data subjects.
POPIA openness condition (collection notice).

soc-2-cc-3.1.title: Objectives and commitments
soc-2-cc-3.1.requirement: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives.
soc-2-cc-3.1.pb_interpretation: Contractual and operational commitments written precisely. Service commitments register; scope statement. Avoid vague promises like "fully secure by default".
soc-2-cc-3.1.type_i_evidence: Service commitments register; system requirements register.
soc-2-cc-3.1.type_ii_evidence: Current commitments across the period; contract-to-control mapping.
soc-2-cc-3.1.failure_modes: Marketing claims exceed control reality; sales language outruns documented scope.
soc-2-cc-3.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox documents service commitments and system requirements.

crosswalk.note: ISMS objectives.

soc-2-cc-3.2.title: Risk identification and analysis
soc-2-cc-3.2.requirement: The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives across the entity and analyses risks as a basis for determining how risks should be managed.
soc-2-cc-3.2.pb_interpretation: Risk register including AI, support, deployment, and customer-hosting risks. Include prompt injection, data leakage, unsafe connector use, remote-support misuse, update tampering.
soc-2-cc-3.2.type_ii_evidence: Risk reviews across the period; treatment updates; closures.
soc-2-cc-3.2.failure_modes: One-off risk workshop only; stale register; actions never close.
soc-2-cc-3.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates a documented risk-management process.

crosswalk.note: ISMS risk assessment.
AIMS AI risk methodology.
King V Principle 8 risk.

soc-2-cc-3.3.title: Fraud risk
soc-2-cc-3.3.requirement: The entity considers the potential for fraud in assessing risks to the achievement of objectives.
soc-2-cc-3.3.pb_interpretation: Insider misuse, credential abuse, falsified approvals, audit-log tampering. For AI tooling, include prompt abuse and fabricated output workflows where relied upon.
soc-2-cc-3.3.type_i_evidence: Fraud scenarios in risk register; abuse cases.
soc-2-cc-3.3.type_ii_evidence: Fraud-risk review notes across the period; investigation records (if any).
soc-2-cc-3.3.failure_modes: Fraud considered "not applicable"; AI misuse omitted from risk design.
soc-2-cc-3.3.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox considers misuse and fraud risks in its control design.

soc-2-cc-3.4.title: Change risk
soc-2-cc-3.4.requirement: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could significantly impact the system of internal control.
soc-2-cc-3.4.pb_interpretation: Model changes, new connectors, new support tooling, new data flows trigger re-assessment. Many readiness programmes fail here after fast product iteration.
soc-2-cc-3.4.type_i_evidence: Change-risk trigger list; architecture / risk review gate documentation.
soc-2-cc-3.4.type_ii_evidence: Change review packs across the period; reassessment records for material changes.
soc-2-cc-3.4.failure_modes: Changes shipped without reassessment; model / connector changes treated as routine.
soc-2-cc-3.4.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox reassesses risk when its system changes materially.

crosswalk.note: ISMS planning of changes.
AIMS planning of changes including AI-specific change types.

soc-2-cc-4.1.title: Ongoing and separate monitoring
soc-2-cc-4.1.requirement: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and / or separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning.
soc-2-cc-4.1.pb_interpretation: Combine management reviews, internal reviews, vulnerability testing, periodic independent assessments. Monitoring should cover product, support environment, and customer-facing documentation.
soc-2-cc-4.1.type_ii_evidence: Review minutes across the period; pen-test results; internal audit notes; samples often shown monthly or periodic.
soc-2-cc-4.1.failure_modes: Monitoring cadence slips; monitoring undefined or ad hoc.
soc-2-cc-4.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox performs ongoing and periodic control monitoring.

crosswalk.note: ISMS monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation.
King V periodic assurance over technology.

soc-2-cc-4.2.title: Deficiency communication and remediation
soc-2-cc-4.2.requirement: The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those parties responsible for taking corrective action, including senior management and the board as appropriate.
soc-2-cc-4.2.pb_interpretation: Formal remediation tracker with severity, owner, due date, and closure evidence. Auditor confidence rises sharply when closure evidence is clean.
soc-2-cc-4.2.type_i_evidence: Issue tracker workflow; severity rules and closure validation pattern.
soc-2-cc-4.2.type_ii_evidence: Remediation tickets across the period; ageing reports; closure approvals.
soc-2-cc-4.2.failure_modes: Findings vanish into chats / email; open issues without owner or closure proof.
soc-2-cc-4.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox tracks and remediates control deficiencies.

crosswalk.note: ISMS nonconformity and corrective action.

soc-2-cc-5.1.title: Select and design control activities
soc-2-cc-5.1.requirement: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels.
soc-2-cc-5.1.pb_interpretation: Risk-to-control mapping across product, support, and corporate environments. Good place to align security, support, and AI quality governance in one matrix.
soc-2-cc-5.1.type_i_evidence: Control matrix; risk-control linkage and owner assignment.
soc-2-cc-5.1.type_ii_evidence: Evidence across mapped controls demonstrating execution.
soc-2-cc-5.1.failure_modes: Matrix exists but controls not performed; controls listed but not risk-linked.
soc-2-cc-5.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maps controls to assessed risks.

soc-2-cc-5.2.title: Technology general controls
soc-2-cc-5.2.requirement: The entity selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.
soc-2-cc-5.2.pb_interpretation: Build pipelines, repositories, secrets, environments, release tooling. Usually a high-weight audit area for software vendors.
soc-2-cc-5.2.type_ii_evidence: Pipeline approvals across the period; environment separation evidence; secret rotations.
soc-2-cc-5.2.failure_modes: Shared credentials; no branch protections; repo bypasses; unreviewed changes; stale secrets.
soc-2-cc-5.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates technology controls over the systems that support its service.

crosswalk.note: ISMS secure SDLC + secure architecture + secure coding + security testing.
AIMS responsible design and development.

soc-2-cc-5.3.title: Policies and procedures
soc-2-cc-5.3.requirement: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and procedures that put policies into action.
soc-2-cc-5.3.pb_interpretation: Convert policy to step-by-step SOPs for provisioning, support, change, incident, and release. If only policies exist, Type II evidence usually collapses.
documented_evidence: SOPs with version history + review records
soc-2-cc-5.3.type_ii_evidence: SOP-linked tickets / execution logs across the period; staff don't rely on memory.
soc-2-cc-5.3.failure_modes: Policy-only programme; staff rely on memory or chats.
soc-2-cc-5.3.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operationalises documented policies through repeatable procedures.

soc-2-cc-6.1.title: Access security architecture
soc-2-cc-6.1.requirement: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events to meet the entity's objectives.
soc-2-cc-6.1.pb_interpretation: Inventory product nodes, model artefacts, vectors, connectors, admin APIs, support tools. Require MFA and segmentation where feasible. Customer-hosted systems may implement part via CUECs.
soc-2-cc-6.1.type_i_evidence: Inventory; auth standard; network diagrams; segmentation design.
soc-2-cc-6.1.type_ii_evidence: MFA state samples across the period; inventory reconciliations; config samples; stale-key reviews.
soc-2-cc-6.1.failure_modes: Unknown assets; MFA gaps; stale keys.
soc-2-cc-6.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox designs access protections for product and support components.

crosswalk.note: ISMS access control + privileged access + secure authentication.

soc-2-cc-6.2.title: User provisioning and deprovisioning
soc-2-cc-6.2.requirement: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorises new internal and external users whose access is administered by the entity.
soc-2-cc-6.2.pb_interpretation: Ticketed approvals; named accounts; prompt deprovisioning of local and support access. Distinguish customer-local admins from PB remote-support users.
soc-2-cc-6.2.type_ii_evidence: Access tickets across the period; terminations sampled; deprovisioning timeliness.
soc-2-cc-6.2.failure_modes: Shared accounts; no removal step; late deprovisioning; missing approval.
soc-2-cc-6.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox operates controlled access onboarding and offboarding processes.

crosswalk.note: ISMS identity management + authentication information + access rights.

soc-2-cc-6.3.title: Role-based access and least privilege
soc-2-cc-6.3.requirement: The entity authorises, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or the system design and changes, giving consideration to the concepts of least privilege and segregation of duties.
soc-2-cc-6.3.pb_interpretation: Role matrix for support, engineering, deployment, and customer admins; review privileged access. Support break-glass access should be rare, approved, and logged.
soc-2-cc-6.3.type_i_evidence: Role matrix; admin model; privileged review design.
soc-2-cc-6.3.type_ii_evidence: Access review records across the period; admin lists; SoD checks.
soc-2-cc-6.3.failure_modes: Flat admin rights; no SoD; privilege creep.
soc-2-cc-6.3.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox aligns system access to roles and least-privilege principles.

crosswalk.note: ISMS privileged access rights.

soc-2-cc-6.4.title: Physical access restrictions
soc-2-cc-6.4.requirement: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets to authorised personnel to meet the entity's objectives.
soc-2-cc-6.4.pb_interpretation: PB sites: badge / visitor / media controls. Customer-hosted nodes: minimum customer responsibilities defined as CUECs. In pure customer-hosted scope, much execution may sit with the customer.
soc-2-cc-6.4.type_i_evidence: Physical security standard; visitor controls; deployment prerequisites.
soc-2-cc-6.4.type_ii_evidence: Badge logs and visitor logs across the period; periodic review.
soc-2-cc-6.4.failure_modes: No physical-scope decision; no review of old badges; visitor gaps.
soc-2-cc-6.4.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox defines and operates physical security controls relevant to its scope.

crosswalk.note: ISMS physical entry controls + securing offices.

soc-2-cc-6.5.title: Secure asset disposal
soc-2-cc-6.5.requirement: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished and is no longer required to meet the entity's objectives.
soc-2-cc-6.5.pb_interpretation: Secure wipe / dekey procedures for appliances, RMA devices, removable media, and support exports. Especially important for field hardware and removable support media.
soc-2-cc-6.5.type_i_evidence: Disposal / RMA procedure; crypto-erase or wipe validation.
soc-2-cc-6.5.type_ii_evidence: Wipe / dekey evidence across the period; disposal records.
soc-2-cc-6.5.failure_modes: Disposal undefined for field hardware; missing sanitisation proof.
soc-2-cc-6.5.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox uses secure disposal and sanitisation processes.

crosswalk.note: ISMS secure disposal or re-use of equipment.

soc-2-cc-6.6.title: Boundary protection
soc-2-cc-6.6.requirement: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
soc-2-cc-6.6.pb_interpretation: Disable remote access by default; require customer approval, MFA, session logging, and network restrictions. Major shared-responsibility area for on-prem customers.
soc-2-cc-6.6.type_i_evidence: Network baseline; remote-support policy; default-off remote support and MFA design.
soc-2-cc-6.6.type_ii_evidence: Firewall samples; tunnel approvals; session logs; permanent-exception reviews.
soc-2-cc-6.6.failure_modes: Permanent always-on support tunnel; permanent exceptions; undocumented remote sessions.
soc-2-cc-6.6.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox uses controlled boundary protection for remote access and administration.

crosswalk.note: ISMS network security + segregation + network services.

soc-2-cc-6.7.title: Secure transmission and removal
soc-2-cc-6.7.requirement: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorised internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal to meet the entity's objectives.
soc-2-cc-6.7.pb_interpretation: Encrypt exports, support bundles, backups, and updates; sign packages; document offline- transfer methods. Air-gapped deployments need explicit offline controls.
soc-2-cc-6.7.type_i_evidence: Transfer standard; encryption requirements; signed updates and offline media procedures.
soc-2-cc-6.7.type_ii_evidence: Signed releases across the period; export logs; encryption-evidence samples.
soc-2-cc-6.7.failure_modes: Ad hoc file transfer methods; informal transfers; unsigned packages.
soc-2-cc-6.7.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox uses protected mechanisms for transmitting and removing data.

crosswalk.note: ISMS information transfer + cryptography.

soc-2-cc-6.8.title: Malicious software prevention
soc-2-cc-6.8.requirement: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorised or malicious software to meet the entity's objectives.
soc-2-cc-6.8.pb_interpretation: Scan build artefacts, endpoints, support uploads, imported packages; restrict installs. In customer environments, some execution may be customer-owned.
soc-2-cc-6.8.type_i_evidence: EDR / AV standard; scan process; build / package scanning design.
soc-2-cc-6.8.type_ii_evidence: Scan logs across the period; EDR status; agent activity.
soc-2-cc-6.8.failure_modes: Reliance on customer AV only; agents inactive; packages unscanned.
soc-2-cc-6.8.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox applies malware and unauthorised-software controls across relevant environments.

crosswalk.note: ISMS protection against malware.

soc-2-cc-7.1.title: Vulnerability and configuration monitoring
soc-2-cc-7.1.requirement: To meet its objectives, the entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities, and susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.
soc-2-cc-7.1.pb_interpretation: Maintain hardening baselines; run infra, container, dependency, and configuration scans; issue advisories to customers. Telemetry-off customers may need to self-run some controls with PB guidance.
soc-2-cc-7.1.type_i_evidence: Hardening baseline; scan schedule; FIM and remediation SLA design.
soc-2-cc-7.1.type_ii_evidence: Scan reports across the period; remediation tickets; FIM alert handling.
soc-2-cc-7.1.failure_modes: Scans run but no response design; findings linger.
soc-2-cc-7.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox monitors vulnerabilities and configuration drift relevant to its system.

crosswalk.note: ISMS management of technical vulnerabilities + configuration management.

soc-2-cc-7.2.title: System and anomaly monitoring
soc-2-cc-7.2.requirement: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives; anomalies are analysed to determine whether they represent security events.
soc-2-cc-7.2.pb_interpretation: Define minimum log set, SIEM integration points, alert catalogue, support-event logging. If customer disables telemetry, PB should clearly describe what it cannot observe.
soc-2-cc-7.2.type_i_evidence: Logging standard; alert matrix; minimum log list.
soc-2-cc-7.2.type_ii_evidence: Alert reviews across the period; dashboards; investigations.
soc-2-cc-7.2.failure_modes: "We log everything" with no specifics; alert fatigue; no closed-loop review.
soc-2-cc-7.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox defines monitoring requirements and operates its own in-scope monitoring controls.

crosswalk.note: ISMS logging + monitoring + clock synchronisation.
AIMS AI event logs.

soc-2-cc-7.3.title: Security event evaluation
soc-2-cc-7.3.requirement: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives (security incidents) and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures.
soc-2-cc-7.3.pb_interpretation: Severity matrix and decision tree; include customer-impact and AI-misuse analysis. Customer- originated events still need to be routed into PB support / PSIRT decisions.
soc-2-cc-7.3.type_i_evidence: Triage workflow; severity matrix; impact-assessment design.
soc-2-cc-7.3.type_ii_evidence: Triage tickets across the period; severity records; escalation logs.
soc-2-cc-7.3.failure_modes: No event-to-incident decision rule; events never escalated; severity inconsistent.
soc-2-cc-7.3.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox evaluates security events through a documented triage process.

crosswalk.note: ISMS incident management planning + assessment.

soc-2-cc-7.4.title: Incident response execution
soc-2-cc-7.4.requirement: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident-response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.
soc-2-cc-7.4.pb_interpretation: Joint-ready playbooks for product, support, customer-site, and subservice incidents. Include joint-response roles and evidence boundaries for customer-hosted nodes.
soc-2-cc-7.4.type_i_evidence: IR plan; playbooks; contact trees; tabletop plan.
soc-2-cc-7.4.type_ii_evidence: Incident files across the period; tabletop records; notifications.
soc-2-cc-7.4.failure_modes: Incident plan too generic; plan exists but not followed.
soc-2-cc-7.4.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains a documented incident-response programme.

crosswalk.note: ISMS incident response + learning from incidents.
POPIA security safeguards including s 22 breach notification.

soc-2-cc-7.5.title: Recovery from incidents
soc-2-cc-7.5.requirement: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.
soc-2-cc-7.5.pb_interpretation: Restoration procedures, post-incident reviews, hardening follow-up. If customer environment is primary recovery domain, PB still documents its own obligations and dependencies.
soc-2-cc-7.5.type_i_evidence: Recovery runbooks; PIR process; root-cause and prevention loop design.
soc-2-cc-7.5.type_ii_evidence: PIRs across the period; recovery evidence; change records linked to incidents.
soc-2-cc-7.5.failure_modes: No structured post-incident review; no root cause or recurrence-prevention action.
soc-2-cc-7.5.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox uses recovery and lessons-learned processes after incidents.

crosswalk.note: ISMS learning from information security incidents.

soc-2-cc-8.1.title: Change management
soc-2-cc-8.1.requirement: The entity authorises, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.
soc-2-cc-8.1.pb_interpretation: Cover code, models, prompts, connectors, configs, installers, documentation, and support scripts. Model updates and prompt / config changes must be treated as controlled changes.
soc-2-cc-8.1.type_i_evidence: Change policy; release workflow; rollback design; emergency change path.
soc-2-cc-8.1.type_ii_evidence: PR approvals across the period; tickets; test evidence; release notes; rollback drills.
soc-2-cc-8.1.failure_modes: Informal hotfixes; emergency changes undocumented; weak rollback.
soc-2-cc-8.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox manages system changes through documented approval and testing practices.

crosswalk.note: ISMS change management.
AIMS planning of changes + documentation of AI system development.

soc-2-cc-9.1.title: Business disruption mitigation
soc-2-cc-9.1.requirement: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.
soc-2-cc-9.1.pb_interpretation: Continuity plans for support, releases, credential recovery, repos, and critical teams. Define customer recovery assumptions. Availability execution may sit mainly with the customer, but PB still owns assumptions and support recovery.
soc-2-cc-9.1.type_i_evidence: BCP / DR plan; dependency analysis; BIA and tested recovery assumptions.
soc-2-cc-9.1.type_ii_evidence: BCP reviews across the period; test results; dependency updates.
soc-2-cc-9.1.suggested_observation_period: 6-12 months; at least one test cycle preferred
soc-2-cc-9.1.failure_modes: Plan stops at "call support"; plan stale; no exercise.
soc-2-cc-9.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains continuity and disruption-response planning.

crosswalk.note: ISMS information security during disruption + ICT readiness for business continuity.

soc-2-cc-9.2.title: Vendor and business partner risk
soc-2-cc-9.2.requirement: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners.
soc-2-cc-9.2.pb_interpretation: Govern cloud relays, support tooling, telemetry tools, open-source and model suppliers, code- signing services. Separate PB vendors from customer-selected local infrastructure vendors.
soc-2-cc-9.2.type_i_evidence: Vendor process; contract requirements; tiering; review cadence design.
soc-2-cc-9.2.type_ii_evidence: Due diligence records across the period; annual reviews; issue logs.
soc-2-cc-9.2.failure_modes: No definition of critical vendor; critical vendor not assessed; contract gaps.
soc-2-cc-9.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox manages security risk in relevant supplier relationships.

crosswalk.note: ISMS supplier relationships, contracts, ICT supply chain, monitoring of supplier services.
AIMS supplier responsibility allocation.
POPIA operator duties (ss 20-21).

soc-2-a-1.1.title: Capacity monitoring
soc-2-a-1.1.requirement: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components (infrastructure, data, and software) to manage capacity demand and to enable the implementation of additional capacity to help meet its objectives.
soc-2-a-1.1.pb_interpretation: Scope only if PB makes capacity or throughput commitments. In many local deployments, customer owns runtime capacity.
soc-2-a-1.1.type_ii_evidence: Utilisation trends across the period; threshold actions; capacity reviews.
soc-2-a-1.1.failure_modes: Availability promise with no metrics; capacity reviews skipped.
soc-2-a-1.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains capacity-planning controls where availability commitments apply.

crosswalk.note: ISMS capacity management.

soc-2-a-1.2.title: Environmental protection, backups, and recovery infrastructure
soc-2-a-1.2.requirement: The entity authorises, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
soc-2-a-1.2.pb_interpretation: Define required local backup, environmental, and restore architecture. Often a major customer- owned area for on-prem installations.
soc-2-a-1.2.type_i_evidence: Backup standard; restore standard; environmental controls; recovery-infra design.
soc-2-a-1.2.type_ii_evidence: Backup logs across the period; failure alerts; restore evidence.
soc-2-a-1.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox documents and operates recoverability controls relevant to scope.

crosswalk.note: ISMS information backup.

soc-2-a-1.3.title: Recovery-plan testing
soc-2-a-1.3.requirement: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives.
soc-2-a-1.3.pb_interpretation: Run restore tests for PB-managed elements; document customer test expectations. Valuable differentiator for PB maturity.
soc-2-a-1.3.type_i_evidence: Test schedule; scenario-based restore exercises design.
soc-2-a-1.3.type_ii_evidence: Restore test outputs across the period; exercise reports.
soc-2-a-1.3.cadence: Periodic; usually annual or more often
soc-2-a-1.3.failure_modes: Never-tested DR; tests not executed; failures not fixed.
soc-2-a-1.3.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox tests relevant recovery procedures and documents expected customer tests.

crosswalk.note: ISMS ICT readiness for business continuity.

soc-2-c-1.1.title: Confidential information identification and retention
soc-2-c-1.1.requirement: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
soc-2-c-1.1.pb_interpretation: Classify customer documents, support bundles, model artefacts, and secrets. Define retention rules. Often worth scoping even without full Privacy.
soc-2-c-1.1.type_i_evidence: Classification policy; retention schedule; support-data and secrets coverage.
soc-2-c-1.1.type_ii_evidence: Labelling samples across the period; retention application records.
soc-2-c-1.1.failure_modes: Classification too generic; data stored in wrong class; no retention enforcement.
soc-2-c-1.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox classifies and protects confidential information according to policy.

crosswalk.note: ISMS classification of information + labelling.
POPIA purpose specification and retention.

soc-2-c-1.2.title: Confidential information disposal
soc-2-c-1.2.requirement: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
soc-2-c-1.2.pb_interpretation: Extend disposal rules to support exports, logs, snapshots, update artefacts, retired devices. Keep customer deletion responsibilities explicit where data stays entirely local.
soc-2-c-1.2.type_i_evidence: Disposal process; automated purge plus evidence design.
soc-2-c-1.2.type_ii_evidence: Purge records across the period; deletion tickets.
soc-2-c-1.2.failure_modes: No destruction trigger; retention exceeded; deletion not evidenced.
soc-2-c-1.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox uses secure disposal for confidential information.

crosswalk.note: ISMS information deletion.
POPIA retention destruction.

soc-2-pi-1.1.title: Processing specifications and definitions
soc-2-pi-1.1.requirement: The entity obtains or generates, uses, and communicates relevant, quality information regarding the objectives related to processing, including definitions of data processed and product and service specifications, to support the use of products and services.
soc-2-pi-1.1.pb_interpretation: Only scope if PB makes bounded processing commitments. Do not scope generic model truthfulness here.
soc-2-pi-1.1.type_ii_evidence: Approved specs across the period; test updates.
soc-2-pi-1.1.failure_modes: Vague correctness claims; commitments change without control update.
soc-2-pi-1.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox defines objective processing specifications where relevant.

soc-2-pi-1.2.title: Input controls
soc-2-pi-1.2.requirement: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs, including controls over completeness, accuracy, and timeliness, to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives.
soc-2-pi-1.2.pb_interpretation: Apply to imports, connectors, user uploads, batch jobs, support actions where PB makes commitments. Shared when customers feed local data directly.
soc-2-pi-1.2.type_ii_evidence: Validation logs across the period; rejected-input records.
soc-2-pi-1.2.failure_modes: Inputs accepted without validation; bad inputs bypass checks.
soc-2-pi-1.2.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox applies controlled input-validation measures where processing-integrity commitments apply.

soc-2-pi-1.3.title: Processing controls
soc-2-pi-1.3.requirement: The entity implements policies and procedures over system processing to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives.
soc-2-pi-1.3.pb_interpretation: Job controls, exception handling, reconciliations, regression tests for deterministic workflows. Most suitable for deterministic pipelines, not open-ended answer quality.
soc-2-pi-1.3.type_i_evidence: Workflow definitions; reconciliation or regression-test design.
soc-2-pi-1.3.type_ii_evidence: Job logs across the period; reconciliations; exception tickets.
soc-2-pi-1.3.failure_modes: Black-box reliance without controls; silent failures; no exception follow-up.
soc-2-pi-1.3.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox uses controlled processing procedures for scoped workflows.

crosswalk.note: AIMS V&V and robustness for AI-system processing.

soc-2-pi-1.4.title: Output controls
soc-2-pi-1.4.requirement: The entity implements policies and procedures to deliver output, including its completeness, accuracy, and timeliness, to meet the entity's objectives.
soc-2-pi-1.4.pb_interpretation: Define output recipients, export approvals, audit reports, error corrections. Good fit for audit exports, workflow actions, or decision-support packages.
soc-2-pi-1.4.type_ii_evidence: Delivery logs across the period; review checks; export approvals.
soc-2-pi-1.4.failure_modes: No control over exports or reports; wrong recipient; incomplete export.
soc-2-pi-1.4.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox protects and controls output distribution where such commitments are made.

soc-2-pi-1.5.title: Storage controls for items in process
soc-2-pi-1.5.requirement: The entity implements policies and procedures to store inputs, items in processing, and outputs completely, accurately, and timely to meet the entity's objectives.
soc-2-pi-1.5.pb_interpretation: Control staging areas, vector stores, caches, output archives, audit records. Often overlaps with Confidentiality and Availability.
soc-2-pi-1.5.type_i_evidence: Storage / archival standard; integrity checks design.
soc-2-pi-1.5.type_ii_evidence: Archival logs across the period; integrity checks.
soc-2-pi-1.5.failure_modes: Temporary stores unmanaged; staging data unmanaged.
soc-2-pi-1.5.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox protects and manages stored processing artefacts where relevant.

soc-2-p-1.1.title: Privacy notice
soc-2-p-1.1.requirement: The entity provides notice to data subjects about its privacy practices to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy. The notice is updated and communicated to data subjects in a timely manner for changes to the entity's privacy practices.
soc-2-p-1.1.pb_interpretation: Scope only if PB's in-scope system collects personal information from data subjects or acts as controller / processor in a way that requires notice. Customer-only data collection may make this not relevant for the system scope.
soc-2-p-1.1.type_ii_evidence: Notice versions across the period; publication evidence.
soc-2-p-1.1.failure_modes: Privacy scoped with no notice basis; privacy practices change silently.
soc-2-p-1.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox documents privacy practices for in-scope personal information handling where applicable.

crosswalk.note: PIMS controllers privacy notice.
POPIA openness / collection notice.

soc-2-p-2.1.title: Choice and consent
soc-2-p-2.1.requirement: The entity communicates choices available regarding the collection, use, retention, disclosure, and disposal of personal information to data subjects and obtains and documents consent or authority for the collection, use, retention, disclosure, and disposal of personal information.
soc-2-p-2.1.pb_interpretation: Scope only where PB collects or uses personal information directly for support, telemetry, or account services. Often mostly customer-owned in local deployments.
soc-2-p-2.1.type_ii_evidence: Consent logs across the period; lawful-basis records.
soc-2-p-2.1.failure_modes: Consent implied but undocumented; missing consent trail.
soc-2-p-2.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox documents consent or lawful-basis handling for in-scope personal information where applicable.

crosswalk.note: POPIA processing limitation including s 11 consent / justification.

soc-2-p-3.1.title: Collection limitation
soc-2-p-3.1.requirement: Personal information is collected consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
soc-2-p-3.1.pb_interpretation: Minimise support, telemetry, and admin-personal-data collection. This may be out of scope if customers, not PB, collect end-user data.
soc-2-p-3.1.type_i_evidence: Data minimisation standard; telemetry / support-data field reviews.
soc-2-p-3.1.type_ii_evidence: Field inventories across the period; telemetry reviews.
soc-2-p-3.1.failure_modes: Over-collection "just in case"; PI fields creep over time.
soc-2-p-3.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox applies data-minimisation principles to in-scope personal information.

crosswalk.note: POPIA minimisation principle.

soc-2-p-4.title: Use, retention, and disposal of personal information
soc-2-p-4.requirement: The entity limits the use of personal information to the purposes identified in the entity's objectives related to privacy; the entity retains personal information consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy; the entity securely disposes of personal information.
soc-2-p-4.pb_interpretation: Define precise uses and retention for support tickets, telemetry, logs, account data, exports. Strong overlap with Confidentiality and records management.
soc-2-p-4.type_i_evidence: Privacy retention / deletion process; purpose-based restrictions and purge workflow.
soc-2-p-4.type_ii_evidence: Purpose checks across the period; retention reports; purge evidence.
soc-2-p-4.failure_modes: Retention undefined; data kept too long; unauthorised use.
soc-2-p-4.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox restricts use and retention of in-scope personal information.

crosswalk.note: POPIA purpose specification + retention.

soc-2-p-5.title: Access and amendment rights
soc-2-p-5.requirement: The entity provides data subjects with access to their personal information for review and, upon request, provides physical or electronic copies of that information; the entity corrects, amends, or appends personal information based on information provided by data subjects and communicates such information to third parties.
soc-2-p-5.pb_interpretation: Only scope if PB is responsible for responding to those requests for in-scope PI. Frequently customer-owned for customer data.
soc-2-p-5.type_i_evidence: Request workflow; identity verification, denial logic design.
soc-2-p-5.type_ii_evidence: Request log across the period; responses.
soc-2-p-5.failure_modes: No ownership for responses; no identity verification; overdue responses.
soc-2-p-5.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains a documented process for in-scope personal-information access or correction requests where applicable.

crosswalk.note: POPIA data subject participation.

soc-2-p-6.title: Third-party disclosure, breach notice, and accounting
soc-2-p-6.requirement: The entity discloses personal information to third parties consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy and notifies data subjects of incidents involving personal information consistent with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
soc-2-p-6.pb_interpretation: Privacy terms into vendor contracts; breach notification decision paths and records. Particularly relevant if PB uses telemetry, support processors, or identity services.
soc-2-p-6.type_i_evidence: Vendor clauses; breach notice process; disclosure log design.
soc-2-p-6.type_ii_evidence: Contracts across the period; incident records; notices.
soc-2-p-6.failure_modes: Privacy obligations absent from third-party contracts; processor not notified; no record of disclosure.
soc-2-p-6.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox governs third-party and incident-related privacy obligations for in-scope personal information.

crosswalk.note: POPIA security safeguards (s 22 breach + s 20-21 operator).

soc-2-p-7.1.title: Personal information quality
soc-2-p-7.1.requirement: The entity collects and maintains accurate, up-to-date, complete, and relevant personal information to meet the entity's objectives related to privacy.
soc-2-p-7.1.pb_interpretation: Define necessary PI fields only; correction workflow for account / support records. Usually narrow in system scope for on-prem vendors.
soc-2-p-7.1.failure_modes: Bad data persists without correction path.
soc-2-p-7.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains data-quality controls for in-scope personal information.

crosswalk.note: POPIA information quality.

soc-2-p-8.1.title: Privacy inquiries, complaints, and enforcement
soc-2-p-8.1.requirement: The entity implements a process for receiving, addressing, resolving, and communicating the resolution of inquiries, complaints, and disputes from data subjects and others, and monitors compliance with the entity's objectives related to privacy.
soc-2-p-8.1.pb_interpretation: Intake path, escalation, and remediation for privacy issues. Relevant if Privacy is in scope or if procurement requires a privacy-operating model.
soc-2-p-8.1.type_i_evidence: Complaint process; escalation and compliance monitoring.
soc-2-p-8.1.type_ii_evidence: Complaint log across the period; closure evidence.
soc-2-p-8.1.failure_modes: Privacy issue handling ad hoc; complaints unresolved.
soc-2-p-8.1.external_safe_phrasing: PrivateBox maintains processes for privacy inquiries and remediation where applicable.

crosswalk.note: POPIA data subject participation including complaints.

nist.version: AI RMF 1.0 + GenAI Profile Jul 2024 + SSDF 1.1 + SP 800-218A Final May 2024
nist.full_name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework + Secure Software Development Framework + AI Overlay

effective_dates.milestone: AI RMF GenAI Profile published (NIST AI 600-1)

pb_application.why_chosen: NIST's AI RMF + SSDF + 218A bundle is the dominant US-government-and-enterprise voluntary framework family for AI and secure software development. AI RMF 1.0 sets the four-function structure (GOVERN / MAP / MEASURE / MANAGE) that has become the lingua franca for AI risk governance in US procurement; SSDF 1.1 is the federally-mandated baseline for secure software development under EO 14028 and subsequent OMB guidance (M-22-18 / M-23-16) — vendors selling into US federal agencies attest against SSDF practices; SP 800-218A overlays AI-model-specific additions onto SSDF covering training-data protection, model weights, adversarial testing, prompt-injection risk, and AI supply chain. PB's commercial reality: US enterprise procurement (especially financial services, healthcare, federal-adjacent) expects evidence of AI RMF alignment and SSDF attestation alongside ISO 27001 / SOC 2. Unlike ISO standards (certifiable) or POPIA (binding law), NIST frameworks are voluntary — self-attestation is the standard model. PB's positioning: "designed in alignment with NIST AI RMF 1.0 and SSDF 1.1, with AI Overlay (SP 800-218A) recommendations applied to the AI development pipeline." Never claim "NIST certified" — there is no such thing.

on_prem.rationale: AI RMF outcomes apply to PB-as-AI-developer regardless of deployment. SSDF tasks apply to the development pipeline that builds PB on its own infrastructure — customer deployment environment is mostly out of SSDF scope. 218A overlay covers PB model sourcing, training data handling, model weights, adversarial testing — entirely PB-side. Customer-hosted runtime monitoring uses the AI RMF MANAGE function with shared responsibility (PB provides the design and the optional telemetry, customer operates in their environment).

ai_factory.rationale: AI Factory partner-DC deployment introduces a subprocessor relationship that AI RMF GOV 6.1 / 6.2 and SSDF PO.1.3 cover. Pending partner-DC selection, supplier-governance evidence is provisional. 218A overlay applies identically since it concerns PB's model development pipeline regardless of deployment substrate.

top_risks: AI RMF treated as marketing alignment rather than operational implementation — outcomes named in procurement responses without corresponding evidence in the engineering process.
SSDF tasks attested without recurring evidence collection across SDLC — Type II-style operating-effectiveness evidence absent at audit time.

218A overlay items skipped because "AI Overlay is voluntary" — but the items it adds (model weight protection, training-data integrity, adversarial testing, prompt-injection coverage) are the highest-leverage AI-security controls.
Model provenance and dataset provenance undocumented — particularly for open-weight models pulled from public hubs without signed verification.
Prompt-injection and jailbreak testing skipped in favour of generic security testing — known 218A gap pattern.
Third-party AI components (foundation models, embedding services, vector stores) entering the pipeline without intake review — AI RMF GOV 6.1 / SSDF PW.4.1 high-risk gap.
No documented kill-switch / disengagement procedure for production AI features — MANAGE 2.4 + 218A model update/rollback failure mode.
Evaluation suites not versioned or reproducible — MEASURE 2.1 / SSDF PW.8.1 gap that undermines every other AI quality claim.

shared_responsibility_model.area: AI governance pack (AI RMF GOV + SSDF PO)
shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: AI policy, RACI, launch criteria, exception process, supplier governance pack.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer's own AI governance for their use of PrivateBox.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Unified governance pack aligns AI RMF GOV 1-6 with SSDF PO.1-PO.5.

shared_responsibility_model.area: System and model inventory (AI RMF GOV 1.6 + SSDF PS.3.2 + 218A model registry)
shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: System inventory, approved model inventory, component register, SBOM / MBOM.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer's deployment-side inventory of which models are in use.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: PB ships the approved-models list; customer logs which are activated.

shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: SDLC SOPs, branch protections, CI/CD hardening, code review, testing artefacts.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: N/A — SDLC is PB's pipeline.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: 218A overlay extends SDLC into model artefacts and training environments.

shared_responsibility_model.area: Model and data provenance (AI RMF MAP 4.1 + SSDF PS.3.2 + 218A model/dataset provenance)
shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: Source records, model source approval, dataset lineage, signatures, digests.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer's own knowledge-base / RAG provenance.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: PB provides templates; customer applies them to their corpus.

shared_responsibility_model.area: TEVV and red teaming (AI RMF MAP 2.3 + MEASURE 1-4 + SSDF PW.7-8 + 218A adversarial)
shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: TEVV plan, red-team reports, evaluation sets, regression suite, security findings.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer's deployment-context evaluation where applicable.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: PB delivers eval baseline; customer extends with sector-specific scenarios.

shared_responsibility_model.area: Runtime and incident operations (AI RMF MANAGE + SSDF RV)
shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: Monitoring plan, incident SOP, customer communication templates, kill-switch runbook.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer's local monitoring of deployed system per agreed telemetry scope.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: 15-day EU AI Act + 72-hour POPIA notification timing baked into PB SOPs.

shared_responsibility_model.area: Customer handoff and shared responsibility (AI RMF MAP 1.1 + 3.5 + MANAGE 1.4)
shared_responsibility_model.pb_owned: Deployment guide, hardening guide, shared-responsibility matrix, residual-risk statement.
shared_responsibility_model.cl_owned: Customer reads, configures, and operates per handoff.
shared_responsibility_model.shared_note: Procurement-gold artefact set — Workday / Microsoft Service Trust Portal pattern.

evidence_families.name: AI governance policy set (AI RMF GOV + SSDF PO anchor)
examples: AI policy with accountability, risk tiers, approvals, exceptions
Third-party AI rules and approved-models list
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: AI RMF GOV outcomes can't be evidenced without a policy stack tying back to engineering.

evidence_families.name: System and model inventory (AI RMF GOV 1.6 / MANAGE 3.2 + SSDF PS.3.2 + 218A registry)
examples: Approved-models register (version, source, approval, retirement triggers)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Essential for AI RMF GOV 1.6 / MANAGE 3.2 and SSDF provenance. Cannot test what you cannot inventory.

evidence_families.name: Secure SDLC SOP + RACI + toolchain artefacts (SSDF PO.3 / PW + 218A)
examples: SDLC SOPs covering repos, CI/CD, code review, testing, signing, release gating
218A-extended scope to model artefacts and training environments
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Core evidence for SSDF and 218A readiness; highest-volume audit area for software vendors.

evidence_families.name: SBOM / provenance / release integrity bundle (SSDF PS.3.2 + 218A model provenance)
examples: Component inventory with origins and licences
Dataset lineage for training / fine-tuning data
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: One of the strongest attestation-style artefact families; expected in US federal procurement under M-22-18.

evidence_families.name: Model / system card (AI RMF transparency artefact)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Key AI RMF transparency artefact (GOV 4.2 / MAP 2.2 / MEASURE 2.8 / 2.9).

evidence_families.name: Evaluation and red-team pack (AI RMF MEASURE + SSDF PW.7-8 + 218A adversarial)
examples: AI red-team reports (prompt injection, jailbreak, exfiltration)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Converts AI governance into auditable evidence; SSDF Type II evidence collapses without it.
evidence_families.cadence: Per release + drift trigger + scheduled red-team cycle

evidence_families.name: Vulnerability and incident SOP (SSDF RV + AI RMF MANAGE)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Bridges SSDF RV and AI RMF MANAGE; visible to customers as advisories.

evidence_families.name: Customer deployment and shared-responsibility guide (AI RMF MAP 1.1 + 3.5 + MANAGE)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: Critical for on-prem credibility; mirrors SOC 2 CUEC pattern and ISO 42001 customer-relationship controls.

evidence_families.name: AI training and competence records (AI RMF GOV 2.2 + SSDF PO.2.2)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: GOV 2.2 + PO.2.2 cross-cut all other outcomes; sampling-friendly Type II evidence.

evidence_families.name: Third-party AI component governance (AI RMF GOV 6 + SSDF PW.4 + 218A)
examples: Supplier assessment per model / library / dataset
Model card dossier per third-party model
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: GOV 6.1 / 6.2 is the most-asked third-party-AI question in procurement.
evidence_families.cadence: Per onboarding + annual + on upstream change

evidence_families.name: AI risk register and treatment plan (AI RMF MANAGE 1)
examples: Risk register with AI-specific entries (hallucination, prompt injection, drift, abuse)
evidence_families.why_auditor_cares: MANAGE 1.1-1.4 traceable from risk to treatment to acceptance.

scenarios.name: AI RMF baseline profile (general AI systems)
scenarios.description: PrivateBox applies AI RMF 1.0 outcomes to its on-prem AI platform as a general AI system. GenAI Profile considerations applied where generative features are material.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Legal / regulatory register covers AI-specific obligations.
Intended-use register per deployment.

scenarios.notes: Baseline posture for procurement responses.

scenarios.name: AI RMF GenAI Profile (NIST AI 600-1) activated
scenarios.description: Generative AI features (chat, summarisation, generation) trigger the GenAI Profile (NIST AI 600-1, Jul 2024). Adds CBRN, confabulation, intellectual-property, harmful-content, environmental impact, human-AI configuration considerations.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: GenAI security testing (jailbreak, prompt injection) becomes mandatory.
Privacy risk in GenAI prompts and outputs.

scenarios.notes: PB's primary AI feature set is generative; profile applies.

scenarios.name: SSDF 1.1 readiness for US federal procurement (M-22-18 / M-23-16)
scenarios.description: SSDF 1.1 self-attestation per OMB M-22-18 / M-23-16 — required for software sold to US federal agencies. PB attests its own production of the platform per CISA's SSDF attestation form.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Foundational PO tasks must be attested.
SBOM / provenance is the M-22-18 high-bar item.

scenarios.notes: Trigger for any US-federal-adjacent deal.

scenarios.name: 218A AI Overlay activated for AI model pipeline
scenarios.description: SP 800-218A overlay applied on top of SSDF for PB's AI model pipeline — training data protection, model weight protection, adversarial testing, AI vulnerability intake.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Training / fine-tuning data protected against unauthorised modification.
Model weights protected.

scenarios.notes: Activated when PB does any fine-tuning or guardrail-model production.

scenarios.name: AI-specific incident disclosure pathway
scenarios.description: Behavioural AI incident (harmful output, prompt-injection breach, model exfiltration attempt) triggers cross-framework notification — MANAGE 4.3 + SSDF RV.1.3 + EU AI Act Art. 73 + POPIA s 22 where personal information involved.

triggered_requirements.scenario_specific_note: Communication plan invoked.
VDP coordination.

scenarios.notes: Aligns with established EU AI Act 15-day and POPIA 72-hour windows.

open_questions.question: Does PrivateBox attest against AI RMF general profile only, or also activate GenAI Profile (NIST AI 600-1)?
candidate_answers: GenAI Profile activated (covers PB's generative features explicitly)
Both — general baseline plus GenAI overlay on chat / summarisation features
open_questions.notes: PB's primary feature set is generative. GenAI Profile activation strengthens procurement story for high-stakes use cases but adds CBRN / confabulation / IP-specific evidence requirements.

open_questions.question: When does PrivateBox submit the CISA SSDF attestation form (M-22-18 / M-23-16)?
candidate_answers: Wait until attestation form path stabilises (current status uncertain post-2025)
open_questions.notes: M-22-18 attestation form path has been in flux 2024-2026. Verify current CISA / OMB requirements before submission.

open_questions.question: Which SBOM generation and signing tooling does PrivateBox standardise on?
open_questions.notes: Syft+Cosign is the de facto standard for CycloneDX / SPDX SBOMs and signature attestation. Choice affects PS.3.2 implementation depth.

open_questions.question: Which model registry / approval tooling does PrivateBox use?
candidate_answers: Custom internal registry with manual approval workflow
open_questions.decision_owner: AI Security Lead + Head of AI
open_questions.notes: 218A explicitly requires controlled model registry. Tooling choice affects every model-related 218A overlay item.

open_questions.question: What is PrivateBox's AI red-team cadence?
candidate_answers: Annual external red-team + continuous internal regression
Triggered by significant model change only
open_questions.notes: 218A overlay emphasises adversarial testing. Cadence affects MEASURE 2.7 evidence depth.

open_questions.question: What is the documented workflow for approving a new third-party / foundation model?
candidate_answers: Pre-sales + engineering + security + legal joint review (full)
open_questions.notes: GOV 6.1 + 218A third-party model governance are the highest-leverage AI supply-chain controls. Workflow rigour affects every downstream model decision.

requirements.title: Legal and regulatory AI requirements understood and documented
requirements.requirement: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: PB-owned with customer input. AI legal and obligations register covering contract clauses, model / data / IP / privacy duties; reviewed before releases and major customer deployments.
requirements.failure_modes: Assuming on-prem removes compliance analysis.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox is designed with documented AI legal and contractual controls aligned to NIST AI RMF GOV 1.1.

crosswalk.note: AIMS context (Clause 4.1) parallels AI RMF GOV 1.1 legal / regulatory understanding.

requirements.title: Trustworthy-AI characteristics integrated into policies
requirements.requirement: Trustworthy AI characteristics are integrated into organisational policies and practices.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI Policy set covering security, reliability, privacy, transparency, human oversight. SDLC gates enforce trustworthy-AI characteristics at design and release.
documented_evidence: AI Policy set (security / reliability / privacy / transparency / oversight)
SDLC gate checklist mapped to trustworthy-AI characteristics
requirements.cadence: Annual policy review + per release gate
requirements.failure_modes: Values statement with no operational checks.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox implements documented controls aligned to NIST AI RMF trustworthy-AI characteristics.

crosswalk.note: AIMS AI Policy (Clause 5.2) operationalises trustworthy-AI characteristics.

requirements.title: Risk-management activity level matches organisational risk tolerance
requirements.requirement: Processes are in place to determine the activity level of risk-management based on organisational risk tolerance.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI risk-tiering standard with low / medium / high tiers. Healthcare, HR, legal, autonomous-agent features escalate to high-risk treatment.
requirements.cadence: Per feature design + annual rubric review
requirements.failure_modes: Identical treatment for trivial and sensitive uses.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses risk-proportionate AI governance and testing.

crosswalk.note: AIMS AI risk methodology covers tiered treatment.

requirements.title: Transparent AI risk-management process
requirements.requirement: The risk-management process and its outcomes are established through transparent policies, procedures and other controls.
requirements.pb_interpretation: End-to-end AI risk procedure with stages, approvers, required artefacts, escalation, acceptance authority. Consistent use across releases.
requirements.failure_modes: Informal approvals in chat.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox is managed through documented AI governance procedures.

crosswalk.note: AIMS risk treatment / SoA pattern.

requirements.title: Ongoing monitoring and periodic review planned with roles defined
requirements.requirement: Ongoing monitoring and periodic review of the risk-management process and its outcomes are planned, with organisational roles and responsibilities clearly defined.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Review calendar and role map. Annual policy review; quarterly risk review; defined runtime review cadence. Separate optional-support obligations for customer-deployed instances.
requirements.failure_modes: No cadence after deployment.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports periodic review and lifecycle governance.

crosswalk.note: AIMS management review pattern.

requirements.title: AI systems inventoried according to risk priorities
requirements.requirement: Mechanisms are in place to inventory AI systems and are resourced according to organisational risk priorities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI system and model inventory — approved-models list, feature inventory, deployment inventory, connector inventory, customer-instance log, optional-telemetry / support flags.
requirements.failure_modes: No record of which model / version is where.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains a controlled inventory of AI components and approved models.

crosswalk.note: AIMS resource documentation and data-resource inventory.
Asset inventory pattern.

requirements.title: Safe decommissioning procedures exist
requirements.requirement: Processes and procedures are in place for decommissioning and phasing out AI systems safely and in a manner that does not increase risks or decrease the organisation's trustworthiness.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI decommissioning SOP — archive period, model / data retention, credential revocation, rollback disablement, hardware-wipe responsibilities, support termination.
requirements.failure_modes: Abandoned models, tokens, knowledge stores.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox includes documented retirement and decommissioning practices.

requirements.title: Roles, responsibilities and communication lines documented
requirements.requirement: Roles, responsibilities and lines of communication related to AI risk-management are documented and clear to those individuals and teams throughout the organisation.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI governance RACI assigning product, security, legal, ops, support, model owner, release approver, incident owner, customer contact.
requirements.failure_modes: Everyone owns it.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox assigns clear accountability for AI risks and controls.

crosswalk.note: AIMS roles, responsibilities and authorities.

requirements.title: AI risk-management training for personnel and partners
requirements.requirement: The organisation's personnel and partners receive AI risk-management training to enable them to perform their duties and responsibilities consistent with related policies and procedures.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Role-based AI training — security, prompt-injection, model misuse, support-access, customer- safe deployment training with refresher cycle.
requirements.failure_modes: Once-off awareness only.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains role-based AI security and governance training.

crosswalk.note: AIMS competence.

requirements.title: Executive leadership takes AI risk responsibility
requirements.requirement: Executive leadership of the organisation takes responsibility for decisions about risks associated with AI system development and deployment.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Executive risk acceptance and launch approval. Defined approver authority; leadership sign-off for major models / features.
documented_evidence: Board / Exco minutes referencing AI launches
requirements.failure_modes: Unowned residual risk.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox subjects material AI risks to executive oversight.

crosswalk.note: King V Principle 1 (Leadership).

requirements.title: Diverse teams inform AI risk decisions
requirements.requirement: Decision-making related to mapping, measuring and managing AI risks throughout the lifecycle is informed by a diverse team.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Review panels with security, domain, legal, operations, UX, customer-facing perspectives. Affected-user representation where relevant.
requirements.failure_modes: Only engineers review AI risk.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses interdisciplinary review in AI risk decisions.

requirements.title: Human-AI configuration and oversight roles defined
requirements.requirement: Policies and procedures are in place to define and differentiate roles and responsibilities for human-AI configurations and oversight of AI systems.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Human-oversight design standard — approval points, override rights, escalation paths, no-autonomous-final-decision rules where needed.
requirements.failure_modes: Humans nominal but not empowered.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines human oversight and override mechanisms where appropriate.

crosswalk.note: AIMS human oversight.
EU AI Act human oversight obligation.

requirements.title: Critical-thinking and safety-first culture
requirements.requirement: Organisational policies and practices are in place to foster a critical-thinking and safety-first mindset in the design, development, deployment and uses of AI systems to minimise potential negative impacts.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Pre-mortems, red-team reviews, "stop ship" authority, no-blame incident learning. Safety concerns block release when needed.
requirements.failure_modes: Schedule pressure overrides risk concerns.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox emphasises safety-first design and release discipline.

requirements.title: AI risks and impacts documented and communicated
requirements.requirement: Organisational teams document the risks and potential impacts of the AI technology they design, develop, deploy, evaluate and use, and they communicate about the impacts more broadly.
requirements.pb_interpretation: System / model card + deployment guidance covering intended use, prohibited use, limitations, residual risk, evaluation summary, support assumptions.
requirements.failure_modes: Outdated marketing copy.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox publishes evidence-backed guidance on intended use and limitations.

crosswalk.note: AIMS system characteristics / documentation.

requirements.title: AI testing, incident identification, information sharing
requirements.requirement: Organisational practices are in place to enable AI testing, identification of incidents and information sharing.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI incident and testing programme — red-team cadence, issue intake, triage, severity, notification, learning loop.
requirements.failure_modes: Security incidents handled but behavioural incidents ignored.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains AI-focused testing and incident response procedures.

requirements.title: External feedback on individual and societal impacts collected
requirements.requirement: Organisational policies and practices are in place to collect, consider, prioritise and integrate feedback from those external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system regarding the potential individual and societal impacts related to AI risks.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Formal external feedback channels — support tickets, procurement feedback, pilot reviews, issue reporting, harmful-output reporting.
requirements.failure_modes: No route for customers to appeal or report misuse.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox captures and reviews customer feedback on AI risk and performance.

requirements.title: Adjudicated feedback incorporated regularly
requirements.requirement: Mechanisms are established to enable AI actors to regularly incorporate adjudicated feedback from relevant AI actors into system design and implementation.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Feedback-to-change procedure — classify feedback, adjudicate, convert to releases / docs / config changes, communicate outcomes.
requirements.failure_modes: Feedback collected but not actioned.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox incorporates validated customer and stakeholder feedback into product controls.

requirements.title: Third-party AI / data / IP / supply-chain risk policies
requirements.requirement: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply-chain issues.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Third-party model / data / component governance — vet dependencies; require provenance; review licences; restrict unapproved models; maintain approved-source list.
requirements.failure_modes: Open-source model adoption without provenance or licence review.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox governs third-party models, software and data through documented due diligence.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility allocation.

requirements.title: Contingency for high-risk third-party AI failures
requirements.requirement: Contingency processes are in place to handle failures or incidents in third-party data or AI systems deemed to be high-risk.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Supplier failure fallback plan — kill switch, rollback, local disablement, alternate model path, notification triggers.
requirements.failure_modes: No action path when a model or library is withdrawn or compromised.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains contingency procedures for material third-party AI dependencies.

requirements.title: Intended purpose and deployment context documented
requirements.requirement: Intended purposes, potentially beneficial uses, context-specific laws, norms and expectations, and prospective settings in which the AI system will be deployed are understood and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Intended-use and deployment-context register per customer deployment — use cases, prohibited uses, user groups, deployment setting, data classes, norms.
documented_evidence: Use-case form + solution design record per deployment
requirements.failure_modes: One generic use case for all clients.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents intended use, limitations and deployment context.

crosswalk.note: AIMS context + intended-use enforcement.

requirements.title: Interdisciplinary AI actors for context setting
requirements.requirement: Inter-disciplinary AI actors, competencies, skills and capacities for establishing context reflect demographic diversity and broad domain and user experience expertise.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Contextual review participants list — domain, security, legal, product, deployment ops, client stakeholders.
requirements.failure_modes: Deployment designed only by sales and engineering.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses cross-functional context-setting before deployment.

requirements.title: Mission and AI goals documented
requirements.requirement: The organisation's mission and relevant goals for AI technology are understood and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI product purpose statement — why the feature exists; what business / security / privacy value it should create; what it will not do.
requirements.failure_modes: Adding AI features without clear purpose.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox ties AI capabilities to documented product and customer objectives.

requirements.title: Business value or business-use context defined
requirements.requirement: The business value or business-use context is clearly defined or — in the case of assessing existing AI systems — re-evaluated.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Value-versus-risk review — quantify value, operational benefit, residual risk, non-AI alternatives. Re-evaluation after scope changes.
requirements.failure_modes: "AI because AI."
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox evaluates AI use cases against business value and risk.

requirements.title: Organisational risk tolerances determined and documented
requirements.requirement: Organisational risk tolerances are determined and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Customer-specific AI risk tolerance statement — acceptable error classes, approved domains, prohibited automation, incident thresholds.
requirements.failure_modes: Vendor assumptions override client tolerance.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox can be configured against documented customer risk tolerances.

requirements.title: System requirements account for socio-technical risk
requirements.requirement: System requirements (e.g., "the system shall respect the privacy of its users") are elicited from and understood by relevant AI actors. Design decisions take socio-technical implications into account to address AI risks.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Requirements log covering privacy, access control, retention, auditability, oversight, safe response, domain boundaries, language handling.
requirements.failure_modes: Only functional requirements captured.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox incorporates security, oversight and operational requirements into AI design.

requirements.title: Tasks and methods documented
requirements.requirement: The specific tasks and methods used to implement the tasks that the AI system will support are defined (e.g., classifiers, generative models, recommenders).
requirements.pb_interpretation: Model-function classification — generative Q&A, summarisation, extraction, search-grounded response, agent action, classification.
requirements.failure_modes: Architecture ambiguity.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents the AI methods and tasks used for each feature.

requirements.title: Knowledge limits and human oversight documented
requirements.requirement: Information about the AI system's knowledge limits and how system output may be utilised and overseen by humans is documented. Documentation provides sufficient information to assist relevant AI actors when making decisions and taking subsequent actions.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Limitations and oversight register — known failure modes, unsupported domains, confidence / uncertainty cues, mandatory human review points.
documented_evidence: Model / system card + customer guide + UI notices
requirements.failure_modes: Oversold capabilities.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents model limitations and human review requirements.

crosswalk.note: AIMS human oversight.

requirements.title: Scientific integrity and TEVV considerations documented
requirements.requirement: Scientific integrity and TEVV considerations are identified and documented, including those related to experimental design, data collection and selection, system trustworthiness and construct validation.
