requirements.pb_interpretation: Evaluation design protocol — datasets, acceptance criteria, representativeness, baseline comparisons, reproducibility.
requirements.failure_modes: Ad-hoc demos standing in for validation.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses documented evaluation protocols for AI performance and risk testing.

crosswalk.note: AIMS V&V and robustness.

requirements.title: Potential benefits documented
requirements.requirement: Potential benefits of intended AI system functionality and performance are examined and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Benefit statement per feature — measurable positive outcomes and success metrics.
requirements.failure_modes: Only harms documented.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox records expected benefits and success criteria for AI features.

requirements.title: Potential costs and non-monetary costs documented
requirements.requirement: Potential costs, including non-monetary costs, which result from expected or realised AI errors or system functionality and trustworthiness are examined and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Harm and cost analysis — confidentiality exposure, wrong-answer cost, workflow disruption, user over-reliance, reputational risk.
requirements.failure_modes: Ignoring non-financial harms.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox assesses both operational benefit and downside risk of AI use.

requirements.title: Targeted application scope documented
requirements.requirement: Targeted application scope is specified and documented based on the system's capability, established context and AI system categorisation.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Scope boundary record — in-scope users, data sources, actions, integrations, geographies, languages, prohibited extensions.
requirements.failure_modes: Scope creep through connectors or agent tools.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines and constrains intended application scope.

requirements.title: Operator and practitioner proficiency processes defined
requirements.requirement: Processes for operator and practitioner proficiency with AI system performance and trustworthiness — and relevant technical standards and certifications — are defined, assessed and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Operator competency standard — admin training, support runbooks, customer enablement, escalation criteria.
requirements.failure_modes: Powerful admin features without competency checks.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports trained administration and customer enablement.

requirements.title: Human oversight processes defined
requirements.requirement: Processes for human oversight, in accordance with organisational policies from GOVERN function, are defined, assessed and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Oversight workflow — approval gates, fallback to search / manual process, escalation if unsafe / low confidence.
documented_evidence: Oversight SOP + UX design + audit logs
requirements.failure_modes: Human review theoretically required but practically bypassed.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox includes documented human oversight and fallback procedures.

crosswalk.note: AIMS human oversight.

requirements.title: Component risks including third-party mapped
requirements.requirement: Approaches for mapping AI technology and legal risks of its components — including third-party AI technologies — are in place.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Component risk map — models, embedding engines, vector stores, open-source packages, licences, datasets, update channels.
documented_evidence: Component register + legal review + SBOM / MBOM
requirements.failure_modes: Hidden components in packaged solutions.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maps technical, legal and supply-chain risks across AI components.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility allocation.

requirements.title: Internal risk controls for components documented
requirements.requirement: Internal risk controls for components of the AI system, including third-party AI technologies, are identified and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Component control matrix — controls for vendor models, repository sources, update approvals, sandboxing, data isolation.
requirements.failure_modes: Generic controls with no component focus.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents control coverage for each significant AI component.

requirements.title: Likelihood and magnitude of impacts identified
requirements.requirement: Likelihood and magnitude of each identified impact (both potentially beneficial and harmful) based on expected use, past uses of AI systems in similar contexts, public incident reports, feedback from those external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system or other data are identified and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI impact assessment — score confidentiality, integrity, availability, harmful output, misrouting, abuse, productivity benefit.
requirements.failure_modes: Purely qualitative, unranked impact statements.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses documented impact assessment before material AI deployments.

crosswalk.note: AIMS AI System Impact Assessment (AISIA).

requirements.title: Stakeholder engagement and impact feedback practices
requirements.requirement: Practices and personnel for supporting regular engagement with relevant AI actors and integrating feedback about positive, negative and unanticipated impacts are in place and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Stakeholder-impact review mechanism — post-go-live reviews, adverse-event intake, procurement feedback loop.
requirements.failure_modes: No field-learning loop.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains recurring stakeholder review for AI impacts.

requirements.title: Risk metrics selected and unmeasured risks documented
requirements.requirement: Approaches and metrics for measurement of AI risks enumerated during the MAP function are selected for implementation, starting with the most significant AI risks.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Metric selection register — quality, security, privacy, hallucination / confabulation, abuse, latency, drift, override rates. Record what is not measurable.
requirements.failure_modes: Metrics only for accuracy.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines documented metrics for material AI risks and records current measurement gaps.

requirements.title: Metrics and control effectiveness regularly assessed
requirements.requirement: Appropriateness of AI metrics and effectiveness of existing controls are regularly assessed and updated, including reports of errors and potential impacts.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Metric review cycle — quarterly metric review, post-incident metric updates, client-specific tuning where needed.
requirements.failure_modes: Frozen benchmark set.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox reviews measurement methods and control effectiveness on a recurring basis.

requirements.title: Independent assessors involved
requirements.requirement: Internal experts who did not serve as front-line developers for the system and / or independent assessors are involved in regular assessments and updates. Domain experts, users, AI actors external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system, and affected communities are consulted in support of assessments as necessary per organisational risk tolerance.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Independent assurance review — security review, peer review outside dev team, red team, domain reviewer. At least one independent review per major release.
requirements.failure_modes: Developers self-score.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox includes independent review in AI testing and assurance.

requirements.title: TEVV test sets, metrics and tools documented
requirements.requirement: Test sets, metrics and details about the tools used during TEVV are documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: TEVV documentation standard — version test sets; record tools, prompts, seeds, configs, expected outcomes. Reproducible evaluations.
requirements.failure_modes: Missing dataset lineage.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains traceable test and evaluation records.

requirements.title: Human-subject evaluations meet requirements
requirements.requirement: Evaluations involving human subjects meet applicable requirements (including human subject protection) and are representative of the relevant population.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Human-subject testing protocol where used — consent, ethics, sample representativeness, safe data handling. Often not applicable; document if not.
documented_evidence: Test protocol + consent forms where applicable
requirements.failure_modes: Informal customer testing without safeguards.
requirements.external_safe_claim: Where human-subject testing is used, PrivateBox applies a documented protocol.

requirements.title: Performance measured under deployment-like conditions
requirements.requirement: AI system performance or assurance criteria are measured qualitatively or quantitatively and demonstrated for conditions similar to deployment setting(s). Measures are documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Environment-realistic evaluation — test with local documents, likely prompt styles, adversarial inputs, role access, retrieval context.
requirements.failure_modes: Only lab benchmarks.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox evaluates AI behaviour under deployment-relevant conditions.

requirements.title: Production monitoring of functionality and behaviour
requirements.requirement: The functionality and behaviour of the AI system and its components — as identified in the MAP function — are monitored when in production.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Runtime monitoring plan — log errors, refusals, overrides, tool failures, drift indicators, abuse signals; respect privacy constraints.
requirements.failure_modes: No feedback from production.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports monitored AI behaviour after go-live, subject to agreed operational scope.

crosswalk.note: AIMS monitoring and KPI dashboard.

requirements.title: System shown valid and reliable; generalisation limits documented
requirements.requirement: The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be valid and reliable. Limitations of the generalisability beyond the conditions under which the technology was developed are documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Validity and reliability dossier — benchmark results, scenario tests, failure rates, known limits, non-generalisation statement.
requirements.failure_modes: No documented limitations.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents validation results and known generalisation limits.

requirements.title: Safety risks regularly evaluated; fail-safe behaviour exists
requirements.requirement: AI system is evaluated regularly for safety risks — as identified in the MAP function. The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be safe, its residual negative risk does not exceed the risk tolerance, and it can fail safely, particularly if made to operate beyond its knowledge limits. Safety metrics reflect system reliability and robustness, real-time monitoring and response times for AI system failures.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Safety evaluation and fail-safe design — refusal patterns, escalation, human review, disablement on unsafe tool calls, safe fallback.
requirements.failure_modes: Unsafe answers degrade gracefully only in theory.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox implements documented safeguards and fail-safe responses for identified safety risks.

requirements.title: AI security and resilience evaluated
requirements.requirement: AI system security and resilience — as identified in the MAP function — are evaluated and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI security test programme — prompt-injection testing, model abuse scenarios, secret-leakage tests, authz bypass, malicious-document / retrieval tests.
requirements.failure_modes: Traditional appsec only, no AI-specific testing.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox evaluates AI-specific security and resilience risks before and after release.

crosswalk.note: AIMS V&V and robustness with adversarial testing.

requirements.title: Transparency and accountability risks examined
requirements.requirement: Risks associated with transparency and accountability — as identified in the MAP function — are examined and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Transparency review — system card, logs proving operator actions, disclosure text, role accountability.
requirements.failure_modes: Cannot explain who approved what.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains traceable records supporting accountability and customer transparency.

requirements.title: Model explained and contextually interpreted
requirements.requirement: The AI model is explained, validated and documented, and AI system output is interpreted within its context — as identified in the MAP function — to inform responsible use and governance.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Explanation and contextual interpretation guidance — model family, RAG grounding, limits, human interpretation steps.
requirements.failure_modes: End users treat outputs as authoritative.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox provides contextual documentation to support responsible interpretation of outputs.

requirements.title: Privacy risk examined
requirements.requirement: Privacy risk of the AI system — as identified in the MAP function — is examined and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI privacy assessment — sensitive-data exposure, retention, prompting leakage, logs, admin access, optional telemetry.
requirements.failure_modes: Logs capture more than intended.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox assesses and documents privacy risks for each material AI use case.

crosswalk.note: POPIA processing limitation + s 71 automated decisions.

requirements.title: Fairness and bias evaluated
requirements.requirement: Fairness and bias — as identified in the MAP function — are evaluated and results are documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Fairness review — identify protected or sensitive contexts; evaluate disparities where relevant; document non-applicability decision per deployment if not.
requirements.failure_modes: Assuming enterprise-assistant use means bias is irrelevant.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox evaluates fairness risk where the use case makes it material.

crosswalk.note: AIMS bias methodology.

requirements.title: Environmental impact and sustainability assessed
requirements.requirement: Environmental impact and sustainability of AI model training and management activities — as identified in the MAP function — are assessed and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Compute and footprint record — model size, hardware load, optimisation, inference footprint for local deployments.
requirements.failure_modes: Ignoring local compute burden for clients.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents material compute and resource considerations for deployment.

requirements.title: TEVV metric / process effectiveness reviewed
requirements.requirement: Effectiveness of the employed TEVV metrics and processes in the MEASURE function are evaluated and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Evaluation-of-evaluation review — false assurance, missing scenarios, benchmark drift, red-team coverage gaps.
requirements.failure_modes: Treating benchmark suite as final truth.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox reviews and improves its own evaluation methods over time.

requirements.title: Tracking emergent and unanticipated risks
requirements.requirement: Approaches, personnel and documentation are in place to regularly identify and track existing, unanticipated and emergent AI risks based on factors such as intended and actual performance in deployed contexts.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Emergent-risk monitoring — incidents, user reports, model updates, threat intel, misuse patterns.
requirements.failure_modes: Static pre-launch-only risk list.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox tracks known and emergent AI risks throughout the lifecycle.

requirements.title: Risk tracking where measurement is immature
requirements.requirement: Risk tracking approaches are considered for settings where AI risks are difficult to assess using currently available measurement techniques or where metrics are not yet available.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Non-quantifiable risk log — uncertain risks, proxy indicators, monitoring plans, decision rationale.
requirements.failure_modes: "Can't measure" becomes "not managed."
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents proxy measures and monitoring plans for hard-to-measure AI risks.

requirements.title: Feedback processes enable end-user reporting and appeals
requirements.requirement: Feedback processes for end users and impacted communities to report problems and appeal system outcomes are established and integrated into AI system evaluation metrics.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Reporting and appeal channel — in-product or help-desk route; severity path; human review route; response SLA.
requirements.failure_modes: No appeal or dispute route.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports customer reporting and escalation channels for AI issues.

requirements.title: Measurement tied to deployment context and expert input
requirements.requirement: Measurement approaches for identifying AI risks are connected to deployment context(s) and informed through consultation with domain experts and other end users.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Context-specific evaluation profile — tailor test sets and thresholds per sector and workflow.
requirements.failure_modes: Generic evals for specialised workflows.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox can tailor testing to deployment-specific contexts and expert input.

requirements.title: Trustworthiness results validated with stakeholders
requirements.requirement: Measurement results regarding AI system trustworthiness in deployment context(s) and across the AI lifecycle are informed by input from domain experts and relevant AI actors to validate whether the system is performing consistently as intended. Results are documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Stakeholder validation review — share results, capture objections, approve or amend interpretation.
requirements.failure_modes: Metrics reviewed without user-context sanity check.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox validates material evaluation results with relevant stakeholders where appropriate.

requirements.title: Performance changes identified and documented
requirements.requirement: Measurable performance improvements or declines based on consultations with relevant AI actors, including affected communities, and field data about context-relevant risks and trustworthiness characteristics are identified and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Post-deployment improvement register — regressions, drift, newly observed harms, corrective actions.
requirements.failure_modes: No documented learning from production.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents performance changes and improvement actions across the AI lifecycle.

requirements.title: Go/no-go decision on whether to proceed
requirements.requirement: A determination is made as to whether the AI system achieves its intended purpose and stated objectives and whether its development or deployment should proceed.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI go / no-go gate — require risk, validation, security, documentation, support readiness before release or deployment.
requirements.failure_modes: Commercial pressure bypasses evidence.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses documented go/no-go criteria for material AI releases.

crosswalk.note: AIMS pre-deployment approval gate.

requirements.title: Risk treatment prioritised by impact and resources
requirements.requirement: Treatment of documented AI risks is prioritised based on impact, likelihood, available resources or methods.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI risk treatment plan — rank findings; set deadlines and owners; distinguish accept / mitigate / avoid / transfer.
requirements.failure_modes: Long risk list with no prioritisation.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox prioritises AI risk treatment using documented criteria.

requirements.title: High-priority AI risk responses planned
requirements.requirement: Responses to the AI risks deemed high priority — as identified by the MAP function — are developed, planned and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: High-risk mitigation plans — extra controls for unsafe outputs, privileged actions, sensitive documents, third-party models.
requirements.failure_modes: Risks documented but not engineered out.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox incorporates targeted mitigations for high-priority AI risks.

requirements.title: Residual negative risks documented for users
requirements.requirement: Negative residual risks (defined as the sum of all unmitigated risks) to both downstream acquirers of AI systems and end users are documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Residual-risk statement — remaining hallucination, misuse, and operational risks; safe-use conditions and customer dependencies.
documented_evidence: Residual risk register + customer handoff note
requirements.failure_modes: Implying zero-risk solution.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents residual risks and customer operating assumptions.

requirements.title: Non-AI alternatives considered
requirements.requirement: Resources required to manage AI risks are taken into account, along with viable non-AI alternative systems, approaches or methods, to reduce the magnitude or likelihood of potential impacts.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Alternative-method review — non-AI workflow, search-only mode, or manual fallback where preferable.
requirements.failure_modes: Ignoring simpler safer alternatives.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox evaluates AI use against viable non-AI alternatives and fallbacks.

requirements.title: Mechanisms sustain AI system value
requirements.requirement: Mechanisms are in place and applied to sustain the value of deployed AI systems.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Lifecycle maintenance plan — update schedules, model / version support, retraining / fine-tuning criteria, knowledge refresh, support policy.
requirements.failure_modes: Value decays after installation.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines lifecycle maintenance processes for sustained AI system value.

requirements.title: Procedures respond to unknown risk
requirements.requirement: Procedures are followed to respond to and recover from a previously unknown risk when it is identified.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Novel-risk response plan — emergency disablement, temporary guardrails, hotfix, customer communication, root cause.
requirements.failure_modes: No path for novel AI failure.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains procedures to respond to newly observed AI risks.

requirements.title: Mechanisms supersede, disengage or deactivate AI systems
requirements.requirement: Mechanisms are in place and applied, and responsibilities are assigned and understood, to supersede, disengage or deactivate AI systems that demonstrate performance or outcomes inconsistent with intended use.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Kill switch and downgrade path — manual disable, revert to search / manual process, feature flag off, customer emergency procedure.
requirements.failure_modes: No graceful way to turn it off.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox includes documented disengagement and fallback procedures.

requirements.title: Third-party AI risks monitored
requirements.requirement: AI risks and benefits from third-party resources are regularly monitored, and risk controls are applied and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Third-party monitoring programme — upstream models, libraries, vulnerability feeds, licence changes, provider notices.
requirements.failure_modes: Upstream changes unnoticed.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox actively monitors material third-party AI dependencies and related controls.

requirements.title: Pre-trained models monitored
requirements.requirement: Pre-trained models which are used for development are monitored as part of AI system regular monitoring and maintenance.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Pretrained-model surveillance — track model versions, approvals, benchmarks, known limitations, retirement triggers.
requirements.failure_modes: Shadow model swaps.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox manages approved pre-trained models through a controlled inventory and review process.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility for third-party models.

requirements.title: Post-deployment monitoring plans
requirements.requirement: Post-deployment AI system monitoring plans are implemented, including mechanisms for capturing and evaluating input from users and other relevant AI actors, appeal and override, decommissioning, incident response, recovery and change management.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Post-deployment AI monitoring plan — who monitors, what is logged, how overrides work, how changes are reviewed, how incidents are handled.
requirements.failure_modes: Handoff ends at installation.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports post-deployment monitoring and change governance through documented procedures.

requirements.title: Continual improvement integrated into updates
requirements.requirement: Measurable activities for continual improvements are integrated into AI system updates and include regular engagement with interested parties, including relevant AI actors.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Continuous-improvement cycle — quarterly backlog from incidents, support feedback, benchmark drift, security findings.
requirements.failure_modes: Updates driven only by features.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses monitoring and stakeholder feedback to drive continual AI control improvement.

requirements.title: Incidents communicated with tracking and recovery
requirements.requirement: Incidents and errors are communicated to relevant AI actors, including affected communities. Processes for tracking, responding to and recovering from incidents and errors are followed and documented.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI incident communication plan — severity, customer notice rules, internal escalation, RCA, corrective actions, shared-responsibility boundaries.
requirements.failure_modes: Silent handling of harmful outputs or behavioural incidents.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents AI incident handling and communication procedures.

crosswalk.note: AIMS incident communication.
POPIA security safeguards including s 22 breach notification.

requirements.title: Security requirements for development infrastructure
requirements.requirement: Define security requirements for software development infrastructure and processes.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Secure-SDLC requirements for repos, CI/CD, secrets, agents, admin tooling.
requirements.failure_modes: Undocumented tooling assumptions.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a documented secure development standard.

crosswalk.note: ISMS secure development lifecycle.

requirements.title: Security requirements for organization-developed software
requirements.requirement: Define security requirements for organisation-developed software.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Product security requirements — logging, authz, data isolation, update integrity, safe defaults.
requirements.failure_modes: Missing non-functional security requirements.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines product security requirements throughout the SDLC.

requirements.title: Communicate requirements to suppliers
requirements.requirement: Communicate security requirements to third-party suppliers and services.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Contract and assess model, library, hosting, update, support, and tooling suppliers.
requirements.failure_modes: Inherited weakness from unmanaged vendors.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox extends security expectations to relevant third parties.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility.

requirements.title: Define SDLC roles and responsibilities
requirements.requirement: Define and maintain SDLC roles and responsibilities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Repo owners, security approvers, release approvers, incident lead, supplier owner, support owner.
requirements.failure_modes: Unclear ownership.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox assigns clear SDLC and security responsibilities.

requirements.title: Role-based training
requirements.requirement: Provide role-based training to personnel with security responsibilities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Train devs, reviewers, DevOps, support, customer-facing staff on AI-secure development.
requirements.failure_modes: Generic awareness only.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains role-based security training.

requirements.title: Management commitment and messaging
requirements.requirement: Obtain upper management or authorising official commitment to secure development.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Executive sponsorship for secure development and release discipline.
requirements.failure_modes: Security seen as optional.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's secure development programme is management-backed.

requirements.title: Specify required tools and integration
requirements.requirement: Specify the security-related tools and tool integrations that the organisation requires.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Approved SDLC toolchain and integration map.
requirements.failure_modes: Shadow tools.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses an approved and documented secure toolchain.

requirements.title: Securely deploy and operate tools
requirements.requirement: Follow recommended security practices to deploy, operate, and maintain tools and toolchains.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Harden CI/CD, code repos, issue trackers, package registries.
requirements.failure_modes: CI pipeline takeover.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox secures and maintains its development toolchain.

crosswalk.note: ISMS separation of development / test / production.

requirements.title: Configure tools to generate artefacts
requirements.requirement: Configure tools to generate artefacts supporting secure development.
requirements.pb_interpretation: CI/CD emits logs, build records, approvals, scan outputs, signatures.
requirements.failure_modes: No audit trail.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox retains artefacts demonstrating secure development controls.

requirements.title: Define criteria for security checks
requirements.requirement: Define criteria for software security checks and track them.
requirements.pb_interpretation: What scans, tests, reviews, and gates are required by risk tier.
requirements.failure_modes: Inconsistent checks.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies defined release gates and security checks.

requirements.title: Exception handling
requirements.requirement: Implement processes, mechanisms, etc., to gather and safeguard the necessary information in support of the criteria.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Formal exception workflow with expiry, rationale, compensating controls.
requirements.failure_modes: Permanent waivers.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox manages exceptions through a documented approval process.

requirements.title: Protect development environments
requirements.requirement: Separate and protect each environment involved in software development.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Segregate dev / test / prod; restrict admin; monitor CI/CD and build hosts.
requirements.failure_modes: Shared admin credentials.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox protects development environments against unauthorized access and tampering.

crosswalk.note: ISMS separation of dev / test / prod.

requirements.title: Secure development endpoints
requirements.requirement: Secure and harden development endpoints.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Harden engineer workstations, support laptops, build runners.
requirements.failure_modes: Privileged unmanaged laptops.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox hardens endpoints used for development and support.

requirements.title: Store code with least privilege
requirements.requirement: Store all forms of code — including source code, executable code and configuration-as-code — based on the principle of least privilege so that only authorised personnel, tools, services, etc., have access.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Protect source, config-as-code, prompts, templates, evaluation assets, deployment scripts.
requirements.failure_modes: Over-broad write access.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox stores code and configuration under least-privilege controls.

requirements.title: Release integrity verification info available to acquirers
requirements.requirement: Make software integrity verification information available to acquirers.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Provide signatures, hashes, release notes, verification instructions.
requirements.failure_modes: Unverifiable updates.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports verification of delivered release integrity.

requirements.title: Securely archive release files
requirements.requirement: Securely archive the necessary files and supporting data to be retained for each software release.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Archive installers, manifests, configs, signatures, release approvals.
requirements.failure_modes: Cannot reconstruct what was shipped.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox archives release artefacts for traceability and incident response.

requirements.title: Maintain provenance data for release components (SBOM)
requirements.requirement: Collect, safeguard, maintain and share provenance data for all components of each software release (e.g., in a software bill of materials [SBOM]).
requirements.pb_interpretation: SBOM, build provenance, dependency records, model provenance where relevant.
requirements.failure_modes: No provenance for third-party components.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains provenance data for software components and releases.

requirements.title: Use risk modelling
requirements.requirement: Use forms of risk modelling — such as threat modelling, attack modelling or attack-surface mapping — to help assess the security risk for the software.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Threat model AI workflows, admin planes, RAG, support access, update channel.
requirements.failure_modes: Missing model-specific attack paths.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses documented threat modelling and risk analysis.

requirements.title: Track security requirements and design decisions
requirements.requirement: Track and maintain the software's security requirements, risks and design decisions.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Design-decision log, accepted risks, approved exceptions.
requirements.failure_modes: No rationale history.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains design and risk traceability.

requirements.title: Build in standard security features
requirements.requirement: Where appropriate, build in support for using standardised security features and services.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use standard authn / authz, logging, secrets, encryption, identity integration.
requirements.failure_modes: Custom security where standard controls exist.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses standard security services and patterns wherever feasible.

requirements.title: Review design for compliance
requirements.requirement: Review the software design to verify compliance with security requirements and risk information.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Design review before build / release. Unresolved design issues block progression.
documented_evidence: Signed design review + issue tracker items
requirements.failure_modes: Reviews after build only.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox verifies design against defined security requirements before release.

requirements.title: Acquire and maintain well-secured third-party components
requirements.requirement: Acquire and maintain well-secured software components from commercial, open-source and other third-party developers for use by the organisation's software.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Approved dependency process for packages, models, libraries, container bases.
requirements.failure_modes: Unmanaged open-source intake.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses controlled intake for third-party components.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility allocation.

requirements.title: Maintain provenance for components
requirements.requirement: Create and maintain provenance data for components.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Preserve source, version, licence, digest, origin, build metadata.
requirements.failure_modes: Incomplete component metadata.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox keeps provenance records for key software components.

requirements.title: Verify component integrity
requirements.requirement: Verify the integrity of all software components.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Verify hashes / signatures of dependencies and releases.
requirements.failure_modes: Blind trust in upstream packages / models.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox verifies component integrity before use and release.

requirements.title: Review human-readable third-party software
requirements.requirement: Review human-readable third-party software to confirm it meets security requirements.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Source review for critical libraries, agent tools, deployment scripts.
requirements.failure_modes: Critical component accepted without review.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies risk-based review to material third-party code.

requirements.title: Reuse well-secured internal software
requirements.requirement: Reuse existing, well-secured software components from internal sources where feasible.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Use approved internal components rather than ad hoc reinvention.
requirements.failure_modes: Duplicate insecure utilities.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox reuses approved internal components to reduce avoidable risk.

requirements.title: Follow secure coding practices
requirements.requirement: Follow secure coding practices.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Coding standards for auth, input handling, secret use, logging, file parsing, command execution.
requirements.failure_modes: Secure coding guidance not enforced.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox follows documented secure coding practices.

crosswalk.note: ISMS secure coding.

requirements.title: Detect and prevent coding defects
requirements.requirement: Detect and prevent coding defects through automated tooling.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Linters, secret scanning, code quality and security tooling in PR workflow.
requirements.failure_modes: Manual review only.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses automated checks to support secure coding.

requirements.title: Configure compile / build for executable security
requirements.requirement: Configure compilation, interpreter and build processes to improve executable security.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Harden builds, enable safer defaults, dependency locking, reproducible builds where feasible.
requirements.failure_modes: Insecure build configurations.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox hardens build and packaging processes.

requirements.title: Document build tools and settings
requirements.requirement: Determine and document appropriate build / packaging tools and settings.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Build policy for compilers, containers, packagers, signing steps, warnings-as-failures where appropriate.
requirements.failure_modes: Inconsistent build practices.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox standardises and documents secure build settings.

requirements.title: Define code review strategy
requirements.requirement: Define and use criteria for code review and analysis.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Peer review depth, SAST rules, high-risk branch protections.
requirements.failure_modes: Inconsistent review expectations.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines structured code review and analysis requirements.

requirements.title: Review human-readable code
requirements.requirement: Review human-readable code for vulnerabilities and verify compliance with security requirements.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Peer review, SAST, IaC review, prompt / agent logic review.
requirements.failure_modes: Review rubber-stamping.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox conducts documented code review and static analysis before release.

requirements.title: Define testing strategy
requirements.requirement: Define and use criteria for executable-code testing.
requirements.pb_interpretation: DAST, API tests, integration tests, security tests, environment-specific regression tests.
requirements.failure_modes: Functional tests only.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines release-testing criteria that include security-relevant scenarios.

requirements.title: Test executable code
requirements.requirement: Test executable code and verify compliance with security requirements.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Run automated and manual tests; block release on critical findings.
requirements.failure_modes: Findings waived without control.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox validates releases against documented security test criteria.

requirements.title: Define secure default settings
requirements.requirement: Define secure settings by default for software, including configuration values.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Default deny external connectivity, least privilege, telemetry off unless agreed, admin features disabled by default where sensible.
requirements.failure_modes: Insecure convenience defaults.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox ships with security-conscious default settings.

requirements.title: Implement and document secure configuration changes
requirements.requirement: Implement and document secure default configuration and changes.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Maintain config guide, hardening guide, and approved change path.
requirements.failure_modes: Undocumented configuration drift.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents secure baseline configuration and controlled changes.

requirements.title: Gather vulnerability information
requirements.requirement: Gather information from software acquirers, users and public sources on potential vulnerabilities in the software.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Monitor CVEs, provider notices, AI-specific issue feeds, customer reports, model-library security news.
requirements.failure_modes: No AI-specific feed coverage.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox continuously monitors relevant vulnerability and security reports.

requirements.title: Review code for undetected vulnerabilities
requirements.requirement: Review, analyse and / or test the organisation's code to identify or confirm the presence of previously undetected vulnerabilities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Recurring reassessment, including AI-specific tests after changes.
requirements.failure_modes: Testing only pre-release.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox performs recurring reassessment for previously undetected vulnerabilities.

requirements.title: Vulnerability disclosure and remediation policy
requirements.requirement: Have a policy that addresses vulnerability disclosure and remediation, and implement the roles, responsibilities and processes needed to support that policy.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Public / internal reporting path, response SLAs, coordination rules, remediation workflow.
requirements.failure_modes: No route for external researchers or customers.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains a structured vulnerability disclosure and remediation process.

requirements.title: Analyse vulnerabilities for remediation planning
requirements.requirement: Analyse each vulnerability sufficiently to plan remediation or other response.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Severity, exploitability, affected versions, customer exposure, compensating controls.
requirements.failure_modes: Remediation with poor scoping.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox triages vulnerabilities using documented risk criteria.

requirements.title: Plan and implement risk responses
requirements.requirement: Plan and implement risk responses for vulnerabilities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Patch, workaround, disable feature, rotate credentials, notify customers, publish advisory.
requirements.failure_modes: Fix exists but customers cannot operationalise it.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines and communicates risk responses for identified vulnerabilities.

requirements.title: Identify root causes
requirements.requirement: Identify root causes of vulnerabilities.
requirements.pb_interpretation: RCA for code, design, toolchain, model / config, supplier failure.
requirements.failure_modes: Patching symptoms only.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox performs root-cause analysis for material vulnerabilities.

requirements.title: Remediate root causes
requirements.requirement: Remediate root causes through process and tool improvements.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Update standards, training, build checks, review gates, supplier controls.
requirements.failure_modes: Repeating same class of defect.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses vulnerability learnings to strengthen its SDLC.

requirements.title: Capture lessons learned
requirements.requirement: Capture lessons learned and improve practices.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Feed lessons into engineering standards, procurement answers, customer guides.
requirements.failure_modes: Postmortem not actioned.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses structured lessons learned to improve security practices.

requirements.title: Share remediation information
requirements.requirement: Share root-cause and remediation information as appropriate.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Internal knowledge base and customer advisories when relevant.
requirements.failure_modes: Inconsistent communications.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox communicates material remediation information through defined channels.

requirements.title: Monitor software execution in dev environments
requirements.overlay_modifies: Adds AI-specific dev-environment monitoring on top of SSDF PO.5.1 — covers build runners, training / fine-tuning hosts, model APIs, weights, configs, datasets with alerting on suspicious activity.
requirements.requirement: Continuously monitor software execution performance and behaviour in development environments.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Monitor build runners, training / fine-tuning hosts, model APIs, weights, configs, datasets. Alerting on suspicious activity.
requirements.failure_modes: Model assets unmonitored in dev.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox continuously monitors environments that host AI model development assets.

requirements.title: Protect training / fine-tuning / alignment data
requirements.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF PS.1.1 (code storage least privilege) to specifically cover training, testing, fine-tuning and alignment datasets — segregation, write-access restriction, checksums, approval workflow for dataset changes.
requirements.requirement: Protect training, testing, fine-tuning and alignment data against unauthorised access and modification.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Segregate datasets, restrict write access, checksum critical sets, approval for dataset changes.
requirements.failure_modes: Silent dataset poisoning or corruption.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox protects AI development datasets with controlled access and change records.

requirements.title: Protect model weights and configuration parameters
requirements.overlay_modifies: New 218A task — adds model-weight and configuration-parameter protection on top of SSDF PS.1.1. Separate weights from datasets; least privilege; encryption / hashes / signatures; optional air gap for sensitive weights.
requirements.requirement: Protect model weights and configuration parameters against unauthorised access and modification.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Separate weights from datasets; least privilege; encryption / hashes / signatures; optional air gap for sensitive weights.
requirements.failure_modes: Model theft or tampering.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies access, integrity and provenance controls to model weights and parameters.

requirements.title: Code storage extended to AI artefacts
requirements.overlay_modifies: Clarifies SSDF PS.1.1 to include AI models, model weights, pipelines, reward models, evaluation suites and other AI elements as protected code artefacts.
requirements.requirement: Treat prompts, system instructions, pipelines, reward models, evaluation suites as protected code artefacts under PS.1.1 controls.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Protected repositories and registries for AI elements alongside traditional code.
requirements.failure_modes: Protecting code but not model artefacts.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox protects model artefacts and AI pipeline elements as part of software security.

requirements.title: Model provenance tracking
requirements.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF PS.3.2 (provenance for components) to specifically include AI models — track provenance of models, weights, sources, versions, and approvals.
requirements.requirement: Track provenance of models, weights, sources, versions and approvals.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Approved model inventory and source provenance package. Model cards, registry, approval records.
requirements.failure_modes: No authoritative model registry.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains provenance and approval records for approved AI models.

requirements.title: Dataset provenance tracking
requirements.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF PS.3.2 to cover training, testing, fine-tuning and alignment data provenance. Records unknown provenance where applicable.
requirements.requirement: Track provenance of training, testing, fine-tuning and alignment data; record unknown provenance where applicable.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Data-source register and known / unknown provenance flags. Lineage register for training data.
requirements.failure_modes: Undocumented sources and rights.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox tracks provenance information for AI development data where known and records gaps where it is not.

requirements.title: Adversarial samples in training and testing
requirements.overlay_modifies: Adds 218A recommendation to include adversarial samples in training and testing data to improve attack prevention — most relevant where PB fine-tunes or runs its own classifiers / guardrails.
requirements.requirement: Include adversarial samples in training and testing data to improve attack prevention.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Adversarial prompt library and malicious-document tests. Rerun before major releases.
requirements.failure_modes: No adversarial coverage.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses adversarial test cases to strengthen AI safeguards where technically applicable.

requirements.title: Prompt-injection and jailbreak risk testing
requirements.overlay_modifies: Highlights AI-specific injection-style attacks not covered by traditional security testing. Direct and indirect prompt injection, malicious retrieved content, connector abuse, rogue instructions in documents.
requirements.requirement: Test for direct and indirect prompt injection, malicious retrieved content, connector abuse, and rogue instructions embedded in documents.
requirements.pb_interpretation: AI red-team playbooks and results. Regression suite. Both direct prompts and retrieved- content vectors.
requirements.failure_modes: Only direct prompts tested.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox tests for prompt-injection and jailbreak-style attacks in relevant workflows.

crosswalk.note: AIMS V&V with adversarial testing.

requirements.title: Unsafe tool or agent use controls
requirements.overlay_modifies: Adds controls for agentic / tool-enabled workflows — scoped actions and execution controls for AI-initiated tool use.
requirements.requirement: Restrict tool permissions, sandbox execution, require human approval for sensitive actions, record tool calls.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Tool allow-list, sandbox config, logs. Human approval for sensitive actions.
requirements.failure_modes: Over-privileged agents.
requirements.external_safe_claim: Where tool use is enabled, PrivateBox constrains and logs AI-initiated actions.

crosswalk.note: AIMS human oversight for AI tool use.

requirements.title: AI model release integrity
requirements.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF PS.2.1 (release integrity) to specifically cover AI model artefacts — signing, hashing, controlled publication.
requirements.requirement: Sign installers and, where feasible, model artefacts; preserve verification instructions.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Signed releases for software AND model artefacts. Preserve verification instructions for customers / auditors.
requirements.failure_modes: Unsigned or mutable model packages.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses integrity controls for software and AI model delivery.

requirements.title: Model update and rollback control
requirements.overlay_modifies: Adds AI model change control beyond standard software change — approval, testing, reversibility, customer advisory.
requirements.requirement: Define model change CAB, regression testing, rollback package, customer advisory.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Change tickets, rollback SOP, drilled rollback.
requirements.failure_modes: Silent model swap.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox controls model changes through approval, testing and rollback procedures.

crosswalk.note: AIMS planning of changes including AI-specific change types.

requirements.title: Secure fine-tuning and RAG
requirements.overlay_modifies: Adds controls for fine-tuning, retrieval and data-grounding workflows — additional poisoning and leakage risk beyond standard data security.
requirements.requirement: Separate knowledge stores per tenant; approve ingestion sources; scan documents; bound retrieval scope.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Connector policy, ingestion SOP, malicious-document injection tests.
requirements.failure_modes: Cross-tenant or poisoned retrieval context.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies controls to knowledge ingestion and retrieval-grounded workflows.

requirements.title: Vulnerability intake for AI models
requirements.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF RV.1.1 to include AI-model and framework vulnerabilities — model-library advisories, AI-framework CVEs, model-incident databases.
requirements.requirement: Monitor AI-framework, model and library vulnerability sources and incident databases.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Watchlists and ticket feed covering AI-framework and model-library vulnerabilities.
requirements.failure_modes: App vulnerabilities tracked, model vulnerabilities ignored.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox includes AI-model and framework issues in vulnerability monitoring.

requirements.title: AI model scanning and audits
requirements.overlay_modifies: Adds recurring AI model security scans and periodic audit review to SSDF RV.1.2.
requirements.requirement: Regular model security scans, behaviour tests, and periodic audit review.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Recurring model security testing and audit review per policy cadence.
requirements.failure_modes: No retesting after retraining or config changes.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox performs recurring model security testing and audit review.

requirements.title: AI-specific disclosure to users
requirements.overlay_modifies: Adds AI-specific disclosure obligations to customers — limitations and reporting mechanisms.
requirements.requirement: Provide safe-use instructions, limitations, issue-reporting route, unsupported use cases.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Customer deployment guide + UI notices conveying AI limitations and issue-reporting.
requirements.failure_modes: Customers unaware of reporting mechanism.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox provides documented limitations and issue-reporting guidance for customers.

requirements.title: Rebuild / replace / retire economic criteria
requirements.overlay_modifies: Adds AI-specific risk-response decision criteria — when to patch, retrain, replace, retire or disable a model given rebuild cost vs risk.
requirements.requirement: Define criteria for patch, retrain, replace, retire, or disable decisions for AI models.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Decision matrix applied to hypothetical compromised model scenarios.
requirements.failure_modes: Keeping a risky model because retraining is expensive.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses documented criteria for retraining, replacement and retirement of AI models.

requirements.title: Third-party model governance and monitoring
requirements.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF PW.4.1 (acquire well-secured third-party components) to AI-specific intake — transparency artefacts, source commits, checkpoints, licences.
requirements.requirement: Review third-party model transparency artefacts and monitor external models used in systems.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Third-party model dossiers — model cards, system cards, source commits, checkpoints, licences, terms.
requirements.failure_modes: Adopting a model with opaque provenance.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies due diligence to third-party models before approval.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility allocation.

requirements.title: Generated-content provenance
requirements.overlay_modifies: Adds 218A and GenAI Profile recommendation for provenance data tracking and synthetic- content transparency where content generation is material.
requirements.requirement: Where content generation is material, attach provenance metadata or watermark strategy if feasible; document limits if not used.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Content provenance design note. Document limits where provenance / watermarking is not feasible.
requirements.failure_modes: Generated content indistinguishable by policy and process.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents provenance and transparency measures for generated content where applicable.

requirements.title: AI model supply chain security
requirements.overlay_modifies: Adds 218A model-signing and registry controls to SSDF PW.4.3 (verify component integrity).
requirements.requirement: Use signed internal model registry, controlled mirroring, checksum verification, approval workflow for model intake.
requirements.pb_interpretation: Model registry controls including signed mirroring and approval.
requirements.failure_modes: Model pulled directly from public hub into production workflow.
requirements.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies provenance and approval controls to the AI model supply chain.

nist-gov-1.1.title: Legal and regulatory AI requirements understood and documented
nist-gov-1.1.requirement: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed and documented.
nist-gov-1.1.pb_interpretation: PB-owned with customer input. AI legal and obligations register covering contract clauses, model / data / IP / privacy duties; reviewed before releases and major customer deployments.
nist-gov-1.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox is designed with documented AI legal and contractual controls aligned to NIST AI RMF GOV 1.1.

crosswalk.note: AIMS context (Clause 4.1) parallels AI RMF GOV 1.1 legal / regulatory understanding.

nist-gov-1.2.title: Trustworthy-AI characteristics integrated into policies
nist-gov-1.2.requirement: Trustworthy AI characteristics are integrated into organisational policies and practices.
nist-gov-1.2.pb_interpretation: AI Policy set covering security, reliability, privacy, transparency, human oversight. SDLC gates enforce trustworthy-AI characteristics at design and release.
documented_evidence: AI Policy set (security / reliability / privacy / transparency / oversight)
SDLC gate checklist mapped to trustworthy-AI characteristics
nist-gov-1.2.cadence: Annual policy review + per release gate
nist-gov-1.2.failure_modes: Values statement with no operational checks.
nist-gov-1.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox implements documented controls aligned to NIST AI RMF trustworthy-AI characteristics.

crosswalk.note: AIMS AI Policy (Clause 5.2) operationalises trustworthy-AI characteristics.

nist-gov-1.3.title: Risk-management activity level matches organisational risk tolerance
nist-gov-1.3.requirement: Processes are in place to determine the activity level of risk-management based on organisational risk tolerance.
nist-gov-1.3.pb_interpretation: AI risk-tiering standard with low / medium / high tiers. Healthcare, HR, legal, autonomous-agent features escalate to high-risk treatment.
nist-gov-1.3.cadence: Per feature design + annual rubric review
nist-gov-1.3.failure_modes: Identical treatment for trivial and sensitive uses.
nist-gov-1.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses risk-proportionate AI governance and testing.

crosswalk.note: AIMS AI risk methodology covers tiered treatment.

nist-gov-1.4.title: Transparent AI risk-management process
nist-gov-1.4.requirement: The risk-management process and its outcomes are established through transparent policies, procedures and other controls.
nist-gov-1.4.pb_interpretation: End-to-end AI risk procedure with stages, approvers, required artefacts, escalation, acceptance authority. Consistent use across releases.
nist-gov-1.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox is managed through documented AI governance procedures.

crosswalk.note: AIMS risk treatment / SoA pattern.

nist-gov-1.5.title: Ongoing monitoring and periodic review planned with roles defined
nist-gov-1.5.requirement: Ongoing monitoring and periodic review of the risk-management process and its outcomes are planned, with organisational roles and responsibilities clearly defined.
nist-gov-1.5.pb_interpretation: Review calendar and role map. Annual policy review; quarterly risk review; defined runtime review cadence. Separate optional-support obligations for customer-deployed instances.
nist-gov-1.5.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports periodic review and lifecycle governance.

crosswalk.note: AIMS management review pattern.

nist-gov-1.6.title: AI systems inventoried according to risk priorities
nist-gov-1.6.requirement: Mechanisms are in place to inventory AI systems and are resourced according to organisational risk priorities.
nist-gov-1.6.pb_interpretation: AI system and model inventory — approved-models list, feature inventory, deployment inventory, connector inventory, customer-instance log, optional-telemetry / support flags.
nist-gov-1.6.failure_modes: No record of which model / version is where.
nist-gov-1.6.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains a controlled inventory of AI components and approved models.

crosswalk.note: AIMS resource documentation and data-resource inventory.
Asset inventory pattern.

nist-gov-1.7.title: Safe decommissioning procedures exist
nist-gov-1.7.requirement: Processes and procedures are in place for decommissioning and phasing out AI systems safely and in a manner that does not increase risks or decrease the organisation's trustworthiness.
nist-gov-1.7.pb_interpretation: AI decommissioning SOP — archive period, model / data retention, credential revocation, rollback disablement, hardware-wipe responsibilities, support termination.
nist-gov-1.7.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox includes documented retirement and decommissioning practices.

nist-gov-2.1.title: Roles, responsibilities and communication lines documented
nist-gov-2.1.requirement: Roles, responsibilities and lines of communication related to AI risk-management are documented and clear to those individuals and teams throughout the organisation.
nist-gov-2.1.pb_interpretation: AI governance RACI assigning product, security, legal, ops, support, model owner, release approver, incident owner, customer contact.
nist-gov-2.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox assigns clear accountability for AI risks and controls.

crosswalk.note: AIMS roles, responsibilities and authorities.

nist-gov-2.2.title: AI risk-management training for personnel and partners
nist-gov-2.2.requirement: The organisation's personnel and partners receive AI risk-management training to enable them to perform their duties and responsibilities consistent with related policies and procedures.
nist-gov-2.2.pb_interpretation: Role-based AI training — security, prompt-injection, model misuse, support-access, customer- safe deployment training with refresher cycle.
nist-gov-2.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains role-based AI security and governance training.

crosswalk.note: AIMS competence.

nist-gov-2.3.title: Executive leadership takes AI risk responsibility
nist-gov-2.3.requirement: Executive leadership of the organisation takes responsibility for decisions about risks associated with AI system development and deployment.
nist-gov-2.3.pb_interpretation: Executive risk acceptance and launch approval. Defined approver authority; leadership sign-off for major models / features.
documented_evidence: Board / Exco minutes referencing AI launches
nist-gov-2.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox subjects material AI risks to executive oversight.

crosswalk.note: King V Principle 1 (Leadership).

nist-gov-3.1.title: Diverse teams inform AI risk decisions
nist-gov-3.1.requirement: Decision-making related to mapping, measuring and managing AI risks throughout the lifecycle is informed by a diverse team.
nist-gov-3.1.pb_interpretation: Review panels with security, domain, legal, operations, UX, customer-facing perspectives. Affected-user representation where relevant.
nist-gov-3.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses interdisciplinary review in AI risk decisions.

nist-gov-3.2.title: Human-AI configuration and oversight roles defined
nist-gov-3.2.requirement: Policies and procedures are in place to define and differentiate roles and responsibilities for human-AI configurations and oversight of AI systems.
nist-gov-3.2.pb_interpretation: Human-oversight design standard — approval points, override rights, escalation paths, no-autonomous-final-decision rules where needed.
nist-gov-3.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines human oversight and override mechanisms where appropriate.

crosswalk.note: AIMS human oversight.
EU AI Act human oversight obligation.

nist-gov-4.1.title: Critical-thinking and safety-first culture
nist-gov-4.1.requirement: Organisational policies and practices are in place to foster a critical-thinking and safety-first mindset in the design, development, deployment and uses of AI systems to minimise potential negative impacts.
nist-gov-4.1.pb_interpretation: Pre-mortems, red-team reviews, "stop ship" authority, no-blame incident learning. Safety concerns block release when needed.
nist-gov-4.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox emphasises safety-first design and release discipline.

nist-gov-4.2.title: AI risks and impacts documented and communicated
nist-gov-4.2.requirement: Organisational teams document the risks and potential impacts of the AI technology they design, develop, deploy, evaluate and use, and they communicate about the impacts more broadly.
nist-gov-4.2.pb_interpretation: System / model card + deployment guidance covering intended use, prohibited use, limitations, residual risk, evaluation summary, support assumptions.
nist-gov-4.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox publishes evidence-backed guidance on intended use and limitations.

crosswalk.note: AIMS system characteristics / documentation.

nist-gov-4.3.title: AI testing, incident identification, information sharing
nist-gov-4.3.requirement: Organisational practices are in place to enable AI testing, identification of incidents and information sharing.
nist-gov-4.3.pb_interpretation: AI incident and testing programme — red-team cadence, issue intake, triage, severity, notification, learning loop.
nist-gov-4.3.failure_modes: Security incidents handled but behavioural incidents ignored.
nist-gov-4.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains AI-focused testing and incident response procedures.

nist-gov-5.1.title: External feedback on individual and societal impacts collected
nist-gov-5.1.requirement: Organisational policies and practices are in place to collect, consider, prioritise and integrate feedback from those external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system regarding the potential individual and societal impacts related to AI risks.
nist-gov-5.1.pb_interpretation: Formal external feedback channels — support tickets, procurement feedback, pilot reviews, issue reporting, harmful-output reporting.
nist-gov-5.1.failure_modes: No route for customers to appeal or report misuse.
nist-gov-5.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox captures and reviews customer feedback on AI risk and performance.

nist-gov-5.2.title: Adjudicated feedback incorporated regularly
nist-gov-5.2.requirement: Mechanisms are established to enable AI actors to regularly incorporate adjudicated feedback from relevant AI actors into system design and implementation.
nist-gov-5.2.pb_interpretation: Feedback-to-change procedure — classify feedback, adjudicate, convert to releases / docs / config changes, communicate outcomes.
nist-gov-5.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox incorporates validated customer and stakeholder feedback into product controls.

nist-gov-6.1.title: Third-party AI / data / IP / supply-chain risk policies
nist-gov-6.1.requirement: Policies and procedures are in place to address AI risks and benefits arising from third-party software and data and other supply-chain issues.
nist-gov-6.1.pb_interpretation: Third-party model / data / component governance — vet dependencies; require provenance; review licences; restrict unapproved models; maintain approved-source list.
nist-gov-6.1.failure_modes: Open-source model adoption without provenance or licence review.
nist-gov-6.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox governs third-party models, software and data through documented due diligence.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility allocation.

nist-gov-6.2.title: Contingency for high-risk third-party AI failures
nist-gov-6.2.requirement: Contingency processes are in place to handle failures or incidents in third-party data or AI systems deemed to be high-risk.
nist-gov-6.2.pb_interpretation: Supplier failure fallback plan — kill switch, rollback, local disablement, alternate model path, notification triggers.
nist-gov-6.2.failure_modes: No action path when a model or library is withdrawn or compromised.
nist-gov-6.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains contingency procedures for material third-party AI dependencies.

nist-map-1.1.title: Intended purpose and deployment context documented
nist-map-1.1.requirement: Intended purposes, potentially beneficial uses, context-specific laws, norms and expectations, and prospective settings in which the AI system will be deployed are understood and documented.
nist-map-1.1.pb_interpretation: Intended-use and deployment-context register per customer deployment — use cases, prohibited uses, user groups, deployment setting, data classes, norms.
documented_evidence: Use-case form + solution design record per deployment
nist-map-1.1.failure_modes: One generic use case for all clients.
nist-map-1.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents intended use, limitations and deployment context.

crosswalk.note: AIMS context + intended-use enforcement.

nist-map-1.2.title: Interdisciplinary AI actors for context setting
nist-map-1.2.requirement: Inter-disciplinary AI actors, competencies, skills and capacities for establishing context reflect demographic diversity and broad domain and user experience expertise.
nist-map-1.2.pb_interpretation: Contextual review participants list — domain, security, legal, product, deployment ops, client stakeholders.
nist-map-1.2.failure_modes: Deployment designed only by sales and engineering.
nist-map-1.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses cross-functional context-setting before deployment.

nist-map-1.3.title: Mission and AI goals documented
nist-map-1.3.requirement: The organisation's mission and relevant goals for AI technology are understood and documented.
nist-map-1.3.pb_interpretation: AI product purpose statement — why the feature exists; what business / security / privacy value it should create; what it will not do.
nist-map-1.3.failure_modes: Adding AI features without clear purpose.
nist-map-1.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox ties AI capabilities to documented product and customer objectives.

nist-map-1.4.title: Business value or business-use context defined
nist-map-1.4.requirement: The business value or business-use context is clearly defined or — in the case of assessing existing AI systems — re-evaluated.
nist-map-1.4.pb_interpretation: Value-versus-risk review — quantify value, operational benefit, residual risk, non-AI alternatives. Re-evaluation after scope changes.
nist-map-1.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox evaluates AI use cases against business value and risk.

nist-map-1.5.title: Organisational risk tolerances determined and documented
nist-map-1.5.requirement: Organisational risk tolerances are determined and documented.
nist-map-1.5.pb_interpretation: Customer-specific AI risk tolerance statement — acceptable error classes, approved domains, prohibited automation, incident thresholds.
nist-map-1.5.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox can be configured against documented customer risk tolerances.

nist-map-1.6.title: System requirements account for socio-technical risk
nist-map-1.6.requirement: System requirements (e.g., "the system shall respect the privacy of its users") are elicited from and understood by relevant AI actors. Design decisions take socio-technical implications into account to address AI risks.
nist-map-1.6.pb_interpretation: Requirements log covering privacy, access control, retention, auditability, oversight, safe response, domain boundaries, language handling.
nist-map-1.6.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox incorporates security, oversight and operational requirements into AI design.

nist-map-2.1.title: Tasks and methods documented
nist-map-2.1.requirement: The specific tasks and methods used to implement the tasks that the AI system will support are defined (e.g., classifiers, generative models, recommenders).
nist-map-2.1.pb_interpretation: Model-function classification — generative Q&A, summarisation, extraction, search-grounded response, agent action, classification.
nist-map-2.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents the AI methods and tasks used for each feature.

nist-map-2.2.title: Knowledge limits and human oversight documented
nist-map-2.2.requirement: Information about the AI system's knowledge limits and how system output may be utilised and overseen by humans is documented. Documentation provides sufficient information to assist relevant AI actors when making decisions and taking subsequent actions.
nist-map-2.2.pb_interpretation: Limitations and oversight register — known failure modes, unsupported domains, confidence / uncertainty cues, mandatory human review points.
documented_evidence: Model / system card + customer guide + UI notices
nist-map-2.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents model limitations and human review requirements.

crosswalk.note: AIMS human oversight.

nist-map-2.3.title: Scientific integrity and TEVV considerations documented
nist-map-2.3.requirement: Scientific integrity and TEVV considerations are identified and documented, including those related to experimental design, data collection and selection, system trustworthiness and construct validation.
nist-map-2.3.pb_interpretation: Evaluation design protocol — datasets, acceptance criteria, representativeness, baseline comparisons, reproducibility.
nist-map-2.3.failure_modes: Ad-hoc demos standing in for validation.
nist-map-2.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses documented evaluation protocols for AI performance and risk testing.

crosswalk.note: AIMS V&V and robustness.

nist-map-3.1.title: Potential benefits documented
nist-map-3.1.requirement: Potential benefits of intended AI system functionality and performance are examined and documented.
nist-map-3.1.pb_interpretation: Benefit statement per feature — measurable positive outcomes and success metrics.
nist-map-3.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox records expected benefits and success criteria for AI features.

nist-map-3.2.title: Potential costs and non-monetary costs documented
nist-map-3.2.requirement: Potential costs, including non-monetary costs, which result from expected or realised AI errors or system functionality and trustworthiness are examined and documented.
nist-map-3.2.pb_interpretation: Harm and cost analysis — confidentiality exposure, wrong-answer cost, workflow disruption, user over-reliance, reputational risk.
nist-map-3.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox assesses both operational benefit and downside risk of AI use.

nist-map-3.3.title: Targeted application scope documented
nist-map-3.3.requirement: Targeted application scope is specified and documented based on the system's capability, established context and AI system categorisation.
nist-map-3.3.pb_interpretation: Scope boundary record — in-scope users, data sources, actions, integrations, geographies, languages, prohibited extensions.
nist-map-3.3.failure_modes: Scope creep through connectors or agent tools.
nist-map-3.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines and constrains intended application scope.

nist-map-3.4.title: Operator and practitioner proficiency processes defined
nist-map-3.4.requirement: Processes for operator and practitioner proficiency with AI system performance and trustworthiness — and relevant technical standards and certifications — are defined, assessed and documented.
nist-map-3.4.pb_interpretation: Operator competency standard — admin training, support runbooks, customer enablement, escalation criteria.
nist-map-3.4.failure_modes: Powerful admin features without competency checks.
nist-map-3.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports trained administration and customer enablement.

nist-map-3.5.title: Human oversight processes defined
nist-map-3.5.requirement: Processes for human oversight, in accordance with organisational policies from GOVERN function, are defined, assessed and documented.
