nist-map-3.5.pb_interpretation: Oversight workflow — approval gates, fallback to search / manual process, escalation if unsafe / low confidence.
documented_evidence: Oversight SOP + UX design + audit logs
nist-map-3.5.failure_modes: Human review theoretically required but practically bypassed.
nist-map-3.5.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox includes documented human oversight and fallback procedures.

crosswalk.note: AIMS human oversight.

nist-map-4.1.title: Component risks including third-party mapped
nist-map-4.1.requirement: Approaches for mapping AI technology and legal risks of its components — including third-party AI technologies — are in place.
nist-map-4.1.pb_interpretation: Component risk map — models, embedding engines, vector stores, open-source packages, licences, datasets, update channels.
documented_evidence: Component register + legal review + SBOM / MBOM
nist-map-4.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maps technical, legal and supply-chain risks across AI components.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility allocation.

nist-map-4.2.title: Internal risk controls for components documented
nist-map-4.2.requirement: Internal risk controls for components of the AI system, including third-party AI technologies, are identified and documented.
nist-map-4.2.pb_interpretation: Component control matrix — controls for vendor models, repository sources, update approvals, sandboxing, data isolation.
nist-map-4.2.failure_modes: Generic controls with no component focus.
nist-map-4.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents control coverage for each significant AI component.

nist-map-5.1.title: Likelihood and magnitude of impacts identified
nist-map-5.1.requirement: Likelihood and magnitude of each identified impact (both potentially beneficial and harmful) based on expected use, past uses of AI systems in similar contexts, public incident reports, feedback from those external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system or other data are identified and documented.
nist-map-5.1.pb_interpretation: AI impact assessment — score confidentiality, integrity, availability, harmful output, misrouting, abuse, productivity benefit.
nist-map-5.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses documented impact assessment before material AI deployments.

crosswalk.note: AIMS AI System Impact Assessment (AISIA).

nist-map-5.2.title: Stakeholder engagement and impact feedback practices
nist-map-5.2.requirement: Practices and personnel for supporting regular engagement with relevant AI actors and integrating feedback about positive, negative and unanticipated impacts are in place and documented.
nist-map-5.2.pb_interpretation: Stakeholder-impact review mechanism — post-go-live reviews, adverse-event intake, procurement feedback loop.
nist-map-5.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains recurring stakeholder review for AI impacts.

nist-measure-1.1.title: Risk metrics selected and unmeasured risks documented
nist-measure-1.1.requirement: Approaches and metrics for measurement of AI risks enumerated during the MAP function are selected for implementation, starting with the most significant AI risks.
nist-measure-1.1.pb_interpretation: Metric selection register — quality, security, privacy, hallucination / confabulation, abuse, latency, drift, override rates. Record what is not measurable.
nist-measure-1.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines documented metrics for material AI risks and records current measurement gaps.

nist-measure-1.2.title: Metrics and control effectiveness regularly assessed
nist-measure-1.2.requirement: Appropriateness of AI metrics and effectiveness of existing controls are regularly assessed and updated, including reports of errors and potential impacts.
nist-measure-1.2.pb_interpretation: Metric review cycle — quarterly metric review, post-incident metric updates, client-specific tuning where needed.
nist-measure-1.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox reviews measurement methods and control effectiveness on a recurring basis.

nist-measure-1.3.title: Independent assessors involved
nist-measure-1.3.requirement: Internal experts who did not serve as front-line developers for the system and / or independent assessors are involved in regular assessments and updates. Domain experts, users, AI actors external to the team that developed or deployed the AI system, and affected communities are consulted in support of assessments as necessary per organisational risk tolerance.
nist-measure-1.3.pb_interpretation: Independent assurance review — security review, peer review outside dev team, red team, domain reviewer. At least one independent review per major release.
nist-measure-1.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox includes independent review in AI testing and assurance.

nist-measure-2.1.title: TEVV test sets, metrics and tools documented
nist-measure-2.1.requirement: Test sets, metrics and details about the tools used during TEVV are documented.
nist-measure-2.1.pb_interpretation: TEVV documentation standard — version test sets; record tools, prompts, seeds, configs, expected outcomes. Reproducible evaluations.
nist-measure-2.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains traceable test and evaluation records.

nist-measure-2.2.title: Human-subject evaluations meet requirements
nist-measure-2.2.requirement: Evaluations involving human subjects meet applicable requirements (including human subject protection) and are representative of the relevant population.
nist-measure-2.2.pb_interpretation: Human-subject testing protocol where used — consent, ethics, sample representativeness, safe data handling. Often not applicable; document if not.
documented_evidence: Test protocol + consent forms where applicable
nist-measure-2.2.external_safe_claim: Where human-subject testing is used, PrivateBox applies a documented protocol.

nist-measure-2.3.title: Performance measured under deployment-like conditions
nist-measure-2.3.requirement: AI system performance or assurance criteria are measured qualitatively or quantitatively and demonstrated for conditions similar to deployment setting(s). Measures are documented.
nist-measure-2.3.pb_interpretation: Environment-realistic evaluation — test with local documents, likely prompt styles, adversarial inputs, role access, retrieval context.
nist-measure-2.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox evaluates AI behaviour under deployment-relevant conditions.

nist-measure-2.4.title: Production monitoring of functionality and behaviour
nist-measure-2.4.requirement: The functionality and behaviour of the AI system and its components — as identified in the MAP function — are monitored when in production.
nist-measure-2.4.pb_interpretation: Runtime monitoring plan — log errors, refusals, overrides, tool failures, drift indicators, abuse signals; respect privacy constraints.
nist-measure-2.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports monitored AI behaviour after go-live, subject to agreed operational scope.

crosswalk.note: AIMS monitoring and KPI dashboard.

nist-measure-2.5.title: System shown valid and reliable; generalisation limits documented
nist-measure-2.5.requirement: The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be valid and reliable. Limitations of the generalisability beyond the conditions under which the technology was developed are documented.
nist-measure-2.5.pb_interpretation: Validity and reliability dossier — benchmark results, scenario tests, failure rates, known limits, non-generalisation statement.
nist-measure-2.5.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents validation results and known generalisation limits.

nist-measure-2.6.title: Safety risks regularly evaluated; fail-safe behaviour exists
nist-measure-2.6.requirement: AI system is evaluated regularly for safety risks — as identified in the MAP function. The AI system to be deployed is demonstrated to be safe, its residual negative risk does not exceed the risk tolerance, and it can fail safely, particularly if made to operate beyond its knowledge limits. Safety metrics reflect system reliability and robustness, real-time monitoring and response times for AI system failures.
nist-measure-2.6.pb_interpretation: Safety evaluation and fail-safe design — refusal patterns, escalation, human review, disablement on unsafe tool calls, safe fallback.
nist-measure-2.6.failure_modes: Unsafe answers degrade gracefully only in theory.
nist-measure-2.6.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox implements documented safeguards and fail-safe responses for identified safety risks.

nist-measure-2.7.title: AI security and resilience evaluated
nist-measure-2.7.requirement: AI system security and resilience — as identified in the MAP function — are evaluated and documented.
nist-measure-2.7.pb_interpretation: AI security test programme — prompt-injection testing, model abuse scenarios, secret-leakage tests, authz bypass, malicious-document / retrieval tests.
nist-measure-2.7.failure_modes: Traditional appsec only, no AI-specific testing.
nist-measure-2.7.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox evaluates AI-specific security and resilience risks before and after release.

crosswalk.note: AIMS V&V and robustness with adversarial testing.

nist-measure-2.8.title: Transparency and accountability risks examined
nist-measure-2.8.requirement: Risks associated with transparency and accountability — as identified in the MAP function — are examined and documented.
nist-measure-2.8.pb_interpretation: Transparency review — system card, logs proving operator actions, disclosure text, role accountability.
nist-measure-2.8.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains traceable records supporting accountability and customer transparency.

nist-measure-2.9.title: Model explained and contextually interpreted
nist-measure-2.9.requirement: The AI model is explained, validated and documented, and AI system output is interpreted within its context — as identified in the MAP function — to inform responsible use and governance.
nist-measure-2.9.pb_interpretation: Explanation and contextual interpretation guidance — model family, RAG grounding, limits, human interpretation steps.
nist-measure-2.9.failure_modes: End users treat outputs as authoritative.
nist-measure-2.9.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox provides contextual documentation to support responsible interpretation of outputs.

nist-measure-2.10.title: Privacy risk examined
nist-measure-2.10.requirement: Privacy risk of the AI system — as identified in the MAP function — is examined and documented.
nist-measure-2.10.pb_interpretation: AI privacy assessment — sensitive-data exposure, retention, prompting leakage, logs, admin access, optional telemetry.
nist-measure-2.10.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox assesses and documents privacy risks for each material AI use case.

crosswalk.note: POPIA processing limitation + s 71 automated decisions.

nist-measure-2.11.title: Fairness and bias evaluated
nist-measure-2.11.requirement: Fairness and bias — as identified in the MAP function — are evaluated and results are documented.
nist-measure-2.11.pb_interpretation: Fairness review — identify protected or sensitive contexts; evaluate disparities where relevant; document non-applicability decision per deployment if not.
nist-measure-2.11.failure_modes: Assuming enterprise-assistant use means bias is irrelevant.
nist-measure-2.11.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox evaluates fairness risk where the use case makes it material.

crosswalk.note: AIMS bias methodology.

nist-measure-2.12.title: Environmental impact and sustainability assessed
nist-measure-2.12.requirement: Environmental impact and sustainability of AI model training and management activities — as identified in the MAP function — are assessed and documented.
nist-measure-2.12.pb_interpretation: Compute and footprint record — model size, hardware load, optimisation, inference footprint for local deployments.
nist-measure-2.12.failure_modes: Ignoring local compute burden for clients.
nist-measure-2.12.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents material compute and resource considerations for deployment.

nist-measure-2.13.title: TEVV metric / process effectiveness reviewed
nist-measure-2.13.requirement: Effectiveness of the employed TEVV metrics and processes in the MEASURE function are evaluated and documented.
nist-measure-2.13.pb_interpretation: Evaluation-of-evaluation review — false assurance, missing scenarios, benchmark drift, red-team coverage gaps.
nist-measure-2.13.failure_modes: Treating benchmark suite as final truth.
nist-measure-2.13.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox reviews and improves its own evaluation methods over time.

nist-measure-3.1.title: Tracking emergent and unanticipated risks
nist-measure-3.1.requirement: Approaches, personnel and documentation are in place to regularly identify and track existing, unanticipated and emergent AI risks based on factors such as intended and actual performance in deployed contexts.
nist-measure-3.1.pb_interpretation: Emergent-risk monitoring — incidents, user reports, model updates, threat intel, misuse patterns.
nist-measure-3.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox tracks known and emergent AI risks throughout the lifecycle.

nist-measure-3.2.title: Risk tracking where measurement is immature
nist-measure-3.2.requirement: Risk tracking approaches are considered for settings where AI risks are difficult to assess using currently available measurement techniques or where metrics are not yet available.
nist-measure-3.2.pb_interpretation: Non-quantifiable risk log — uncertain risks, proxy indicators, monitoring plans, decision rationale.
nist-measure-3.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents proxy measures and monitoring plans for hard-to-measure AI risks.

nist-measure-3.3.title: Feedback processes enable end-user reporting and appeals
nist-measure-3.3.requirement: Feedback processes for end users and impacted communities to report problems and appeal system outcomes are established and integrated into AI system evaluation metrics.
nist-measure-3.3.pb_interpretation: Reporting and appeal channel — in-product or help-desk route; severity path; human review route; response SLA.
nist-measure-3.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports customer reporting and escalation channels for AI issues.

nist-measure-4.1.title: Measurement tied to deployment context and expert input
nist-measure-4.1.requirement: Measurement approaches for identifying AI risks are connected to deployment context(s) and informed through consultation with domain experts and other end users.
nist-measure-4.1.pb_interpretation: Context-specific evaluation profile — tailor test sets and thresholds per sector and workflow.
nist-measure-4.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox can tailor testing to deployment-specific contexts and expert input.

nist-measure-4.2.title: Trustworthiness results validated with stakeholders
nist-measure-4.2.requirement: Measurement results regarding AI system trustworthiness in deployment context(s) and across the AI lifecycle are informed by input from domain experts and relevant AI actors to validate whether the system is performing consistently as intended. Results are documented.
nist-measure-4.2.pb_interpretation: Stakeholder validation review — share results, capture objections, approve or amend interpretation.
nist-measure-4.2.failure_modes: Metrics reviewed without user-context sanity check.
nist-measure-4.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox validates material evaluation results with relevant stakeholders where appropriate.

nist-measure-4.3.title: Performance changes identified and documented
nist-measure-4.3.requirement: Measurable performance improvements or declines based on consultations with relevant AI actors, including affected communities, and field data about context-relevant risks and trustworthiness characteristics are identified and documented.
nist-measure-4.3.pb_interpretation: Post-deployment improvement register — regressions, drift, newly observed harms, corrective actions.
nist-measure-4.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents performance changes and improvement actions across the AI lifecycle.

nist-manage-1.1.title: Go/no-go decision on whether to proceed
nist-manage-1.1.requirement: A determination is made as to whether the AI system achieves its intended purpose and stated objectives and whether its development or deployment should proceed.
nist-manage-1.1.pb_interpretation: AI go / no-go gate — require risk, validation, security, documentation, support readiness before release or deployment.
nist-manage-1.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses documented go/no-go criteria for material AI releases.

crosswalk.note: AIMS pre-deployment approval gate.

nist-manage-1.2.title: Risk treatment prioritised by impact and resources
nist-manage-1.2.requirement: Treatment of documented AI risks is prioritised based on impact, likelihood, available resources or methods.
nist-manage-1.2.pb_interpretation: AI risk treatment plan — rank findings; set deadlines and owners; distinguish accept / mitigate / avoid / transfer.
nist-manage-1.2.failure_modes: Long risk list with no prioritisation.
nist-manage-1.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox prioritises AI risk treatment using documented criteria.

nist-manage-1.3.title: High-priority AI risk responses planned
nist-manage-1.3.requirement: Responses to the AI risks deemed high priority — as identified by the MAP function — are developed, planned and documented.
nist-manage-1.3.pb_interpretation: High-risk mitigation plans — extra controls for unsafe outputs, privileged actions, sensitive documents, third-party models.
nist-manage-1.3.failure_modes: Risks documented but not engineered out.
nist-manage-1.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox incorporates targeted mitigations for high-priority AI risks.

nist-manage-1.4.title: Residual negative risks documented for users
nist-manage-1.4.requirement: Negative residual risks (defined as the sum of all unmitigated risks) to both downstream acquirers of AI systems and end users are documented.
nist-manage-1.4.pb_interpretation: Residual-risk statement — remaining hallucination, misuse, and operational risks; safe-use conditions and customer dependencies.
documented_evidence: Residual risk register + customer handoff note
nist-manage-1.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents residual risks and customer operating assumptions.

nist-manage-2.1.title: Non-AI alternatives considered
nist-manage-2.1.requirement: Resources required to manage AI risks are taken into account, along with viable non-AI alternative systems, approaches or methods, to reduce the magnitude or likelihood of potential impacts.
nist-manage-2.1.pb_interpretation: Alternative-method review — non-AI workflow, search-only mode, or manual fallback where preferable.
nist-manage-2.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox evaluates AI use against viable non-AI alternatives and fallbacks.

nist-manage-2.2.title: Mechanisms sustain AI system value
nist-manage-2.2.requirement: Mechanisms are in place and applied to sustain the value of deployed AI systems.
nist-manage-2.2.pb_interpretation: Lifecycle maintenance plan — update schedules, model / version support, retraining / fine-tuning criteria, knowledge refresh, support policy.
nist-manage-2.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines lifecycle maintenance processes for sustained AI system value.

nist-manage-2.3.title: Procedures respond to unknown risk
nist-manage-2.3.requirement: Procedures are followed to respond to and recover from a previously unknown risk when it is identified.
nist-manage-2.3.pb_interpretation: Novel-risk response plan — emergency disablement, temporary guardrails, hotfix, customer communication, root cause.
nist-manage-2.3.failure_modes: No path for novel AI failure.
nist-manage-2.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains procedures to respond to newly observed AI risks.

nist-manage-2.4.title: Mechanisms supersede, disengage or deactivate AI systems
nist-manage-2.4.requirement: Mechanisms are in place and applied, and responsibilities are assigned and understood, to supersede, disengage or deactivate AI systems that demonstrate performance or outcomes inconsistent with intended use.
nist-manage-2.4.pb_interpretation: Kill switch and downgrade path — manual disable, revert to search / manual process, feature flag off, customer emergency procedure.
nist-manage-2.4.failure_modes: No graceful way to turn it off.
nist-manage-2.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox includes documented disengagement and fallback procedures.

nist-manage-3.1.title: Third-party AI risks monitored
nist-manage-3.1.requirement: AI risks and benefits from third-party resources are regularly monitored, and risk controls are applied and documented.
nist-manage-3.1.pb_interpretation: Third-party monitoring programme — upstream models, libraries, vulnerability feeds, licence changes, provider notices.
nist-manage-3.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox actively monitors material third-party AI dependencies and related controls.

nist-manage-3.2.title: Pre-trained models monitored
nist-manage-3.2.requirement: Pre-trained models which are used for development are monitored as part of AI system regular monitoring and maintenance.
nist-manage-3.2.pb_interpretation: Pretrained-model surveillance — track model versions, approvals, benchmarks, known limitations, retirement triggers.
nist-manage-3.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox manages approved pre-trained models through a controlled inventory and review process.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility for third-party models.

nist-manage-4.1.title: Post-deployment monitoring plans
nist-manage-4.1.requirement: Post-deployment AI system monitoring plans are implemented, including mechanisms for capturing and evaluating input from users and other relevant AI actors, appeal and override, decommissioning, incident response, recovery and change management.
nist-manage-4.1.pb_interpretation: Post-deployment AI monitoring plan — who monitors, what is logged, how overrides work, how changes are reviewed, how incidents are handled.
nist-manage-4.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports post-deployment monitoring and change governance through documented procedures.

nist-manage-4.2.title: Continual improvement integrated into updates
nist-manage-4.2.requirement: Measurable activities for continual improvements are integrated into AI system updates and include regular engagement with interested parties, including relevant AI actors.
nist-manage-4.2.pb_interpretation: Continuous-improvement cycle — quarterly backlog from incidents, support feedback, benchmark drift, security findings.
nist-manage-4.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses monitoring and stakeholder feedback to drive continual AI control improvement.

nist-manage-4.3.title: Incidents communicated with tracking and recovery
nist-manage-4.3.requirement: Incidents and errors are communicated to relevant AI actors, including affected communities. Processes for tracking, responding to and recovering from incidents and errors are followed and documented.
nist-manage-4.3.pb_interpretation: AI incident communication plan — severity, customer notice rules, internal escalation, RCA, corrective actions, shared-responsibility boundaries.
nist-manage-4.3.failure_modes: Silent handling of harmful outputs or behavioural incidents.
nist-manage-4.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents AI incident handling and communication procedures.

crosswalk.note: AIMS incident communication.
POPIA security safeguards including s 22 breach notification.

nist-po-1.1.title: Security requirements for development infrastructure
nist-po-1.1.requirement: Define security requirements for software development infrastructure and processes.
nist-po-1.1.pb_interpretation: Secure-SDLC requirements for repos, CI/CD, secrets, agents, admin tooling.
nist-po-1.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox operates a documented secure development standard.

crosswalk.note: ISMS secure development lifecycle.

nist-po-1.2.title: Security requirements for organization-developed software
nist-po-1.2.requirement: Define security requirements for organisation-developed software.
nist-po-1.2.pb_interpretation: Product security requirements — logging, authz, data isolation, update integrity, safe defaults.
nist-po-1.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines product security requirements throughout the SDLC.

nist-po-1.3.title: Communicate requirements to suppliers
nist-po-1.3.requirement: Communicate security requirements to third-party suppliers and services.
nist-po-1.3.pb_interpretation: Contract and assess model, library, hosting, update, support, and tooling suppliers.
nist-po-1.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox extends security expectations to relevant third parties.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility.

nist-po-2.1.title: Define SDLC roles and responsibilities
nist-po-2.1.requirement: Define and maintain SDLC roles and responsibilities.
nist-po-2.1.pb_interpretation: Repo owners, security approvers, release approvers, incident lead, supplier owner, support owner.
nist-po-2.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox assigns clear SDLC and security responsibilities.

nist-po-2.2.title: Role-based training
nist-po-2.2.requirement: Provide role-based training to personnel with security responsibilities.
nist-po-2.2.pb_interpretation: Train devs, reviewers, DevOps, support, customer-facing staff on AI-secure development.

nist-po-2.3.title: Management commitment and messaging
nist-po-2.3.requirement: Obtain upper management or authorising official commitment to secure development.
nist-po-2.3.pb_interpretation: Executive sponsorship for secure development and release discipline.
nist-po-2.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox's secure development programme is management-backed.

nist-po-3.1.title: Specify required tools and integration
nist-po-3.1.requirement: Specify the security-related tools and tool integrations that the organisation requires.
nist-po-3.1.pb_interpretation: Approved SDLC toolchain and integration map.
nist-po-3.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses an approved and documented secure toolchain.

nist-po-3.2.title: Securely deploy and operate tools
nist-po-3.2.requirement: Follow recommended security practices to deploy, operate, and maintain tools and toolchains.
nist-po-3.2.pb_interpretation: Harden CI/CD, code repos, issue trackers, package registries.
nist-po-3.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox secures and maintains its development toolchain.

crosswalk.note: ISMS separation of development / test / production.

nist-po-3.3.title: Configure tools to generate artefacts
nist-po-3.3.requirement: Configure tools to generate artefacts supporting secure development.
nist-po-3.3.pb_interpretation: CI/CD emits logs, build records, approvals, scan outputs, signatures.
nist-po-3.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox retains artefacts demonstrating secure development controls.

nist-po-4.1.title: Define criteria for security checks
nist-po-4.1.requirement: Define criteria for software security checks and track them.
nist-po-4.1.pb_interpretation: What scans, tests, reviews, and gates are required by risk tier.
nist-po-4.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies defined release gates and security checks.

nist-po-4.2.title: Exception handling
nist-po-4.2.requirement: Implement processes, mechanisms, etc., to gather and safeguard the necessary information in support of the criteria.
nist-po-4.2.pb_interpretation: Formal exception workflow with expiry, rationale, compensating controls.
nist-po-4.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox manages exceptions through a documented approval process.

nist-po-5.1.title: Protect development environments
nist-po-5.1.requirement: Separate and protect each environment involved in software development.
nist-po-5.1.pb_interpretation: Segregate dev / test / prod; restrict admin; monitor CI/CD and build hosts.
nist-po-5.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox protects development environments against unauthorized access and tampering.

crosswalk.note: ISMS separation of dev / test / prod.

nist-po-5.2.title: Secure development endpoints
nist-po-5.2.pb_interpretation: Harden engineer workstations, support laptops, build runners.
nist-po-5.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox hardens endpoints used for development and support.

nist-ps-1.1.title: Store code with least privilege
nist-ps-1.1.requirement: Store all forms of code — including source code, executable code and configuration-as-code — based on the principle of least privilege so that only authorised personnel, tools, services, etc., have access.
nist-ps-1.1.pb_interpretation: Protect source, config-as-code, prompts, templates, evaluation assets, deployment scripts.
nist-ps-1.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox stores code and configuration under least-privilege controls.

nist-ps-2.1.title: Release integrity verification info available to acquirers
nist-ps-2.1.requirement: Make software integrity verification information available to acquirers.
nist-ps-2.1.pb_interpretation: Provide signatures, hashes, release notes, verification instructions.
nist-ps-2.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox supports verification of delivered release integrity.

nist-ps-3.1.title: Securely archive release files
nist-ps-3.1.requirement: Securely archive the necessary files and supporting data to be retained for each software release.
nist-ps-3.1.pb_interpretation: Archive installers, manifests, configs, signatures, release approvals.
nist-ps-3.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox archives release artefacts for traceability and incident response.

nist-ps-3.2.title: Maintain provenance data for release components (SBOM)
nist-ps-3.2.requirement: Collect, safeguard, maintain and share provenance data for all components of each software release (e.g., in a software bill of materials [SBOM]).
nist-ps-3.2.pb_interpretation: SBOM, build provenance, dependency records, model provenance where relevant.
nist-ps-3.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains provenance data for software components and releases.

nist-pw-1.1.title: Use risk modelling
nist-pw-1.1.requirement: Use forms of risk modelling — such as threat modelling, attack modelling or attack-surface mapping — to help assess the security risk for the software.
nist-pw-1.1.pb_interpretation: Threat model AI workflows, admin planes, RAG, support access, update channel.
nist-pw-1.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses documented threat modelling and risk analysis.

nist-pw-1.2.title: Track security requirements and design decisions
nist-pw-1.2.requirement: Track and maintain the software's security requirements, risks and design decisions.
nist-pw-1.2.pb_interpretation: Design-decision log, accepted risks, approved exceptions.
nist-pw-1.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains design and risk traceability.

nist-pw-1.3.title: Build in standard security features
nist-pw-1.3.requirement: Where appropriate, build in support for using standardised security features and services.
nist-pw-1.3.pb_interpretation: Use standard authn / authz, logging, secrets, encryption, identity integration.
nist-pw-1.3.failure_modes: Custom security where standard controls exist.
nist-pw-1.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses standard security services and patterns wherever feasible.

nist-pw-2.1.title: Review design for compliance
nist-pw-2.1.requirement: Review the software design to verify compliance with security requirements and risk information.
nist-pw-2.1.pb_interpretation: Design review before build / release. Unresolved design issues block progression.
documented_evidence: Signed design review + issue tracker items
nist-pw-2.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox verifies design against defined security requirements before release.

nist-pw-4.1.title: Acquire and maintain well-secured third-party components
nist-pw-4.1.requirement: Acquire and maintain well-secured software components from commercial, open-source and other third-party developers for use by the organisation's software.
nist-pw-4.1.pb_interpretation: Approved dependency process for packages, models, libraries, container bases.
nist-pw-4.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses controlled intake for third-party components.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility allocation.

nist-pw-4.2.title: Maintain provenance for components
nist-pw-4.2.requirement: Create and maintain provenance data for components.
nist-pw-4.2.pb_interpretation: Preserve source, version, licence, digest, origin, build metadata.
nist-pw-4.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox keeps provenance records for key software components.

nist-pw-4.3.title: Verify component integrity
nist-pw-4.3.requirement: Verify the integrity of all software components.
nist-pw-4.3.pb_interpretation: Verify hashes / signatures of dependencies and releases.
nist-pw-4.3.failure_modes: Blind trust in upstream packages / models.
nist-pw-4.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox verifies component integrity before use and release.

nist-pw-4.4.title: Review human-readable third-party software
nist-pw-4.4.requirement: Review human-readable third-party software to confirm it meets security requirements.
nist-pw-4.4.pb_interpretation: Source review for critical libraries, agent tools, deployment scripts.
nist-pw-4.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies risk-based review to material third-party code.

nist-pw-4.5.title: Reuse well-secured internal software
nist-pw-4.5.requirement: Reuse existing, well-secured software components from internal sources where feasible.
nist-pw-4.5.pb_interpretation: Use approved internal components rather than ad hoc reinvention.
nist-pw-4.5.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox reuses approved internal components to reduce avoidable risk.

nist-pw-5.1.title: Follow secure coding practices
nist-pw-5.1.pb_interpretation: Coding standards for auth, input handling, secret use, logging, file parsing, command execution.
nist-pw-5.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox follows documented secure coding practices.

crosswalk.note: ISMS secure coding.

nist-pw-5.2.title: Detect and prevent coding defects
nist-pw-5.2.requirement: Detect and prevent coding defects through automated tooling.
nist-pw-5.2.pb_interpretation: Linters, secret scanning, code quality and security tooling in PR workflow.
nist-pw-5.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses automated checks to support secure coding.

nist-pw-6.1.title: Configure compile / build for executable security
nist-pw-6.1.requirement: Configure compilation, interpreter and build processes to improve executable security.
nist-pw-6.1.pb_interpretation: Harden builds, enable safer defaults, dependency locking, reproducible builds where feasible.
nist-pw-6.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox hardens build and packaging processes.

nist-pw-6.2.title: Document build tools and settings
nist-pw-6.2.requirement: Determine and document appropriate build / packaging tools and settings.
nist-pw-6.2.pb_interpretation: Build policy for compilers, containers, packagers, signing steps, warnings-as-failures where appropriate.
nist-pw-6.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox standardises and documents secure build settings.

nist-pw-7.1.title: Define code review strategy
nist-pw-7.1.requirement: Define and use criteria for code review and analysis.
nist-pw-7.1.pb_interpretation: Peer review depth, SAST rules, high-risk branch protections.
nist-pw-7.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines structured code review and analysis requirements.

nist-pw-7.2.title: Review human-readable code
nist-pw-7.2.requirement: Review human-readable code for vulnerabilities and verify compliance with security requirements.
nist-pw-7.2.pb_interpretation: Peer review, SAST, IaC review, prompt / agent logic review.
nist-pw-7.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox conducts documented code review and static analysis before release.

nist-pw-8.1.title: Define testing strategy
nist-pw-8.1.requirement: Define and use criteria for executable-code testing.
nist-pw-8.1.pb_interpretation: DAST, API tests, integration tests, security tests, environment-specific regression tests.
nist-pw-8.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines release-testing criteria that include security-relevant scenarios.

nist-pw-8.2.title: Test executable code
nist-pw-8.2.requirement: Test executable code and verify compliance with security requirements.
nist-pw-8.2.pb_interpretation: Run automated and manual tests; block release on critical findings.
nist-pw-8.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox validates releases against documented security test criteria.

nist-pw-9.1.title: Define secure default settings
nist-pw-9.1.requirement: Define secure settings by default for software, including configuration values.
nist-pw-9.1.pb_interpretation: Default deny external connectivity, least privilege, telemetry off unless agreed, admin features disabled by default where sensible.
nist-pw-9.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox ships with security-conscious default settings.

nist-pw-9.2.title: Implement and document secure configuration changes
nist-pw-9.2.requirement: Implement and document secure default configuration and changes.
nist-pw-9.2.pb_interpretation: Maintain config guide, hardening guide, and approved change path.
nist-pw-9.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents secure baseline configuration and controlled changes.

nist-rv-1.1.title: Gather vulnerability information
nist-rv-1.1.requirement: Gather information from software acquirers, users and public sources on potential vulnerabilities in the software.
nist-rv-1.1.pb_interpretation: Monitor CVEs, provider notices, AI-specific issue feeds, customer reports, model-library security news.
nist-rv-1.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox continuously monitors relevant vulnerability and security reports.

nist-rv-1.2.title: Review code for undetected vulnerabilities
nist-rv-1.2.requirement: Review, analyse and / or test the organisation's code to identify or confirm the presence of previously undetected vulnerabilities.
nist-rv-1.2.pb_interpretation: Recurring reassessment, including AI-specific tests after changes.
nist-rv-1.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox performs recurring reassessment for previously undetected vulnerabilities.

nist-rv-1.3.title: Vulnerability disclosure and remediation policy
nist-rv-1.3.requirement: Have a policy that addresses vulnerability disclosure and remediation, and implement the roles, responsibilities and processes needed to support that policy.
nist-rv-1.3.pb_interpretation: Public / internal reporting path, response SLAs, coordination rules, remediation workflow.
nist-rv-1.3.failure_modes: No route for external researchers or customers.
nist-rv-1.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains a structured vulnerability disclosure and remediation process.

nist-rv-2.1.title: Analyse vulnerabilities for remediation planning
nist-rv-2.1.requirement: Analyse each vulnerability sufficiently to plan remediation or other response.
nist-rv-2.1.pb_interpretation: Severity, exploitability, affected versions, customer exposure, compensating controls.
nist-rv-2.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox triages vulnerabilities using documented risk criteria.

nist-rv-2.2.title: Plan and implement risk responses
nist-rv-2.2.requirement: Plan and implement risk responses for vulnerabilities.
nist-rv-2.2.pb_interpretation: Patch, workaround, disable feature, rotate credentials, notify customers, publish advisory.
nist-rv-2.2.failure_modes: Fix exists but customers cannot operationalise it.
nist-rv-2.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox defines and communicates risk responses for identified vulnerabilities.

nist-rv-3.1.title: Identify root causes
nist-rv-3.1.pb_interpretation: RCA for code, design, toolchain, model / config, supplier failure.
nist-rv-3.1.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox performs root-cause analysis for material vulnerabilities.

nist-rv-3.2.title: Remediate root causes
nist-rv-3.2.requirement: Remediate root causes through process and tool improvements.
nist-rv-3.2.pb_interpretation: Update standards, training, build checks, review gates, supplier controls.
nist-rv-3.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses vulnerability learnings to strengthen its SDLC.

nist-rv-3.3.title: Capture lessons learned
nist-rv-3.3.requirement: Capture lessons learned and improve practices.
nist-rv-3.3.pb_interpretation: Feed lessons into engineering standards, procurement answers, customer guides.
nist-rv-3.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses structured lessons learned to improve security practices.

nist-rv-3.4.title: Share remediation information
nist-rv-3.4.requirement: Share root-cause and remediation information as appropriate.
nist-rv-3.4.pb_interpretation: Internal knowledge base and customer advisories when relevant.
nist-rv-3.4.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox communicates material remediation information through defined channels.

nist-218a-po-5.3.title: Monitor software execution in dev environments
nist-218a-po-5.3.overlay_modifies: Adds AI-specific dev-environment monitoring on top of SSDF PO.5.1 — covers build runners, training / fine-tuning hosts, model APIs, weights, configs, datasets with alerting on suspicious activity.
nist-218a-po-5.3.requirement: Continuously monitor software execution performance and behaviour in development environments.
nist-218a-po-5.3.pb_interpretation: Monitor build runners, training / fine-tuning hosts, model APIs, weights, configs, datasets. Alerting on suspicious activity.
nist-218a-po-5.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox continuously monitors environments that host AI model development assets.

nist-218a-ps-1.2.title: Protect training / fine-tuning / alignment data
nist-218a-ps-1.2.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF PS.1.1 (code storage least privilege) to specifically cover training, testing, fine-tuning and alignment datasets — segregation, write-access restriction, checksums, approval workflow for dataset changes.
nist-218a-ps-1.2.requirement: Protect training, testing, fine-tuning and alignment data against unauthorised access and modification.
nist-218a-ps-1.2.pb_interpretation: Segregate datasets, restrict write access, checksum critical sets, approval for dataset changes.
nist-218a-ps-1.2.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox protects AI development datasets with controlled access and change records.

nist-218a-ps-1.3.title: Protect model weights and configuration parameters
nist-218a-ps-1.3.overlay_modifies: New 218A task — adds model-weight and configuration-parameter protection on top of SSDF PS.1.1. Separate weights from datasets; least privilege; encryption / hashes / signatures; optional air gap for sensitive weights.
nist-218a-ps-1.3.requirement: Protect model weights and configuration parameters against unauthorised access and modification.
nist-218a-ps-1.3.pb_interpretation: Separate weights from datasets; least privilege; encryption / hashes / signatures; optional air gap for sensitive weights.
nist-218a-ps-1.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies access, integrity and provenance controls to model weights and parameters.

nist-218a-ps-1.1-overlay.title: Code storage extended to AI artefacts
nist-218a-ps-1.1-overlay.overlay_modifies: Clarifies SSDF PS.1.1 to include AI models, model weights, pipelines, reward models, evaluation suites and other AI elements as protected code artefacts.
nist-218a-ps-1.1-overlay.requirement: Treat prompts, system instructions, pipelines, reward models, evaluation suites as protected code artefacts under PS.1.1 controls.
nist-218a-ps-1.1-overlay.pb_interpretation: Protected repositories and registries for AI elements alongside traditional code.
nist-218a-ps-1.1-overlay.failure_modes: Protecting code but not model artefacts.
nist-218a-ps-1.1-overlay.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox protects model artefacts and AI pipeline elements as part of software security.

nist-218a-x-model-provenance.title: Model provenance tracking
nist-218a-x-model-provenance.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF PS.3.2 (provenance for components) to specifically include AI models — track provenance of models, weights, sources, versions, and approvals.
nist-218a-x-model-provenance.requirement: Track provenance of models, weights, sources, versions and approvals.
nist-218a-x-model-provenance.pb_interpretation: Approved model inventory and source provenance package. Model cards, registry, approval records.
nist-218a-x-model-provenance.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox maintains provenance and approval records for approved AI models.

nist-218a-x-dataset-provenance.title: Dataset provenance tracking
nist-218a-x-dataset-provenance.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF PS.3.2 to cover training, testing, fine-tuning and alignment data provenance. Records unknown provenance where applicable.
nist-218a-x-dataset-provenance.requirement: Track provenance of training, testing, fine-tuning and alignment data; record unknown provenance where applicable.
nist-218a-x-dataset-provenance.pb_interpretation: Data-source register and known / unknown provenance flags. Lineage register for training data.
nist-218a-x-dataset-provenance.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox tracks provenance information for AI development data where known and records gaps where it is not.

nist-218a-pw-3.3.title: Adversarial samples in training and testing
nist-218a-pw-3.3.overlay_modifies: Adds 218A recommendation to include adversarial samples in training and testing data to improve attack prevention — most relevant where PB fine-tunes or runs its own classifiers / guardrails.
nist-218a-pw-3.3.requirement: Include adversarial samples in training and testing data to improve attack prevention.
nist-218a-pw-3.3.pb_interpretation: Adversarial prompt library and malicious-document tests. Rerun before major releases.
nist-218a-pw-3.3.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses adversarial test cases to strengthen AI safeguards where technically applicable.

nist-218a-x-prompt-injection.title: Prompt-injection and jailbreak risk testing
nist-218a-x-prompt-injection.overlay_modifies: Highlights AI-specific injection-style attacks not covered by traditional security testing. Direct and indirect prompt injection, malicious retrieved content, connector abuse, rogue instructions in documents.
nist-218a-x-prompt-injection.requirement: Test for direct and indirect prompt injection, malicious retrieved content, connector abuse, and rogue instructions embedded in documents.
nist-218a-x-prompt-injection.pb_interpretation: AI red-team playbooks and results. Regression suite. Both direct prompts and retrieved- content vectors.
nist-218a-x-prompt-injection.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox tests for prompt-injection and jailbreak-style attacks in relevant workflows.

crosswalk.note: AIMS V&V with adversarial testing.

nist-218a-x-unsafe-tool-use.title: Unsafe tool or agent use controls
nist-218a-x-unsafe-tool-use.overlay_modifies: Adds controls for agentic / tool-enabled workflows — scoped actions and execution controls for AI-initiated tool use.
nist-218a-x-unsafe-tool-use.requirement: Restrict tool permissions, sandbox execution, require human approval for sensitive actions, record tool calls.
nist-218a-x-unsafe-tool-use.pb_interpretation: Tool allow-list, sandbox config, logs. Human approval for sensitive actions.
nist-218a-x-unsafe-tool-use.external_safe_claim: Where tool use is enabled, PrivateBox constrains and logs AI-initiated actions.

crosswalk.note: AIMS human oversight for AI tool use.

nist-218a-x-model-release-integrity.title: AI model release integrity
nist-218a-x-model-release-integrity.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF PS.2.1 (release integrity) to specifically cover AI model artefacts — signing, hashing, controlled publication.
nist-218a-x-model-release-integrity.requirement: Sign installers and, where feasible, model artefacts; preserve verification instructions.
nist-218a-x-model-release-integrity.pb_interpretation: Signed releases for software AND model artefacts. Preserve verification instructions for customers / auditors.
nist-218a-x-model-release-integrity.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses integrity controls for software and AI model delivery.

nist-218a-x-model-update-rollback.title: Model update and rollback control
nist-218a-x-model-update-rollback.overlay_modifies: Adds AI model change control beyond standard software change — approval, testing, reversibility, customer advisory.
nist-218a-x-model-update-rollback.requirement: Define model change CAB, regression testing, rollback package, customer advisory.
nist-218a-x-model-update-rollback.pb_interpretation: Change tickets, rollback SOP, drilled rollback.
nist-218a-x-model-update-rollback.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox controls model changes through approval, testing and rollback procedures.

crosswalk.note: AIMS planning of changes including AI-specific change types.

nist-218a-x-secure-finetuning-rag.title: Secure fine-tuning and RAG
nist-218a-x-secure-finetuning-rag.overlay_modifies: Adds controls for fine-tuning, retrieval and data-grounding workflows — additional poisoning and leakage risk beyond standard data security.
nist-218a-x-secure-finetuning-rag.requirement: Separate knowledge stores per tenant; approve ingestion sources; scan documents; bound retrieval scope.
nist-218a-x-secure-finetuning-rag.pb_interpretation: Connector policy, ingestion SOP, malicious-document injection tests.
nist-218a-x-secure-finetuning-rag.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies controls to knowledge ingestion and retrieval-grounded workflows.

nist-218a-x-ai-vuln-intake.title: Vulnerability intake for AI models
nist-218a-x-ai-vuln-intake.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF RV.1.1 to include AI-model and framework vulnerabilities — model-library advisories, AI-framework CVEs, model-incident databases.
nist-218a-x-ai-vuln-intake.requirement: Monitor AI-framework, model and library vulnerability sources and incident databases.
nist-218a-x-ai-vuln-intake.pb_interpretation: Watchlists and ticket feed covering AI-framework and model-library vulnerabilities.
nist-218a-x-ai-vuln-intake.failure_modes: App vulnerabilities tracked, model vulnerabilities ignored.
nist-218a-x-ai-vuln-intake.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox includes AI-model and framework issues in vulnerability monitoring.

nist-218a-x-model-scanning-audits.title: AI model scanning and audits
nist-218a-x-model-scanning-audits.overlay_modifies: Adds recurring AI model security scans and periodic audit review to SSDF RV.1.2.
nist-218a-x-model-scanning-audits.requirement: Regular model security scans, behaviour tests, and periodic audit review.
nist-218a-x-model-scanning-audits.pb_interpretation: Recurring model security testing and audit review per policy cadence.
nist-218a-x-model-scanning-audits.failure_modes: No retesting after retraining or config changes.
nist-218a-x-model-scanning-audits.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox performs recurring model security testing and audit review.

nist-218a-x-ai-disclosure.title: AI-specific disclosure to users
nist-218a-x-ai-disclosure.overlay_modifies: Adds AI-specific disclosure obligations to customers — limitations and reporting mechanisms.
nist-218a-x-ai-disclosure.requirement: Provide safe-use instructions, limitations, issue-reporting route, unsupported use cases.
nist-218a-x-ai-disclosure.pb_interpretation: Customer deployment guide + UI notices conveying AI limitations and issue-reporting.
nist-218a-x-ai-disclosure.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox provides documented limitations and issue-reporting guidance for customers.

nist-218a-x-rebuild-replace.title: Rebuild / replace / retire economic criteria
nist-218a-x-rebuild-replace.overlay_modifies: Adds AI-specific risk-response decision criteria — when to patch, retrain, replace, retire or disable a model given rebuild cost vs risk.
nist-218a-x-rebuild-replace.requirement: Define criteria for patch, retrain, replace, retire, or disable decisions for AI models.
nist-218a-x-rebuild-replace.pb_interpretation: Decision matrix applied to hypothetical compromised model scenarios.
nist-218a-x-rebuild-replace.failure_modes: Keeping a risky model because retraining is expensive.
nist-218a-x-rebuild-replace.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox uses documented criteria for retraining, replacement and retirement of AI models.

nist-218a-x-third-party-model-governance.title: Third-party model governance and monitoring
nist-218a-x-third-party-model-governance.overlay_modifies: Extends SSDF PW.4.1 (acquire well-secured third-party components) to AI-specific intake — transparency artefacts, source commits, checkpoints, licences.
nist-218a-x-third-party-model-governance.requirement: Review third-party model transparency artefacts and monitor external models used in systems.
nist-218a-x-third-party-model-governance.pb_interpretation: Third-party model dossiers — model cards, system cards, source commits, checkpoints, licences, terms.
nist-218a-x-third-party-model-governance.failure_modes: Adopting a model with opaque provenance.
nist-218a-x-third-party-model-governance.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies due diligence to third-party models before approval.

crosswalk.note: AIMS supplier responsibility allocation.

nist-218a-x-content-provenance.title: Generated-content provenance
nist-218a-x-content-provenance.overlay_modifies: Adds 218A and GenAI Profile recommendation for provenance data tracking and synthetic- content transparency where content generation is material.
nist-218a-x-content-provenance.requirement: Where content generation is material, attach provenance metadata or watermark strategy if feasible; document limits if not used.
nist-218a-x-content-provenance.pb_interpretation: Content provenance design note. Document limits where provenance / watermarking is not feasible.
nist-218a-x-content-provenance.failure_modes: Generated content indistinguishable by policy and process.
nist-218a-x-content-provenance.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox documents provenance and transparency measures for generated content where applicable.

nist-218a-x-model-supply-chain.title: AI model supply chain security
nist-218a-x-model-supply-chain.overlay_modifies: Adds 218A model-signing and registry controls to SSDF PW.4.3 (verify component integrity).
nist-218a-x-model-supply-chain.requirement: Use signed internal model registry, controlled mirroring, checksum verification, approval workflow for model intake.
nist-218a-x-model-supply-chain.pb_interpretation: Model registry controls including signed mirroring and approval.
nist-218a-x-model-supply-chain.failure_modes: Model pulled directly from public hub into production workflow.
nist-218a-x-model-supply-chain.external_safe_claim: PrivateBox applies provenance and approval controls to the AI model supply chain.

Addressing security in supplier agreements — DPA flow-down is the privacy form

Secure SDLC + secure architecture + secure coding practices.
Secure SDLC + secure architecture + secure coding.

ISO/IEC 27701:2025 A.3 controls explicitly align to ISO/IEC 27002:2022 themes — same numbering, privacy lens.

scn-27701-a.name: Pure on-prem, no remote support — vendor never touches client data
scn-27701-a.description: PrivateBox is deployed on customer infrastructure with no support layers active. PB has no role for client data; PB is only a controller for its own corporate data.
scn-27701-a.why_role_allocation: Vendor never accesses customer PII when no support layers active.
scn-27701-a.contractual_implication: Customer-only DPA mostly N/A re client data; standard EULA + PB privacy notice for own data.
scn-27701-a.required_clauses_or_terms: Standard EULA + privacy notice for PB-side data only.
scn-27701-a.risk_if_misclassified: Treating PB as 'always processor' inflates obligations and complicates contracts.
scn-27701-a.sales_safe_explanation: When deployed without optional support, PrivateBox does not process client data on the customer's behalf.

direct.note: Scope statement should distinguish this baseline mode.

via_crosswalks.note: Annex SL High-Level Structure — clauses 4-10 are 1:1 across ISO management-system standards.

scn-27701-b.name: On-prem with vendor update delivery
scn-27701-b.description: Update channel is vendor-driven; metadata may flow back. PB acts as processor (limited) for any PII in update telemetry, controller for diagnostics where it sets purposes.
scn-27701-b.why_role_allocation: Update channel is vendor-driven; metadata flow makes PB processor for any PII.
scn-27701-b.contractual_implication: DPA needed if any PII in update telemetry.
scn-27701-b.risk_if_misclassified: If treated as 'no role', any PII leak becomes silent breach.
scn-27701-b.sales_safe_explanation: Updates are delivered via signed channels under documented terms.

direct.note: Customer agreement covers update channel.
Update telemetry must not be repurposed for own use.
Update infra vendors registered as sub-processors.

via_crosswalks.note: Supplier relationships — sub-processor register is privacy operationalisation
Managing security in the ICT supply chain

scn-27701-c.name: On-prem with telemetry
scn-27701-c.description: Telemetry pipeline streams metadata back to PB. PB role depends on telemetry contents: controller for diagnostics PB defines purposes for, processor when customer content present.
scn-27701-c.why_role_allocation: Depends on what telemetry contains and who decides purposes.
scn-27701-c.risk_if_misclassified: Misclassifying as controller-only when content present is a breach exposure.
scn-27701-c.sales_safe_explanation: Telemetry is minimised, configurable, and governed by dual-role terms.

direct.note: Identify and document purposes for telemetry.
Lawful basis for diagnostic processing.
Customer instructions where telemetry contains customer data.
No repurposing of customer content.

scn-27701-d.name: On-prem with support access
scn-27701-d.description: PB engineer accesses customer environment to diagnose; sessions may include access to prompts, outputs, system parameters, customer data.
scn-27701-d.why_role_allocation: Vendor processes customer data on instruction during support sessions.
scn-27701-d.contractual_implication: DPA with explicit support-access terms; per-session approval.
scn-27701-d.required_operational_control: Support access SOP; PAM logs; session recording.
scn-27701-d.risk_if_misclassified: Standing access without DPA = unauthorised processing exposure.
scn-27701-d.sales_safe_explanation: Support sessions are time-boxed, recorded, and approved per request.

direct.note: Customer instructions cover support sessions.
Confidentiality undertakings for support engineers.
DSAR-assist obligations during support.
Notify on disclosure requests.

via_crosswalks.note: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements — same control with privacy emphasis
Privacy and protection of PII — assist obligations are operational instance

scn-27701-e.name: Managed deployment support
scn-27701-e.description: PB performs initial deployment configuration touching customer PII (e.g., directory sync, initial ingestion).
scn-27701-e.why_role_allocation: PB performs configuration touching PII on customer instruction.
scn-27701-e.risk_if_misclassified: Treating as 'consulting' with no DPA = silent breach.
scn-27701-e.sales_safe_explanation: Managed deployment is performed under a processor agreement.

direct.note: DPA covers deployment scope.
Deletion at end of deployment engagement.

scn-27701-f.name: Troubleshooting sessions involving customer data
scn-27701-f.description: Live troubleshooting sessions where PB engineer interactively processes customer PII (reading logs, examining outputs).
scn-27701-f.why_role_allocation: Direct PII processing on customer instruction during session.
scn-27701-f.required_operational_control: Session log; deletion record; per-session approval.
scn-27701-f.risk_if_misclassified: Logs retained beyond session purpose = retention violation.
scn-27701-f.sales_safe_explanation: Troubleshooting sessions follow documented privacy controls.

direct.note: Temp files and session captures must be deleted.

scn-27701-g.name: Document ingestion / RAG setup support
scn-27701-g.description: PB structures and ingests customer documents into RAG corpus; potentially involves sensitive PII at scale.
scn-27701-g.why_role_allocation: PB processes customer documents on customer instruction; setup is processor activity.
scn-27701-g.risk_if_misclassified: RAG corpus retained for own model improvement = no-train violation.
scn-27701-g.sales_safe_explanation: RAG setup is performed under processor terms with no-train commitment.

direct.note: Customer instructions define RAG corpus scope.
No-train commitment for RAG content.
Privacy by design in RAG architecture.

via_crosswalks.note: Secure development lifecycle — PbD is the privacy lens of secure design
Secure system architecture and engineering principles

scn-27701-h.name: Backup support
scn-27701-h.description: PB performs backup operations on customer's behalf; storage and handling of backup data falls under PB's processor scope.
scn-27701-h.why_role_allocation: PB stores/handles backups containing customer PII.
scn-27701-h.risk_if_misclassified: Backups offshore without lawful basis = transfer violation.
scn-27701-h.sales_safe_explanation: Backup support is governed by processor terms with regional storage options.

direct.note: DPA covers backup scope.
Backup deletion at end of contract.

scn-27701-i.name: Integration / connector support
scn-27701-i.description: PB designs and deploys integrations / connectors between PrivateBox and customer systems (CRM, email, file shares); connector design touches PII flow.
scn-27701-i.why_role_allocation: PB designs PII flows on customer instruction; connector implementation is processor activity.
scn-27701-i.sales_safe_explanation: Connectors are deployed after a privacy review.

direct.note: Privacy by design in connector architecture.
Customer instructions define connector scope.

via_crosswalks.note: Secure development lifecycle — PbD is the privacy lens of secure design
Secure system architecture and engineering principles

scn-27701-j.name: Hosted update repository / model package distribution
scn-27701-j.description: PB hosts an update / model-package repository serving customer deployments; metadata (deployment IDs, versions, last-checked timestamps) flows to PB.
scn-27701-j.why_role_allocation: Processor for any PII in metadata; controller for repo-side telemetry purposes PB defines.
scn-27701-j.risk_if_misclassified: Implicit cross-border transfers without lawful basis.
scn-27701-j.sales_safe_explanation: Update repository operations are governed and disclosed.

direct.note: Transfer basis for repo telemetry.
Disclose processing locations to customer.

via_crosswalks.note: Information transfer rules — privacy adds jurisdictional/legal basis layer

scn-27701-k.name: Future Teraco-hosted single-tenant deployment
scn-27701-k.description: PB activates Teraco hosting; customer deploys into a single-tenant ringfenced footprint in Teraco DC. PB acts as processor in full; ISO 27018:2025 obligations attach directly. POPIA s.72 + GDPR Ch. V transfer rules become live for non-SA customers.
scn-27701-k.why_role_allocation: PB operates the host (single-tenant) — full processor scope.
scn-27701-k.required_clauses_or_terms: Customer-key option; data residency in SA; audit rights.
required_evidence: Teraco ISAE 3402 / PCI DSS attestation copies
scn-27701-k.risk_if_misclassified: Mistreating as 'still on-prem' = inheriting wrong controls.
scn-27701-k.sales_safe_explanation: Teraco-hosted PrivateBox operates as a processor with cloud-PII protections aligned to ISO/IEC 27018:2025.

direct.note: Hosting DPA covers entire processor scope.
Exit and portability mechanism critical.
POPIA s.72 + GDPR Ch. V transfer evidence.

scn-eu-a.name: Pure on-prem enterprise chat assistant, customer-supplied data only
scn-eu-a.description: PrivateBox runs locally inside customer's environment. Customer's employees use it for general drafting, Q&A, summarisation. No third-party model invoked over the internet at runtime. No customer data leaves the customer environment.
scn-eu-a.legal_basis: Art 2(1)(a)–(b); Art 3(1) AI system definition; Commission Guidelines on AI System Definition (Feb 2025).
scn-eu-a.on_prem_note: On-prem deployment does not change 'placing on the market' / 'putting into service'. Act applies.
scn-eu-a.shared_responsibility: PrivateBox: Art 4 literacy, Art 5 prohibited-use screening, AI system documentation, Art 50 transparency by design. Customer: deployer-side Art 4 literacy, lawful use, workplace consultation if applicable.
scn-eu-a.common_misclassification: Believing 'on-prem means out of scope'. Wrong.
scn-eu-a.sales_safe_wording: PrivateBox is an AI system within the meaning of Article 3(1) of the EU AI Act and is placed on the market in the EU when delivered to EU customers, regardless of the on-prem deployment topology.

direct.note: Putting into service in the EU.
Both PB and customer have AI literacy duty.
Prohibited-use screening at customer onboarding.
Persistent disclosure that user is interacting with AI.

via_crosswalks.note: Competence — AI literacy is the AI-specific instance of competence requirement
Awareness — AI literacy programme is the AI awareness instance
Competence — AI literacy programme parallels privacy competence
Awareness — AI literacy and privacy awareness share form
Privacy notice — Art 50 disclosure parallels privacy transparency principle

scn-eu-b.name: On-prem enterprise search / RAG over customer documents
scn-eu-b.description: Same as A but with retrieval-augmented generation over customer's own documents. Customer corpus stays local; no internet retrieval.
scn-eu-b.on_prem_note: Same as A — RAG over local corpus does not change scope or topology analysis.
scn-eu-b.shared_responsibility: PrivateBox: same as A plus document-source citation, retrieval guardrails. Customer: same as A plus corpus quality (Art 16 deployer's input-data appropriateness duty applies if high-risk variant).
scn-eu-b.common_misclassification: Treating RAG as 'just search' — outputs are still AI generation under Art 50(2).
scn-eu-b.sales_safe_wording: PrivateBox supports retrieval-augmented generation over customer-supplied documents within the customer environment, with citations preserved at point of use.

direct.note: Same as A.
Same as A.
Same as A.
Same as A; output marking applies to generated summaries.

scn-eu-c.name: PrivateBox embeds a third-party GPAI model unmodified
scn-eu-c.description: PrivateBox bundles an unmodified third-party GPAI model (e.g., Llama, Mistral) as the runtime LLM. Model is shipped to customer environment via the PrivateBox installer.
scn-eu-c.legal_basis: Art 2; Art 3(63) GPAI model definition; Art 53 GPAI provider documentation; Art 25(4) supplier agreements.
scn-eu-c.on_prem_note: Topology irrelevant — GPAI obligations attach to model layer.
scn-eu-c.shared_responsibility: PrivateBox: due diligence on upstream GPAI provider, preserve Annex XII downstream documentation, pass through to customer, do not strip provenance. Upstream GPAI provider: Art 53 documentation duties (their problem, not PB's). Customer: deployer duties.
scn-eu-c.common_misclassification: Confusing GPAI **model** obligations with GPAI **system** obligations. PB ships a system; the model is upstream.
scn-eu-c.sales_safe_wording: PrivateBox documents the third-party general-purpose AI models it integrates and preserves the downstream documentation those providers supply under Article 53.

direct.note: Confirm model is GPAI under Art 3(63) (≥10²³ FLOP + generative).
Pass-through duty: preserve and forward Annex XII docs.
Art 25(4) written-agreement template for upstream supplier.

scn-eu-d.name: PrivateBox fine-tunes / substantially modifies a model and ships modified weights
scn-eu-d.description: PrivateBox takes a base GPAI model, fine-tunes or substantially modifies it, and ships the modified model as part of the platform.
scn-eu-d.legal_basis: Art 25(1)(b) substantial modification; Art 53 (own GPAI duties for modifications); Annex XI, XII; Commission GPAI Guidelines (Jul 2025) one-third compute threshold.
scn-eu-d.shared_responsibility: PrivateBox: documentation of fine-tune dataset, copyright policy for fine-tune data, training-data summary covering the fine-tune, evaluation results, intended purpose; decide whether to sign GPAI Code of Practice. Customer: deployer obligations.
scn-eu-d.common_misclassification: Assuming 'we just fine-tuned' means 'we have no GPAI duties'. Threshold is one-third of compute used to train base.
scn-eu-d.sales_safe_wording: PrivateBox-modified models are accompanied by documentation describing the modifications, the data used for fine-tuning, and a copyright policy applicable to those modifications.

direct.note: Art 25(1)(b) substantial modification triggers GPAI provider role.
Annex XI internal docs + Annex XII downstream pack + copyright policy + training-data summary, all specific to the fine-tune.
AR required if PB is non-EU and triggers GPAI provider role.
Decide whether to sign GPAI CoP.

scn-eu-e.name: Customer uses PrivateBox to build an HR screening / ranking tool
scn-eu-e.description: Customer uses PrivateBox to ingest CVs and produce candidate rankings, sift, scoring, or interview questions personalised to candidates.
scn-eu-e.legal_basis: Annex III(4)(a)–(b) recruitment/selection; Art 6(2); Art 6(3) profiling carve-out unavailable; Art 25(1)(c) role-flip.
scn-eu-e.on_prem_note: On-prem does not lower the risk class.
scn-eu-e.shared_responsibility: PrivateBox: clear instructions stating the system is **not intended** for high-risk Annex III use without further deployer assessment; technical preconditions (logging, oversight) for high-risk operation; pass-through GPAI documentation. Customer: full Article 9-15 compliance, Art 49 registration, Art 27 FRIA, Art 26(7) worker information.
scn-eu-e.common_misclassification: Letting the customer slip into Annex III use without acknowledging it; PB pulled into Art 25(1)(c) provider status because a salesperson said 'yes you can use it for that'.
scn-eu-e.sales_safe_wording: PrivateBox is not intended to be used as a high-risk AI system under Annex III of the EU AI Act, including for HR screening, ranking, or selection. Customers who choose to operate PrivateBox in such a use case become providers of a high-risk AI system under Article 25(1)(c) and must independently meet the obligations in Articles 9-15, 16, 26, 27, and 49.

direct.note: Annex III(4) classification.
Art 25(1)(c) role-flip: customer becomes provider of high-risk system.
Customer-side deployer duties.
FRIA required.
Customer registers in EU database.

scn-eu-f.name: Customer uses PrivateBox for credit scoring / insurance pricing decisions
scn-eu-f.description: Customer (bank or insurer) uses PrivateBox to support creditworthiness decisions or pricing of life/health insurance for natural persons.
scn-eu-f.legal_basis: Annex III(5)(b)/(c) creditworthiness, life/health insurance pricing; Art 25(1)(c); DORA in parallel.
scn-eu-f.on_prem_note: Reduces sub-processor and DORA-third-party risks but does not lower AI Act class.
scn-eu-f.shared_responsibility: PrivateBox: instructions excluding such use without sign-off; logging hooks; deployer oversight tooling. Customer: full provider obligations, FRIA (financial actors listed in Art 27), registration.
scn-eu-f.common_misclassification: Same as E. Customer may try to claim 'fraud detection' carve-out incorrectly — narrow.
scn-eu-f.sales_safe_wording: PrivateBox is not intended to be used to evaluate the creditworthiness of natural persons or to assess risk and price life or health insurance. Customers who use PrivateBox for those purposes assume provider obligations for a high-risk AI system.

direct.note: Annex III(5)(b)/(c).
Role-flip same as E.
FRIA required for financial actors.

scn-eu-g.name: Healthcare / medical decision support
scn-eu-g.description: Customer (hospital, clinic, pharma) uses PrivateBox for clinical decision support, triage, or patient-facing chat.
scn-eu-g.legal_basis: Art 6(1) + Annex I (MDR/IVDR safety components); Annex III(5)(d) eligibility for essential public services.
scn-eu-g.on_prem_note: Reduces patient-data exposure. Does not by itself lower risk class.
scn-eu-g.shared_responsibility: PrivateBox: written instructions for use, AUP excluding medical-device incorporation absent contractual route, logging hooks. Customer: clinical validation, MDR/IVDR conformity if applicable. Note Art 25(3): if customer integrates into a regulated medical device, the device manufacturer becomes the provider of the AI system.
scn-eu-g.common_misclassification: Underestimating MDR/IVDR overlap. Treating 'administrative' hospital tools as not high-risk when in fact they touch eligibility decisions.
scn-eu-g.sales_safe_wording: PrivateBox is not a medical device and is not intended to be a safety component of any product subject to EU medical-device legislation. Use cases requiring conformity under MDR/IVDR or under Annex III(5)(d) require a separate scoping discussion.

direct.note: Annex I + Annex III(5)(d).
Art 25(3) device-manufacturer-as-provider rule.
FRIA where public-sector patient services.

via_crosswalks.note: Supplier relationships — Art 25(4) written agreements
Addressing security in supplier agreements
Contracts with processors — Art 25(4) written agreements
Joint controllership — analogous role-allocation discipline
Supplier security

scn-eu-h.name: Public-facing chatbot / customer-service assistant
scn-eu-h.description: PrivateBox is exposed to end-users (customers, citizens) as a chatbot or assistant.
scn-eu-h.legal_basis: Art 50(1) provider duty; Art 50(2) synthetic-output marking; Art 50(4) deepfake/public-interest text (deployer side).
scn-eu-h.on_prem_note: Irrelevant to Art 50: duty travels with system and deployer's use, not hosting model.
scn-eu-h.shared_responsibility: PrivateBox: in-product disclosure UI; machine-readable marking of generated outputs; persistent disclosure surface; technical guidance for deployer. Customer: deepfake labelling (Art 50(4)); ensuring users informed at first interaction (Art 50(5)).
scn-eu-h.common_misclassification: Treating 'obvious it's AI' as universal carve-out — burden of proof is on the provider.
scn-eu-h.sales_safe_wording: PrivateBox surfaces a clear and persistent indication that users are interacting with an AI system, and where it generates synthetic content, marks outputs in a machine-readable format. The product is designed in alignment with Article 50, applicable from 2 August 2026.

direct.note: Art 50(1) provider duty + Art 50(2) marking.

via_crosswalks.note: Privacy notice — Art 50 disclosure parallels privacy transparency principle

scn-eu-i.name: Content generation / synthetic media features
scn-eu-i.description: PrivateBox produces drafts, summaries, images, audio, or video.
scn-eu-i.legal_basis: Art 50(2) machine-readable marking; Art 50(4) deepfakes/public-interest text; Code of Practice on Marking and Labelling (voluntary).
scn-eu-i.on_prem_note: Marking duty applies regardless of hosting topology.
scn-eu-i.shared_responsibility: PrivateBox: marking pipeline (metadata, watermark, fingerprint depending on modality); provenance preservation; configurable export-time options. Customer: deepfake labelling at publication; editorial review for public-interest text.
scn-eu-i.common_misclassification: Stripping upstream provenance metadata. Skipping marking for 'low-risk' modalities. Misusing the assistive-editing carve-out.
scn-eu-i.sales_safe_wording: PrivateBox marks AI-generated outputs in a machine-readable manner, in line with Article 50(2) of the EU AI Act and the technical approach reflected in the European Commission's draft Code of Practice on marking and labelling of AI-generated content (currently voluntary).

direct.note: Art 50(2) provider duty for synthetic outputs.

via_crosswalks.note: Privacy notice — Art 50 disclosure parallels privacy transparency principle

scn-eu-j.name: PrivateBox-hosted model packaging / update delivery
scn-eu-j.description: PrivateBox ships periodic updates: model upgrades, fine-tunes, security patches, prompt-template changes. Updates are pushed (with customer consent) over a managed channel.
scn-eu-j.legal_basis: Art 16 provider obligations; Art 18 record-keeping; Art 20 corrective actions; Art 72 post-market monitoring.
scn-eu-j.on_prem_note: On-prem update channels often use signed packages and outbound-only fetch — *reduces* attack surface but creates evidence requirement: integrity, provenance, change-control traceability.
scn-eu-j.shared_responsibility: PrivateBox: change-control pipeline, signed releases, release notes describing AI-functional changes, regression tests, rollback. Customer: receive, test, deploy, log.
scn-eu-j.common_misclassification: Silent 'improvement' pushes that change behaviour without notice. Treating prompt-template changes as not material.
scn-eu-j.sales_safe_wording: PrivateBox uses a controlled, signed update channel. Each release includes versioned release notes that describe AI-functional changes, supporting deployer-side change-control and post-market obligations under Article 72 of the AI Act where applicable.

direct.note: Update channel is part of post-market monitoring.

scn-eu-k.name: Remote support / temporary access by PrivateBox personnel

scn-eu-m.name: Optional web search / external retrieval (RAG over the live web)
scn-eu-m.description: Customer enables an optional connector that lets the assistant fetch live web content.
scn-eu-m.legal_basis: Art 13 transparency (high-risk); Art 50 transparency duties; Art 53(1)(c) copyright (GPAI providers, not runtime web fetch — but customers will ask).

scn-kingv-founder-transition.notes: Key-person risk realised; institutional memory and documented authority become critical.

scn-soc-2-type-i.name: Type I attestation — point-in-time design
scn-soc-2-type-i.description: SOC 2 Type I report addresses whether the system description is presented in accordance with the description criteria and whether controls were suitably designed as of a point in time. No operating-effectiveness evidence required.
scn-soc-2-type-i.notes: Suitable as first-time SOC 2 milestone; ~6-month preparation cycle. Type II follow-up typical.

direct.note: All CC criteria in scope by design.
Risk-to-control mapping is the primary Type I artefact.

via_crosswalks.note: ISMS management responsibilities; ethical-conduct overlay.
King V Principle 2 ethics + Practice 12 code of ethics.
King V Principle 2 ethics + Practice 12 code of ethics.

scn-soc-2-type-ii-short.name: Type II attestation — 3-month observation period (first-time)
scn-soc-2-type-ii-short.description: First-time Type II report covering a short observation period (typically 3 months). Establishes operating-effectiveness baseline; report acceptable for procurement but with explicit period caveat.
scn-soc-2-type-ii-short.notes: Public examples show 3-month and 11-month and 12-month periods in use.

direct.note: Monitoring activities must be evidenced over the period.
Procedure execution evidence for the period.

via_crosswalks.note: ISMS monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation.
King V periodic assurance over technology.

scn-soc-2-type-ii-full.name: Type II attestation — 11-12 month observation period (mature)
scn-soc-2-type-ii-full.description: Mature Type II report covering full annual cycle. Procurement-preferred. Subsequent reports may "bridge" between periods.
scn-soc-2-type-ii-full.notes: Standard for procurement diligence at scale; bridge letters between periods.

direct.note: Annual monitoring cycle traced through evidence.
Change management evidence across releases.

via_crosswalks.note: ISMS monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation.

ISMS change management.

AIMS planning of changes + documentation of AI system development.
