scn-soc-2-subservice-carveout.description: PrivateBox identifies the subservice (e.g., update delivery, ticketing, code signing) in the system description but the auditor does not test the subservice's controls. The customer is expected to obtain assurance over the subservice (typically via that subservice's own SOC 2 report).
scn-soc-2-subservice-carveout.notes: Default treatment for most PrivateBox subservices; reduces audit scope.

direct.note: Vendor due diligence covers carved-out subservices.

scn-soc-2-subservice-inclusive.name: Subservice organisation — inclusive treatment
scn-soc-2-subservice-inclusive.description: PrivateBox treats the subservice as part of its own system; the auditor tests subservice controls or relies on subservice management's representations. Typical only where PrivateBox has significant operational control over the subservice.
scn-soc-2-subservice-inclusive.notes: Rare for PrivateBox; only for tightly-integrated subservices where audit visibility is high.

direct.note: Subservice subject to PB-led vendor governance.

scn-nist-rmf-baseline-profile.description: PrivateBox applies AI RMF 1.0 outcomes to its on-prem AI platform as a general AI system. GenAI Profile considerations applied where generative features are material.

direct.note: Legal / regulatory register covers AI-specific obligations.
Intended-use register per deployment.

via_crosswalks.note: AIMS context (Clause 4.1) parallels AI RMF GOV 1.1 legal / regulatory understanding.
AIMS context + intended-use enforcement.

scn-nist-rmf-genai-profile.name: AI RMF GenAI Profile (NIST AI 600-1) activated
scn-nist-rmf-genai-profile.description: Generative AI features (chat, summarisation, generation) trigger the GenAI Profile (NIST AI 600-1, Jul 2024). Adds CBRN, confabulation, intellectual-property, harmful-content, environmental impact, human-AI configuration considerations.
scn-nist-rmf-genai-profile.notes: PB's primary AI feature set is generative; profile applies.

direct.note: GenAI security testing (jailbreak, prompt injection) becomes mandatory.
Privacy risk in GenAI prompts and outputs.

via_crosswalks.note: AIMS V&V and robustness with adversarial testing.

POPIA processing limitation + s 71 automated decisions.

scn-nist-ssdf-readiness.name: SSDF 1.1 readiness for US federal procurement (M-22-18 / M-23-16)
scn-nist-ssdf-readiness.description: SSDF 1.1 self-attestation per OMB M-22-18 / M-23-16 — required for software sold to US federal agencies. PB attests its own production of the platform per CISA's SSDF attestation form.

direct.note: Foundational PO tasks must be attested.
SBOM / provenance is the M-22-18 high-bar item.

via_crosswalks.note: ISMS secure development lifecycle.

scn-nist-218a-overlay-activated.name: 218A AI Overlay activated for AI model pipeline
scn-nist-218a-overlay-activated.description: SP 800-218A overlay applied on top of SSDF for PB's AI model pipeline — training data protection, model weight protection, adversarial testing, AI vulnerability intake.
scn-nist-218a-overlay-activated.notes: Activated when PB does any fine-tuning or guardrail-model production.

direct.note: Training / fine-tuning data protected against unauthorised modification.
Model weights protected.

scn-nist-incident-disclosure.name: AI-specific incident disclosure pathway
scn-nist-incident-disclosure.description: Behavioural AI incident (harmful output, prompt-injection breach, model exfiltration attempt) triggers cross-framework notification — MANAGE 4.3 + SSDF RV.1.3 + EU AI Act Art. 73 + POPIA s 22 where personal information involved.
scn-nist-incident-disclosure.notes: Aligns with established EU AI Act 15-day and POPIA 72-hour windows.

direct.note: Communication plan invoked.
VDP coordination.

via_crosswalks.note: AIMS incident communication.
POPIA security safeguards including s 22 breach notification.

oq-partner-dc-selection.question: Which DC partner(s) for AI Factory deployments?
oq-partner-dc-selection.notes: Partner DC selection cascades into physical perimeter, entry, monitoring, environmental, equipment siting, utilities, cabling, and maintenance controls. Until selected, AI Factory ownership of A.7 controls is pending.

oq-ai-factory-jurisdiction.question: Which legal jurisdiction(s) for AI Factory hosting?
oq-ai-factory-jurisdiction.notes: Jurisdiction determines applicable laws (POPIA only vs POPIA+GDPR+others), cross-border transfer rules, and data residency commitments to customers.

oq-ai-factory-tenancy-model.question: Tenancy isolation posture for AI Factory?
candidate_answers: Single-tenant ringfenced (1 customer per ringfenced infra)
Hybrid (single-tenant for high-sensitivity, multi-tenant for others)
oq-ai-factory-tenancy-model.notes: Tenancy model determines multi-tenant isolation control posture, test data handling, and environment separation requirements.

oq-ai-factory-subprocessor-governance.question: How is the partner DC governed contractually as a sub-processor?
oq-ai-factory-subprocessor-governance.notes: Partner DC becomes a sub-processor under AI Factory model. Contractual posture determines supplier governance controls applicable to that relationship.

oq-ai-factory-data-residency.question: Data residency commitments to AI Factory customers?
oq-ai-factory-data-residency.notes: Residency commitments shape customer-facing claims, contract templates, and constrain DC partner selection. Cross-references oq-partner-dc-selection.

oq-ai-factory-incident-response-split.question: Who handles which incidents in the AI Factory operational stack?
candidate_answers: PB owns all incidents, partner DC notifies PB
Layered: partner DC owns physical/network, PB owns app/AI
oq-ai-factory-incident-response-split.notes: Incident response split affects shared-responsibility documentation, incident playbooks, and customer-facing incident notification commitments.

oq-ai-factory-customer-contract-template.question: What does the customer contract look like for AI Factory?
candidate_answers: Adapted on-prem template with hosting addendum
oq-ai-factory-customer-contract-template.notes: Contract template determines what PB commits to, what's customer-owned, what's the partner DC's obligation, and how regulatory/legal duties allocate.

oq-ai-factory-offering-definition.question: What exactly is the AI Factory product?
candidate_answers: Ringfenced seats on shared partner DC infra
Dedicated server(s) per customer at partner DC
Customer-owned hardware co-located at partner DC
oq-ai-factory-offering-definition.notes: The AI Factory product definition cascades into nearly every framework. Until defined, AI Factory scope decisions are placeholders.

oq-ai-factory-exit-portability.question: Customer exit / data portability mechanism for AI Factory?
oq-ai-factory-exit-portability.notes: Exit/portability affects continuity, deletion controls, customer trust, and contract termination clauses.

oq-ai-factory-hardware-lifecycle.question: Hardware lifecycle for AI Factory (procurement, sizing, disposal)?
candidate_answers: PB procures and owns; partner DC operates
Partner DC procures; PB pays operating fee
oq-ai-factory-hardware-lifecycle.notes: Hardware lifecycle determines secure disposal posture, equipment maintenance responsibilities, and storage media controls.

oq-27701-certification-target.question: Pursue accredited ISO 27701:2025 certification, and on what timeline?
candidate_answers: Yes — within 12 months (alongside or after 27001:2022 cert)
Defer until first qualified procurement demand
No — operate in alignment without seeking accredited cert
oq-27701-certification-target.notes: Drives investment levels in evidence quality, internal audit independence, and management review formality. Without a target cert date, there's no forcing function on evidence.

oq-27701-dpo-appointment.question: Formal Privacy Lead/DPO designation — internal or outsourced?
candidate_answers: Internal Privacy Lead with outsourced fractional DPO
oq-27701-dpo-appointment.notes: Required for procurement DDQs. Conflict-free reporting line to management is the discriminator.

oq-27701-no-train-commitment.question: Is the 'no customer-content training' commitment engineering-true and contractually enforceable?
candidate_answers: Yes — engineering attestation + DPA clause + telemetry inspection on demand
Partial — DPA clause yes, engineering controls in flight
No — currently rely on customer trust without technical enforcement
oq-27701-no-train-commitment.notes: The 'no-train' claim is the single most procurement-relevant differentiator for AI vendors. Vague claims here are a red flag in any DDQ.

oq-27701-cmk-support.question: Customer-managed key (CMK) support — default or option?
candidate_answers: Vendor-managed default with CMK upgrade path
oq-27701-cmk-support.notes: Confidential-firm customers (legal, finance) often require CMK. Drives KMS architecture decisions and customer-facing key-management UX.

oq-27701-subprocessor-list.question: What goes on the public sub-processor register?
candidate_answers: All upstream model API providers + monitoring + support tooling vendors
Only those that touch customer PII (excluding vendor-internal tooling)
Defer publication until first accredited cert
oq-27701-subprocessor-list.notes: Public sub-processor register is procurement-table-stakes. Stale or selectively-maintained registers fail DDQ scrutiny.

oq-27701-teraco-activation.question: When does Teraco hosting activate (triggers ISO 27018:2025)?
oq-27701-teraco-activation.notes: Teraco-hosted scenario activates 27018:2025 directly. POPIA s.72 + GDPR Ch. V transfer rules become live for non-SA customers hosted there.

oq-eu-customer-base.question: Do we have or actively pursue EU customers?
candidate_answers: Yes — EU is a primary target market
oq-eu-customer-base.notes: Determines applicability scope. Even one EU customer brings entire framework into scope. Outputs used in EU also count under Art 2(1)(c) extraterritorial reach.

oq-eu-ar-appointment.question: Will we appoint an EU Authorised Representative?
candidate_answers: Yes — for any high-risk variant (mandatory under Art 22)
Yes — for GPAI provider role (mandatory under Art 54 if triggered)
Voluntary EU contact for non-high-risk customer expectation
oq-eu-ar-appointment.notes: Required for non-EU providers shipping high-risk systems or GPAI models to EU. Even where not legally required, EU customers expect an EU contact.

oq-eu-high-risk-sku.question: Will we offer a high-risk-ready SKU/variant?
candidate_answers: Yes — productise a high-risk-ready configuration with full Art 8-15 compliance
No — explicitly out of scope; route customers to refusal or contractual carve-out
oq-eu-high-risk-sku.notes: Determines whether Articles 8-17 + 49 are in scope as binding obligations or as aspirational scaffolding. Strong commercial decision; impacts AUP, MSA, and product roadmap.

oq-eu-gpai-cop-signing.question: Will we sign the GPAI Code of Practice if we trigger GPAI provider status?
candidate_answers: Yes — sign at first GPAI provider trigger
No — demonstrate compliance by other adequate means
Decide per case based on upstream supplier signatures
oq-eu-gpai-cop-signing.notes: GPAI CoP is voluntary but creates presumption of compliance. Non-signatories must demonstrate compliance 'by other adequate means' — heavier evidence burden.

oq-eu-fine-tune-policy.question: Will we ship fine-tuned models, and if so, who owns the GPAI provider duties?
candidate_answers: We do not fine-tune; only ship third-party models unmodified
We fine-tune below the one-third compute threshold (no GPAI provider role)
We fine-tune above threshold and assume GPAI provider duties
Customer fine-tunes, customer owns GPAI provider role for modifications
oq-eu-fine-tune-policy.notes: One-third compute threshold from Commission GPAI Guidelines (Jul 2025). Determines whether PB triggers GPAI provider role for modifications.

oq-eu-art-50-marking-tech.question: What technical approach for Art 50(2) machine-readable output marking?
candidate_answers: Wait for final Code of Practice (June 2026) and adopt then
oq-eu-art-50-marking-tech.notes: Art 50(2) binding from Aug 2026. Code of Practice on Marking and Labelling — first draft Dec 2025, second draft Mar 2026, final June 2026.

oq-eu-aup-template.question: What is the AUP template for EU deployments?
candidate_answers: Single global AUP with EU-specific addendum
oq-eu-aup-template.notes: AUP is the primary contractual mechanism for excluding Annex III use, preventing Art 25(1)(c) role-flip surprises, and screening Art 5 prohibited practices.

oq-42001-annex-numbering.question: How does PrivateBox align Annex A control numbering with the published ISO/IEC 42001:2023 standard?
candidate_answers: Adopt the PECB-aligned 39-control numbering used in source pack §5
Adopt the published ISO numbering once the official PDF is licensed
Adopt a hybrid PB-internal numbering with mapping table to ISO
oq-42001-annex-numbering.notes: Source §5 explicitly flags 38 vs 39 vs 42 control discrepancies across secondary sources. Authoritative count is in the paywalled ISO PDF. Interim: this YAML uses the PECB-aligned numbering from source §5; SoA must align to published numbering before Stage 1 audit. v1 YAML carries 39 Annex A controls.

oq-42001-cb-selection.question: Which accredited certification body will PrivateBox engage for ISO/IEC 42001 certification?
oq-42001-cb-selection.notes: Choice is partly geographic (UK, EU, ZA, US presence) and partly impartiality (any CB used for advisory cannot then audit — see source §13). Decision affects audit-time calculation per ISO/IEC 42006 and the timing of Stage 1 readiness. Track in roadmap §16 LATER bucket.

oq-42001-cert-scope.question: What subset of Annex A controls comprises PrivateBox's Stage-1 certification scope?
candidate_answers: Source §16 NOW phase subset (A.2 + A.3 + AIMS scope + risk methodology + AI Policy)
Full Annex A coverage (most rigorous, longer Stage 1)
Phased scope expanding by surveillance cycle
oq-42001-cert-scope.notes: PB's commercial position favours product-scoped certification (per case studies — AWS, Google, Workday, Microsoft Service Trust Portal pattern in §14). Setting maturity_target:Cert on the Stage-1 subset commits PB publicly. Until decided, all requirements default maturity_target:AR.

oq-42001-42005-separate.question: Should ISO/IEC 42005 be promoted to a separate framework YAML?
candidate_answers: Keep as companion only (current state); 42005's 10-element AISIA model is absorbed into Clause 6.1.4 and A.5 controls.
Author iso-42005.yaml as a separate framework so the OS can render an AISIA-specific view.
oq-42001-42005-separate.notes: Promoting to separate framework gives the OS a dedicated AISIA-methodology view (useful for procurement when buyers ask "show me your impact-assessment process"). Current state suffices for v1; revisit when AISIA Methodology is published.

oq-42001-king-v-trigger.question: When does PrivateBox add King V as a framework and activate AI-related crosswalks?
candidate_answers: Now (King V effective for financial years from 2026-01-01; PB FY2026 starts soon)
At authoring of king-v.yaml; pending crosswalks captured in PENDING-CROSSWALKS.md until then
oq-42001-king-v-trigger.notes: King V is binding for SA companies effective FYs starting on/after 2026-01-01. PB's board narrative (source §17) already references King V principles. Pending crosswalks captured in frameworks/iso-42001/PENDING-CROSSWALKS.md.

oq-42001-iso-pdf-license.question: When does PrivateBox license the official ISO/IEC 42001:2023 PDF (and 42005, 42006)?
candidate_answers: When current PB-curated source pack reaches a known gap
oq-42001-iso-pdf-license.notes: Source §3 caveat — current authoring is from PB-curated authoritative summaries (NIST crosswalk, Schellman/BSI/DNV/A-LIGN guidance). Final claim language and SoA numbering require the official paywalled text. Resolve before any external Stage-1 readiness review or accredited audit.

oq-popia-io-registration.question: Is PrivateBox's Information Officer designated and registered with the Information Regulator?
candidate_answers: Designated and registered (current state should be verified)
oq-popia-io-registration.decision_owner: Top Management + AI Governance Lead (potentially same as IO)
oq-popia-io-registration.notes: Section 55 requires designation; Section 56 + Regulation 4 set out the duties. Registration with the Information Regulator is a public filing — buyers verify it. Active operating cadence (training, awareness, framework review) is the additional bar.

oq-popia-paia-manual.question: Does PrivateBox publish a current PAIA Manual and access-request forms?
oq-popia-paia-manual.notes: PAIA Manual is hygiene-factor evidence buyers expect within 5 minutes of asking. Form-based request handling links to POPIA s 23-25 rights workflow.

oq-popia-cross-border-routing.question: Which countries are approved for PrivateBox support engineer access to customer environments?
candidate_answers: SA + named approved countries with safeguards
Per-deal scoping with no default approved list
oq-popia-cross-border-routing.notes: Section 72 transfer controls require adequacy / binding agreement / consent. Default geography decision must precede customer engagements; per-deal scoping creates audit complexity.

oq-popia-subprocessor-chain.question: What is PrivateBox's documented sub-operator / sub-processor chain and customer-notice approach?
candidate_answers: Maintain a public sub-processor list with email-on-change notifications
Maintain an NDA-gated sub-processor list with per-customer notification
Per-deal disclosure with no maintained list
oq-popia-subprocessor-chain.notes: Section 21 requires sub-operator authorisation by RP. Public sub-processor lists (Workday, Microsoft, Anthropic patterns) are procurement-friendly but require maintenance discipline.

oq-popia-marketing-posture.question: What is PrivateBox's POPIA-aligned posture for B2B direct marketing?
candidate_answers: Strict opt-in for all electronic marketing including B2B
Soft opt-in (existing customer exception) per s 69(3)
Per-channel decision (email vs SMS vs phone)
oq-popia-marketing-posture.notes: Section 69 has electronic-marketing rules with limited customer exception. B2B is in scope. Posture decision affects CRM workflow, opt-in records, suppression list maintenance.

oq-popia-special-data-profile.question: Does PrivateBox publish a documented "special data deployment profile" for healthcare, pharmacy, education customers?
candidate_answers: Yes — per-sector profile with restricted-control configurations
Refer to customer scoping engagement only
oq-popia-special-data-profile.notes: Sections 26-33 (special PI) + 34-35 (children). Healthcare, pharmacy and education are PB target sectors. Generic templates are a finding pattern in these deployments.

oq-popia-codes-of-conduct.question: Does PrivateBox align to or contribute to a sectoral Code of Conduct under POPIA Chapter 7?
candidate_answers: Track and align where one exists (e.g., financial services Code)
oq-popia-codes-of-conduct.notes: POPIA Chapter 7 (ss 60-68) provides for sectoral Codes of Conduct. Aligning to a Code can shape sector-specific obligations. Current SA Code landscape is sparse; tracking is sufficient for v1.

oq-popia-prior-authorisation.question: Has PrivateBox screened its current customer use cases for prior-authorisation triggers?
oq-popia-prior-authorisation.notes: Sections 57-58 trigger prior authorisation for specific high-risk processing (unique identifier linking, criminal-behaviour processing for third parties, credit reporting, certain foreign transfers). Pre-sales screening is the intervention.

oq-kingv-independent-ned-appointment.question: When does PrivateBox appoint its first independent NED, and what is the target trajectory for board independence?
candidate_answers: Within 6 months (with Lead Independent designation in interim)
Tied to Series B / institutional investment milestone
oq-kingv-independent-ned-appointment.notes: First independent NED is the foundational governance signal. Until appointed, Lead Independent NED designation is the compensating measure if founder is Chair. Source §14 "Now (0-90 days)" recommends immediate appointment.

oq-kingv-committee-split-timing.question: At what stage does PrivateBox split combined committees into separate Audit, Risk, Technology, S&E, and Remuneration committees?
candidate_answers: At PI Score threshold (statutory S&E split mandatory)
Phased — Audit first, then Risk/Technology, then S&E and Remuneration
oq-kingv-committee-split-timing.notes: Combined committees are proportionate at PB's current stage but architecture must pre-build the split so the PI Score crossing is incremental, not disruptive.

oq-kingv-cosec-arrangement.question: Cosec-as-a-service vs in-house company secretary?
candidate_answers: Hybrid — outsourced filings, in-house governance support
oq-kingv-cosec-arrangement.notes: Source §14 "Now" recommends cosec-as-a-service. Practice 84-90 governance support function must be arms-length from management.

oq-kingv-ai-oversight-constitution.question: How is the AI Oversight Working Group constituted and what does it formally report into?
candidate_answers: Cross-functional management group reporting into Audit/Risk/Technology committee
AI oversight responsibility absorbed into existing CTO mandate
oq-kingv-ai-oversight-constitution.notes: Source pack §7 recommends cross-functional management working group reporting into Audit/Risk/Tech committee — proportionate substitute for a full AI committee. Practice 109c human oversight requirements must trace through the working group's records.

oq-kingv-disclosure-publication.question: When does PrivateBox publish its first King V Governance Disclosure?
candidate_answers: v0.5 draft now (early-adoption signal in Trust Pack)
Full v1.0 within 12 months (with FY2026 reporting cycle)
Wait for FY2026 reports issued in 2027 (mandatory cycle)
oq-kingv-disclosure-publication.notes: Source §1 recommends early-adoption signal in Trust Pack from now. First mandatory cycle is FY2026 reports issued in 2027. Publishing earlier is procurement-friendly.

oq-kingv-pi-score-current.question: What is PrivateBox's current Public Interest Score and projected threshold-crossing date?
candidate_answers: Approaching Reg 43 statutory S&E threshold (projected within 12 months)
oq-kingv-pi-score-current.notes: PI Score is calculated per Companies Act formula (staff, turnover, beneficial interests, third-party liabilities). Knowing current value and trajectory enables architecture-ahead-of- requirement planning.

oq-kingv-internal-audit-arrangement.question: How does PrivateBox staff internal audit — co-sourced, outsourced, or build in-house?
oq-kingv-internal-audit-arrangement.notes: Practice 131c permits co-sourced for a scaling firm. External quality review every 5 years (Practice 131f) applies regardless of arrangement.

oq-kingv-founder-succession.question: Is the founder-CEO succession plan documented and refreshed?
oq-kingv-founder-succession.notes: Practice 79 explicit. Investor diligence pre-Series B will demand evidence. Key-person risk materialises if not documented.

oq-soc-2-type-i-or-direct-ii.question: Does PrivateBox pursue Type I first, then Type II, or go straight to Type II?
candidate_answers: Type I first (3-6 months), then Type II (covering 3-12 months)
Straight to Type II (3-month initial period, then 12-month subsequent periods)
Bridge approach (Type I plus simultaneous Type II observation)
oq-soc-2-type-i-or-direct-ii.notes: Type I provides faster market signal but requires Type II follow-up for procurement credibility. Many vendors do Type I → Type II in sequence over 9-12 months.

oq-soc-2-service-auditor.question: Which CPA firm performs the SOC 2 attestation?
candidate_answers: EY / KPMG / PwC / Deloitte (Big 4; high enterprise prestige)
oq-soc-2-service-auditor.notes: Service auditor choice affects cost, audit-time, prestige signal, and depth of process. Impartiality: any firm used for advisory cannot then perform the attestation engagement.

oq-soc-2-additional-categories.question: Which additional Trust Services categories beyond Security are in scope?
candidate_answers: Security + Confidentiality (procurement-friendly for legal / finance / healthcare)
oq-soc-2-additional-categories.notes: Each additional category adds audit cost and effort. Confidentiality is the natural next addition; Privacy is often customer-owned in customer-hosted deployments and may be excluded with DC8 rationale.

oq-soc-2-observation-period.question: What observation period does PrivateBox commit to for its first Type II report?
oq-soc-2-observation-period.decision_owner: Top Management + Audit / Risk / Tech committee
oq-soc-2-observation-period.notes: Source pack notes public reports range from 3-month to 12-month observation periods. Shorter periods get a report faster but with explicit period caveat.

oq-soc-2-subservice-treatment.question: What is PrivateBox's default subservice organisation treatment — carve-out or inclusive?
candidate_answers: Carve-out by default (reduces audit scope; customer obtains subservice assurance)
oq-soc-2-subservice-treatment.notes: Carve-out is standard for most subservices. Inclusive is rare and only where PB has significant operational control.

oq-soc-2-customer-distribution.question: How does PrivateBox distribute SOC 2 reports to customers?
candidate_answers: Trust Center / portal under NDA (Workday / Microsoft / Anthropic pattern)
Per-customer access via legal review per request
oq-soc-2-customer-distribution.notes: Trust Center model is procurement-friendly and scales. NDA pattern matters for SOC 2 distribution by AICPA distribution restrictions.

oq-soc-2-readiness-disclosure.question: How does PrivateBox disclose SOC 2 readiness pre-first-attestation?

candidate_answers.Aligned-to: PB has implemented controls aligned to SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria; first audit in progress

candidate_answers.In progress: PB is preparing for first SOC 2 attestation; readiness review complete

oq-soc-2-readiness-disclosure.notes: Source pack flags marketing language as a top risk. Pre-attestation phrasing must avoid implying a report has been issued.

oq-nist-rmf-profile-choice.question: Does PrivateBox attest against AI RMF general profile only, or also activate GenAI Profile (NIST AI 600-1)?
candidate_answers: GenAI Profile activated (covers PB's generative features explicitly)
Both — general baseline plus GenAI overlay on chat / summarisation features
oq-nist-rmf-profile-choice.notes: PB's primary feature set is generative. GenAI Profile activation strengthens procurement story for high-stakes use cases but adds CBRN / confabulation / IP-specific evidence requirements.

oq-nist-ssdf-attestation-timing.question: When does PrivateBox submit the CISA SSDF attestation form (M-22-18 / M-23-16)?
candidate_answers: Wait until attestation form path stabilises (current status uncertain post-2025)
oq-nist-ssdf-attestation-timing.notes: M-22-18 attestation form path has been in flux 2024-2026. Verify current CISA / OMB requirements before submission.

oq-nist-sbom-tooling.question: Which SBOM generation and signing tooling does PrivateBox standardise on?
oq-nist-sbom-tooling.notes: Syft+Cosign is the de facto standard for CycloneDX / SPDX SBOMs and signature attestation. Choice affects PS.3.2 implementation depth.

oq-nist-model-registry-tool.question: Which model registry / approval tooling does PrivateBox use?
candidate_answers: Custom internal registry with manual approval workflow
oq-nist-model-registry-tool.decision_owner: AI Security Lead + Head of AI
oq-nist-model-registry-tool.notes: 218A explicitly requires controlled model registry. Tooling choice affects every model-related 218A overlay item.

oq-nist-redteam-cadence.question: What is PrivateBox's AI red-team cadence?
candidate_answers: Annual external red-team + continuous internal regression
Triggered by significant model change only
oq-nist-redteam-cadence.notes: 218A overlay emphasises adversarial testing. Cadence affects MEASURE 2.7 evidence depth.

oq-nist-third-party-model-workflow.question: What is the documented workflow for approving a new third-party / foundation model?
candidate_answers: Pre-sales + engineering + security + legal joint review (full)
oq-nist-third-party-model-workflow.notes: GOV 6.1 + 218A third-party model governance are the highest-leverage AI supply-chain controls. Workflow rigour affects every downstream model decision.
